project_goal.value: CLI tool for NGO field coordinators to bulk-export, deduplicate, and compress beneficiary registration records from offline SQLite databases before syncing to a central server
project_goal.rationale: Core sync bottleneck in low-bandwidth humanitarian deployments requiring an automated export pipeline

target_users.value: NGO field coordinators operating Ubuntu laptops in remote camps with intermittent connectivity
target_users.rationale: Primary operators identified in field research sessions

project_class.rationale: Runs on field laptops as an invocable command; no browser UI required

capabilities.rationale: Core capabilities are local data processing, SQLite access, and manual/scheduled batch export

complexity_level.rationale: Deduplication logic and AES-256 compression pipeline add non-trivial complexity above simple data extraction

risk_level.rationale: Handles personal data of vulnerable displaced beneficiaries under GDPR and UNHCR guidelines

data_sensitivity.rationale: Beneficiary records contain names, national IDs, biometric hashes, and displacement histories

external_exposure.rationale: Tool runs on NGO-managed field hardware; no public endpoints exposed

access_model.value: JWT coordinator authentication validated before any PII-reading subcommand
access_model.rationale: Prevents unauthorized bulk extraction of beneficiary PII from shared field laptops

feature_scope.value: Export unsynced records, deduplicate by national ID, validate completeness, compress to gzip JSONL, sign SHA-256 manifest, generate rejection report
feature_scope.rationale: Full export pipeline features for field operations

mvp_scope.value: Export, deduplicate by national ID, validate required fields, compress with AES-256 encryption, rejection log
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for Q2 pilot camp deployments

security_baseline.value: JWT authentication, AES-256-GCM archive encryption, SHA-256 manifest signatures, no plaintext PII in log files
security_baseline.rationale: GDPR and UNHCR minimum controls for beneficiary data portability

frontend_stack.value: N/A - CLI only, no browser UI
frontend_stack.rationale: cli_tool project class requires no frontend runtime

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Click CLI framework; sqlite3 stdlib module for database access
backend_stack.rationale: Click provides composable subcommand structure; stdlib sqlite3 avoids external dependencies on air-gapped laptops

data_platform.value: SQLite 3 on local disk for source records; gzip-compressed JSONL as export transport format
data_platform.rationale: SQLite runs without a server process; JSONL provides line-delimited streaming ingestion on central server

hosting_target.value: Bare-metal field laptops running Ubuntu 22.04 LTS; distributed as Python wheel via internal PyPI mirror
hosting_target.rationale: No cloud hosting for the tool itself; runs entirely offline at runtime

privacy_retention_policy.value: Export archives purged 72 hours after sync confirmation; rejection reports PII-redacted after 30 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR data minimization and UNHCR retention guidelines for beneficiary data

future_scope.value: Biometric hash deduplication, incremental delta exports, direct SFTP push to central server
future_scope.rationale: Wave 2 features planned after field pilot validation

constraints.value: Must run offline on Ubuntu 22.04 with Python 3.12; no internet dependency at runtime; wheel under 50MB
constraints.rationale: Field laptops have no reliable connectivity; storage is constrained

observability_baseline.value: Structured JSON logs to rotating local file; export run summaries to stdout; Prometheus pushgateway on sync events when connectivity available
observability_baseline.rationale: Ops team monitors sync health via Grafana during connectivity windows

execution_preference.value: Single-command invocation with sensible defaults; supports --dry-run, --verbose, and --config-file flags
execution_preference.rationale: Field coordinators are not developers; CLI must be operable with minimal training

compliance_context.value: GDPR Article 9 for sensitive personal data of displaced persons; UNHCR data protection guidelines; organizational data-sharing agreements with host governments
compliance_context.rationale: Beneficiary records are special-category data requiring explicit compliance controls

project.goal: Bulk-export, deduplicate, and compress beneficiary registration records for low-bandwidth sync
project.target_users: NGO field coordinators on Ubuntu field laptops

security.auth: JWT token validation before PII-reading subcommands

devops.ci: GitHub Actions matrix on ubuntu-22.04 Python 3.12
devops.hosting: Python wheel on internal PyPI mirror

reasoner_reviews.product: The export pipeline addresses a genuine operational bottleneck where field coordinators currently copy SQLite files manually and lose track of which records have been synchronized. Deduplication by national ID is the right primary key for MVP; biometric hash dedup should be Wave 2 once hash generation is standardized across offices.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Click's command group architecture cleanly separates export, validate, and dedup subcommands into domain modules. Using only stdlib sqlite3 and hashlib avoids any external runtime dependencies that could break on air-gapped machines. The gzip+JSONL format is correct: human-inspectable after unzip and line-delimited for streaming ingestion on the central server.
reasoner_reviews.security: JWT authentication must be validated before any subcommand that reads PII. The export archive must be AES-256-GCM encrypted in-place before writing to final path to eliminate the window where plaintext beneficiary data sits in a temp directory. SHA-256 manifests allow the central server to detect tampering during SFTP transit.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 50MB wheel constraint is achievable with pure Python and Click. The offline-first constraint means all deduplication must be self-contained without calling a remote API. Rejection report PII redaction must run autonomously via cron without requiring coordinator action.

backend.service_design: Single installable Python package 'bene-sync' exposing a Click CLI with three command groups: export (pull, validate, compress), dedup (run, report), and admin (status, purge). Each group maps to a domain module in the package.
backend.business_modules: beneficiary_reader.py reads unsynced beneficiary_records and registration_events from SQLite using last_synced_at IS NULL predicate; deduplicator.py builds in-memory dict keyed by normalized national_id retaining latest registration_event per duplicate cluster; validator.py checks required fields against configurable JSON schema and emits ValidationResult with field-level errors; compressor.py streams validated records to gzip JSONL tempfile encrypted with AES-256-GCM then renames to final export path; manifest_signer.py computes SHA-256 over record IDs and timestamps.
backend.failure_handling: Atomic write pattern: compress to tempfile, validate archive integrity, then rename to final path. On validator failure batch is not written and coordinator sees field-by-field rejection counts. SQLite reads wrapped in try/finally to release fcntl.flock.
backend.backend_risks: Concurrent exports on a shared laptop can corrupt sync_manifests table; mitigated by fcntl.flock acquired at export start. National ID normalization inconsistencies across Arabic and Somali scripts may cause missed duplicates; mitigated by configurable Unicode NFC normalization rules in config.yml.

security.auth_design: JWT stored in ~/.bene-sync/credentials.json, loaded at CLI startup, validated against embedded public key. Token expiry enforced; expired token prompts re-authentication via 'bene-sync auth login' on next connectivity window.
security.secrets_management: AES-256-GCM passphrase derived from JWT sub claim plus device hardware ID via PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256; never stored to disk.
security.privacy_controls: PII fields (name, dob, national_id) never written to log files; logs reference record UUIDs only. Rejection reports include field names and error codes but not PII values. Post-30-day cron redacts remaining rejection report PII.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Structured JSON log to ~/.bene-sync/logs/bene-sync.log with rotation at 10MB retaining 3 files. Each export run logs: coordinator_id, office_id, record_count, duplicate_count, rejection_count, archive_path_hash, manifest_id, run_duration_ms.

data.entities: beneficiary_records (id UUID PK, national_id TEXT NOT NULL, name_encrypted TEXT, dob_encrypted TEXT, registration_office_id TEXT, biometric_hash TEXT NULL, last_synced_at DATETIME NULL, created_at DATETIME). registration_events (id UUID PK, beneficiary_id UUID FK, event_type ENUM intake/update/exit, recorded_by TEXT, event_date DATE, details_json TEXT, synced BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE). sync_manifests (id UUID PK, office_id TEXT, export_timestamp DATETIME, record_ids_hash TEXT, record_count INT, archive_filename TEXT, status ENUM pending/uploaded/confirmed). deduplication_logs (id UUID PK, manifest_id UUID FK, canonical_record_id UUID, duplicate_ids_json TEXT, resolution_reason TEXT, resolved_at DATETIME). export_batches (id UUID PK, manifest_id UUID FK, record_count INT, rejected_count INT, compressed_size_bytes INT, created_at DATETIME).
data.storage_design: Source: existing SQLite 3 databases on field laptops. Export: gzip JSONL archives in ~/.bene-sync/exports/. Dedup state: in-memory during run, summary written to deduplication_logs in local SQLite.
data.retention_and_deletion: Archives purged 72 hours after manifest status=confirmed via daily cron. Rejection reports PII-redacted after 30 days via daily cron installed by bene-sync installer.

devops.deployment_topology: Python wheel built via GitHub Actions on ubuntu-22.04, published to internal PyPI mirror on version tag push. Connected laptops install via pip; offline laptops receive wheel via USB transfer procedure.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: matrix test on ubuntu-22.04 Python 3.12. Pytest suite with SQLite fixture databases. Build wheel with python -m build. Publish to internal PyPI on tag push.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus pushgateway metrics: bene_sync_export_total, bene_sync_duplicate_total, bene_sync_rejection_total, bene_sync_last_export_unixtime. Grafana dashboard: last export time per office, daily export volume, rejection rate per office.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing an offline-first CLI tool for humanitarian field operations where connectivity is intermittent and beneficiary data is GDPR-sensitive special-category personal data. Key decisions: Click for composable subcommand groups; stdlib-only runtime dependencies to guarantee operation on air-gapped Ubuntu laptops; AES-256-GCM archive encryption before disk write to eliminate plaintext PII window in temp directory; fcntl.flock to prevent concurrent exports on shared laptops.
target_output.title: Beneficiary Sync CLI Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: An offline-first Python 3.12 CLI tool installable on NGO Ubuntu field laptops that extracts unsynced beneficiary registration records from local SQLite databases, deduplicates entries by normalized national ID, validates field completeness against configurable schemas, and produces AES-256-GCM encrypted gzip-JSONL export batches signed with SHA-256 manifests ready for upload to the central humanitarian data server.
target_output.architecture_overview: Single-process Python 3.12 CLI packaged as a wheel and invoked as 'bene-sync export' on field laptops. Click command groups structure the pipeline into three domains: export subgroup (pull from SQLite, validate completeness, compress and encrypt), dedup subgroup (run deduplication pass, emit cluster report), and admin subgroup (status, purge confirmed archives). No network calls occur at runtime; all operations are local disk and SQLite reads. On connectivity windows a separate 'bene-sync push' command uploads signed archives via SFTP and records confirmation in sync_manifests. JWT coordinator authentication is enforced before any PII-reading subcommand, with the token public key embedded in the installed wheel.
target_output.technology_stack: Runtime: Python 3.12 with stdlib only at core (sqlite3, gzip, hashlib, json, fcntl, pathlib, logging). CLI framework: Click 8.x for composable command groups with --dry-run, --verbose, and --config-file flags. Cryptography: cryptography 42.x for AES-256-GCM archive encryption; PyJWT 2.x for coordinator token validation. Config: PyYAML for required-field schemas and normalization rules. Testing: pytest with SQLite in-memory fixture databases, Click CliRunner. Build: python-build and twine for wheel publication to internal PyPI mirror.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Export Pipeline: pull beneficiary_records and registration_events where last_synced_at IS NULL, deduplicate by normalized national_id retaining latest event per cluster, validate each record against camp-specific required-field schema, stream valid records to AES-256-GCM encrypted gzip JSONL archive named {office_id}_{timestamp}.jsonl.gz. Rejection Report: records failing validation written to rejection_report_{timestamp}.jsonl with field name and error code only, PII values excluded. Manifest Signing: SHA-256 computed over record IDs and timestamps, written to sync_manifests SQLite table and embedded in archive. Admin Commands: 'bene-sync status' displays pending archives and last sync timestamp; 'bene-sync admin purge' removes confirmed archives older than 72 hours; 'bene-sync validate --schema-file' reports field-completeness rates before export.
target_output.system_components: beneficiary_reader module: reads unsynced records from local SQLite using fcntl.flock to prevent concurrent access. deduplicator module: builds in-memory dict keyed by Unicode NFC-normalized national_id, retains most-recent registration_event per cluster, writes cluster summaries to deduplication_logs. validator module: loads required-field schema from config.yml, checks each record, emits ValidationResult with field-level errors. compressor module: streams valid records to gzip JSONL tempfile, encrypts in-place with AES-256-GCM, renames to final export path atomically. manifest_signer module: computes SHA-256 over record_id list, inserts sync_manifests row with status=pending. cron jobs installed by bene-sync installer: archive purge daily at 02:00, rejection report PII redaction daily at 03:00.
target_output.workflows: Export Workflow: coordinator runs 'bene-sync export --office-id KEN-001 --dry-run' to preview record count and duplicate estimate without writing output, then re-runs without --dry-run to produce encrypted archive. CLI acquires fcntl.flock on SQLite, reads all records where last_synced_at IS NULL, passes batch to deduplicator then validator. Validator emits rejection counts. Compressor writes tempfile, encrypts in-place, renames to exports/. manifest_signer inserts sync_manifests row status=pending. Coordinator sees: records exported, duplicates removed, records rejected, archive path, manifest ID. Validation Workflow: 'bene-sync validate --schema-file camp_schema.yml' reads a sample of beneficiary_records and emits field-completeness rates per required field, enabling data quality remediation before the actual export run.
target_output.data_model: beneficiary_records (id UUID PK, national_id TEXT NOT NULL, name_encrypted TEXT, dob_encrypted TEXT, registration_office_id TEXT, biometric_hash TEXT NULL, last_synced_at DATETIME NULL, created_at DATETIME). registration_events (id UUID PK, beneficiary_id UUID FK, event_type ENUM intake/update/exit, recorded_by TEXT, event_date DATE, details_json TEXT, synced BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE). sync_manifests (id UUID PK, office_id TEXT, export_timestamp DATETIME, record_ids_hash TEXT, record_count INT, archive_filename TEXT, status ENUM pending/uploaded/confirmed, confirmed_at DATETIME NULL). deduplication_logs (id UUID PK, manifest_id UUID FK, canonical_record_id UUID, duplicate_ids_json TEXT, resolution_reason TEXT, resolved_at DATETIME). export_batches (id UUID PK, manifest_id UUID FK, record_count INT, rejected_count INT, compressed_size_bytes INT, created_at DATETIME).
target_output.api_design: No HTTP API. Internal Python module interfaces: ExportService.run(office_id: str, dry_run: bool) -> ExportResult. DeduplicatorService.run(batch: list[dict]) -> DeduplicatedBatch. ValidatorService.validate(batch, schema_path: Path) -> ValidationResult. CompressorService.compress(valid_records, output_dir: Path, passphrase: bytes) -> Path. ManifestSigner.sign(record_ids: list[str], office_id: str) -> str. Click CLI surface: 'bene-sync export [--office-id] [--dry-run] [--config-file] [--verbose]', 'bene-sync validate [--schema-file]', 'bene-sync dedup report', 'bene-sync admin status', 'bene-sync admin purge', 'bene-sync auth login'.
target_output.security_and_compliance: GDPR Article 9 controls for special-category personal data of displaced beneficiaries processed under UNHCR data processor agreements. AES-256-GCM archive encryption executed before final disk write eliminates plaintext PII window in temp directory. Passphrase derived via PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 from JWT sub plus device hardware ID; never stored to disk. JWT coordinator authentication with embedded public key blocks any PII-reading subcommand for expired or missing tokens. SHA-256 manifest signatures allow central server to detect archive tampering. PII fields (name, dob, national_id) excluded from all log entries; logs reference UUIDs only. Rejection reports include error codes not field values for PII. fcntl.flock prevents concurrent exports producing overlapping batches. Cron redacts rejection report PII after 30 days per GDPR data minimization.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Distribution: Python wheel built via GitHub Actions on ubuntu-22.04 matrix, published to internal PyPI mirror on version tag push. Installation on connected laptops: pip install bene-sync --extra-index-url https://pypi.internal.ngo.org. Offline laptops: USB transfer of wheel plus pip install --no-index. Post-install: bene-sync installer registers cron jobs for archive purge at 02:00 and rejection report redaction at 03:00. Config stored at ~/.bene-sync/config.yml with sqlite_path, office_id, exports_dir, and schema_path. Upgrade: pull new wheel via LAN during connectivity window, reinstall. Rollback: pip install bene-sync==previous_version from cached wheel on USB.
target_output.observability: Structured JSON logs to ~/.bene-sync/logs/bene-sync.log (rotation 10MB, 3 files) with fields: coordinator_id, office_id, command, record_count, duplicate_count, rejection_count, archive_path_hash, manifest_id, run_duration_ms, status. Prometheus pushgateway metrics on connectivity: bene_sync_export_total (labels: office_id), bene_sync_duplicate_total, bene_sync_rejection_total, bene_sync_last_export_unixtime gauge. Grafana dashboard: last export time per office, daily export volume, rejection rate per office. Alert: last export timestamp exceeding 48h during declared active operation windows triggers Slack notification to field coordinator supervisor.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero cloud cost for the CLI tool itself; runs on existing field hardware. Internal PyPI mirror is a 5GB S3 bucket behind nginx on NGO intranet. Grafana and Prometheus on shared VPS at approximately $40/month. Scales to 200 field offices with no architecture change as each office SQLite is independent. Central server ingestion capacity is the eventual scale bottleneck for large simultaneous uploads.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): beneficiary_reader, validator with configurable schema, AES-256-GCM compressor, manifest_signer, basic CLI commands (export, validate, admin status, admin purge), JWT auth, structured logging, pytest suite covering happy path and rejection scenarios on 5 SQLite fixture databases, wheel publication to internal PyPI. Phase 2 (3 weeks): national_id deduplication with deduplication_logs, rejection report with PII-safe output, --dry-run flag, Prometheus pushgateway integration, cron installer. Phase 3 (4 weeks): biometric hash deduplication, delta export for records modified since last confirmed manifest, direct SFTP push subcommand with exponential-backoff retry.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python type hints throughout; mypy strict mode in CI. Pytest with SQLite in-memory fixtures for all module unit tests; 90% branch coverage target for deduplicator and validator modules. Click command tests via CliRunner without real filesystem. No external network calls in any test. PII-handling convention: any variable holding decrypted beneficiary name, dob, or national_id named with _pii suffix to flag in code review. Config loaded once at CLI startup via ConfigLoader singleton; no global mutable state.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: two coordinators triggering concurrent exports on a shared laptop produce overlapping batches and corrupt sync_manifests. Mitigation: fcntl.flock on SQLite at export start; second invocation exits with clear error and manifest ID of the active run. Risk: national_id normalization inconsistencies across Arabic and Somali scripts cause missed duplicates. Mitigation: Unicode NFC normalization as default plus configurable normalization rules in config.yml; deduplication_logs enable post-hoc cluster audit. Tradeoff: in-memory deduplication dict limits practical batch size to approximately 500K records on 4GB field laptops; accepted for MVP with streaming algorithm planned for Phase 3.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose gzip JSONL over SQLite dump for transport format because central server ingestion already streams JSONL. Confirmed SHA-256 manifest over HMAC because public-key verification avoids shared-secret management across offices. Selected Click over argparse for composable command groups. Deferred biometric hash deduplication to Phase 3 pending hash standardization.

metadata.notes: Offline-first humanitarian CLI with GDPR-compliant beneficiary dedup and AES-256 encrypted batch export.

project_goal.value: Mobile application for frontline health workers in refugee camps to record vaccination campaigns, capturing patient consent, dose administration, and adverse event reporting per WHO immunization protocols
project_goal.rationale: Replaces paper vaccination cards and enables real-time campaign monitoring for health ministries

target_users.value: Frontline health workers operating in refugee camps with intermittent connectivity
target_users.rationale: Primary operators identified in WHO field consultations

project_class.rationale: Requires native device capabilities for offline storage and digital consent signatures

capabilities.rationale: Mobile UI, REST API, patient data persistence, Cognito auth, and WHO reporting system integration

complexity_level.rationale: Offline-first sync with conflict resolution, digital consent signatures, and WHO protocol compliance add significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: Contains protected health information for vulnerable displaced persons under GDPR and WHO standards

data_sensitivity.rationale: Vaccination records, adverse event reports, and patient consent records are health data

external_exposure.rationale: Cognito authentication required for all app access; no public-facing endpoints

access_model.value: RBAC with health_worker, supervisor, and campaign_admin roles enforced via Cognito user pool groups
access_model.rationale: Supervisors can review and edit worker submissions; admins manage campaign schedules and vaccine_lots

feature_scope.value: Patient registration with digital consent, dose administration recording with lot number and site, adverse event reporting, offline-first sync, campaign schedule management, WHO indicator export
feature_scope.rationale: Complete immunization campaign workflow features

mvp_scope.value: Patient registration, consent capture with digital signature, single-dose administration recording, basic offline sync, campaign schedule view
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for Q3 camp pilot covering single-antigen campaigns

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3, Cognito MFA, encrypted local storage on device, server-side encryption at rest with KMS, PHI access audit logging
security_baseline.rationale: WHO and GDPR minimum controls for patient health records in field deployments

frontend_stack.value: React Native with TypeScript; Expo for device API access; WatermelonDB for offline-first local storage
frontend_stack.rationale: React Native enables cross-platform iOS and Android from a single codebase; WatermelonDB provides SQLite-backed offline sync

backend_stack.value: Node.js 20 with Express; TypeORM for PostgreSQL ORM; Jest for testing
backend_stack.rationale: TypeScript end-to-end with React Native frontend; Express is lightweight for API-only backend

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on AWS RDS Multi-AZ; Redis 7 on ElastiCache for session caching and sync token deduplication
data_platform.rationale: Relational model suits vaccination record relationships; Redis handles concurrent sync deduplication

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in eu-west-1; CloudFront for static assets
hosting_target.rationale: EU-west hosting for GDPR data residency; Fargate for managed containers

privacy_retention_policy.value: Vaccination records retained 10 years per WHO immunization data standards; consent records retained for patient lifetime; device local data purged after 30-day confirmed sync
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: WHO immunization data retention standards and national health ministry requirements

future_scope.value: Multi-antigen campaign scheduling, barcode scanning for vaccine_lots, SMS reminder notifications, national health registry integration
future_scope.rationale: Wave 2 features planned after pilot validation

constraints.value: Must operate offline for up to 72 hours; iOS 16+ and Android 10+ support; app size under 80MB
constraints.rationale: Camp connectivity unreliable; device support aligned with field hardware inventory

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM on ECS tasks; structured JSON logging with correlation IDs; custom campaign progress metrics; PagerDuty alerts for sync failure rates
observability_baseline.rationale: Campaign health monitoring and sync reliability are operationally critical

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints with separate mobile and backend tracks; feature flags for phased rollout to field teams
execution_preference.rationale: Team structure and phased deployment to camps

compliance_context.value: WHO immunization data standards (EIR guidelines), GDPR for EU-funded health programs, national health ministry reporting requirements, UNHCR data protection for camp populations
compliance_context.rationale: Multiple regulatory frameworks apply given multinational donor funding and cross-border deployments

project.goal: Record and track vaccination campaigns for refugee camp populations with offline-first mobile app
project.target_users: Frontline health workers in refugee camps

frontend.state: Redux Toolkit for global state, React Query for server sync

security.auth: AWS Cognito user pools with MFA and RBAC groups
security.encryption: KMS at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit, encrypted device local storage

data.cache: ElastiCache Redis 7 for sync token deduplication

reasoner_reviews.product: The scope correctly prioritizes single-antigen campaigns for MVP given the complexity of multi-antigen scheduling with dose intervals and contraindications. Digital consent capture is non-negotiable for GDPR compliance and patient rights. Offline-first is essential given that many camp deployments have zero reliable connectivity.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: WatermelonDB is the right choice for offline-first React Native; it provides a SQLite adapter with a sync protocol that maps well to the vaccination record domain. The conflict resolution strategy for concurrent health worker edits needs explicit design: last-write-wins is insufficient for medical records. Recommend versioned records with supervisor merge workflow for conflicts.
reasoner_reviews.security: PHI on-device storage must be encrypted using device keychain or equivalent; WatermelonDB stores in SQLite which is plaintext unless the underlying SQLite is encrypted with SQLCipher. Cognito MFA must be enforced even in offline scenarios by caching a time-limited offline token. Adverse event reports containing patient identifiers must never be cached in app logs.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 72-hour offline window requires careful sync token design to detect and reject duplicate submissions. App size under 80MB will require aggressive asset optimization for React Native bundle. ECS Fargate in eu-west-1 satisfies GDPR data residency for EU donor-funded projects.

backend.service_design: Express REST API organized into domain modules: patients, vaccinations, consent, adverse_events, campaigns, sync. Each module exposes versioned REST endpoints at /api/v1/{module}.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.0. Idempotency keys on POST /vaccinations and POST /adverse-events to handle duplicate sync submissions. Sync endpoint POST /sync accepts batched device payloads and returns server-side updates since last_sync_token.
backend.failure_handling: Sync endpoint wrapped in database transaction; partial batch failures roll back and return per-record error codes. Idempotency keys stored in Redis with 7-day TTL to deduplicate retried sync payloads.
backend.scaling_notes: ECS auto-scaling on CPU 60% target. Connection pooling via TypeORM with max 20 connections per task to stay within RDS limits. Read replica for campaign reporting queries.

frontend.app_structure: React Native Expo app with expo-router for file-based navigation. WatermelonDB with SQLCipher for encrypted offline storage. Background sync using expo-background-fetch when connectivity detected.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Flows: Campaign Dashboard (active campaigns list), Patient Registration (demographics plus consent signature canvas), Dose Administration (patient lookup, lot selection, site recording), Adverse Event Report (symptom checklist, severity, timestamp), Sync Status (pending records count, last sync timestamp).
frontend.state_management: Redux Toolkit for authentication state and campaign metadata. WatermelonDB reactive queries for local patient and vaccination records. React Query for server-side campaign schedule fetching.
frontend.frontend_risks: SQLCipher adds 15-20% bundle size; must be included in the 80MB app size budget. Consent signature canvas requires 'expo-signature-canvas' or custom WebView implementation tested on low-end Android 10 devices.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pools with TOTP MFA. Offline authentication via cached time-limited JWT (4-hour TTL stored in device SecureStore). On JWT expiry in offline mode, health worker can view but not submit records until re-authenticated.
security.authorization_model: RBAC via Cognito groups: health_worker (create patients, record doses, submit adverse events), supervisor (review and approve flagged records, unlock conflicts), campaign_admin (manage campaign_schedules and vaccine_lots).
security.privacy_controls: WatermelonDB backed by SQLCipher for encrypted on-device storage. Patient name and date of birth stored encrypted in PostgreSQL with AWS KMS. Adverse event reports purged from device after confirmed sync plus 7 days.

data.entities: patients (id UUID PK, camp_id TEXT, first_name_encrypted, last_name_encrypted, dob_encrypted, sex ENUM, registration_date). vaccination_events (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, vaccine_lot_id FK, campaign_id FK, health_worker_id FK, dose_number INT, administration_site ENUM, administered_at DATETIME, synced BOOLEAN). vaccine_lots (id UUID PK, antigen_code TEXT, manufacturer, lot_number, expiry_date, doses_available INT). consent_records (id UUID PK, patient_id FK, campaign_id FK, consent_given BOOLEAN, signature_data_uri TEXT, captured_by FK, captured_at DATETIME). adverse_event_reports (id UUID PK, vaccination_event_id FK, symptom_codes TEXT[], severity ENUM mild/moderate/severe, onset_at DATETIME, reported_by FK, reported_at DATETIME). campaign_schedules (id UUID PK, antigen_code, target_population, start_date, end_date, camp_id, target_doses INT, administered_doses INT).
data.storage_design: Server: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ with KMS encryption at rest. Device: WatermelonDB with SQLCipher for offline vaccination_events, patients, consent_records. Redis: sync token deduplication and session cache.
data.consistency_notes: Sync uses vector-clock-based versioning per record. Conflicts (same patient record edited by two workers offline) surfaced to supervisor dashboard for manual merge. Server is source of truth post-merge.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate in eu-west-1 with ALB. Two environments: staging (single AZ, reduced instance count) and production (Multi-AZ, auto-scaling 2-8 tasks). RDS Multi-AZ with automated failover. Mobile app distributed via TestFlight and internal MDM for Android.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: lint, Jest unit tests (80% coverage gate), build Docker image, push ECR, deploy ECS rolling update. Mobile: Expo EAS Build for iOS and Android on main branch push.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM sidecar on ECS tasks. Custom metrics: vaccination_events_synced_total, sync_conflict_rate, adverse_events_submitted_total, campaign_coverage_pct. PagerDuty alert on sync error rate exceeding 5% per 15-minute window.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing an offline-first mobile app for humanitarian vaccination campaigns where connectivity is unreliable and patient health data requires GDPR and WHO compliance. Key decisions: React Native with WatermelonDB and SQLCipher for encrypted offline storage; versioned record sync with supervisor merge for conflict resolution rather than last-write-wins; Cognito RBAC with cached offline JWT for field authentication; ECS Fargate in eu-west-1 for GDPR data residency.
target_output.title: Vaccination Campaign Mobile App Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: An offline-first React Native mobile application for frontline health workers in refugee camps to record informed consent, dose administration with vaccine lot traceability, and adverse event reports per WHO immunization protocols. Backend is a Node.js Express API on AWS ECS Fargate in eu-west-1 with PostgreSQL 16, supporting 72-hour offline operation with versioned conflict-resolution sync.
target_output.architecture_overview: Offline-first mobile architecture with a React Native Expo app using WatermelonDB backed by SQLCipher for encrypted on-device storage of vaccination_events, patients, and consent_records. When connectivity is available, a background sync process batches device records to POST /api/v1/sync, which validates idempotency keys via Redis and persists to PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ. Cognito user pools with TOTP MFA govern authentication; offline scenarios use cached 4-hour JWTs stored in device SecureStore. The Express REST API on ECS Fargate is organized into domain modules for patients, vaccinations, consent, adverse_events, campaigns, and sync. All PHI encrypted at rest with KMS and in transit with TLS 1.3. Campaign supervisors access a web dashboard on the same API for conflict resolution and WHO indicator export.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native with TypeScript, Expo SDK, expo-router, WatermelonDB with SQLCipher adapter, Redux Toolkit, React Query. Backend: Node.js 20, Express 4, TypeORM, class-validator. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ with KMS encryption. Cache: ElastiCache Redis 7 for sync idempotency tokens and session cache. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate eu-west-1, ALB, CloudFront, ECR, Cognito user pools. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, Expo EAS Build. Monitoring: Datadog APM, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Patient Registration: health worker registers displaced patient with demographics and captures informed consent as digital signature stored in consent_records. Dose Administration: worker selects campaign_schedule, searches patient, selects vaccine_lot, records administration site and dose number; vaccination_events entry created locally and queued for sync. Adverse Event Reporting: worker files adverse_event_report with symptom codes, severity, and onset time linked to vaccination_event_id. Offline Sync: background sync batches pending local records to server when connectivity detected; idempotency prevents duplicate submissions on retry. Campaign Dashboard: supervisor views campaign_coverage_pct per camp, reviews flagged conflicts, approves or merges contested patient records. WHO Indicator Export: admin exports campaign_schedules aggregates in WHO EIR-compatible CSV format.
target_output.system_components: React Native App: WatermelonDB SQLCipher local store, Redux Toolkit auth slice, expo-background-fetch sync trigger, expo-router navigation, consent signature canvas. Express API: patients module, vaccinations module (idempotent POST with Joi validation), sync module (batch ingest with Redis idempotency check), campaigns module, adverse_events module. PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ: primary with read replica for reporting. ElastiCache Redis 7: sync token TTL store, session cache. ECS Fargate: ALB with target group health checks, auto-scaling 2-8 tasks on CPU 60%. Cognito: user pools with TOTP MFA, groups for RBAC. Datadog: APM sidecar, custom vaccination metrics dashboard.
target_output.workflows: Dose Administration Workflow: health worker opens Campaign Dashboard, selects active campaign_schedule for measles-rubella, searches patient by name or registration number, confirms patient identity, selects vaccine_lot from available_lots list, records injection site as left deltoid, taps 'Record Dose'. App creates vaccination_events row in WatermelonDB with synced=false. Next connectivity window: background sync packages pending vaccination_events and consent_records into batch payload with device_id and last_sync_token, POSTs to /api/v1/sync. Server validates idempotency key in Redis, inserts records into PostgreSQL, returns new sync_token and any server-side campaign updates. Device marks records synced=true. Adverse Event Workflow: within 48 hours of a dose, worker opens patient record, selects 'Report Adverse Event', completes symptom checklist (fever, swelling, anaphylaxis indicators), records severity as moderate, submits. adverse_event_reports row created locally and synced to server on next connectivity window. Supervisor notified via in-app alert.
target_output.data_model: patients (id UUID PK, camp_id TEXT, first_name_encrypted TEXT, last_name_encrypted TEXT, dob_encrypted TEXT, sex ENUM male/female/other, registration_date DATE, created_by UUID FK). vaccination_events (id UUID PK, patient_id UUID FK, vaccine_lot_id UUID FK, campaign_id UUID FK, health_worker_id UUID FK, dose_number INT, administration_site ENUM left_deltoid/right_deltoid/left_thigh/right_thigh, administered_at DATETIME, device_id TEXT, synced BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, version INT DEFAULT 1). vaccine_lots (id UUID PK, antigen_code TEXT, manufacturer TEXT, lot_number TEXT UNIQUE, expiry_date DATE, doses_available INT). consent_records (id UUID PK, patient_id UUID FK, campaign_id UUID FK, consent_given BOOLEAN, signature_data_uri TEXT, captured_by UUID FK, captured_at DATETIME). adverse_event_reports (id UUID PK, vaccination_event_id UUID FK, symptom_codes TEXT[], severity ENUM mild/moderate/severe, onset_at DATETIME, reported_by UUID FK, reported_at DATETIME, synced BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE). campaign_schedules (id UUID PK, antigen_code TEXT, target_population TEXT, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, camp_id TEXT, target_doses INT, administered_doses INT DEFAULT 0).
target_output.api_design: RESTful API at /api/v1. POST /patients (register patient, returns patient_id). GET /patients/:id (profile with vaccination history). POST /vaccinations (record dose, idempotency-key header required). POST /sync (batch upload with last_sync_token, returns new sync_token and server updates). GET /campaigns (active campaign_schedules for worker camp). POST /adverse-events (report with vaccination_event_id). GET /campaigns/:id/coverage (supervisor: doses administered vs target). GET /campaigns/:id/export?format=who_eir (WHO EIR CSV export). Auth: Cognito JWT validated at ALB and Express middleware. Rate limit: 200 req/min per user.
target_output.security_and_compliance: WHO EIR compliance: vaccination_events include antigen_code, lot_number, dose_number, administration_site per EIR data dictionary. GDPR compliance: patient PHI (name, dob) encrypted with AWS KMS; data processing agreements with camp operators; consent captured per GDPR Article 7 with digital signature and timestamp. On-device: WatermelonDB backed by SQLCipher prevents plaintext patient data on device storage. Authentication: Cognito TOTP MFA enforced; offline mode uses 4-hour cached JWT in device SecureStore; expired offline JWT blocks record submission. RBAC via Cognito groups prevents health_workers from modifying campaign_schedules or vaccine_lots. PHI access audit logged to CloudTrail and structured application logs with patient_id and health_worker_id.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Infrastructure: ECS Fargate in eu-west-1 Multi-AZ (eu-west-1a, eu-west-1b) behind ALB with target group health checks. Auto-scaling: 2-8 tasks on CPU 60% target. RDS Multi-AZ with automated failover. GitHub Actions CI: lint, Jest with 80% coverage gate, Docker build, ECR push, ECS rolling deploy. Mobile CI: Expo EAS Build on main branch push producing iOS IPA and Android APK. Staging environment: single AZ, 1-2 tasks, RDS single-AZ. Production secrets in AWS Secrets Manager with 30-day rotation. Database migrations via TypeORM migration runner in ECS one-off task before deploy.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM sidecar on each ECS task tracing Express request lifecycle. Structured JSON logs with correlation IDs: health_worker_id, campaign_id, patient_id (hashed for PHI), action. Custom metrics: vaccination_events_synced_total (counter, labels: camp_id, antigen_code), sync_conflict_rate (gauge), adverse_events_submitted_total, campaign_coverage_pct per schedule. PagerDuty alerts: sync error rate exceeding 5% per 15-minute window, RDS connection utilization above 80%, API p99 latency above 3 seconds.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $900/month for 50 concurrent health workers across 5 camps. ECS auto-scaling handles campaign launch spikes. RDS read replica provisioned only during end-of-campaign WHO export generation. ElastiCache Redis cache.t3.small sufficient for sync token deduplication volume. EAS Build credits approximately $50/month for mobile CI. Cost grows linearly with camp count; 50 camps estimated at $4000/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Patient registration with digital consent signature, single-dose administration recording with vaccine_lot selection, basic offline sync with idempotency, Cognito auth with RBAC, ECS Fargate deployment, GitHub Actions CI, Expo EAS Build, structured logging, Jest unit tests. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Adverse event reporting, sync conflict detection with supervisor merge dashboard, campaign_schedule view on mobile, WHO EIR export endpoint, WatermelonDB sync optimization for 72-hour offline window. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Multi-antigen campaign scheduling with dose intervals, barcode scanning for vaccine_lot selection, push notifications for campaign alerts via Expo push.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode for both React Native and Express. ESLint with React Native community config. Jest for backend unit tests with 80% coverage gate on vaccination and sync modules. Detox for React Native E2E tests covering dose administration and offline sync scenarios. PHI handling: patient_id and health_worker_id in all log events; never log name, dob, or signature_data_uri. WatermelonDB schema migrations require backward-compatible changes until 100% of field devices have updated. Feature flags via LaunchDarkly for phased rollout to individual camps.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: SQLCipher adds significant build complexity and 15-20% to app bundle size; mitigated by early inclusion in CI and monitoring against 80MB budget. Risk: 72-hour offline sync creates large batch payloads on reconnect that may exceed API timeout; mitigated by chunked sync with batch_size=100 records per request. Risk: Consent signature data_uri bloats WatermelonDB local storage on low-storage Android 10 devices; mitigated by compressing signature PNG before storage and purging from device after confirmed sync plus 7 days. Tradeoff: version-based conflict detection over last-write-wins adds supervisor workflow complexity but is required for medical record integrity.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose WatermelonDB over Realm for offline storage due to open-source licensing and SQLCipher integration. Confirmed eu-west-1 over us-east-1 for GDPR data residency. Selected Expo EAS Build over bare React Native for easier cross-platform CI. Decided on supervisor merge workflow over last-write-wins for vaccination record conflicts.

metadata.notes: Offline-first vaccination campaign mobile app with WHO compliance, GDPR PHI protection, and versioned conflict resolution.

project_goal.value: Web application for humanitarian organizations to manage grant applications, track funding cycles, and report on impact metrics to institutional donors including USAID, EU, and UN agencies
project_goal.rationale: Replaces fragmented spreadsheet-based grant tracking and enables automated compliance reporting to major institutional donors

target_users.value: Program staff at implementing partner organizations and grant officers at institutional donor agencies
target_users.rationale: Dual audience identified in discovery: partners submit and report; donors review and approve

project_class.rationale: Multi-tenant web application with distinct partner and donor portal experiences

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, admin_panel, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Requires portal UI, REST API, financial data persistence, Azure AD auth, impact dashboards, admin tools, and donor system integrations

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-tenant RBAC, multi-stage approval workflows, financial variance detection, and USAID/EU reporting format integrations are individually complex

risk_level.rationale: Handles confidential grant financial data subject to USAID audit and EU financial regulation

data_sensitivity.rationale: Grant budgets, disbursements, and expenditure reports contain sensitive financial program data

external_exposure.rationale: Implementing partner organizations and donor agencies access the platform from outside the organizational network

access_model.value: Multi-tenant RBAC via Azure AD B2C with tenant isolation per partner organization; donor users access read-only grant views scoped to their funded programs
access_model.rationale: Partners must not see each other financial data; donors see only programs they fund

feature_scope.value: Grant application submission with logical framework, multi-stage donor review, budget breakdown tracking, expenditure reporting with variance detection, impact indicator updates, narrative reports, audit trail, donor dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: Complete grant lifecycle management features

mvp_scope.value: Grant application submission, two-stage review (program officer plus donor approval), budget tracking, basic expenditure reporting, audit trail
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for pilot with 3 partner organizations and 1 donor agency in Q3

security_baseline.value: Azure AD B2C with MFA, row-level security for tenant isolation, TLS 1.3, field-level encryption for budget figures, immutable audit_trails table
security_baseline.rationale: USAID ADS and EU financial regulation require strong access controls and audit evidence

frontend_stack.value: Next.js 14 with TypeScript and App Router; Tailwind CSS; shadcn/ui components
frontend_stack.rationale: Next.js SSR improves load time for document-heavy grant forms; App Router enables server components for report pages

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Django REST Framework; Celery for async background jobs
backend_stack.rationale: Django ORM with row-level security extensions fits the multi-tenant financial data model; Celery for variance detection and report generation jobs

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server with row-level security; Redis 7 on Azure Cache for Redis
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL RLS enforces tenant isolation at the database level; Azure-native services for Azure ecosystem cohesion

hosting_target.value: Azure Kubernetes Service in westeurope region; Azure Container Registry; Azure Blob Storage for report documents
hosting_target.rationale: AKS for container orchestration; westeurope for EU data residency compliance

privacy_retention_policy.value: Grant financial records retained 7 years per EU financial regulation; audit_trails retained 10 years; personal data of named staff in narratives anonymized after 5 years
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: USAID ADS 591 and EU financial regulation retention requirements

future_scope.value: Automated USAID FFATA report generation, EU financial regulation XML export, M&E framework integration with KoboToolbox, multi-currency disbursement tracking
future_scope.rationale: Wave 2 compliance automation and monitoring integration features

constraints.value: Must meet SOC 2 Type II within 18 months; all data must reside in westeurope Azure region; support for IE11 not required
constraints.rationale: SOC 2 required by major donor agency procurement; EU data residency for GDPR

observability_baseline.value: Azure Monitor with Application Insights; structured logging to Log Analytics workspace; Azure Alerts for approval queue backlog and expenditure variance anomalies
observability_baseline.rationale: Azure-native monitoring stack aligned with AKS deployment

execution_preference.value: Azure DevOps pipelines with 2-week sprints; separate frontend and backend teams; staging environment mirrors production for donor UAT
execution_preference.rationale: Azure DevOps aligns with organizational tooling; separate tracks for parallel delivery

compliance_context.value: USAID Automated Directives System (ADS) financial management requirements, EU financial regulation 2018/1046, SOC 2 Type II, GDPR for EU-funded project staff personal data
compliance_context.rationale: Multiple donor regulatory frameworks require demonstrable financial controls and audit evidence

project.goal: Manage grant application lifecycle, funding cycles, and impact reporting for institutional donors
project.target_users: Program staff at partner organizations and grant officers at donor agencies
project.timeline: Q3 pilot with 3 partners and 1 donor

frontend.state: React Query for server state, Zustand for form state

security.auth: Azure AD B2C with MFA and tenant isolation
security.encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit, field-level encryption for budget figures
security.compliance: USAID ADS, EU financial regulation, SOC 2, GDPR

data.db: PostgreSQL 16 Azure Flexible Server with RLS

reasoner_reviews.product: The grant lifecycle scope is well-defined for MVP with application submission and two-stage review. Variance detection against approved budgets is the highest-value feature for donor compliance and should be implemented in Phase 1 even if the UI is basic. Narrative reports with structured impact indicators are distinct from financial expenditure reports and should have separate data models.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Django REST Framework with PostgreSQL row-level security is a sound choice for multi-tenant financial data. RLS policies must be set at the session level via SET LOCAL app.current_tenant_id to ensure isolation holds even for superuser connections in migrations. Celery workers for variance detection and PDF report generation avoid blocking the API request cycle for long-running compliance tasks.
reasoner_reviews.security: Tenant isolation must be enforced at three layers: Azure AD B2C tenant claims in the JWT, Django middleware setting the RLS tenant variable, and PostgreSQL RLS policies on grant_applications, disbursements, and expenditure_reports. The immutable audit_trails table must use a PostgreSQL trigger to prevent UPDATE and DELETE. SOC 2 requires comprehensive logging of all data access events.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: westeurope region satisfies GDPR data residency for EU-funded projects. SOC 2 Type II readiness requires formalizing change management procedures and access review processes from project inception; cannot be retrofitted in month 16.

backend.service_design: Django project with apps: grants (application lifecycle), finance (budgets, disbursements, expenditure), reporting (narratives, impact indicators), orgs (implementing partners, donor organizations), and audit (immutable audit trail). DRF serializers with nested validation for budget line items.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful at /api/v1/. Nested resources: /grants/{id}/budget-lines, /grants/{id}/expenditures, /grants/{id}/reports. Bulk endpoints for expenditure uploads. Webhook receiver for Azure AD B2C login events.
backend.background_jobs: Celery tasks: compute_budget_variance (triggered on expenditure submission; flags line items exceeding 10% of approved budget), generate_pdf_report (async PDF generation via WeasyPrint), send_review_notifications (email via SendGrid on stage transitions), anonymize_old_pii (monthly job for records older than 5 years).
backend.failure_handling: Idempotent Celery tasks with task_id stored in expenditure_reports. Failed variance computation retried 3 times with exponential backoff. PDF generation failures logged to Application Insights and reported to submitting user.

frontend.app_structure: Next.js 14 App Router with parallel routes for partner portal (/partner/...) and donor portal (/donor/...). Server components for read-heavy report pages. Client components for multi-step grant application form.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Partner Portal: Grant Application Wizard (5-step: objectives, logical framework, budget breakdown, team, attachments), Expenditure Report submission with line-item variance preview, Narrative Report editor, Impact Indicator update form. Donor Portal: Funded Programs dashboard, Grant detail with expenditure vs budget chart, Approval queue with comment thread.
frontend.state_management: React Query for all server data with 60-second stale time. Zustand store for multi-step application form draft state persisted to sessionStorage. Optimistic updates for approval status changes.

security.auth_design: Azure AD B2C with custom policies for partner and donor user journeys. MFA enforced via Conditional Access. JWT contains tenant_id and role claims validated in Django middleware.
security.authorization_model: Partner roles: program_officer (submit grants, submit reports), finance_officer (submit expenditures, view budget), org_admin (manage users). Donor roles: grant_officer (review and approve), observer (read-only). Cross-tenant: donors see only grant_applications where funded_by=donor_org_id.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Immutable audit_trails table with PostgreSQL trigger blocking UPDATE/DELETE. Logs: user_id, tenant_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, ip_address, timestamp. Application Insights for API request logs. Azure Monitor alert on failed login spikes.

data.entities: grant_applications (id UUID PK, tenant_id, title, donor_org_id FK, status ENUM draft/submitted/under_review/approved/rejected/closed, submitted_at). budget_lines (id UUID PK, grant_id FK, cost_category TEXT, description TEXT, approved_amount NUMERIC, currency TEXT). disbursements (id UUID PK, grant_id FK, amount NUMERIC, currency TEXT, disbursed_at DATE, reference_number TEXT). expenditure_reports (id UUID PK, grant_id FK, reporting_period_start DATE, reporting_period_end DATE, status ENUM draft/submitted/approved, submitted_by FK, submitted_at). expenditure_line_items (id UUID PK, report_id FK, budget_line_id FK, amount_spent NUMERIC, variance_pct NUMERIC, flagged BOOLEAN). impact_indicators (id UUID PK, grant_id FK, indicator_code TEXT, baseline_value NUMERIC, target_value NUMERIC, current_value NUMERIC, unit TEXT, updated_at). narrative_reports (id UUID PK, grant_id FK, reporting_period TEXT, executive_summary TEXT, challenges TEXT, lessons_learned TEXT, document_blob_url TEXT). audit_trails (id UUID PK, tenant_id, user_id, action, resource_type, resource_id, ip_address, created_at). donor_organizations (id UUID PK, name, country, primary_contact_email, reporting_format ENUM usaid_ffata/eu_financial_regulation/un_standard). implementing_partners (id UUID PK, tenant_id, org_name, country, registration_number, compliance_status).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 Azure Flexible Server with RLS policies keyed on app.current_tenant_id session variable. Azure Blob Storage for narrative_report attachments and PDF exports. Redis for session cache and Celery broker.
data.retention_and_deletion: grant_applications and financials retained 7 years. audit_trails retained 10 years. Named staff PII in narrative_reports anonymized after 5 years via Celery monthly task.

devops.deployment_topology: AKS cluster in westeurope with 3-node system pool and workload node pools for API and Celery workers. Horizontal Pod Autoscaler on API deployment targeting CPU 70%. Azure Container Registry for images. Ingress via nginx ingress controller with cert-manager for TLS.
devops.ci_cd_design: Azure DevOps Pipelines: lint, pytest with 80% coverage gate, Docker build, push ACR, Helm chart deploy to AKS. Separate pipeline for Next.js build and Azure Static Web Apps deploy for frontend.
devops.observability_stack: Azure Monitor with Application Insights SDK in Django and Next.js. Log Analytics workspace for structured logs. Custom metrics: grant_approvals_total, expenditure_variance_flags_total, approval_queue_depth. Alert: approval_queue_depth > 20 for more than 2 hours.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a multi-tenant grant management web application with financial data sensitivity and partner-facing exposure to institutional donors. Key architectural decisions: PostgreSQL row-level security as the primary tenant isolation mechanism enforced at session level; immutable audit_trails via database trigger for SOC 2 evidence; Celery for async variance detection and PDF generation to keep API responsive; Azure-native stack for GDPR data residency in westeurope.
target_output.title: Humanitarian Grant Management Platform Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A multi-tenant Next.js and Django REST Framework web application deployed on Azure AKS westeurope for humanitarian organizations to manage the full grant lifecycle from application through expenditure reporting and impact indicator tracking. PostgreSQL row-level security enforces tenant isolation for financial data shared between implementing partners and institutional donors. Supports USAID ADS, EU financial regulation 2018/1046, SOC 2 Type II, and GDPR compliance requirements.
target_output.architecture_overview: Multi-tenant web application with a Next.js 14 App Router frontend serving distinct partner and donor portal experiences. The Django REST Framework API backend runs on AKS with PostgreSQL 16 row-level security enforcing per-tenant financial data isolation at the database session level via SET LOCAL app.current_tenant_id. Celery workers handle async tasks: budget variance computation triggered on expenditure submission, PDF narrative report generation, and reviewer notification emails. Azure AD B2C governs authentication with Conditional Access MFA; JWT tenant_id and role claims flow through Django middleware to activate RLS. An immutable audit_trails table with a PostgreSQL trigger blocking UPDATE/DELETE provides SOC 2 and USAID audit evidence. All financial and grant data resides in Azure westeurope for GDPR compliance.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Next.js 14 with TypeScript, App Router, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui, React Query, Zustand. Backend: Python 3.12, Django 4.2, Django REST Framework, Celery with Redis broker, WeasyPrint for PDF generation. Database: PostgreSQL 16 Azure Flexible Server with RLS, pgcrypto for field-level encryption of budget figures. Cache/Broker: Azure Cache for Redis 7. Infrastructure: AKS westeurope, Azure Container Registry, nginx ingress with cert-manager, Azure Blob Storage, Azure AD B2C. CI/CD: Azure DevOps Pipelines. Monitoring: Azure Monitor, Application Insights, Log Analytics workspace.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Grant Application Wizard: 5-step form (objectives, logical framework, budget_lines with cost categories, team members, attachments); auto-saved draft in Zustand to sessionStorage. Multi-Stage Review: application transitions through draft/submitted/under_review/approved/rejected with comment threads at each stage; Celery tasks notify reviewers by email. Budget Variance Detection: Celery task compute_budget_variance triggered on expenditure_report submission; flags expenditure_line_items exceeding 10% of approved budget_line amount; flagged items appear in donor approval dashboard. Impact Indicator Tracking: program staff update current_value per indicator_code tied to grant logical framework; indicators displayed in donor dashboard with baseline and target. Narrative Report Submission: rich-text editor for executive summary, challenges, and lessons learned; PDF generated async via WeasyPrint and stored in Azure Blob.
target_output.system_components: Next.js 14 App Router: parallel route groups /partner and /donor, server components for report pages, client components for multi-step grant form, React Query cache with 60-second stale time. Django REST API: apps grants, finance, reporting, orgs, audit; RLS middleware setting session variable; DRF serializers with nested budget_line validation. Celery Workers: separate AKS deployment, Redis broker, tasks for variance computation, PDF export, email notifications, PII anonymization. PostgreSQL 16 with RLS: policies on grant_applications, budget_lines, disbursements, expenditure_reports keyed on tenant_id session variable; trigger on audit_trails blocking modification. Azure Blob Storage: narrative report attachments and generated PDF exports with SAS token access. Azure AD B2C: partner and donor user journeys with Conditional Access MFA.
target_output.workflows: Grant Application Workflow: program_officer at partner org logs in via Azure AD B2C, starts new grant application for EU funding cycle, completes 5-step wizard entering program objectives, logical framework with impact_indicators baseline values, budget_lines with cost categories and amounts, team members, and uploads attachments to Azure Blob. Submits application; status transitions to submitted; Celery sends email to assigned donor grant_officer. Grant officer reviews application in donor portal, adds comment requesting budget justification, transitions status to under_review. Program officer responds and resubmits. Grant officer approves; status set to approved; disbursement_schedule created. Expenditure Reporting Workflow: at end of reporting period, finance_officer submits expenditure_report with expenditure_line_items mapped to approved budget_lines. Celery task compute_budget_variance calculates variance_pct for each line item; items exceeding 10% threshold flagged=true. Donor grant_officer sees flagged line items highlighted in their review dashboard with variance amounts. Finance officer provides explanatory narrative; grant officer approves report.
target_output.data_model: grant_applications (id UUID PK, tenant_id UUID NOT NULL, title TEXT, donor_org_id UUID FK, funding_cycle_id UUID FK, status ENUM draft/submitted/under_review/approved/rejected/closed, submitted_at DATETIME). budget_lines (id UUID PK, grant_id UUID FK, cost_category TEXT, description TEXT, approved_amount NUMERIC(14,2), currency CHAR(3)). disbursements (id UUID PK, grant_id UUID FK, amount NUMERIC(14,2), currency CHAR(3), disbursed_at DATE, reference_number TEXT). expenditure_reports (id UUID PK, grant_id UUID FK, reporting_period_start DATE, reporting_period_end DATE, status ENUM draft/submitted/approved, submitted_by UUID FK, submitted_at DATETIME). expenditure_line_items (id UUID PK, report_id UUID FK, budget_line_id UUID FK, amount_spent NUMERIC(14,2), variance_pct NUMERIC(5,2), flagged BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE). impact_indicators (id UUID PK, grant_id UUID FK, indicator_code TEXT, baseline_value NUMERIC, target_value NUMERIC, current_value NUMERIC, unit TEXT, updated_at DATETIME). narrative_reports (id UUID PK, grant_id UUID FK, reporting_period TEXT, executive_summary TEXT, challenges TEXT, lessons_learned TEXT, document_blob_url TEXT). audit_trails (id UUID PK, tenant_id UUID, user_id UUID, action TEXT, resource_type TEXT, resource_id UUID, ip_address INET, created_at DATETIME). donor_organizations (id UUID PK, name TEXT, country TEXT, reporting_format ENUM usaid_ffata/eu_financial_regulation/un_standard). implementing_partners (id UUID PK, tenant_id UUID, org_name TEXT, country TEXT, registration_number TEXT).
target_output.api_design: RESTful at /api/v1. GET /grants (list, filtered by status and donor); POST /grants (create application); PATCH /grants/:id/status (stage transition with comment); GET /grants/:id/budget-lines; POST /grants/:id/budget-lines (bulk create); POST /grants/:id/expenditure-reports (submit); GET /grants/:id/expenditure-reports/:rid (with flagged line items); POST /grants/:id/impact-indicators/update (bulk update current values); GET /grants/:id/narrative-reports; POST /grants/:id/narrative-reports (submit, triggers async PDF); GET /donors/:id/dashboard (summary: active grants, pending approvals, total disbursed). Auth: Azure AD B2C JWT; tenant_id claim enforced in middleware. Rate limit: 300 req/min per org tenant.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Tenant isolation at three layers: Azure AD B2C tenant_id claim in JWT, Django RLSMiddleware setting SET LOCAL app.current_tenant_id per request, PostgreSQL RLS policies on all financial tables. Immutable audit_trails table protected by trigger blocking UPDATE/DELETE; provides SOC 2 Type II audit evidence and USAID ADS audit trail. Budget figures in budget_lines encrypted with pgcrypto using org-specific keys to limit breach exposure. TLS 1.3 for all client and inter-service traffic. Conditional Access MFA for all Azure AD B2C users. Monthly anonymization of named staff PII in narrative_reports older than 5 years. EU financial regulation variance controls enforced programmatically by compute_budget_variance Celery task.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: AKS westeurope cluster: system node pool (3 nodes Standard_D2s_v3) plus API workload pool (auto-scaling 2-6 nodes) and Celery worker pool (auto-scaling 1-4 nodes). Deployments managed via Helm charts in Azure DevOps Pipelines: lint, pytest 80% coverage gate, Docker build, push ACR, helm upgrade --install. Database migrations via Django management command in pre-deploy Kubernetes Job. Secrets in Azure Key Vault mounted via CSI driver. Staging environment: single-AZ PostgreSQL, 1-replica AKS deployment for donor UAT. Production: PostgreSQL Flexible Server with standby in paired region. Rollback: Helm rollback to previous chart revision.
target_output.observability: Application Insights SDK in Django (opencensus-ext-django) and Next.js for distributed tracing. Log Analytics workspace receiving structured JSON logs with fields: tenant_id, user_id, action, resource_type, grant_id, duration_ms. Custom metrics: grant_approvals_total (counter, labels: donor_org), expenditure_variance_flags_total, approval_queue_depth (gauge per donor_org), pdf_generation_duration_ms. Azure Alerts: approval_queue_depth exceeding 20 for 2 hours triggers email to operations team; Celery task failure rate above 5% triggers PagerDuty.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $1200/month for 20 active partner organizations and 5 donor agencies. AKS auto-scaling handles peak reporting period load. PostgreSQL Flexible Server compute scales independently of storage. Azure Blob Storage costs grow with narrative report volume at approximately $0.02/GB/month. Celery worker pool scales to zero during off-peak periods. Projected $3500/month at 100 partner organizations.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): grant application wizard, two-stage review with comment thread, budget_lines tracking, basic expenditure submission with variance detection, immutable audit_trail, Azure AD B2C with RBAC, AKS deployment, Azure DevOps CI pipeline, Application Insights. Phase 2 (6 weeks): impact_indicator tracking, narrative_report with async PDF export, donor dashboard with coverage charts, expenditure report approval workflow, flagged variance notification emails. Phase 3 (6 weeks): USAID FFATA automated report export, EU financial regulation XML export, SOC 2 Type II evidence package automation, multi-currency disbursement tracking.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python type hints throughout Django with mypy strict mode. DRF serializers validate all financial amounts as NUMERIC with explicit currency. pytest with 80% coverage gate on finance and grants apps; factory_boy for test fixture generation. React Testing Library for Next.js component tests. Every grant status transition must go through a state machine service class, not direct ORM updates, to ensure audit_trail entries are always written. Tenant context must be explicitly asserted in all pytest database tests to prevent cross-tenant data leakage in the test suite.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: PostgreSQL RLS policies not activated in migration scripts could expose cross-tenant financial data during schema changes. Mitigation: dedicated migration test suite asserting RLS tenant isolation with two-tenant fixtures. Risk: Celery variance computation delays may leave expenditure reports in unvalidated state during Redis outages. Mitigation: Redis Sentinel or Azure Cache for Redis with geo-replication; variance computation can be retried idempotently. Tradeoff: WeasyPrint PDF generation is single-threaded and slow for large narrative reports; accepted at MVP scale; will migrate to headless Chrome rendering at 100+ concurrent report submissions. Risk: SOC 2 evidence collection not formalized from day 1. Mitigation: audit_trails schema and access review process designed in Phase 1.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose Django DRF over FastAPI for mature multi-tenant ORM support and RLS integration. Confirmed PostgreSQL RLS over application-layer filtering as the primary isolation control. Selected Azure-native monitoring over Datadog to align with AKS deployment and reduce cross-cloud costs. Decided on WeasyPrint over Puppeteer for PDF generation to avoid Node.js dependency in Python service.

metadata.notes: Multi-tenant humanitarian grant management platform with PostgreSQL RLS, SOC 2, USAID/EU compliance, and immutable audit trails.

project_goal.value: Research prototype exploring decomposition of a monolithic humanitarian case management system into microservices for needs assessment, shelter allocation, food distribution, and psychosocial support tracking, each independently deployable across field offices
project_goal.rationale: Current monolith cannot be independently scaled or deployed per service domain; field offices need to operate subsets of the system during connectivity outages

target_users.value: Platform engineers and solution architects at the humanitarian organization evaluating microservices feasibility
target_users.rationale: This is a research prototype for internal architecture evaluation, not an end-user product

project_class.rationale: Purpose is architectural feasibility exploration and pattern validation, not production deployment

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, devops, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Prototype must demonstrate full stack including inter-service communication, auth propagation, and event-driven integrations

complexity_level.rationale: Microservices decomposition with event-driven async communication and distributed data ownership is architecturally high-scale even for a prototype

risk_level.rationale: Confidential displaced person data in a prototype context with controlled internal access; lower operational risk than production but still requires data protection

data_sensitivity.rationale: Prototype uses synthetic but structurally realistic displaced person case data requiring organizational confidentiality controls

external_exposure.rationale: Prototype runs on internal GCP project accessible only to engineering team

access_model.value: Keycloak OIDC with service-level RBAC; each microservice validates JWT and enforces its own authorization rules
access_model.rationale: Demonstrates auth propagation pattern across microservice boundaries for production design evaluation

feature_scope.value: Case registration service, needs assessment service, shelter allocation service, food distribution tracking service, psychosocial support tracking service, event bus routing, consolidated case timeline UI, inter-service saga patterns
feature_scope.rationale: Five domain microservices plus orchestration infrastructure sufficient to evaluate decomposition patterns

mvp_scope.value: Case registration, needs assessment, and shelter allocation microservices with Pub/Sub event bus; basic consolidated case timeline UI; Keycloak auth propagation
mvp_scope.rationale: Three services sufficient to demonstrate event-driven choreography and data ownership patterns

security_baseline.value: Keycloak OIDC JWT propagation, mTLS between microservices via Istio service mesh, network policies restricting cross-namespace traffic
security_baseline.rationale: Demonstrates production-viable inter-service security pattern for internal evaluation

frontend_stack.value: React 18 with TypeScript; Vite for development; React Query for inter-service data aggregation
frontend_stack.rationale: Lightweight frontend for prototype; React Query handles multi-service API calls on the case timeline

backend_stack.value: Go 1.22 with Gin for all microservices; gRPC for synchronous inter-service calls; GCP Pub/Sub for async events
backend_stack.rationale: Go provides low memory footprint per service and fast startup for GKE pods; Gin is minimal overhead REST; Pub/Sub for durable event delivery

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 per microservice (database-per-service pattern); Redis 7 for event deduplication and saga state
data_platform.rationale: Database-per-service isolation is the core microservices data ownership pattern being evaluated

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot cluster in a dedicated GCP project; separate Kubernetes namespace per microservice
hosting_target.rationale: GKE Autopilot manages node provisioning; namespace isolation reflects future production topology

privacy_retention_policy.value: Synthetic case data only; no real displaced person records; data purged at prototype retirement
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Prototype uses structurally realistic but entirely synthetic data; no real beneficiary PII

future_scope.value: Saga orchestration for multi-service workflows, service mesh observability with Kiali, eventual consistency conflict resolution patterns, field-office autonomous operation mode
future_scope.rationale: Patterns identified in prototype will inform production migration roadmap

constraints.value: Prototype must complete within 10 weeks; team of 3 engineers; GCP project budget under $500/month
constraints.rationale: Time-boxed research engagement with limited team and budget

observability_baseline.value: Grafana and Prometheus on GKE; distributed tracing via Jaeger; per-service dashboards for event lag and saga success rate
observability_baseline.rationale: Observability is a key evaluation criterion for the microservices pattern assessment

execution_preference.value: 3 engineers working in parallel on separate microservices with weekly architecture syncs; GitLab CI for each service repository
execution_preference.rationale: Parallel development validates independent deployability of services

compliance_context.value: UNHCR data protection standards for displaced person case data; GDPR for EU-funded program scope; organizational confidentiality policies restricting prototype data to named engineering team members
compliance_context.rationale: Even synthetic data that mirrors real case structures requires organizational data governance controls

project.goal: Evaluate microservices decomposition patterns for humanitarian case management system
project.target_users: Platform engineers and architects at humanitarian organization

frontend.state: React Query for multi-service data aggregation
frontend.purpose: Consolidated case timeline UI demonstrating BFF aggregation pattern

backend.framework: Gin REST plus gRPC for inter-service sync calls
backend.messaging: GCP Pub/Sub for async domain events

security.auth: Keycloak OIDC JWT propagation across service boundaries

data.event_dedup: Redis 7 for idempotency keys and saga state

reasoner_reviews.product: The five-service decomposition maps cleanly to the monolith domain boundaries. For the prototype, the most valuable patterns to demonstrate are choreography-based saga (case registration triggers downstream needs assessment and shelter allocation events) and the consolidated timeline BFF pattern that aggregates data across service boundaries without shared database access. Limit Phase 1 to three services to validate the event bus and auth propagation before adding food distribution and psychosocial services.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Database-per-service with PostgreSQL 16 per namespace is the right choice to demonstrate true domain data ownership. Each service should publish domain events to its own Pub/Sub topic; the case timeline BFF subscribes to all five topics to build the aggregated view. gRPC for synchronous calls where latency matters (shelter availability check before allocation) and Pub/Sub for fire-and-forget domain events. Istio service mesh adds complexity for a prototype but is necessary to demonstrate the mTLS pattern.
reasoner_reviews.security: JWT propagation via X-Forwarded-Auth or direct Bearer token re-use across service boundaries needs explicit design. Recommend the downstream services validate the same Keycloak JWT rather than issuing new service tokens, to demonstrate the simpler propagation pattern first. Kubernetes NetworkPolicies must restrict each namespace to only ingress from Istio ingress gateway and named service namespaces.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: GKE Autopilot at $500/month limits the prototype to development-scale pod sizing. PostgreSQL 16 per service is expensive at full size; recommend Cloud SQL instances shared-core (1 vCPU) for the prototype. Three engineers in parallel on separate service repos validates independent deployability as a prototype success criterion.
