backend.service_design: Five Go microservices each in its own GitLab repository and Kubernetes namespace: case-registration-svc, needs-assessment-svc, shelter-allocation-svc, food-distribution-svc, psychosocial-svc. Each exposes a Gin REST API and publishes domain events to its own Pub/Sub topic.
backend.api_patterns: REST at /api/v1/ within each service. Synchronous gRPC for shelter availability check (shelter-allocation-svc exposes ShelterService.CheckAvailability RPC). Pub/Sub topics: case.registered, needs_assessment.completed, shelter_allocation.assigned, food_distribution.event_recorded, psychosocial.session_recorded.
backend.business_modules: case-registration-svc: registers displaced person case, publishes case.registered event with case_id and triage_priority. needs-assessment-svc: subscribes to case.registered, creates needs_assessments record, publishes needs_assessment.completed with service_referrals list. shelter-allocation-svc: subscribes to needs_assessment.completed if shelter referral present, calls own availability API, allocates shelter_allocations record, publishes shelter_allocation.assigned.
backend.failure_handling: At-least-once Pub/Sub delivery with Redis idempotency keys keyed by (topic, message_id) to handle duplicate events. Saga failure: if shelter-allocation-svc cannot find available shelter, publishes shelter_allocation.failed event; case-registration-svc marks case status=pending_shelter. Dead letter topic for messages exceeding 5 delivery attempts.

frontend.app_structure: React 18 SPA with Vite served from GKE nginx deployment. BFF aggregation: React Query calls GET /timeline/:case_id on a dedicated bff-svc that calls case-registration-svc, needs-assessment-svc, and shelter-allocation-svc and assembles a chronological service_events list.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Case Registration form submitting to case-registration-svc REST API. Case Timeline view showing service_events from all microservices in chronological order. Needs Assessment detail panel. Shelter Allocation status panel.
frontend.performance_notes: BFF aggregates with Promise.all() for parallel service calls. Timeline renders incrementally as services respond. Cache-Control headers on BFF responses since case history is append-only.

security.auth_design: Keycloak OIDC with realm for prototype team. React app authenticates via Authorization Code PKCE. JWT propagated as Bearer token to BFF and downstream services. Each Go service validates JWT signature against Keycloak JWKS endpoint.
security.authorization_model: Single realm with roles: case_worker (create cases, record services), supervisor (view all cases), admin (manage Keycloak users). Each microservice enforces its own role checks on the shared JWT claims.
security.security_risks: JWT propagation from BFF to downstream services means compromise of BFF allows impersonation. Mitigated for prototype by Istio mTLS and NetworkPolicy restricting BFF egress to named service namespaces only.

data.entities: case-registration-svc DB: cases (id UUID PK, external_reference TEXT, registration_date DATE, triage_priority ENUM low/medium/high, status ENUM active/closed, registered_by TEXT, field_office_id TEXT). needs-assessment-svc DB: needs_assessments (id UUID PK, case_id UUID, assessed_at DATETIME, shelter_needed BOOLEAN, food_needed BOOLEAN, psychosocial_needed BOOLEAN, assessed_by TEXT). service_referrals (id UUID PK, assessment_id UUID FK, service_type ENUM shelter/food/psychosocial, priority INT, notes TEXT). shelter-allocation-svc DB: shelter_allocations (id UUID PK, case_id UUID, unit_id UUID FK, allocated_at DATETIME, end_date DATE, status ENUM active/expired/vacated). shelter_units (id UUID PK, field_office_id TEXT, capacity INT, occupied INT, location TEXT). food-distribution-svc DB: food_distribution_events (id UUID PK, case_id UUID, event_date DATE, ration_type TEXT, quantity NUMERIC, distribution_point TEXT). psychosocial_sessions DB: psychosocial_sessions (id UUID PK, case_id UUID, session_date DATE, session_type ENUM individual/group, facilitator_id TEXT, notes_encrypted TEXT).
data.storage_design: One Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 shared-core instance per microservice namespace. No shared tables across service boundaries. Events carry case_id as correlation key for BFF aggregation.
data.migration_notes: Prototype migration scripts in each service repo under /migrations. golang-migrate for schema versioning. Each service runs migrations at startup via InitDB() in main.go.

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot cluster in a single GCP project. One Kubernetes namespace per microservice (case-registration, needs-assessment, shelter-allocation, food-distribution, psychosocial) plus shared namespaces: istio-system, monitoring, keycloak. Istio sidecar injection enabled on service namespaces.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI per service: golangci-lint, go test ./..., docker build, push GCR, kubectl set image for rolling deploy to GKE. Feature branch deploys to dev namespace; main branch deploys to prototype namespace.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scraping Go /metrics endpoints (service-level: event_consumer_lag, saga_success_total, saga_failure_total, grpc_request_duration_seconds). Grafana dashboards per service. Jaeger for distributed trace sampling across Pub/Sub message chains.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a research prototype to validate microservices decomposition patterns for a humanitarian case management monolith. Core architectural decisions: database-per-service with PostgreSQL 16 per namespace to enforce domain data ownership; choreography-based saga via GCP Pub/Sub for needs triage triggering downstream service intakes; Istio mTLS for inter-service security; JWT propagation using shared Keycloak JWT rather than service tokens to demonstrate the simpler pattern first; BFF aggregation service for the consolidated case timeline to avoid frontend coupling to five service APIs.
target_output.title: Humanitarian Case Management Microservices Decomposition Prototype
target_output.executive_summary: A time-boxed 10-week research prototype on GKE Autopilot validating the decomposition of a monolithic humanitarian case management system into five domain microservices (case registration, needs assessment, shelter allocation, food distribution, psychosocial support) communicating via GCP Pub/Sub choreography and gRPC synchronous calls. Demonstrates database-per-service data ownership, Keycloak JWT propagation across service boundaries, and Istio mTLS. Produces architectural decision records for the production migration roadmap.
target_output.architecture_overview: Five Go 1.22 microservices each in its own GitLab repository and Kubernetes namespace on GKE Autopilot, communicating via GCP Pub/Sub for domain events and gRPC for synchronous service calls requiring low-latency responses. case-registration-svc accepts new displaced person case registrations and publishes case.registered events. needs-assessment-svc subscribes and creates a needs assessment, publishing needs_assessment.completed with service_referrals. shelter-allocation-svc subscribes to shelter referrals, calls its own availability endpoint, and allocates a shelter_units record, publishing shelter_allocation.assigned. food-distribution-svc and psychosocial-svc each independently process their domain referral events. A BFF aggregation service assembles the consolidated case timeline by calling all five service REST APIs in parallel. React 18 frontend communicates exclusively with the BFF. Keycloak OIDC JWT propagated as Bearer token through all service calls; each service validates against Keycloak JWKS. Istio service mesh enforces mTLS between all service-to-service calls within the cluster.
target_output.technology_stack: Microservices: Go 1.22 with Gin REST framework and google/uuid. Inter-service sync: gRPC with protobuf (shelter availability check). Event bus: GCP Pub/Sub with at-least-once delivery. Idempotency: Redis 7 keyed by (topic, message_id) for duplicate event handling. Databases: PostgreSQL 16 Cloud SQL shared-core instance per service. Auth: Keycloak 23 with OIDC; golang-jwt for JWT validation in each service. Service mesh: Istio 1.20 with mTLS in STRICT mode on service namespaces. Frontend: React 18, TypeScript, Vite, React Query. Infrastructure: GKE Autopilot, GCR, Pub/Sub, Cloud SQL. CI/CD: GitLab CI per repository. Observability: Prometheus, Grafana, Jaeger.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Case Registration: case_worker submits displaced person demographics and triage_priority to case-registration-svc; case record created; case.registered Pub/Sub event emitted with case_id and triage_priority. Needs Triage: needs-assessment-svc consumes case.registered, creates needs_assessments record with shelter/food/psychosocial flags, emits needs_assessment.completed with service_referrals list. Shelter Allocation: shelter-allocation-svc consumes shelter referral events, checks shelter_units availability via internal availability API, allocates unit, emits shelter_allocation.assigned or shelter_allocation.failed saga compensation event. Food Distribution Recording: food-distribution-svc consumes food referral event, records ration distribution events independently. Psychosocial Session Tracking: psychosocial-svc records session notes (encrypted) independently. Consolidated Timeline: BFF aggregates events from all five services into chronological service_events list for case worker UI.
target_output.system_components: case-registration-svc: Gin REST API, cases Postgres table, Pub/Sub publisher for case.registered. needs-assessment-svc: Pub/Sub subscriber for case.registered, needs_assessments and service_referrals Postgres tables, publisher for needs_assessment.completed. shelter-allocation-svc: Pub/Sub subscriber, gRPC ShelterService server, shelter_allocations and shelter_units Postgres tables, publisher for shelter_allocation.assigned/failed. food-distribution-svc: Pub/Sub subscriber, food_distribution_events Postgres table. psychosocial-svc: Pub/Sub subscriber, psychosocial_sessions Postgres table with encrypted notes. bff-svc: Go service aggregating parallel REST calls to all five services, assembling case timeline. Keycloak: OIDC provider in keycloak namespace. Istio: sidecar injection on service namespaces, mTLS in STRICT mode. Prometheus + Grafana + Jaeger: monitoring namespace.
target_output.workflows: Case Intake and Triage Workflow: case_worker submits POST /api/v1/cases to case-registration-svc with demographics, triage_priority=high, field_office_id. Service creates cases row, publishes case.registered event to GCP Pub/Sub topic 'case-events'. needs-assessment-svc receives event (idempotency key checked in Redis), creates needs_assessments row with shelter_needed=true, food_needed=true, psychosocial_needed=false based on triage interview data, creates two service_referrals rows, publishes needs_assessment.completed event. shelter-allocation-svc receives shelter referral event, calls internal CheckAvailability gRPC on shelter_units, finds available unit in camp-7-block-C, inserts shelter_allocations row, publishes shelter_allocation.assigned. food-distribution-svc receives food referral event, records food_distribution_events row for initial ration. Case Timeline View Workflow: case_worker opens case timeline in React app; app calls GET /api/v1/timeline/:case_id on bff-svc; BFF fires parallel HTTP requests to all five service /cases/:id/events endpoints; assembles service_events sorted by created_at; returns unified timeline in single response. React Query renders incrementally.
target_output.data_model: case-registration-svc DB: cases (id UUID PK, external_reference TEXT, first_name_synthetic TEXT, registration_date DATE, triage_priority ENUM low/medium/high, status ENUM active/pending_shelter/closed, registered_by TEXT, field_office_id TEXT, created_at DATETIME). needs-assessment-svc DB: needs_assessments (id UUID PK, case_id UUID NOT NULL, assessed_at DATETIME, shelter_needed BOOLEAN, food_needed BOOLEAN, psychosocial_needed BOOLEAN, assessed_by TEXT). service_referrals (id UUID PK, assessment_id UUID FK, service_type ENUM shelter/food/psychosocial, priority INT). shelter-allocation-svc DB: shelter_allocations (id UUID PK, case_id UUID, unit_id UUID FK, allocated_at DATETIME, end_date DATE, status ENUM active/expired). shelter_units (id UUID PK, field_office_id TEXT, capacity INT, occupied INT, block_reference TEXT). food-distribution-svc DB: food_distribution_events (id UUID PK, case_id UUID, event_date DATE, ration_type TEXT, quantity NUMERIC, distribution_point TEXT). psychosocial-svc DB: psychosocial_sessions (id UUID PK, case_id UUID, session_date DATE, session_type ENUM individual/group, facilitator_id TEXT, notes_encrypted TEXT, created_at DATETIME).
target_output.api_design: Per-service REST APIs at /api/v1. case-registration-svc: POST /cases, GET /cases/:id, GET /cases/:id/events. needs-assessment-svc: GET /assessments/:case_id, GET /cases/:id/events. shelter-allocation-svc: GET /allocations/:case_id, GET /shelter-units/:office_id/availability, GET /cases/:id/events; gRPC ShelterService.CheckAvailability(field_office_id) -> AvailableUnits. bff-svc: GET /timeline/:case_id (aggregated service_events). All services: Keycloak JWT validated via Authorization header. Pub/Sub topics: case-events (case.registered), needs-events (needs_assessment.completed), shelter-events (shelter_allocation.assigned, shelter_allocation.failed), food-events (food_distribution.event_recorded), psychosocial-events (psychosocial.session_recorded).
target_output.security_and_compliance: UNHCR data protection: prototype uses entirely synthetic case data generated via Faker; no real displaced person records. Organizational confidentiality: access to GCP project restricted to 3 named engineers via IAM. Keycloak OIDC with Authorization Code PKCE for React app; JWT Bearer propagated to all downstream services and validated against JWKS endpoint in each Go service. Istio mTLS in STRICT mode on all service namespaces prevents unencrypted inter-service traffic. Kubernetes NetworkPolicies restrict each namespace to ingress from Istio ingress gateway and explicitly named upstream namespaces only; default-deny all egress except required GCP APIs. psychosocial_sessions notes encrypted at application layer before PostgreSQL storage.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GKE Autopilot cluster with five service namespaces (case-registration, needs-assessment, shelter-allocation, food-distribution, psychosocial) plus istio-system, keycloak, monitoring, and bff namespaces. Istio sidecar injection via namespace labels. Each service deployed via GitLab CI: golangci-lint, go test ./..., Docker multi-stage build, push GCR, kubectl set image rolling update. Database migrations via golang-migrate run at Pod startup. Keycloak deployed via Bitnami Helm chart in keycloak namespace. Prototype retirement procedure: delete GCP project to purge all synthetic data. Budget control: GKE Autopilot spot nodes for non-production pods; Cloud SQL shared-core (1 vCPU, 3.75GB) per service.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scraping /metrics on each Go service (default Go runtime metrics plus custom: events_consumed_total by topic, saga_success_total, saga_failure_total, shelter_availability_grpc_duration_seconds, event_consumer_lag). Grafana dashboards per service showing event consumer lag, saga success rate, and API request rate. Jaeger distributed tracing with Pub/Sub message headers carrying trace context (traceparent W3C header); end-to-end traces from case.registered event through shelter_allocation.assigned visible in Jaeger UI. Alert: saga_failure_total rate above 10% per 5-minute window triggers Slack notification.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $320/month for prototype on GKE Autopilot spot nodes and 5x Cloud SQL shared-core instances at $35/instance/month plus Pub/Sub, Redis, and GCR storage. Well within $500/month budget. Prototype not designed for scale; each service handles 100 concurrent cases sufficient for architecture validation with synthetic load generation. Scaling notes documented in ADRs for production sizing.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Prototype Core (6 weeks): case-registration-svc, needs-assessment-svc, shelter-allocation-svc with Pub/Sub choreography and gRPC availability check; Keycloak JWT propagation; bff-svc with parallel aggregation; React 18 case timeline UI; Istio mTLS; GitLab CI for each service; PostgreSQL per service; Prometheus and Grafana. Phase 2 Completion (3 weeks): food-distribution-svc, psychosocial-svc with encrypted session notes; Jaeger distributed tracing across Pub/Sub chains; dead letter topic handling; saga compensation (shelter_allocation.failed) demonstration; load generation scripts with k6. Phase 3 Documentation (1 week): architecture decision records for each pattern evaluated; decomposition recommendations report; production migration roadmap draft.
target_output.development_guidelines: Each microservice repository follows the same Go project layout: cmd/server/main.go, internal/handler, internal/service, internal/repository, internal/events. golangci-lint with errcheck and staticcheck in CI. go test ./... with -race flag. Each service must expose /healthz and /readyz endpoints for Kubernetes probes. Pub/Sub consumer must check Redis idempotency key before processing each message; failure to insert idempotency key means duplicate message, skip processing. No shared Go modules between service repositories to enforce domain boundaries; duplication of small types is acceptable.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Pub/Sub at-least-once delivery with Redis idempotency adds operational complexity for a prototype team of 3. Mitigation: standardized idempotency middleware library shared via internal Go module. Risk: 5 Cloud SQL instances at $35/month each consumes 35% of budget; if additional services are added, budget is exceeded. Mitigation: evaluate Cloud SQL single instance with separate schemas as an alternative for prototype cost control. Tradeoff: choreography-based saga over orchestration-based saga chosen to demonstrate simpler pattern first; orchestration complexity deferred to production design phase. Risk: Istio installation on GKE Autopilot may require specific version pinning; mitigated by using Google-managed Istio (ASM) instead of open-source Istio.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose GCP Pub/Sub over Kafka for event bus to avoid running Kafka cluster in prototype budget. Confirmed database-per-service over shared PostgreSQL with schema isolation to demonstrate full data ownership. Selected Go for all services over polyglot stack to reduce prototype complexity. Chose JWT propagation (shared token) over service-to-service tokens for simpler auth pattern demonstration.

metadata.notes: Humanitarian case management microservices decomposition prototype with GCP Pub/Sub choreography, database-per-service, Istio mTLS, and Keycloak JWT propagation.

project_goal.value: Public-facing landing page for a disaster relief fund-matching platform connecting individual donors with vetted NGOs responding to active crises, featuring real-time campaign progress bars and embeddable donation widgets via a public API
project_goal.rationale: Provides donor-facing entry point for crisis fund matching without managing donor transactions directly

target_users.value: Individual donors and NGO partners embedding the donation widget on their own sites
target_users.rationale: Dual audience identified in product discovery

project_class.rationale: Static HTML/JS page with no server-side runtime; campaign data fetched client-side from public API

capabilities.rationale: Static page with client-side analytics; no backend, no database, no auth required

complexity_level.rationale: Static deployment with API calls to external fund-matching platform; no server-side logic

risk_level.rationale: No PII collected; no authentication; read-only public data display

data_sensitivity.rationale: Landing page displays only public campaign data; no user data stored

external_exposure.rationale: Public-facing landing page accessible without authentication

access_model.value: No authentication required; fully public read access; donation widget initiates redirect to external donation processor
access_model.rationale: Landing page is a marketing and awareness surface, not an authenticated application

feature_scope.value: Crisis event hero section, campaign progress bars pulling live data from public API, featured NGO cards, embeddable donation widget iframe, CMS-driven content updates, Vercel Analytics
feature_scope.rationale: Complete landing page feature set

mvp_scope.value: Static hero section, CMS-driven crisis event cards, campaign progress bars from public API, basic analytics
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum viable for launch with editorial CMS updates

security_baseline.value: Content Security Policy headers, no inline scripts, HTTPS enforced via Vercel, no user data collection
security_baseline.rationale: Standard static site security posture; no sensitive data to protect

frontend_stack.value: Astro 4 with TypeScript; Tailwind CSS; content from Contentful CMS via build-time API; Vercel Edge Network for hosting
frontend_stack.rationale: Astro zero-JS-by-default is ideal for a mostly static landing page; Contentful for editorial control

backend_stack.value: No server-side runtime; campaign progress data fetched client-side from external public API
backend_stack.rationale: landing_page project class requires no backend runtime

data_platform.value: No database; Contentful CMS as content source at build time; external public API for live campaign data
data_platform.rationale: No server-side data storage required for a static landing page

hosting_target.value: Vercel Edge Network with automatic CDN distribution; custom domain via Vercel DNS
hosting_target.rationale: Vercel provides zero-config static hosting with global edge distribution

privacy_retention_policy.value: No personal data collected or stored; Vercel Analytics uses cookieless tracking; privacy policy page discloses API calls to external platform
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: No data retention obligations for a static page with cookieless analytics

future_scope.value: Crisis alert email subscribe form via third-party service, donor impact story CMS section, partner NGO application form
future_scope.rationale: Future features remain static or delegate to external services

constraints.value: No server-side runtime; no database; must score 95+ on Lighthouse performance; editorial CMS updates must not require code deployment
constraints.rationale: Static hosting constraint is firm; performance target for donor trust; CMS for non-developer editorial team

project.goal: Public landing page for disaster relief fund-matching with live campaign progress and embeddable donation widgets
project.target_users: Individual donors and NGO partners

frontend.content: Contentful CMS at build time; live campaign data via client-side fetch from public API

security.headers: Content Security Policy, X-Frame-Options for widget iframe control

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: lint, astro build, Lighthouse CI

reasoner_reviews.product: The landing page scope is well-bounded: CMS-driven crisis content plus client-side campaign progress data from the external API. The donation widget is an iframe embed pointing to the external platform; no financial transaction logic belongs in this landing page. Editorial team updating Contentful should trigger an automated Vercel rebuild via webhook, not require engineer intervention.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The previous plan round 1 incorrectly specified an Express.js API backend and a PostgreSQL database for campaign data caching, which contradicts the landing_page project class and the explicit no-server-side-runtime constraint. Round 2 must correct this: campaign progress data should be fetched client-side via the public external API, not proxied through a server. Contentful webhook should trigger Vercel redeploy for CMS content updates.
reasoner_reviews.security: CSP headers must be configured in vercel.json to allow script-src from the external donation widget iframe origin and connect-src for the public campaign API domain. No user input is collected; CSRF and XSS vectors are minimal. X-Frame-Options on the landing page itself should be SAMEORIGIN to allow the embeddable widget pattern.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Lighthouse 95+ is achievable with Astro zero-JS default; the campaign progress bar island component must be explicitly marked as client:idle to defer hydration. No server-side runtime eliminates the backend concerns from round 1.

frontend.app_structure: Astro 4 project with src/pages/index.astro as the single landing page. Astro islands pattern: CampaignProgressBar.tsx as client:idle island for live API data; all other sections render as static HTML at build time.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Single page sections: Hero (CMS crisis event title and image, full static), Featured NGOs grid (Contentful entries, static), Campaign Progress section (CampaignProgressBar island, client:idle fetching external API), Donation Widget (iframe embed to external platform), About and Partners footer (static).
frontend.performance_notes: Astro ships zero JS for static sections. CampaignProgressBar island deferred with client:idle. Images served via Astro Image component with WebP conversion and responsive srcset. Target: Lighthouse Performance 95+, LCP under 1.5s on Vercel Edge.
frontend.frontend_risks: External public API CORS policy must permit requests from the landing page origin; if not, a Vercel Edge Function rewrite may be needed as a thin proxy. However adding an Edge Function moves toward server-side logic; confirm API CORS support before committing.

security.auth_design: No authentication. Public read-only page.
security.abuse_prevention: Rate limiting not applicable for static page. CSP headers in vercel.json restrict script-src and connect-src to known origins (Contentful image CDN, external donation platform, analytics). X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN on the landing page; donation widget iframe permitted via specific frame-ancestors CSP directive.
security.security_risks: If external campaign API begins returning attacker-controlled content (supply chain), client-side rendered campaign data could display misleading crisis information. Mitigated by validating API response shape in CampaignProgressBar component before rendering.

devops.deployment_topology: Vercel project connected to GitHub repository. Automatic preview deployments on every PR branch. Production deploy on merge to main. Custom domain via Vercel DNS with automatic TLS. Contentful webhook triggers Vercel redeploy on content publish.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions on PR: eslint, astro build --check (TypeScript type check), Lighthouse CI against Vercel preview URL. Merge to main triggers Vercel production deploy automatically.
devops.observability_stack: Vercel Analytics: page views, Core Web Vitals (LCP, CLS, FID), campaign widget click events via data-analytics-event attributes. No server-side APM required. Vercel dashboard for deployment status and function errors (none expected for static site).

previous_plan.title: Disaster Relief Fund-Matching Landing Page - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: A public-facing Astro 4 landing page for the disaster relief fund-matching platform serving campaign progress bars and embeddable donation widgets. Deployed on Vercel Edge Network with Contentful CMS for editorial crisis content updates.
previous_plan.technology_stack: Astro 4, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Contentful CMS. Backend: Express.js API on ECS Fargate for campaign data caching. Database: PostgreSQL RDS for cached campaign records. Cache: Redis ElastiCache. Hosting: Vercel for frontend, AWS ECS for backend.
previous_plan.system_components: Astro static site on Vercel CDN. Express.js REST API on ECS Fargate. PostgreSQL RDS for campaign data. Redis ElastiCache for API response caching. Contentful CMS. CloudFront in front of API.
previous_plan.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI: lint, astro build, Docker build for Express API, push ECR, deploy ECS Fargate rolling update. Vercel for frontend deploy on main branch merge. ECS task definitions for API container. Fargate auto-scaling on CPU.

The plan is intentionally flawed: despite knowing about these issues, the round 2 plan output below still contains a subtle infrastructure contradiction in technology_stack (listing a server-side caching note that should not be present) and the fix_report claims ISS-003 is resolved when system_components still ambiguously references server-side caching. This demonstrates a flawed training example where the architect partially addresses issues but introduces an inconsistency between fix_report claims and actual plan content.

target_output.title: Disaster Relief Fund-Matching Landing Page Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A public-facing Astro 4 static landing page for the disaster relief fund-matching platform, deployed on Vercel Edge Network with Contentful CMS for editorial crisis content updates and client-side campaign progress bars fetching live data from the external fund-matching public API. No server-side runtime, no database, no authentication required.
target_output.architecture_overview: Fully static Astro 4 site built at deploy time with Contentful CMS content and deployed to Vercel Edge Network CDN nodes globally. Campaign progress data for active crises is fetched client-side by a CampaignProgressBar Astro island component making requests to the external fund-matching platform public API. Donation widget is an iframe embed pointing to the external platform donation flow. Contentful publish webhook triggers an automated Vercel rebuild to surface editorial CMS updates within minutes. No server-side runtime, no database, and no authentication surface exist in this architecture.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Astro 4 with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 3, Astro Image component for WebP optimization. CMS: Contentful headless CMS (build-time SDK). Live data: client-side fetch to external fund-matching public API with browser-level Cache-Control caching. Note: server-side API response caching via Redis would improve campaign data freshness under high traffic - this enhancement is deferred pending evaluation of whether a Vercel Edge Function thin proxy is warranted. Hosting: Vercel Edge Network. Analytics: Vercel Analytics (cookieless). CI: GitHub Actions with Lighthouse CI.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Crisis Hero Section: CMS-driven headline, subtext, and hero image for the currently active crisis event; updated by editorial team in Contentful triggering Vercel webhook rebuild. Featured NGO Cards: Contentful entries for vetted NGOs with organization name, crisis focus, and campaign link; rendered as static HTML at build time. Campaign Progress Bars: CampaignProgressBar Astro island (client:idle) fetching active_campaigns from external public API at page load; displays donation total, matching cap, and percentage progress. Donation Widget: iframe embed to external platform donation page with campaign_id parameter; clicking 'Donate Now' opens widget in lightbox. Analytics: Vercel Analytics tracking page views, Core Web Vitals, and data-analytics-event click attributes on NGO cards and donation widget.
target_output.system_components: Astro 4 static site builder: transforms Contentful entries and page templates into static HTML at build time. Vercel Edge Network: serves static HTML, CSS, and minimal JS from CDN edge nodes globally. CampaignProgressBar island: client-side React component fetching active_campaigns from external API; renders progress bar with donation_total and matching_cap. Contentful CMS: headless content API providing crisis_events and featured_ngos entries. External fund-matching public API: provides live active_campaigns data including donation totals and matching cap status. Redis API response cache layer: stores external API responses with 60-second TTL to reduce origin calls during traffic spikes. GitHub Actions: runs lint, build, and Lighthouse CI on pull requests. Vercel webhook: receives Contentful publish events and triggers production redeploy.
target_output.workflows: Crisis Content Update Workflow: editorial team publishes updated crisis_event entry in Contentful for the Turkana flood response; Contentful sends webhook POST to Vercel deploy hook URL; Vercel triggers rebuild pulling updated Contentful entries via build-time SDK; rebuilt static HTML deployed to all edge nodes within 3 minutes; updated hero section and NGO cards appear without engineer involvement. Campaign Progress Update Workflow: donor opens landing page; browser loads static HTML from Vercel CDN; CampaignProgressBar island hydrates after page idle; component calls GET https://api.fundmatch.example.org/v1/campaigns?status=active; external API returns active_campaigns list with donation_total and matching_cap_remaining; progress bars render with real-time donation percentages; donor clicks donation widget iframe and completes donation on external platform; landing page updates progress bars on next page load or client-side polling interval.
target_output.data_model: No server-side data model. Client-side data shapes from external API: active_campaigns [{id, ngo_id, crisis_event_id, title, donation_total, matching_cap, matching_cap_remaining, currency, progress_pct}]. Contentful content types: crisis_event (title, description, hero_image, active_ngo_campaign_ids[]). featured_ngo (org_name, logo, crisis_focus, campaign_url, verification_status). donation_milestone (campaign_id, milestone_amount, milestone_label). No database tables, no server-side entities.
target_output.api_design: No server-side API. Client-side integration only. External public API calls (browser to external origin): GET https://api.fundmatch.example.org/v1/campaigns?status=active (returns active_campaigns array). GET https://api.fundmatch.example.org/v1/campaigns/:id (individual campaign detail for widget). Contentful Content Delivery API calls at build time: GET /spaces/:space_id/entries?content_type=crisis_event. GET /spaces/:space_id/entries?content_type=featured_ngo. If external API CORS policy blocks direct browser requests, a Vercel Edge Function rewrite at /api/campaigns proxying the external API may be added; this would be a thin proxy only with no database.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Content Security Policy in vercel.json: default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' https://vercel.com; connect-src 'self' https://api.fundmatch.example.org; frame-src https://donate.fundmatch.example.org; img-src 'self' https://images.ctfassets.net data:. HTTPS enforced via Vercel automatic TLS. No user data collected; Vercel Analytics is cookieless and GDPR-compliant by design. No authentication surface; no CSRF vectors. Donation transactions handled entirely by external platform under their PCI compliance scope. External API response shape validated in CampaignProgressBar before rendering to prevent supply-chain content injection.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Vercel project connected to GitHub repository with automatic preview deployments on every pull request branch and production deployment on merge to main. Custom domain configured in Vercel DNS with automatic Let's Encrypt TLS certificate. Contentful webhook configured to POST to Vercel deploy hook URL on crisis_event or featured_ngo content publish, triggering a production rebuild. GitHub Actions CI pipeline: eslint, astro build --check for TypeScript validation, Lighthouse CI run against Vercel preview URL asserting Performance score above 95. No Docker containers, no container registry, no ECS task definitions, no orchestration infrastructure involved in this deployment pipeline.
target_output.observability: Vercel Analytics: page views per session, Core Web Vitals (LCP target under 1.5s, CLS under 0.1, FID under 100ms), unique visitor counts. Custom analytics events via data-analytics-event attributes: ngo_card_click (ngo_id), donation_widget_open (campaign_id), progress_bar_view (campaign_id). Vercel deployment dashboard: deploy status, edge function invocations (none expected), error rate. No server-side APM or log aggregation required for a static site.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Vercel Pro plan at $20/month covers unlimited bandwidth for a static site. Contentful free tier supports up to 25K API calls/month at build time. External public API calls are browser-side and incur no server hosting cost. Vercel Analytics included in Pro plan. Total estimated cost: $20/month regardless of traffic scale, as Vercel Edge Network handles global CDN distribution with no per-request compute cost for static pages.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Launch (2 weeks): Astro 4 project setup, Contentful CMS space with crisis_event and featured_ngo content types, CampaignProgressBar island integrating external public API, donation widget iframe embed, Tailwind styling, Vercel deployment with Contentful webhook, GitHub Actions CI with Lighthouse assertion, Vercel Analytics. Phase 2 (2 weeks): embeddable widget iframe generation with campaign_id parameter for NGO partner sites, donor impact story section from Contentful CMS, SEO meta tags and Open Graph images generated from Contentful crisis event data. Phase 3 (future): email alert subscription form delegating to Mailchimp or ConvertKit embed, partner NGO application form handled by Typeform embed.
target_output.development_guidelines: Astro components for all static sections; no client-side JS except explicitly marked Astro islands. TypeScript strict mode; astro check in CI. All client-side fetch calls must validate response shape before rendering; use Zod for API response parsing in CampaignProgressBar. No third-party analytics scripts beyond Vercel Analytics to maintain CSP compliance. Contentful content types must have all required fields marked required in Contentful schema to prevent build failures from missing CMS data. Lighthouse CI score gates enforce Performance 95+, Accessibility 95+, Best Practices 95+ on every PR.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: external fund-matching platform public API may not support CORS for direct browser requests from the landing page origin. Mitigation: verify CORS policy before launch; if not supported, add a Vercel Edge Function thin proxy at /api/campaigns (no server runtime, just request forwarding). Risk: Contentful webhook rebuild latency means crisis content updates take 2-3 minutes to appear on the live site. Accepted tradeoff: editorial team informed of rebuild delay; suitable for crisis content which updates hourly not per-second. Risk: campaign progress data is only as fresh as the external API polling interval; browser caches may show stale totals. Mitigation: Cache-Control: no-cache on external API calls in CampaignProgressBar.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed no server-side runtime required; campaign data fetched client-side from external public API. Confirmed Contentful over Sanity for CMS due to existing organizational Contentful license. Selected Astro over Next.js for strict zero-JS-by-default static generation without opt-in required. Vercel Analytics selected over Google Analytics for GDPR-compliant cookieless tracking.

metadata.notes: Flawed revision: fix_report claims ISS-003 Redis resolved but system_components still lists Redis cache layer; technology_stack adds server-side Redis caching note contradicting no-runtime constraint.

project_goal.value: Desktop application for humanitarian logistics coordinators to plan and optimize supply chain routes for delivering food, medicine, and shelter materials across conflict-affected regions with degraded road networks and variable security conditions
project_goal.rationale: Core operational need for field logistics teams

target_users.value: Humanitarian logistics coordinators and field operations staff in conflict-affected regions
target_users.rationale: Primary end users identified in stakeholder discovery

project_class.rationale: Offline-capable desktop required for unreliable connectivity in field environments

capabilities.rationale: Route optimization, GIS data, local persistence, and API integrations with security feeds

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-constraint route optimization with real-time security zone updates and convoy management

risk_level.rationale: Confidential logistics data but no direct PHI; operational risk from routing errors in conflict zones

data_sensitivity.rationale: Delivery routes and security zone data are operationally sensitive and must not leak

external_exposure.rationale: Used exclusively by credentialed logistics staff; no public access

access_model.value: Custom JWT with role-based access for coordinator, supervisor, and read-only analyst roles
access_model.rationale: Segregated access prevents unauthorized route modification

feature_scope.value: Route planning with security-zone constraints, convoy manifest generation, real-time rerouting on incident alerts, warehouse stock visibility, field office delivery confirmation tracking
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for end-to-end logistics coordination

mvp_scope.value: Route optimization engine with security zones, delivery manifest creation, and PostgreSQL-backed waypoint management
mvp_scope.rationale: Core routing capability needed before add-on modules

security_baseline.value: JWT authentication, AES-256 local database encryption, TLS 1.3 for server sync, audit log for all route modifications
security_baseline.rationale: Operational security requirements for conflict-zone deployments

frontend_stack.value: Electron with React and TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Cross-platform desktop with offline capability and rich mapping UI

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI embedded in Electron process plus AWS EC2 sync server
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI for local optimization engine; EC2 for centralized data sync

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on-premise with AWS EC2 sync server running PostgreSQL 16
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for route graphs; bidirectional sync for multi-coordinator collaboration

hosting_target.value: Electron desktop on field laptops; sync server on AWS EC2 in eu-west-1
hosting_target.rationale: EC2 fallback to on-premise if AWS connectivity unavailable

privacy_retention_policy.value: Route and manifests retained 3 years; security zone incident logs retained 1 year then purged
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Organizational data retention guidelines for operational records

future_scope.value: Integration with satellite-derived road-passability feed, mobile companion app for field drivers
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap pending pilot results

constraints.value: Must operate fully offline for up to 72 hours; sync window limited to 30-minute daily connectivity slot
constraints.rationale: Field environments have severely limited and unpredictable internet access

observability_baseline.value: Datadog agent on EC2 sync server; local structured JSON logs on each desktop instance
observability_baseline.rationale: Centralized monitoring only possible when sync is active

execution_preference.value: Iterative sprints with field-coordinator co-design reviews every 3 weeks
execution_preference.rationale: Domain complexity requires frequent user validation

compliance_context.value: OCHA humanitarian logistics guidelines, organizational security protocols for conflict-zone data handling
compliance_context.rationale: data_sensitivity=confidential triggers compliance requirement

project.goal: Route optimization and convoy management desktop tool for conflict-zone logistics
project.target_users: Humanitarian logistics coordinators and field supervisors

frontend.mapping: Mapbox GL JS for route visualization and security zone overlays

backend.optimization: OR-Tools VRP solver for multi-stop route optimization

security.auth: Custom JWT with RS256 signing
security.encryption: SQLite and local files encrypted with SQLCipher AES-256

data.sync: bidirectional delta sync via conflict-free merge

open_questions.rerouting_source: Which security feed API for incident alerts?

reasoner_reviews.product: The route optimization scope is well-defined with clear MVP boundaries. The offline-first constraint is the dominant design driver and must be reflected in every layer of the architecture, from local storage to sync conflict resolution.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Embedding FastAPI as a local Python subprocess inside Electron is an established pattern for compute-heavy desktop apps. The OR-Tools VRP solver for multi-constraint routing is the right choice given waypoint counts up to 50 stops per route. The EC2 sync server must use a last-write-wins with vector-clock approach to handle concurrent coordinator edits during sync windows.
reasoner_reviews.security: Custom JWT with RS256 on a desktop app requires careful private key storage. Recommend OS keychain integration rather than filesystem storage. SQLCipher encryption for the local PostgreSQL-equivalent (SQLite) is critical given laptops can be seized in conflict zones.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 72-hour offline window is non-negotiable and the current plan does not fully address conflict resolution when two coordinators edit the same route simultaneously during separate offline periods. This is the primary blocking issue from round 1.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 plan underspecified the sync conflict-resolution protocol and did not address the risk of route-graph corruption during partial syncs. The OR-Tools integration was described at a too-high level without specifying the constraint model for security zones.

backend.service_design: Local FastAPI process exposes REST endpoints on localhost:8421 consumed by Electron renderer; EC2 sync server exposes /sync/push and /sync/pull endpoints with delta-manifest protocol
backend.api_patterns: Local API: POST /routes/optimize (VRP payload), GET /routes/:id, POST /manifests, GET /warehouses/:id/stock. Sync API: POST /sync/push (delta bundle), GET /sync/pull?since=timestamp
backend.business_modules: RouteOptimizer (OR-Tools VRP wrapper with security-zone exclusion polygons), ManifestBuilder (convoy load calculator), StockVisibility (warehouse inventory reader), SyncEngine (delta bundler and conflict resolver)
backend.failure_handling: OR-Tools solver timeout at 30s with best-found fallback; sync failures queued to local retry journal with exponential backoff up to 72h window

frontend.app_structure: Electron main process manages Python FastAPI subprocess lifecycle; renderer process is React SPA with Mapbox GL JS map canvas and sidebar panels
frontend.pages_and_flows: Route Planner (drag waypoints on map, security zone overlays, run optimization), Manifest Editor (vehicle assignment, load calculator), Warehouse Dashboard (stock levels by item category), Incident Alert Panel (security zone updates)
frontend.state_management: Zustand stores for route-draft, active-manifests, and security-zone overlays; React Query for local API calls with offline cache
frontend.performance_notes: Mapbox vector tiles cached locally for offline map rendering; route polylines rendered as GeoJSON layers for sub-100ms redraw on rerouting

security.auth_design: RS256 JWT issued at login against local user store; token valid 8 hours with refresh requiring connectivity to sync server
security.secrets_management: RS256 private key stored in OS keychain (macOS Keychain / Windows Credential Manager); never written to filesystem
security.privacy_controls: All local SQLite databases encrypted with SQLCipher; route exports as encrypted ZIP with per-operation password

data.entities: delivery_routes (id, name, status ENUM draft/active/completed/disrupted, waypoints_json, vehicle_id, total_km, security_risk_score, created_by, created_at), waypoints (id, route_id, sequence_order, location_geom, location_name, expected_arrival, actual_arrival, field_office_id), supply_requests (id, field_office_id, item_category, quantity, priority ENUM critical/high/normal, requested_at, fulfilled_at), vehicle_manifests (id, route_id, vehicle_id, driver_name, cargo_items_json, departure_time, status), security_zones (id, zone_name, polygon_geom, risk_level ENUM red/amber/green, valid_from, valid_until, source), warehouse_stocks (id, warehouse_id, item_sku, quantity_available, last_updated), delivery_confirmations (id, manifest_id, waypoint_id, confirmed_by, confirmed_at, items_received_json)
data.storage_design: Local SQLite with SQLCipher for all entity tables; EC2 PostgreSQL 16 as canonical sync target; delta sync uses row-level updated_at + coordinator_id vector clocks

devops.deployment_topology: Electron app distributed via GitHub Releases as signed installer (Windows + macOS); EC2 t3.medium in eu-west-1 running Docker Compose with PostgreSQL and FastAPI sync service
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: lint (flake8 + eslint), unit tests (pytest + jest), electron-builder packages signed installers on tag push; EC2 deployment via GitHub Actions SSH deploy step
devops.rollback_strategy: Electron auto-updater with rollback channel; EC2 blue-green with manual cutover via Route53 weighted routing
devops.backup_and_recovery: EC2 PostgreSQL daily RDS snapshot to S3; local SQLite databases backed up to USB during sync window via coordinator checklist

ISS-001.title: Sync conflict resolution protocol underspecified
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan described delta sync without defining conflict resolution strategy for concurrent coordinator edits to the same route during separate offline periods. No merge protocol documented.

history.note: Identified by critic reviewer; plan section on deployment_and_operations mentioned sync but omitted conflict resolution logic

ISS-002.title: OR-Tools constraint model for security zones not specified
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan referenced OR-Tools VRP solver but did not specify how security zone exclusion polygons are modeled as constraints in the VRP problem definition.

history.note: Identified by architect_reasoner; optimization section lacked constraint encoding detail

previous_audits.summary: Round 1 plan establishes a solid foundation with Electron/FastAPI/OR-Tools choices appropriate for the offline-first constraint, but two significant gaps prevent the plan from being implementation-ready: the sync conflict resolution protocol is absent and the OR-Tools constraint model for security zones is underdefined.
strengths: OR-Tools VRP selection is appropriate for multi-stop humanitarian convoy routing
Electron with embedded FastAPI subprocess is a proven pattern for offline-capable compute-heavy desktop apps
SQLCipher encryption choice directly addresses laptop-seizure risk in conflict zones
concerns: No concrete conflict resolution strategy for overlapping coordinator edits during separate offline periods
Security zone exclusion modeled as 'polygons' without specifying VRP constraint encoding method
Sync delta-bundle format and maximum bundle size not defined
blocking_issues: Sync conflict resolution must be specified before implementation to avoid data loss
recommendations: Adopt last-modified-wins for non-overlapping route edits and a manual merge prompt for same-route conflicts identified via coordinator_id vector clocks
Encode security zone exclusions as time-windowed forbidden-arc constraints in the OR-Tools routing model
Document delta-bundle schema: coordinator_id, sync_ts, entity_type, row_id, payload_json, version_vector

previous_plan.title: Humanitarian Logistics Route Optimizer Desktop App - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: Electron desktop application with embedded Python FastAPI optimization engine using Google OR-Tools VRP solver for multi-stop supply chain routing in conflict-affected regions. Bidirectional sync to AWS EC2 PostgreSQL during daily connectivity windows.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Electron main process spawns FastAPI subprocess on localhost:8421; renderer communicates via local REST. Sync server on EC2 runs PostgreSQL 16 and accepts delta bundles via HTTPS. Security zones loaded from JSON file updated at sync time.
previous_plan.data_model: delivery_routes, waypoints, vehicle_manifests, security_zones, warehouse_stocks - all in local SQLite with SQLCipher.
previous_plan.workflows: Route planning workflow triggers OR-Tools VRP solve with waypoints and vehicle capacities. Rerouting workflow triggered by new security zone incident, regenerates affected routes.

target_output.thinking_summary: Fixing two blocking issues from round 1: (1) defining a concrete sync conflict resolution protocol using coordinator_id vector clocks with last-modified-wins for non-overlapping routes and manual merge UI for same-route conflicts; (2) specifying OR-Tools VRP constraint encoding for security zone exclusions as time-windowed forbidden-arc constraints based on polygon intersection pre-computation. The flawed quality reflects that the plan has one intentional gap: it still does not specify maximum delta-bundle size limits or sync compression format, leaving an open operational risk.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined conflict resolution protocol in workflows and deployment sections: coordinator_id vector clocks track edit provenance; last-modified-wins for edits to different waypoints on the same route; same-waypoint conflicts surface a three-panel merge UI showing both versions and a base snapshot
fix_report.expected_outcome: Coordinators editing different legs of the same route simultaneously will auto-merge without data loss; overlapping edits produce an explicit manual merge prompt

fix_report.action_taken: Specified OR-Tools VRP security zone constraint encoding in architecture_overview and system_components: polygon intersection pre-computation marks affected road segments as time-windowed forbidden arcs in the routing model before each solve call
fix_report.expected_outcome: OR-Tools solver will not route convoys through red or amber security zones during their valid_from/valid_until windows

target_output.title: Humanitarian Logistics Route Optimizer - Offline Desktop with Conflict-Aware Sync (Round 2)
target_output.executive_summary: Electron desktop application with an embedded Python 3.12 FastAPI optimization engine using Google OR-Tools VRP for multi-stop convoy route planning across conflict-affected regions. Security zones modeled as time-windowed forbidden-arc constraints in the VRP formulation. Bidirectional delta sync to AWS EC2 PostgreSQL using coordinator_id vector clocks with last-modified-wins merge and manual three-panel UI for same-waypoint conflicts. Fully offline-capable for 72-hour periods with SQLCipher-encrypted local SQLite storage.
target_output.architecture_overview: Electron 29 main process spawns a Python 3.12 FastAPI subprocess on localhost:8421 at startup. The React renderer communicates with the local API for all optimization and data operations. Route optimization: OR-Tools VRP solver ingests waypoints, vehicle capacities, and pre-computed forbidden-arc sets derived from security zone polygon intersections against the road graph. Security zones stored as PostGIS-compatible GeoJSON polygons; at solve time a pre-processor intersects zone polygons against waypoint-to-waypoint segments and encodes blocked segments as time-windowed forbidden arcs in the AddDisjunction callback. Sync: during the daily connectivity window, Electron bundles delta rows (updated_at > last_sync_ts, tagged with coordinator_id and a per-row version_vector) and POSTs to EC2 sync server. EC2 applies last-modified-wins for non-overlapping route edits; same-waypoint conflicts are flagged and returned to the desktop as pending_merge records surfaced in the Conflict Resolution UI. NOTE: delta-bundle compression format and maximum bundle size per sync window remain undocumented - this is a known gap requiring field measurement data.
target_output.technology_stack: Desktop: Electron 29, React 18, TypeScript 5, Mapbox GL JS 3 (offline tile cache), Zustand 4, React Query 5. Optimization: Python 3.12, FastAPI 0.111, Google OR-Tools 9.9 VRP solver, Shapely for polygon intersection. Local storage: SQLite 3 with SQLCipher AES-256. Sync server: FastAPI on EC2 t3.medium, PostgreSQL 16, Docker Compose. Auth: RS256 JWT, OS keychain for key storage. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, electron-builder. Monitoring: Datadog on EC2, structured JSON logs on desktop.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Route Planner: coordinator selects field offices as waypoints on Mapbox map, inputs vehicle type and cargo priority, triggers OR-Tools VRP solve, and views optimized multi-leg route with estimated drive times and security risk scores. Rerouting Panel: when a new security zone incident arrives via sync, affected routes are highlighted; coordinator clicks reroute to re-run solver with updated forbidden arcs. Manifest Editor: auto-populates convoy manifest from optimized route with driver assignment, cargo item list from supply_requests, and departure schedule. Warehouse Stock Dashboard: displays current stock levels by item_sku per warehouse, color-coded against active supply requests to flag shortfalls. Conflict Resolution UI: three-panel diff view showing base snapshot, local version, and remote version for same-waypoint edit conflicts; coordinator selects accepted version per field.
target_output.system_components: Electron Main Process: subprocess manager for FastAPI Python process, IPC bridge to renderer, auto-updater. FastAPI Local API (localhost:8421): RouteOptimizer module (OR-Tools VRP with forbidden-arc pre-processor), ManifestBuilder, StockVisibility reader, SyncEngine (delta bundler, vector clock manager, conflict detector). Mapbox GL JS renderer: offline vector tile cache for conflict-region basemap, GeoJSON layers for routes and security zone overlays. SQLite+SQLCipher: local canonical store for all entities. EC2 Sync Server: FastAPI sync endpoints, PostgreSQL 16 canonical database, conflict detection service that compares incoming version_vectors against stored vectors. GitHub Releases: signed installer distribution for Windows and macOS.
target_output.workflows: Route Optimization Workflow: coordinator opens Route Planner, selects 3-15 waypoints from the field office registry, chooses vehicle from vehicle_manifests, sets cargo priority filter. FastAPI RouteOptimizer calls Shapely to intersect active security_zones polygons against all waypoint-pair road segments, encodes blocked segments as time-windowed forbidden arcs via OR-Tools RoutingModel.AddDisjunction, runs VRP solver with 30s timeout (best-found fallback if timeout reached), returns optimized stop sequence with per-leg distance, estimated time, and security_risk_score. Renderer draws polyline on Mapbox map and populates Manifest Editor. Conflict-Aware Sync Workflow: at sync window start, SyncEngine queries local SQLite for rows with updated_at > last_sync_ts, attaches coordinator_id and version_vector per row, serializes delta bundle as JSON, POSTs to EC2 /sync/push. EC2 compares each incoming version_vector against stored version_vector: if incoming is strictly greater, apply update; if equal or concurrent, flag as conflict. EC2 returns conflict list plus remote deltas. Desktop applies remote deltas; pending_merge conflicts appear in Conflict Resolution UI for coordinator action before next sync.
target_output.data_model: delivery_routes (id UUID PK, name TEXT, status ENUM draft/active/completed/disrupted, waypoints_json JSONB, vehicle_id FK, total_km REAL, security_risk_score REAL, coordinator_id TEXT, version_vector JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMP). waypoints (id UUID PK, route_id FK, sequence_order INT, location_name TEXT, location_lat REAL, location_lon REAL, expected_arrival TIMESTAMP, actual_arrival TIMESTAMP, field_office_id FK). supply_requests (id UUID PK, field_office_id FK, item_category TEXT, item_sku TEXT, quantity INT, priority ENUM critical/high/normal, requested_at TIMESTAMP, fulfilled_at TIMESTAMP). vehicle_manifests (id UUID PK, route_id FK, vehicle_id TEXT, driver_name TEXT, cargo_items_json JSONB, departure_time TIMESTAMP, status ENUM pending/dispatched/delivered). security_zones (id UUID PK, zone_name TEXT, polygon_geom JSONB GeoJSON, risk_level ENUM red/amber/green, valid_from TIMESTAMP, valid_until TIMESTAMP, source TEXT). warehouse_stocks (id UUID PK, warehouse_id FK, item_sku TEXT, quantity_available INT, last_updated TIMESTAMP). delivery_confirmations (id UUID PK, manifest_id FK, waypoint_id FK, confirmed_by TEXT, confirmed_at TIMESTAMP, items_received_json JSONB). sync_journal (id UUID PK, coordinator_id TEXT, sync_ts TIMESTAMP, status ENUM pending/success/partial/failed, conflict_count INT).
target_output.api_design: Local FastAPI (localhost:8421): POST /routes/optimize {waypoints:[{id,lat,lon}], vehicle_id, cargo_priority_filter} -> {route_id, stops:[{waypoint_id,sequence,eta,risk_score}], total_km}. GET /routes/:id -> route detail with waypoints and manifest. POST /manifests {route_id, driver_name, cargo_items} -> manifest_id. GET /warehouses/:id/stock -> [{item_sku, quantity_available}]. POST /sync/push (local delta bundle) -> {applied:N, conflicts:[{entity_type,row_id,local_version,remote_version}]}. GET /sync/pull?since=ISO_TS -> remote delta bundle. EC2 Sync Server: POST /sync/push, GET /sync/pull?coordinator_id&since.
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT Authentication: RS256 tokens issued at login, 8-hour validity, refresh requires connectivity to EC2 sync server. Private key stored in macOS Keychain or Windows Credential Manager via electron-keytar, never written to disk. Local Encryption: all SQLite databases and export files encrypted with SQLCipher AES-256; encryption key derived from user passphrase + hardware GUID using PBKDF2-SHA256. Transport: TLS 1.3 for all EC2 sync calls; certificate pinned in Electron main process. Audit Logging: every route modification logged to local audit_log table with coordinator_id, action, before/after values; exported to EC2 at sync time. OCHA compliance: route and manifest data classified as operationally confidential; export as encrypted ZIP only with per-operation passphrase.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Desktop Distribution: GitHub Releases via electron-builder producing signed NSIS installer (Windows) and DMG (macOS). Auto-updater enabled with rollback channel pinning last-known-good version. EC2 Sync Server: t3.medium in eu-west-1 running Docker Compose (FastAPI sync service + PostgreSQL 16). Deployment via GitHub Actions SSH step on tag push. Blue-green switch via Route53 weighted routing with manual cutover. CI Pipeline: GitHub Actions runs flake8, eslint, pytest (backend), jest (frontend), electron-builder on PR; integration tests spin up SQLite in-memory for optimization and sync engine tests. Environments: dev (local SQLite, mock sync server), staging (EC2 staging instance with sanitized data copy), production (EC2 eu-west-1). Rollback: Electron rollback channel + EC2 PostgreSQL point-in-time recovery from daily S3 snapshots.
target_output.observability: Datadog agent on EC2 sync server: API latency per endpoint, sync push/pull throughput, conflict rate per sync session, EC2 CPU and memory. Desktop structured JSON logs written to %APPDATA%/logistics-tool/logs/ with fields: timestamp, coordinator_id, action, route_id, duration_ms, error. Key desktop metrics logged: OR-Tools solve time per route, solver timeout rate, sync bundle row count, conflict count. Logs forwarded to EC2 at sync time and ingested into Datadog Log Management for aggregated dashboard. Alert: conflict_rate > 5% per sync session triggers Slack notification to logistics ops team.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: EC2 t3.medium (~$35/month) handles up to 50 concurrent sync sessions. PostgreSQL storage ~$20/month for 3-year retention. GitHub Releases storage negligible. Total infrastructure ~$60/month. Scaling path: vertical upgrade to t3.large if sync throughput exceeds 1000 delta rows per session; read replica for reporting queries if analytics use grows. Desktop app is zero-cloud-cost during offline periods.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (12 weeks): Route optimization with OR-Tools VRP and security zone forbidden-arc constraints, waypoint management, vehicle manifest generation, local SQLite with SQLCipher, bidirectional delta sync with conflict resolution UI, RS256 JWT authentication with OS keychain. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Warehouse stock dashboard with supply request fulfillment tracking, delivery confirmation workflow for field office sign-off, satellite-derived road-passability feed integration, Mapbox offline tile cache management tool for pre-loading region maps before field deployment. Phase 3 (future): Mobile companion app for field drivers to view manifests and submit delivery confirmations without laptop hardware.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python backend: black + isort formatting enforced in CI; OR-Tools integration tested with pytest fixtures using synthetic 10-waypoint problem sets. TypeScript: strict mode, ESLint with react-hooks rules. All Electron IPC messages typed with Zod schemas. Sync conflict resolution logic covered by unit tests with vector clock collision scenarios. Pre-commit hook runs SQLCipher encryption smoke test. ADR documents maintained for OR-Tools constraint model decisions and sync protocol design.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Electron + Python subprocess startup time may exceed 10s on low-spec field laptops. Mitigation: FastAPI process pre-warmed on OS login via startup entry; OR-Tools model pre-compiled at startup. Risk: SQLCipher key loss if OS keychain corrupted (catastrophic data loss on field laptop). Mitigation: encrypted key escrow backup generated at first launch and stored on USB drive per coordinator security checklist. Tradeoff: last-modified-wins conflict resolution is simple to implement but may silently overwrite minority-coordinator edits on shared routes; mitigated by manual merge UI for same-waypoint conflicts and full sync_journal audit trail. Known gap: delta-bundle maximum size and compression format undocumented - field measurement phase required before production deployment to avoid sync window overrun.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Conflict resolution strategy selected: vector-clock-based detection with last-modified-wins for non-overlapping edits and manual merge prompt for same-waypoint conflicts. OR-Tools constraint encoding for security zones: polygon-to-forbidden-arc pre-computation via Shapely intersection at solve time. RS256 private key storage: OS keychain via electron-keytar confirmed. Sync server region: eu-west-1 selected for proximity to field operations in MENA and Sub-Saharan Africa.

metadata.notes: Round 2 fixes sync conflict resolution and OR-Tools constraint encoding but intentionally leaves delta-bundle size/compression undocumented as a flawed-quality marker.

profile.domain: Humanitarian Food Insecurity Prediction ML Infrastructure

project_goal.value: Infrastructure provisioning and orchestration for a machine learning pipeline that predicts food insecurity hotspots from satellite imagery, weather data, and market price feeds to inform pre-positioning of humanitarian aid stocks
project_goal.rationale: Core infrastructure need for predictive humanitarian response operations

target_users.value: ML engineers, data scientists, and humanitarian operations planners consuming prediction outputs
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers of infrastructure and pipeline outputs

project_class.rationale: Focus is on Terraform/GKE provisioning and pipeline orchestration, not application UI

capabilities.rationale: Airflow orchestration, ClickHouse analytics, GKE compute, and multi-source data ingestion

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-source ingestion, Vertex AI model training, ClickHouse analytics at region-week granularity across 50+ countries

risk_level.rationale: Internal data with no PII; operational risk from incorrect predictions informing aid pre-positioning

data_sensitivity.rationale: Satellite, weather, and market data classified as internal; no personal-level data in pipeline

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline outputs consumed by internal dashboard and operations planning teams only

access_model.value: GCP Workload Identity Federation for service-to-service auth; GCP IAM roles for human operators
access_model.rationale: No username/password auth; workload identity prevents credential leakage between pipeline components

feature_scope.value: Weekly satellite imagery ingestion and preprocessing, weather and market price feed integration, Vertex AI model training and versioning, food insecurity hotspot prediction batch job, ClickHouse analytics warehouse, automated model validation and canary promotion
feature_scope.rationale: Full ML pipeline from raw ingestion to prediction output

mvp_scope.value: Weekly batch pipeline with satellite and weather ingestion, baseline XGBoost model training on Vertex AI, ClickHouse output table, Grafana dashboard for prediction results
mvp_scope.rationale: End-to-end pipeline demonstrating prediction capability before multi-model ensembling

security_baseline.value: GCP Workload Identity Federation, VPC-native GKE cluster, private ClickHouse nodes, Secret Manager for API keys, Cloud Audit Logs for all data access
security_baseline.rationale: Defense-in-depth for internal infrastructure handling strategically sensitive prediction data

frontend_stack.value: none - infrastructure project with no UI components; Grafana provides dashboards
frontend_stack.rationale: No custom frontend required for pipeline infrastructure

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Apache Airflow 2.9 on GKE, Vertex AI for model training and serving
backend_stack.rationale: Airflow provides DAG-based orchestration; Vertex AI managed training avoids custom GPU cluster ops

data_platform.value: ClickHouse 24.x on GKE for analytics warehouse; GCS for raw data lake; BigQuery for ad-hoc analyst queries
data_platform.rationale: ClickHouse for sub-second aggregation queries over region-week prediction history; GCS for durable raw storage

hosting_target.value: GKE Autopilot cluster in europe-west1 with Vertex AI managed services
hosting_target.rationale: Autopilot reduces node management overhead; Vertex AI integrates with GCS and GKE natively

privacy_retention_policy.value: Raw satellite and weather data retained 5 years in GCS; prediction outputs retained indefinitely in ClickHouse; model artifacts retained 2 years post-deprecation
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Long retention needed for trend analysis and model auditability

future_scope.value: Near-real-time prediction using streaming market price feeds, multi-modal ensemble model, API endpoint for partner NGO consumption
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap pending accuracy validation of baseline model

constraints.value: Inference batch must complete within 6-hour window on weekly schedule; infrastructure costs capped at $8000/month
constraints.rationale: Predictions must be available for Monday morning operations planning meetings

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus for infrastructure metrics; Vertex AI model monitoring for drift detection; Cloud Audit Logs for data access
observability_baseline.rationale: Standard observability stack for GCP infrastructure

execution_preference.value: Terraform for all infrastructure; GitOps with GitLab CI for pipeline deployments
execution_preference.rationale: Infrastructure-as-code required for reproducibility across environments

compliance_context.value: OCHA information sharing protocols; GDPR scope limited to infrastructure access logs only
compliance_context.rationale: data_sensitivity=internal and risk_level=medium; OCHA protocols apply to prediction data sharing

project.goal: GKE-based ML pipeline infrastructure for weekly food insecurity hotspot prediction
project.target_users: ML engineers and humanitarian operations planners
project.timeline: 16-week infrastructure build then ongoing model iteration

backend.ml_platform: Vertex AI Custom Training + Model Registry

security.auth: GCP Workload Identity Federation

data.adhoc: BigQuery federated queries over GCS Parquet

open_questions.satellite_source: NASA MODIS or ESA Sentinel-2 primary source?

reasoner_reviews.product: The ML pipeline scope is appropriately bounded for MVP with a single XGBoost baseline model. The 6-hour inference SLA is achievable for country-level predictions if preprocessing is parallelized across GKE pods. The canary promotion workflow is the right safeguard before full production rollout.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: ClickHouse is the right choice for region-week aggregation queries that operations planners will run interactively. The Airflow DAG topology needs explicit definition with separate DAGs for ingestion, preprocessing, training, and inference for independent retries. Round 1 plan did not specify the Terraform module structure or estimate Vertex AI compute costs against the $8000/month cap.
reasoner_reviews.security: Workload Identity Federation correctly avoids service account key files. All GCS bucket policies must enforce uniform bucket-level access and deny public ACLs. ClickHouse cluster must be placed on private VPC subnets with no external IPs.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The $8000/month budget is tight for weekly Vertex AI Custom Training jobs processing 50+ country satellite tiles. Round 1 plan did not estimate Vertex AI compute costs against the budget ceiling. This is the primary blocking issue.

backend.service_design: Four Airflow DAGs: ingest_dag (satellite + weather + market pulls), preprocess_dag (tile normalization, feature extraction), train_dag (weekly Vertex AI Custom Training job), inference_dag (batch prediction over all regions)
backend.business_modules: SatelliteIngester (MODIS/Sentinel-2 tile downloader via Earth Engine API), WeatherFetcher (ERA5 reanalysis API client), MarketPriceFetcher (FAO GIEWS API client), FeatureExtractor (NDVI, rainfall anomaly, price volatility computation), ModelTrainer (Vertex AI job submitter), InferenceRunner (Vertex AI batch prediction job manager), ValidationGate (accuracy threshold checker against held-out historical data)
backend.background_jobs: Weekly cron-triggered Airflow DAGs; Vertex AI Custom Training jobs run on n1-standard-8 with T4 GPU; inference batch uses n1-standard-4 autoscaling node pool
backend.scaling_notes: GKE Autopilot scales preprocessing pods horizontally across 50 country tile batches; Vertex AI managed autoscaling for training job workers

security.auth_design: GCP Workload Identity Federation binds GKE service accounts to GCP IAM roles; no service account key files anywhere in the infrastructure
security.secrets_management: All API keys (Earth Engine, FAO GIEWS, ClickHouse password) stored in GCP Secret Manager; accessed by pods via Workload Identity at runtime
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Cloud Audit Logs captures all GCS reads/writes and Vertex AI job submissions; ClickHouse query_log table retained 90 days

data.entities: satellite_image_batches (batch_id, country_code, acquisition_date, tile_count, gcs_prefix, status), weather_observations (obs_id, region_code, obs_date, rainfall_mm, drought_index, source), market_price_feeds (feed_id, commodity, market_location, price_usd, collection_date), prediction_runs (run_id, model_version_id, run_date, accuracy_f1, completed_at), hotspot_alerts (alert_id, run_id, region_code, insecurity_probability, severity_tier), model_versions (version_id, artifact_gcs_path, accuracy_f1, deployed_at)
data.storage_design: GCS raw lake gs://humanitarian-ml-raw with 5-year lifecycle; GCS processed gs://humanitarian-ml-processed with 1-year lifecycle; ClickHouse hotspot_alerts and prediction_runs sharded by region_code, 2 replicas; BigQuery external tables over GCS Parquet for analyst queries
data.retention_and_deletion: GCS raw 5-year lifecycle to Nearline then Coldline; model artifacts 2 years post-deprecation via Vertex AI Model Registry lifecycle policy

devops.deployment_topology: GKE Autopilot cluster in europe-west1: Airflow scheduler/webserver pods, Airflow worker pods (autoscale 0-10), ClickHouse StatefulSet (2x n2-highmem-8), Prometheus+Grafana stack. Vertex AI managed compute outside GKE.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI: terraform validate + tflint on MR; terraform plan posted as comment; terraform apply on merge to main via protected runner. DAG deployments sync Python DAG files to GCS DAG bucket on merge.
devops.observability_stack: Prometheus scraping Airflow StatsD and ClickHouse system.metrics; Grafana dashboards for pipeline run success rate, inference job duration, ClickHouse query latency; Vertex AI model monitoring for prediction drift on 4-week rolling window
devops.cost_controls: Vertex AI jobs use preemptible workers for preprocessing; Autopilot scales to zero between weekly runs; GCS Nearline for data older than 6 months; budget alert at $6500/month triggers Slack notification
