target_output.title: Food Insecurity Prediction ML Infrastructure - GKE/Vertex AI/ClickHouse Pipeline (Round 2)
target_output.executive_summary: Terraform-provisioned GKE Autopilot cluster in europe-west1 running Apache Airflow 2.9 orchestrating a weekly ML pipeline that ingests satellite imagery, ERA5 weather reanalysis, and FAO GIEWS market price feeds, extracts food security features, trains XGBoost models on Vertex AI Custom Training, runs batch inference, and writes food insecurity hotspot probability scores to ClickHouse for humanitarian operations planning. Total infrastructure cost estimated at ~$980/month against an $8000/month cap. Canary model promotion validates accuracy on held-out historical data before full deployment. NOTE: satellite source selection and canary accuracy threshold values remain open gaps.
target_output.architecture_overview: Five Terraform modules (gke_cluster, vertex_pipeline, clickhouse, networking, iam) provision the full infrastructure via GitLab CI GitOps workflow with GCS state backend. Four Airflow DAGs on GKE Autopilot coordinate the weekly pipeline: ingest_dag pulls raw satellite tiles and feeds from external APIs into GCS raw lake; preprocess_dag runs parallelized NDVI/rainfall-anomaly/price-volatility feature extraction across country batches as parallel Airflow tasks; train_dag submits a Vertex AI Custom Training job (n1-standard-8 + T4 GPU, ~4h) with the latest feature set and registers the trained model in Vertex AI Model Registry; inference_dag submits a Vertex AI batch prediction job (n1-standard-4, ~2h) and writes hotspot_alerts to ClickHouse with severity tiers. ValidationGate in inference_dag checks F1 accuracy against held-out 2022-2023 data and halts deployment if threshold not met (threshold value TBD). Workload Identity Federation binds all GKE service accounts to scoped IAM roles with no service account key files.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Apache Airflow 2.9 on GKE Autopilot europe-west1. ML Platform: Vertex AI Custom Training (n1-standard-8 + T4 for training, n1-standard-4 for inference). Data Ingestion: Python 3.12 with Earth Engine Python API, cdsapi (ERA5), requests (FAO GIEWS). Feature Engineering: Shapely, rasterio, xarray for geospatial processing. Warehouse: ClickHouse 24.x on GKE (2x n2-highmem-8 replicas). Storage: GCS with lifecycle policies. Analytics: BigQuery external tables over GCS Parquet. IaC: Terraform 1.8. CI/CD: GitLab CI. Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus, Vertex AI Model Monitoring.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Weekly Satellite Ingestion: SatelliteIngester downloads MODIS Terra/Aqua or Sentinel-2 L2A tiles for 50+ country bounding boxes via Earth Engine batch export to GCS. Weather Ingestion: WeatherFetcher pulls ERA5 monthly reanalysis rainfall and temperature anomaly data via CDS API. Market Price Ingestion: MarketPriceFetcher pulls FAO GIEWS commodity price indices for staple foods per country. Feature Extraction: FeatureExtractor computes NDVI deviation from 10-year baseline, rainfall anomaly vs climatology, and staple food price volatility index per region-month. Model Training: Vertex AI Custom Training job trains XGBoost on 3-year rolling training window. Batch Inference: Vertex AI batch prediction job scores all active regions. Canary Promotion: ValidationGate evaluates F1 on held-out data; auto-promotes if threshold met (TBD).
target_output.system_components: GKE Autopilot Cluster (europe-west1): Airflow scheduler and webserver pods, Airflow worker pods (autoscale 0-10 during weekly run), ClickHouse StatefulSet (2x n2-highmem-8 replicas), Prometheus + Grafana monitoring stack. Terraform Modules: gke_cluster (VPC-native cluster, Workload Identity bindings), vertex_pipeline (Custom Training job templates, Model Registry, batch prediction templates), clickhouse (StatefulSet, PVCs, headless service, backup CronJob), networking (VPC, private subnets, firewall rules, private GKE master), iam (service accounts, IAM role bindings, Secret Manager secrets). GCS Buckets: humanitarian-ml-raw (5-year lifecycle), humanitarian-ml-processed (1-year), humanitarian-ml-models (2-year), humanitarian-ml-tfstate (Terraform state, versioning enabled). Vertex AI: Custom Training jobs, Model Registry, Batch Prediction jobs, Model Monitoring endpoint.
target_output.workflows: Weekly Ingestion-to-Prediction Pipeline: Monday 00:00 UTC Airflow scheduler triggers ingest_dag which fans out SatelliteIngester, WeatherFetcher, and MarketPriceFetcher tasks in parallel; each ingester writes raw files to GCS and updates respective ClickHouse staging tables. On ingest_dag success, preprocess_dag triggers FeatureExtractor tasks in parallel per country batch, writing Parquet feature files to GCS processed bucket. On preprocess success, train_dag submits Vertex AI Custom Training job referencing latest processed feature path; job runs ~4h and registers trained model artifact in Vertex AI Model Registry. inference_dag submits Vertex AI batch prediction job over all active regions (~2h); on completion, ValidationGate checks F1 against held-out 2022-2023 set and either promotes model and writes hotspot_alerts to ClickHouse or halts and alerts ops Slack channel. Canary Model Promotion Workflow: when a new model version passes ValidationGate in staging, a shadow inference job runs against 10% of regions (canary set); if accuracy and latency thresholds are met, model alias 'production' is updated in Vertex AI Model Registry; if not, previous alias retained and failure alert fired via Alertmanager.
target_output.data_model: satellite_image_batches (batch_id UUID, country_code CHAR3, acquisition_date DATE, tile_count INT, gcs_prefix TEXT, status ENUM ingested/preprocessed/failed, ingested_at TIMESTAMP). weather_observations (obs_id UUID, region_code TEXT, obs_date DATE, rainfall_mm REAL, temp_c REAL, drought_index REAL, rainfall_anomaly_pct REAL, source TEXT). market_price_feeds (feed_id UUID, commodity TEXT, market_location TEXT, country_code CHAR3, price_usd REAL, price_volatility_30d REAL, collection_date DATE). prediction_runs (run_id UUID, model_version_id TEXT, run_date DATE, region_count INT, accuracy_f1 REAL, completed_at TIMESTAMP, promoted BOOL). hotspot_alerts (alert_id UUID, run_id FK, region_code TEXT, insecurity_probability REAL, severity_tier ENUM watch/alert/emergency, ndvi_deviation REAL, rainfall_anomaly_pct REAL, generated_at TIMESTAMP). model_versions (version_id TEXT, model_type TEXT, artifact_gcs_path TEXT, accuracy_f1 REAL, deployed_at TIMESTAMP, deprecated_at TIMESTAMP). infrastructure_configs (config_id UUID, terraform_workspace TEXT, module_name TEXT, applied_at TIMESTAMP, git_commit_sha TEXT).
target_output.api_design: No external API surface for this infrastructure project. Internal interfaces: Airflow REST API (POST /api/v1/dags/{dag_id}/dagRuns for ad-hoc reruns, GET /api/v1/dagRuns for monitoring). Vertex AI REST API (POST /v1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/trainingPipelines for job submission, GET /v1/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/models for registry). ClickHouse HTTP API used by Grafana (GET /?query=SELECT+region_code,insecurity_probability+FROM+hotspot_alerts+WHERE+run_date=today()). GitLab CI invokes terraform CLI via protected runner for all infrastructure changes.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Workload Identity Federation: GKE Kubernetes service accounts annotated with GCP service account emails; IAM bindings grant least-privilege roles (roles/storage.objectCreator for ingesters, roles/aiplatform.user for training jobs, roles/bigquery.dataViewer for analysts). No service account JSON key files exist anywhere. Network: VPC-native GKE with private master endpoint; ClickHouse pods on private subnet with no external IPs; firewall rules allow only Airflow worker egress to Earth Engine, CDS, and FAO GIEWS APIs on specific destination ports. Secrets: all API keys in GCP Secret Manager accessed via Workload Identity at pod runtime. Audit: Cloud Audit Logs captures all GCS bucket access and Vertex AI job submissions; ClickHouse system.query_log retained 90 days. OCHA compliance: prediction outputs classified as internal; no partner sharing without explicit IAM access grant.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Terraform Module Structure: modules/gke_cluster provisions VPC-native Autopilot cluster with Workload Identity enabled and private master. modules/vertex_pipeline defines Custom Training job templates and Model Registry configuration. modules/clickhouse deploys StatefulSet with 2x n2-highmem-8 nodes, PVCs, headless service, and daily backup CronJob to GCS. modules/networking defines VPC, private subnets, firewall rules, and private GKE master authorized networks. modules/iam creates all service accounts and IAM role bindings. State Backend: GCS bucket humanitarian-ml-tfstate with object versioning; Terraform workspaces dev, staging, prod with isolated state files. GitLab CI Pipeline: on MR - terraform validate + tflint + terraform plan posted as MR comment. On merge to main - terraform apply via protected runner with Workload Identity. DAG Deployments: GitLab CI syncs DAG Python files to gs://humanitarian-ml-dags/ on merge; Airflow GCS DAG sync picks up changes within 30s. Environments: dev (single Autopilot cluster, 1-node ClickHouse), staging (full topology with sanitized historical data), production (full topology europe-west1). Rollback: Terraform state versioning allows rollback to previous apply; Vertex AI Model Registry retains prior model version for instant rollback via model alias re-pointing.
target_output.observability: Prometheus scrapes Airflow StatsD metrics (dag_success_count, dag_failure_count, task_duration by task_id) and ClickHouse system.metrics (query_count, memory_usage, disk_usage). Grafana dashboards: Pipeline Health (DAG run success rate last 4 weeks, task failure heatmap, ingestion lag per source), Prediction Quality (model F1 trend by training run, hotspot_alert count by severity_tier), Infrastructure Cost (GKE pod count, Vertex AI job hours by type, GCS storage growth). Vertex AI Model Monitoring: feature distribution drift detection on rolling 4-week window vs training baseline. Alertmanager rules: two consecutive DAG failures -> PagerDuty on-call; F1 drop >5% week-over-week -> Slack ops channel; GCS ingestion file count zero on Monday 04:00 UTC -> Slack infra channel.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Monthly cost estimate: Vertex AI Custom Training (n1-standard-8 + T4 GPU, ~4h/week) = $18/run x 4.3 weeks = ~$77/month. Vertex AI Batch Inference (n1-standard-4, ~2h/week) = $8/run x 4.3 = ~$34/month. GKE Autopilot during weekly pipeline runs (~12h/week peak, ~20 pods) = ~$200/month. ClickHouse StatefulSet (2x n2-highmem-8, always-on) = ~$430/month. GCS storage (10TB raw + 2TB processed + 500GB models) = ~$210/month. Grafana and Prometheus stack (e2-medium) = ~$30/month. Total ~$981/month against $8000/month cap - 88% headroom. Scaling path: 10x region count increases Vertex AI job to ~$700/month total still within cap; ClickHouse scaled to 3-node cluster at ~$650/month if query load grows.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (16 weeks): Terraform modules for GKE, networking, and IAM provisioned; Airflow 2.9 deployed; ingest_dag for MODIS satellite tiles and ERA5 weather; FeatureExtractor for NDVI and rainfall anomaly features; Vertex AI XGBoost baseline training and batch inference; ClickHouse hotspot_alerts table; Grafana pipeline health and prediction dashboards; GitLab CI with terraform plan/apply and DAG sync workflow. Phase 2 (8 weeks): MarketPriceFetcher for FAO GIEWS price volatility feature; ensemble model (XGBoost + LightGBM); ValidationGate canary promotion with defined accuracy thresholds; BigQuery external tables for analyst ad-hoc access; cost and model quality dashboards. Phase 3 (future): Near-real-time market price streaming via Pub/Sub; partner NGO API endpoint for prediction consumption; mobile alert integration for field planners.
target_output.development_guidelines: All infrastructure changes via Terraform only; no manual GCP console modifications in staging or production enforced via IAM deny policies. DAG code follows Airflow TaskFlow API pattern with typed Python functions and Pydantic models for inter-task data passing. Pytest for all Python ingestion and feature extraction modules with mocked GCS and Vertex AI clients; coverage >80% for business logic. GitLab CI enforces tflint (Terraform), flake8 + mypy (Python). Model training code versioned in Git alongside infrastructure; model artifact SHA recorded in prediction_runs.git_commit_sha column. Terraform workspace promotion: dev -> staging -> prod via manual MR approval gate.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: satellite source unresolved (MODIS vs Sentinel-2) affects tile download volume and preprocessing complexity - MODIS is lower resolution but faster; Sentinel-2 is 10m resolution but 5x larger download. Mitigation: resolve before Phase 1 Week 4 feature extraction sprint. Risk: ERA5 CDS API rate limits during weekly batch could delay ingest_dag by up to 3h. Mitigation: pre-cache ERA5 monthly data the day before weekly pipeline run via a Saturday pre-ingest DAG. Risk: ClickHouse always-on cost ($430/month) is largest single cost item; if budget tightened below $500/month could replace with BigQuery on-demand at lower base cost but higher per-query latency. Tradeoff: GKE Autopilot over Standard reduces ops burden but limits node-level tuning for ClickHouse I/O; accepted given small infrastructure team. Known gap: canary ValidationGate accuracy threshold value is TBD pending baseline model accuracy benchmarking on historical data - this must be resolved before Phase 2 canary promotion is activated.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Terraform module structure defined: five modules with GCS state backend and per-environment workspaces. Vertex AI compute costs estimated at ~$981/month well within $8000 cap providing 88% headroom. Workload Identity Federation confirmed as sole auth mechanism. Outstanding: satellite source (MODIS vs Sentinel-2) and canary accuracy threshold require data science team input before Phase 1 feature extraction sprint.

metadata.notes: Round 2 fixes cost estimate and Terraform module structure but intentionally leaves satellite source selection and canary threshold as unresolved open questions.

project_goal.value: Fullstack volunteer management platform for large humanitarian organizations to recruit, vet, schedule, and retain skilled volunteers including medical professionals, engineers, and translators for disaster response deployments
project_goal.rationale: Core operational need to replace spreadsheet-based volunteer tracking

target_users.value: Volunteers applying and managing their profiles; operations staff searching and scheduling; HR staff managing vetting and compliance
target_users.rationale: Three distinct user groups with different access needs identified in discovery

project_class.rationale: Public volunteer portal plus internal admin and scheduling tools require both UI and backend API

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, analytics, admin_panel, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Public portal, ops admin panel, background check integrations, and deployment analytics

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-role access model, background check integrations, scheduling optimization, and certification expiry tracking

risk_level.rationale: Handles volunteer personal data and sensitive vetting records; no payment processing

data_sensitivity.rationale: Volunteer PII including national ID, passport, medical certifications, and background check results

external_exposure.rationale: Volunteer registration portal is public-facing; admin panel behind authentication

access_model.value: Auth0 with RBAC: volunteer, ops_staff, hr_staff, and admin roles; MFA mandatory for ops_staff and hr_staff
access_model.rationale: Vetting records require HR-only access; scheduling requires ops access; volunteers see own profile only

feature_scope.value: Volunteer registration and credential management, background check workflow, skill certification tracking with expiry alerts, deployment assignment matching, mission roster management, availability calendar, admin analytics dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: Full feature set for end-to-end volunteer lifecycle management

mvp_scope.value: Volunteer registration with credential upload, background screening workflow, ops staff search by skill and availability, deployment assignment with email notification
mvp_scope.rationale: Core recruitment and assignment flow needed before analytics and retention features

security_baseline.value: Auth0 with MFA for staff, TLS 1.3, AES-256 at rest for PII fields, WAF on DigitalOcean App Platform, audit log for all vetting record access
security_baseline.rationale: Personal data protection and GDPR requirements

frontend_stack.value: SvelteKit with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Team expertise; SvelteKit's SSR improves SEO for public volunteer portal and reduces bundle size

backend_stack.rationale: High throughput for background check webhook ingestion; Fastify schema validation reduces boilerplate

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with Redis 7 cache
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for volunteer-assignment-mission relationships; Redis for session cache and availability search results

hosting_target.value: DigitalOcean App Platform with managed PostgreSQL and Redis
hosting_target.rationale: Cost-effective managed hosting for NGO budget constraints; App Platform handles scaling without Kubernetes ops overhead

privacy_retention_policy.value: Volunteer PII retained 3 years post last deployment; background check results retained 5 years per jurisdiction requirements; deleted on verified erasure request within 30 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: GDPR data minimization and right-to-erasure requirements

future_scope.value: Mobile app for volunteers to update availability and receive mission notifications; AI-assisted deployment matching based on skill profiles and language capabilities
future_scope.rationale: Phase 3 roadmap pending platform stability

constraints.value: Hosting budget under $600/month; background check API must support multi-jurisdiction screening (EU, US, UK); deployment to DigitalOcean only
constraints.rationale: NGO budget constraints and existing DigitalOcean contract

observability_baseline.value: Grafana + Prometheus via DigitalOcean metrics; structured JSON logging; email alerts for background check failures and expiring certifications
observability_baseline.rationale: Lightweight monitoring appropriate for NGO operations team size

execution_preference.value: Agile sprints with 2-week iterations; volunteer coordinator co-design review after each sprint
execution_preference.rationale: Domain stakeholders must validate UX at each milestone

compliance_context.value: GDPR for volunteer personal data; background check regulatory requirements by jurisdiction (UK DBS, US FCRA, EU national equivalents)
compliance_context.rationale: data_sensitivity=personal triggers compliance context requirement

project.goal: End-to-end volunteer lifecycle management from recruitment through deployment
project.target_users: Volunteers, operations staff, HR staff, and admins
project.timeline: MVP in 10 weeks, full feature set by week 20

frontend.state: Svelte stores with SvelteKit form actions for mutations

security.auth: Auth0 with RBAC and MFA for staff
security.encryption: AES-256 for PII fields at rest

open_questions.bg_check_vendor: Sterling Volunteers or Checkr for multi-jurisdiction support?

reasoner_reviews.product: The volunteer lifecycle scope is well-defined with clear MVP boundaries. Background check workflow is the most operationally critical feature and must be designed to handle async webhook callbacks from third-party screening providers with idempotent processing. Certification expiry tracking with automated alerts is a high-value retention feature that operations teams rely on heavily.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: SvelteKit with SSR is the right choice for the public volunteer portal for SEO and initial load performance. Fastify's JSON schema validation and serialization throughput is well-suited for the background check webhook ingestion volume. Round 1 plan used a generic RBAC description without specifying the exact PostgreSQL row-level security policy for volunteer data isolation - this was identified as the primary blocking issue.
reasoner_reviews.security: Auth0 RBAC is sound but the plan must specify row-level security policies to ensure volunteers can only read their own records and HR staff cannot access vetting records outside their jurisdiction. GDPR right-to-erasure requires a deletion workflow that anonymizes rather than hard-deletes to preserve referential integrity in deployment_assignments.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: DigitalOcean App Platform at $600/month is achievable with the right instance sizing. The background check vendor selection (Sterling vs Checkr) affects the webhook integration design - both support async callbacks but have different payload schemas. This open question should be resolved before building the screening workflow.
reasoner_reviews.critic: Round 1 plan lacked specificity on PostgreSQL row-level security policies and the GDPR right-to-erasure workflow. The analytics dashboard was described generically without naming specific metrics that operations and HR teams need for volunteer pipeline visibility.

backend.service_design: Fastify app organized into domain plugins: volunteers, assignments, missions, screening, certifications, notifications. Background check webhook endpoint handles async status callbacks from Sterling/Checkr with idempotent processing via webhook_events deduplication table.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1; versioned at /api/v1. Webhook endpoint POST /webhooks/background-check with HMAC-SHA256 signature verification. Admin endpoints gated by Auth0 RBAC middleware checking roles claim.
backend.business_modules: VolunteerRegistration (profile creation, credential upload to DO Spaces), ScreeningWorkflow (background check submission, webhook handler, status state machine), CertificationTracker (expiry calculation, alert scheduling), DeploymentMatcher (availability + skill search query), RosterManager (mission assignment, conflict detection), NotificationDispatcher (email via SendGrid, in-app via Redis pub/sub)
backend.failure_handling: Background check webhook retries handled by exponential backoff queue in Redis; idempotent webhook processing via webhook_event_id unique constraint; certification expiry alerts use pg_cron scheduled job with retry on failure

frontend.app_structure: SvelteKit with file-based routing: /register (public), /portal (volunteer-authenticated), /ops (ops_staff), /hr (hr_staff), /admin (admin). SSR for public pages; client-side rendering for authenticated dashboards.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Volunteer Registration (multi-step: personal details, credentials, skill profile, availability setup), My Profile (view/update certifications, deployment history, availability calendar), Ops Dashboard (search volunteers by skill/availability, create deployment assignment, manage mission rosters), HR Panel (background check queue, vetting approval, erasure request processing), Admin Analytics (volunteer pipeline funnel, deployment utilization, certification expiry report)
frontend.state_management: SvelteKit form actions for all mutations; $page.data for server-loaded state; Svelte writable stores for optimistic UI updates on availability calendar
frontend.accessibility_notes: WCAG 2.1 AA required for public registration portal; form validation errors announced to screen readers via aria-live regions

security.auth_design: Auth0 Universal Login with RBAC; MFA enforced for ops_staff and hr_staff roles via Auth0 Actions; volunteer role allows email/password only
security.authorization_model: PostgreSQL row-level security: volunteers SELECT/UPDATE own rows only; hr_staff SELECT/UPDATE background_checks scoped to their jurisdiction; ops_staff SELECT volunteers and INSERT/UPDATE deployment_assignments; admin has full access
security.privacy_controls: GDPR erasure: soft-delete sets volunteer.status=anonymized, nullifies PII columns, retains deployment_assignments with anonymized volunteer_id for mission record integrity; DO Spaces credential files purged
security.audit_and_logging_controls: All background check record access logged to volunteer_audit_log with user_id, action, volunteer_id, ip_address, timestamp; exported weekly to secure S3-compatible DO Spaces bucket for compliance

data.entities: volunteers (id UUID, auth0_sub, first_name, last_name, email, phone, nationality, passport_number_encrypted, status ENUM pending/active/suspended/anonymized, created_at), skill_certifications (id UUID, volunteer_id FK, skill_category TEXT, certification_name TEXT, issuing_body TEXT, issued_date DATE, expiry_date DATE, credential_file_key TEXT, verified BOOL), deployment_assignments (id UUID, volunteer_id FK, mission_id FK, role TEXT, assigned_at TIMESTAMP, confirmed_at TIMESTAMP, status ENUM pending/confirmed/cancelled/completed), availability_windows (id UUID, volunteer_id FK, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, timezone TEXT, constraints TEXT), background_checks (id UUID, volunteer_id FK, provider TEXT, check_type TEXT, jurisdiction TEXT, submitted_at TIMESTAMP, status ENUM pending/clear/consider/adverse, result_detail_encrypted TEXT, completed_at TIMESTAMP), deployment_missions (id UUID, name TEXT, location TEXT, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, required_skills JSONB, headcount INT, status ENUM planning/active/completed)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on DO managed database; Redis 7 for session cache, availability search result cache TTL 5min, and webhook retry queue; DO Spaces for credential file storage with pre-signed URL access
data.analytics_events: volunteer_registered, background_check_submitted, background_check_completed (with outcome), certification_uploaded, assignment_created, assignment_confirmed, mission_activated - emitted to analytics_events table for dashboard aggregation

devops.deployment_topology: DigitalOcean App Platform: SvelteKit frontend as static site component + Node SSR server, Fastify API as web service component (2x basic-xs instances), DO Managed PostgreSQL (basic-xs), DO Managed Redis (basic-xs). Total ~$90/month base.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: eslint + tsc typecheck + vitest (frontend), jest (backend), doctl app spec validate + deploy on merge to main. Staging environment on App Platform deployed on PR merge to develop.
devops.cost_controls: App Platform basic-xs instances at ~$12/month each; DO Managed PostgreSQL basic-xs $15/month; DO Managed Redis basic-xs $15/month; DO Spaces $5/month. Total ~$130/month well within $600 cap. Scale to professional-xs ($24/month) when API CPU exceeds 70%.

ISS-001.title: PostgreSQL row-level security policies not specified
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan described RBAC with Auth0 but did not define PostgreSQL row-level security policies to enforce data isolation between volunteer, HR, and ops roles at the database layer. Without RLS, authorization relies solely on application-layer checks.

history.note: Identified by security reviewer; Auth0 RBAC without DB-level RLS is insufficient for personal data isolation

ISS-002.title: GDPR erasure workflow not defined
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan noted GDPR requirements but did not specify the erasure workflow. Hard-delete of volunteers breaks referential integrity in deployment_assignments; soft-delete with anonymization pattern not described.

history.note: Identified by security reviewer; legal team flagged erasure as P1 requirement

ISS-003.title: Analytics dashboard metrics not specified
ISS-003.detail: Round 1 plan mentioned an admin analytics dashboard but did not specify which metrics operations and HR teams need for volunteer pipeline visibility.

history.note: Identified by critic; vague analytics description adds implementation ambiguity

focus_issues.title: PostgreSQL row-level security policies not specified
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan described RBAC with Auth0 but did not define PostgreSQL row-level security policies to enforce data isolation at the database layer.

focus_issues.title: GDPR erasure workflow not defined
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan noted GDPR requirements but did not specify the soft-delete anonymization workflow for volunteer erasure requests.

latest_recommendations: Define PostgreSQL row-level security policies per role: volunteers SELECT/UPDATE own rows; hr_staff scoped by jurisdiction; ops_staff read-all/write-assignments
Specify GDPR erasure workflow: soft-delete sets volunteer.status=anonymized, nullifies PII columns, purges DO Spaces credential files, retains deployment_assignments with anonymized reference
Enumerate analytics dashboard metrics: volunteer registration funnel, background check completion rate, certification expiry count, deployment utilization rate by skill category

convergence_state.reason: High-severity RLS gap and medium-severity GDPR erasure gap unresolved

previous_audits.summary: Round 1 plan is solid with appropriate technology choices for a cost-constrained NGO platform. The Auth0 RBAC and Fastify architecture are well-suited to the use case. Two gaps prevent implementation readiness: database-layer row-level security policies are unspecified, and the GDPR erasure workflow is absent.
strengths: SvelteKit SSR for public volunteer portal is the right call for SEO and initial load performance
Fastify background check webhook design with idempotent processing is appropriately specified
DigitalOcean App Platform cost estimate is realistic and within budget
concerns: Auth0 RBAC without PostgreSQL RLS leaves data isolation dependent on application-layer code alone
GDPR erasure requirement cannot be met with hard-delete due to referential integrity in deployment_assignments
Analytics dashboard described as generic without specific metric definitions
blocking_issues: RLS policies must be defined before HR staff access to vetting records is implemented
recommendations: Define PostgreSQL RLS: CREATE POLICY volunteer_self ON volunteers USING (auth0_sub = current_setting('app.current_user_sub')); hr_staff policy scoped by jurisdiction column

Specify erasure workflow: UPDATE volunteers SET status='anonymized', first_name=null, last_name=null, email=null, passport_number_encrypted=null WHERE id=:id; DELETE from DO Spaces credential files; preserve deployment_assignments rows

issue_updates.title: PostgreSQL row-level security policies not specified
issue_updates.detail: security_and_compliance section described Auth0 RBAC only

issue_updates.title: GDPR erasure workflow not defined
issue_updates.detail: privacy_retention_policy contract field present but erasure workflow absent from plan

previous_plan.title: Humanitarian Volunteer Management Platform - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: SvelteKit + Fastify fullstack platform on DigitalOcean App Platform for recruiting, vetting, and deploying humanitarian volunteers. Auth0 RBAC for multi-role access. PostgreSQL for volunteer and assignment data. Background check integration via third-party API.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: SvelteKit SSR frontend and Fastify API deployed on DigitalOcean App Platform. Auth0 for authentication. PostgreSQL 16 managed database. Background check webhooks processed by Fastify endpoint. No PostgreSQL row-level security defined.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Auth0 RBAC with volunteer/ops_staff/hr_staff/admin roles. MFA for staff. TLS 1.3. GDPR compliance noted but erasure workflow not specified.
previous_plan.data_model: volunteers, skill_certifications, deployment_assignments, availability_windows, background_checks, deployment_missions tables.

target_output.thinking_summary: Fixing two blocking issues: (1) specifying PostgreSQL row-level security policies for each role to enforce data isolation at the database layer beyond Auth0 application-layer checks; (2) defining the GDPR erasure workflow using soft-delete anonymization that nullifies PII columns while preserving deployment_assignment referential integrity. Also resolving the analytics dashboard metrics for ISS-003. This is a strong-quality plan with all sections richly detailed.

fix_report.action_taken: Defined PostgreSQL RLS policies in security_and_compliance: volunteer_self_policy using auth0_sub session variable; hr_staff_jurisdiction_policy scoped by background_checks.jurisdiction matching hr_staff.jurisdiction_scope; ops_staff_read_policy on volunteers for search; admin bypasses RLS via BYPASSRLS role attribute
fix_report.expected_outcome: Database enforces data isolation independent of application layer; HR staff cannot read vetting records outside their jurisdiction even if application-layer bug exists

fix_report.action_taken: Specified GDPR erasure workflow in security_and_compliance and workflows sections: erasure sets volunteer.status=anonymized, nullifies all PII columns (first_name, last_name, email, phone, passport_number_encrypted, national_id_encrypted), purges all credential files from DO Spaces, retains deployment_assignments rows with volunteer_id preserved for mission record integrity but volunteer display name replaced with 'Anonymized Volunteer'
fix_report.expected_outcome: Erasure requests processed within 30 days meeting GDPR right-to-erasure; mission historical records preserved with anonymized reference

target_output.title: Humanitarian Volunteer Management Platform - GDPR-Compliant with PostgreSQL RLS (Round 2)
target_output.executive_summary: SvelteKit SSR and Fastify fullstack platform on DigitalOcean App Platform for end-to-end humanitarian volunteer lifecycle management: registration, background screening, certification tracking, deployment matching, and mission rostering. Auth0 RBAC enforced at both application layer and PostgreSQL row-level security policies. GDPR erasure via soft-delete anonymization preserving mission history. Background check async webhooks from Sterling Volunteers processed idempotently. Infrastructure cost ~$130/month against $600/month budget.
target_output.architecture_overview: Three-tier architecture: SvelteKit SSR frontend (public volunteer portal with SEO-optimized server-side rendering + authenticated SPA views) communicating via HTTPS to Fastify 4 REST API on DigitalOcean App Platform, backed by DO Managed PostgreSQL 16 with row-level security and DO Managed Redis 7. Auth0 Universal Login handles authentication; JWT claims passed to Fastify middleware which sets the PostgreSQL session variable app.current_user_sub for RLS policy evaluation. Background check webhooks from Sterling Volunteers arrive at POST /webhooks/background-check with HMAC-SHA256 signature verification, processed idempotently via webhook_events deduplication table. Credential documents stored in DO Spaces with pre-signed URL access. Certification expiry alerts run via pg_cron scheduled daily at 08:00 UTC, dispatching emails via SendGrid for certifications expiring within 30 and 7 days.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: SvelteKit 2, TypeScript 5, Tailwind CSS 3, Svelte stores, SvelteKit form actions. Backend: Node.js 20 LTS, Fastify 4, Zod for request validation, Auth0 JWT middleware, pg (node-postgres) with RLS session variable injection. Database: PostgreSQL 16 (DO Managed) with row-level security and pg_cron extension. Cache: Redis 7 (DO Managed) for sessions and availability search cache. File storage: DigitalOcean Spaces with pre-signed URL generation. Email: SendGrid API for transactional notifications. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Hosting: DigitalOcean App Platform. Monitoring: Grafana + Prometheus (DO Managed).
target_output.functional_feature_map: Volunteer Registration: multi-step public SvelteKit form collecting personal details, professional credentials with DO Spaces file upload, skill profile (SPHERE competencies, language proficiencies), and initial availability windows. Background Screening Workflow: HR staff submits check via POST /screening/submit to Sterling Volunteers API; async webhook updates background_checks.status via state machine (pending -> clear/consider/adverse); HR staff reviews 'consider' results for adjudication decision. Certification Tracker: skill_certifications table with expiry_date; pg_cron daily job queries certifications expiring within 30 and 7 days and enqueues SendGrid notifications. Deployment Matcher: ops staff uses search endpoint filtering volunteers by skill_category[], language[], availability_windows overlap with mission dates, and status=active with background_checks cleared; results cached in Redis TTL 5min. Mission Roster Manager: deployment_assignments with conflict detection (overlapping assignments for same volunteer rejected at DB constraint level). GDPR Erasure Handler: HR admin triggers POST /volunteers/:id/erasure; workflow anonymizes volunteer record and purges DO Spaces files.
target_output.system_components: DigitalOcean App Platform: SvelteKit Node SSR service (2x basic-xs), Fastify API service (2x basic-xs). DO Managed PostgreSQL 16 basic-xs with pg_cron and row-level security enabled. DO Managed Redis 7 basic-xs for session cache, availability search cache (TTL 5min), and webhook retry queue. DO Spaces bucket for volunteer credential files with private ACL and pre-signed URL generation. Auth0 tenant with Universal Login, RBAC permissions, and MFA enforcement Action for ops_staff and hr_staff roles. SendGrid transactional email for certification expiry alerts, background check status updates, and deployment confirmations. GitHub Actions CI pipeline. Grafana + Prometheus via DigitalOcean managed metrics.
target_output.workflows: Background Check Async Workflow: HR staff clicks 'Submit Screening' in HR Panel; Fastify calls Sterling Volunteers API POST /checks with volunteer PII (name, DOB, jurisdiction); records background_checks row with status=pending; Sterling calls back POST /webhooks/background-check with HMAC-SHA256 signed payload; Fastify verifies signature, checks webhook_events for duplicate event_id, updates background_checks.status via state machine transition (pending -> clear/consider/adverse); if 'consider', HR staff receives email alert for manual adjudication; once cleared, volunteer.status transitions to active. GDPR Erasure Workflow: volunteer submits erasure request via portal or HR staff processes request; Fastify creates erasure_requests record; pg transaction: UPDATE volunteers SET status='anonymized', first_name=NULL, last_name=NULL, email=NULL, phone=NULL, passport_number_encrypted=NULL, national_id_encrypted=NULL WHERE id=:id; DELETE all skill_certifications credential files from DO Spaces; background_checks result_detail_encrypted set to NULL; deployment_assignments retained with volunteer_id preserved, display_name overridden to 'Anonymized Volunteer' in query join; erasure_requests.completed_at set; confirmation email sent within 72 hours.
target_output.data_model: volunteers (id UUID PK, auth0_sub TEXT UNIQUE, first_name TEXT, last_name TEXT, email TEXT UNIQUE, phone TEXT, nationality TEXT, passport_number_encrypted TEXT, national_id_encrypted TEXT, status ENUM pending/active/suspended/anonymized, created_at TIMESTAMP). skill_certifications (id UUID PK, volunteer_id FK, skill_category TEXT, certification_name TEXT, issuing_body TEXT, issued_date DATE, expiry_date DATE, credential_file_key TEXT, verified BOOL, verified_by FK, verified_at TIMESTAMP). deployment_assignments (id UUID PK, volunteer_id FK, mission_id FK, role TEXT, assigned_by FK, assigned_at TIMESTAMP, confirmed_at TIMESTAMP, status ENUM pending/confirmed/cancelled/completed). availability_windows (id UUID PK, volunteer_id FK, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, timezone TEXT, notes TEXT). background_checks (id UUID PK, volunteer_id FK, provider TEXT ENUM sterling/checkr, check_type TEXT, jurisdiction TEXT, submitted_at TIMESTAMP, status ENUM pending/clear/consider/adverse, result_detail_encrypted TEXT, adjudication TEXT, completed_at TIMESTAMP). deployment_missions (id UUID PK, name TEXT, location TEXT, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, required_skills JSONB, headcount INT, status ENUM planning/active/completed). webhook_events (id TEXT PK, provider TEXT, received_at TIMESTAMP, processed BOOL). erasure_requests (id UUID PK, volunteer_id FK, requested_at TIMESTAMP, completed_at TIMESTAMP, requested_by FK). PostgreSQL RLS Policies: volunteer_self_rls on volunteers USING (auth0_sub = current_setting('app.current_user_sub', true)); hr_jurisdiction_rls on background_checks USING (jurisdiction = current_setting('app.user_jurisdiction', true) OR current_setting('app.user_role', true) = 'admin'); ops_staff_rls on volunteers FOR SELECT USING (current_setting('app.user_role', true) IN ('ops_staff','hr_staff','admin')).
target_output.api_design: Fastify REST API at /api/v1 with OpenAPI 3.1 spec. POST /volunteers (register, public). GET /volunteers (search by skill[], language[], available_from, available_to - ops_staff+). GET /volunteers/:id (own profile or ops_staff+). PATCH /volunteers/:id (update profile). POST /volunteers/:id/erasure (hr_staff+). POST /screening/submit {volunteer_id, check_type, jurisdiction} (hr_staff+). GET /screening/:volunteer_id (hr_staff+). POST /certifications (upload with multipart - volunteer self). GET /certifications?expiring_within=30 (hr_staff+). POST /assignments {volunteer_id, mission_id, role} (ops_staff+). GET /missions (ops_staff+). GET /analytics/pipeline (admin+). POST /webhooks/background-check (Sterling callback, HMAC verified). Auth: Auth0 JWT Bearer on all /api/v1 routes except POST /volunteers.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Authentication: Auth0 Universal Login; JWT with roles claim propagated to Fastify middleware; MFA enforced for ops_staff and hr_staff via Auth0 Pre-Login Action checking role claim. PostgreSQL Row-Level Security: RLS enabled on volunteers, background_checks, and skill_certifications tables. Fastify middleware injects SET LOCAL app.current_user_sub, app.user_role, and app.user_jurisdiction into each transaction. Policies: volunteer_self_rls ensures volunteers SELECT and UPDATE only their own rows; hr_jurisdiction_rls scopes background_checks reads to matching jurisdiction; ops_read_rls allows ops_staff SELECT on all active volunteers for deployment search; BYPASSRLS granted to admin role only. GDPR Controls: erasure workflow soft-deletes volunteer PII within 30-day SLA; DO Spaces credential files purged synchronously within erasure transaction; pg_cron retention job hard-deletes anonymized records after 3-year retention period. WAF: DigitalOcean App Platform WAF enabled with OWASP Core Rule Set. Audit log: volunteer_audit_log records all background_check reads by HR staff with user_id, target_volunteer_id, action, timestamp; exported weekly to DO Spaces for GDPR compliance review. Background check webhook: HMAC-SHA256 signature verified against Sterling Volunteers signing secret stored in DO environment variable.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: DigitalOcean App Platform spec: SvelteKit SSR service (2x basic-xs, $24/month), Fastify API service (2x basic-xs, $24/month), DO Managed PostgreSQL 16 basic-xs ($15/month), DO Managed Redis 7 basic-xs ($15/month), DO Spaces ($5/month), Grafana Managed ($20/month). Total ~$103/month base; scale to professional-xs at 70% CPU ($48/month per service). GitHub Actions CI: eslint + tsc (frontend), jest + coverage check 80% (backend), doctl app spec validate on PR, doctl app create-deployment on merge to main for staging, manual promotion gate for production. Database migrations: db-migrate with sequential versioning; migrations run as App Platform Job component before API deployment. Environment secrets in DO App Platform encrypted environment variables. Rollback: App Platform one-click rollback to previous deployment SHA.
target_output.observability: Grafana + Prometheus via DigitalOcean Managed Metrics: API request rate and p95 latency per endpoint, App Platform instance CPU/memory, PostgreSQL connection pool utilization and query latency, Redis cache hit rate. Custom business metrics logged as structured JSON: background_check_submitted_count, background_check_completion_rate_7d, certification_expiry_alerts_sent_count, deployment_assignment_created_count, erasure_request_completed_count. SendGrid event webhooks for email delivery/bounce rates. Alerts: background check webhook failure rate >5% -> ops Slack; certification expiry job failure -> hr Slack; API error rate >2% -> on-call email.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Monthly infrastructure: SvelteKit SSR 2x basic-xs $24 + Fastify API 2x basic-xs $24 + PostgreSQL basic-xs $15 + Redis basic-xs $15 + Spaces $5 + Grafana $20 = ~$103/month base. SendGrid Essentials $19.95/month for up to 50K emails. Total ~$123/month against $600/month budget - 79% headroom. Scaling triggers: API CPU >70% for 15min -> scale to professional-xs ($48/month each); PostgreSQL connections >80% pool -> upgrade to professional-xs ($50/month); DO Spaces egress >1TB/month -> review credential file CDN caching.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (10 weeks): Volunteer registration with DO Spaces credential upload, Auth0 RBAC with RLS policies, background check submission and webhook processing (Sterling Volunteers), skill certification tracking, ops staff search by skill and availability, deployment assignment creation with conflict detection, email notifications via SendGrid. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Certification expiry pg_cron alert job, GDPR erasure workflow with DO Spaces purge, mission roster manager with headcount tracking, analytics dashboard with pipeline funnel and deployment utilization metrics. Phase 3 (future): AI-assisted deployment matching via embedding similarity on skill profiles, mobile volunteer app for availability updates, multi-language portal (FR, ES, AR for international volunteer base).
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode for both SvelteKit and Fastify. Zod schemas as source of truth for API request/response types shared via packages/schemas workspace. Jest for Fastify API with >80% coverage on business logic; Vitest + Playwright for SvelteKit E2E tests covering registration, assignment, and erasure flows. PostgreSQL RLS policies tested via integration tests that execute queries as different role sessions. GitHub Actions blocks merge if coverage drops below 80% or if doctl app spec validate fails. All volunteer PII fields in queries must use parameterized queries - no string interpolation. Erasure workflow changes require two-person code review.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Sterling Volunteers webhook latency can delay background check status updates by 24-72h for international jurisdiction checks. Mitigation: background_checks.status displays 'in progress' with estimated completion time in HR Panel; ops staff can provisionally assign volunteers pending clearance with a 'conditional' assignment status. Risk: PostgreSQL RLS policy regression if Fastify middleware fails to set session variables before query execution. Mitigation: integration tests assert RLS policies deny access when session variables are missing; CI enforces these tests on every PR. Tradeoff: soft-delete anonymization over hard-delete preserves mission historical records but increases schema complexity with nullable PII columns; accepted per GDPR right-to-erasure legal advice. Tradeoff: DigitalOcean App Platform over Kubernetes reduces ops overhead at cost of less fine-grained resource control; appropriate for NGO team with no dedicated DevOps staff.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Background check vendor selected as Sterling Volunteers pending contract confirmation (supports UK DBS, US FCRA, EU national equivalents). PostgreSQL RLS policies defined and tested in staging. GDPR erasure workflow documented with 30-day SLA. Analytics dashboard metrics enumerated: volunteer registration funnel (registered/pending/active), background check completion rate (7-day rolling), certification expiry count by category, deployment utilization rate by skill category, mission fill rate.

metadata.notes: Round 2 resolves RLS policies and GDPR erasure workflow; strong quality with richly specified volunteer management domain content.

profile.domain: Nonprofit Cloud Infrastructure Cost Auditing CLI

project_goal.value: CLI tool for nonprofit finance teams to audit cloud infrastructure costs across multiple provider accounts, identify unused resources, and generate rightsizing recommendations to reduce overhead and maximize programmatic spending
project_goal.rationale: Core operational need to reduce cloud waste and redirect savings to program delivery

target_users.value: Nonprofit finance and IT staff running periodic cost audits on AWS accounts
target_users.rationale: Primary users are non-technical finance staff and part-time IT generalists

project_class.rationale: Command-line tool for local execution on finance team laptops; no server-side hosting required

capabilities.rationale: AWS API integration, SQLite local data store, cost analysis logic; no frontend or auth module

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-account AWS enumeration, utilization metric aggregation, and heuristic rightsizing logic

risk_level.rationale: Read-only AWS API access; no data written to cloud resources; internal use only

data_sensitivity.rationale: Cost and resource inventory data is internal financial information; no personal data

external_exposure.rationale: CLI runs locally on finance team laptops with AWS credentials from IAM roles; no network exposure

access_model.value: AWS IAM roles with read-only policies assumed via aws sts assume-role; no custom auth
access_model.rationale: Leverages existing AWS IAM infrastructure; no additional credential management required

feature_scope.value: Multi-account AWS resource enumeration, cost record collection from Cost Explorer, utilization metric pull from CloudWatch, rightsizing recommendation engine, SQLite audit report, human-readable cost summary output
feature_scope.rationale: Complete cost audit workflow from enumeration to actionable recommendations

mvp_scope.value: Single-account EC2 and RDS enumeration with Cost Explorer data, basic idle detection heuristic, SQLite report output, and text summary to stdout
mvp_scope.rationale: Demonstrate core audit workflow before multi-account and multi-resource expansion

security_baseline.value: Read-only IAM policies (ReadOnlyAccess + CostExplorerAccess), AWS credentials never persisted to SQLite or logs, no plaintext secrets in config files
security_baseline.rationale: Minimal attack surface for internal-only CLI tool

frontend_stack.value: none - CLI tool with stdout/stderr output only
frontend_stack.rationale: No UI required; output is text tables and SQLite file

backend_stack.rationale: Go produces single binary with no runtime dependency; Cobra provides structured subcommand parsing

data_platform.value: SQLite 3 for local audit report persistence
data_platform.rationale: Zero-dependency local database; finance staff can open SQLite reports in DB Browser for SQLite

hosting_target.value: Local developer and finance team machines; distributed as pre-compiled Go binary via GitHub Releases
hosting_target.rationale: No server-side hosting; single binary install eliminates runtime dependencies

privacy_retention_policy.value: SQLite audit reports retained by finance team per their record retention policy; tool does not enforce retention; no cloud-side data storage
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Tool generates local files; retention is user responsibility

future_scope.value: Multi-cloud support (Azure Cost Management, GCP Billing), scheduled audit automation via cron, HTML report export
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap after AWS MVP validation

constraints.value: Must run on macOS and Linux; single binary install; AWS read-only IAM only; no external service calls except AWS APIs
constraints.rationale: Finance staff have restricted IT environments; no ability to install language runtimes

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for resource utilization metrics pulled during audit; local structured log file written per audit run
observability_baseline.rationale: CLI tool observability limited to local log output and CloudWatch API reads

execution_preference.value: Single-team Go development with GitHub Actions CI; versioned releases via semantic versioning
execution_preference.rationale: Small team, straightforward release cadence

project.goal: CLI tool to audit AWS cloud costs and generate rightsizing recommendations
project.target_users: Nonprofit finance and IT staff
project.timeline: MVP in 6 weeks, full multi-account support by week 12

backend.framework: Cobra CLI with Viper for config

security.auth: AWS IAM roles via sts:AssumeRole

data.db: SQLite 3 via modernc.org/sqlite (pure Go, no CGO)

devops.distribution: GitHub Releases pre-compiled binaries (darwin/amd64, darwin/arm64, linux/amd64)

open_questions.cloudwatch_granularity: 14-day or 30-day CloudWatch utilization lookback for idle detection?

reasoner_reviews.product: The cost audit workflow is well-scoped for MVP. The rightsizing recommendation heuristic must be clearly documented in the output so finance staff understand the basis for recommendations. The SQLite report format must be self-contained so finance teams can share the report file without the CLI binary.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go with Cobra and aws-sdk-go-v2 is the right stack for a portable single-binary CLI. The modernc.org/sqlite pure Go SQLite driver eliminates CGO compilation complexity. Two rounds have not resolved the CloudWatch lookback period for idle detection heuristics, which directly affects recommendation accuracy. Round 2 still did not specify the exact CLI command surface (subcommands, flags, output formats).
reasoner_reviews.security: Read-only IAM policy is appropriately constrained. The tool must verify that assumed role ARNs are from the expected organization accounts to prevent confused-deputy attacks if an attacker can influence the config file.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 14-day CloudWatch lookback is the minimum for reliable idle detection; 30-day recommended for seasonal workloads. The CloudWatch granularity open question from round 1 is still unresolved in round 2 - this is a persistent gap.

backend.service_design: Cobra CLI with subcommands: audit (main workflow), report (query SQLite output), config (manage account profiles). Each subcommand maps to a domain package: enumerator (AWS resource lister), collector (Cost Explorer and CloudWatch puller), analyzer (rightsizing heuristics), reporter (SQLite writer and stdout formatter)
backend.api_patterns: No REST API. Internal interfaces: enumerator.ListResources(ctx, cfg) returns ResourceInventory slice; collector.PullCostData(ctx, cfg, resources) returns CostRecord slice; analyzer.GenerateRecommendations(resources, costs, metrics) returns Recommendation slice; reporter.WriteReport(db, report) writes to SQLite
backend.business_modules: AccountConnector (sts:AssumeRole, credential chain management), ResourceEnumerator (EC2 DescribeInstances, RDS DescribeDBInstances, ELB DescribeLoadBalancers, S3 ListBuckets), CostCollector (Cost Explorer GetCostAndUsage by resource tag), MetricCollector (CloudWatch GetMetricStatistics for CPU, NetworkIn, IOPS), IdleDetector (threshold heuristics: EC2 avg CPU <5% over lookback, RDS avg connections <1), RightsizingAdvisor (instance family downsizing map, spot recommendation for stateless EC2), ReportWriter (SQLite schema init, insert, query)
backend.failure_handling: AWS API throttling: exponential backoff with jitter via aws-sdk-go-v2 retry config; max 5 retries per API call. CloudWatch metric gaps (sparse data): treat as insufficient data, skip recommendation rather than false positive. SQLite write errors: fatal with cleanup of partial report file.

security.auth_design: AWS credential chain: environment variables first (CI use), then ~/.aws/credentials profile matching account_id in config, then sts:AssumeRole with MFA token support. Role ARN validated against configured org_id prefix (arn:aws:iam::{org_account_id}:role/) to prevent confused-deputy.
security.secrets_management: AWS credentials never written to SQLite schema or structured log file; cost and resource data logged without ARN or account-id in default verbosity; --verbose flag required to include account metadata in logs
security.security_risks: Risk: config file storing role ARNs could be modified to assume unintended roles. Mitigation: role ARN org prefix validation. Risk: SQLite report contains cost breakdowns that reveal budget allocation; treat as internal-confidential and document file handling guidance.

data.entities: cloud_accounts (id INTEGER PK, account_id TEXT, account_alias TEXT, role_arn TEXT, audited_at TIMESTAMP), resource_inventories (id INTEGER PK, audit_id FK, resource_id TEXT, resource_type ENUM ec2/rds/elb/s3, region TEXT, instance_type TEXT, tags_json TEXT, state TEXT, created_at TEXT), cost_records (id INTEGER PK, resource_id FK, period_start DATE, period_end DATE, blended_cost_usd REAL, usage_type TEXT), rightsizing_recommendations (id INTEGER PK, resource_id FK, recommendation_type ENUM downsize/terminate/reserve/spot, current_type TEXT, recommended_type TEXT, estimated_monthly_savings_usd REAL, confidence ENUM high/medium/low, rationale TEXT), audit_reports (id INTEGER PK, run_id TEXT UNIQUE, started_at TIMESTAMP, completed_at TIMESTAMP, account_count INT, resource_count INT, total_monthly_cost_usd REAL, estimated_savings_usd REAL)
data.storage_design: Single SQLite file per audit run: cloud-audit-{YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS}.db written to current working directory or --output-dir flag path. Schema initialized via embedded SQL migration on first write. No WAL mode required; single-writer sequential write pattern.
data.data_risks: Risk: SQLite file contains financial cost breakdowns; not encrypted at rest. Mitigation: document that output files are internal-confidential; future phase may add --encrypt-output flag with passphrase-derived key.

devops.deployment_topology: No server deployment. GitHub Releases provides pre-compiled binaries: darwin/amd64, darwin/arm64, linux/amd64. Users download binary, chmod +x, and add to PATH. No installer required.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: go test ./... + go vet + staticcheck on PR. On tag push (v*): goreleaser builds and publishes multi-arch binaries to GitHub Releases with checksum file. Release notes auto-generated from conventional commits.
devops.devops_risks: Risk: binary distribution without code signing may trigger macOS Gatekeeper quarantine. Mitigation: document notarization process for future release; interim workaround documented in README (xattr -d com.apple.quarantine).

fix_report.expected_outcome: Finance staff can adjust lookback period for their workload seasonality; idle detection heuristics are reproducible and auditable

target_output.title: Nonprofit Cloud Cost Auditing CLI - Full Command Surface and Configurable Heuristics (Round 3)
target_output.executive_summary: Go 1.22 single-binary CLI using Cobra for structured subcommand parsing, auditing AWS EC2, RDS, ELB, and S3 costs across multiple accounts via assumed IAM roles. CloudWatch utilization metrics pulled over a configurable lookback window (default 30 days, minimum 14 days) to identify idle resources. Rightsizing recommendations written to SQLite audit report and formatted as stdout table with columns ResourceID, Type, Region, MonthlyCost, Recommendation, EstimatedSavings, Confidence. Pre-compiled binaries for macOS (amd64/arm64) and Linux (amd64) distributed via GitHub Releases. NOTE: HTML report export described in future scope has no design detail in this round.
target_output.architecture_overview: Go 1.22 CLI binary built with Cobra subcommand framework. Four internal packages: enumerator (aws-sdk-go-v2 calls to list EC2 instances, RDS clusters, ELBs, S3 buckets), collector (Cost Explorer GetCostAndUsage per resource, CloudWatch GetMetricStatistics with configurable lookback_days for CPU/connections/IOPS), analyzer (IdleDetector threshold engine + RightsizingAdvisor instance family downsizing table), reporter (SQLite schema init + insert + table/JSON/CSV stdout formatter). Command invocation: cloud-audit audit --config accounts.yaml --output-dir ./reports --lookback-days 30 --format table. AWS credentials sourced via aws-sdk-go-v2 credential chain (env vars -> ~/.aws/credentials profile -> sts:AssumeRole from config file). Role ARN org prefix validated against configured org_account_prefix before assumption. modernc.org/sqlite pure Go driver eliminates CGO build dependency enabling simple cross-compilation.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: Go 1.22. CLI framework: Cobra 1.8 with Viper 2 for YAML config. AWS SDK: aws-sdk-go-v2 (ec2, rds, elasticloadbalancingv2, s3, costexplorer, cloudwatch, sts modules). Database: SQLite 3 via modernc.org/sqlite (pure Go, no CGO). Output formatting: tablewriter for stdout tables, encoding/json for JSON output. Build: goreleaser for multi-arch binary release. CI: GitHub Actions. Distribution: GitHub Releases with SHA256 checksums.
target_output.functional_feature_map: cloud-audit audit: connects to each account in config.yaml via sts:AssumeRole, enumerates EC2/RDS/ELB/S3 resources, pulls Cost Explorer data for trailing 30 days, pulls CloudWatch metrics over --lookback-days window, runs IdleDetector (EC2 avg CPU <5%, RDS avg Connections <1) and RightsizingAdvisor (instance family downsizing map, Spot recommendation for stateless EC2), writes SQLite report, prints summary table to stdout. cloud-audit report: queries existing SQLite report file, filters by --resource-type, --min-savings, --confidence; outputs table/JSON/CSV. cloud-audit config validate: parses accounts.yaml and verifies role ARN org prefix format without making AWS API calls.
target_output.system_components: cloud-audit binary (Go 1.22 single binary, no runtime dependency). cmd/audit.go: Cobra audit subcommand handler, orchestrates enumerator -> collector -> analyzer -> reporter pipeline. cmd/report.go: Cobra report subcommand, queries SQLite. cmd/config.go: Cobra config subcommand with validate action. internal/enumerator/: AWS resource listing per service. internal/collector/: Cost Explorer and CloudWatch pulls; CloudWatch GetMetricStatistics called with StartTime=now-lookback_days, EndTime=now, Period=86400 (daily), Statistics=Average. internal/analyzer/: IdleDetector (EC2 CPU avg threshold 5%, RDS Connections avg threshold 1.0 over full lookback window); RightsizingAdvisor (embedded JSON map of current_instance_type -> recommended_instance_type with savings_pct). internal/reporter/: SQLite schema init via embedded SQL file, row inserters, table/JSON/CSV formatters. accounts.yaml config schema: {org_account_prefix: str, accounts: [{alias: str, account_id: str, role_arn: str, regions: [str]}]}.
target_output.workflows: Single-Account Audit Workflow: user runs cloud-audit audit --config accounts.yaml --output-dir ./reports --lookback-days 30. CLI reads accounts.yaml, calls sts:AssumeRole for first account, initializes aws-sdk-go-v2 session with temporary credentials. ResourceEnumerator calls DescribeInstances, DescribeDBInstances, DescribeLoadBalancers, ListBuckets sequentially per configured region. CostCollector calls Cost Explorer GetCostAndUsage with GroupBy resource tag for the trailing 30-day period, storing blended costs per resource_id. MetricCollector calls CloudWatch GetMetricStatistics for each EC2 instance (AWS/EC2 CPUUtilization, NetworkIn) and RDS cluster (AWS/RDS DatabaseConnections) with StartTime=now-30d, Period=86400, Statistics=Average. IdleDetector flags resources where avg metric over all daily datapoints is below threshold. RightsizingAdvisor looks up current instance type in embedded downsizing map; if avg CPU 5-40%, recommends next-smaller instance; if avg CPU <5%, recommends termination or Reserved Instance review. ReportWriter initializes SQLite schema, inserts all records, closes database. CLI prints summary table and path to SQLite file. Multi-Account Batch Workflow: audit subcommand iterates over all accounts in config.yaml sequentially; each account adds to the same SQLite report file with account_alias prefixed in resource_id display.
target_output.data_model: cloud_accounts (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, account_id TEXT NOT NULL, account_alias TEXT, role_arn TEXT, regions_json TEXT, audited_at DATETIME). resource_inventories (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, audit_id INTEGER FK, resource_id TEXT NOT NULL, resource_type TEXT CHECK(resource_type IN ('ec2','rds','elb','s3')), region TEXT, instance_type TEXT, tags_json TEXT, state TEXT, launch_date TEXT). cost_records (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, resource_id TEXT FK, period_start DATE, period_end DATE, blended_cost_usd REAL, usage_type TEXT). rightsizing_recommendations (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, resource_id TEXT FK, recommendation_type TEXT CHECK(recommendation_type IN ('downsize','terminate','reserve','spot')), current_type TEXT, recommended_type TEXT, estimated_monthly_savings_usd REAL, confidence TEXT CHECK(confidence IN ('high','medium','low')), rationale TEXT). audit_reports (id INTEGER PK AUTOINCREMENT, run_id TEXT UNIQUE, started_at DATETIME, completed_at DATETIME, account_count INT, resource_count INT, total_monthly_cost_usd REAL, estimated_savings_usd REAL, lookback_days INT).
target_output.api_design: CLI command surface (cloud-audit binary): cloud-audit audit --config PATH (default accounts.yaml) --output-dir PATH (default ./cloud-audit-reports) --lookback-days INT (default 30, min 14, max 90) --format STRING (table|json|csv, default table) --dry-run (enumerate and analyze, skip SQLite write) --verbose (include account IDs and ARNs in log output). cloud-audit report --db PATH (SQLite file) --resource-type STRING (ec2|rds|elb|s3, optional filter) --min-savings FLOAT (filter by minimum monthly savings USD) --confidence STRING (high|medium|low filter) --format STRING (table|json|csv). cloud-audit config validate --config PATH. Stdout table format for audit command: RESOURCE ID | TYPE | REGION | MONTHLY COST | RECOMMENDATION | EST. SAVINGS | CONFIDENCE. Example: i-0abc123 | ec2 | us-east-1 | $47.23 | downsize to t3.small | $23.60 | high. Exit codes: 0 success, 1 config error, 2 AWS API error, 3 SQLite write error.
target_output.security_and_compliance: IAM: each configured role must have ReadOnlyAccess + AWSCostExplorerServiceFullAccess + CloudWatchReadOnlyAccess. Role ARN org prefix validated before sts:AssumeRole call using org_account_prefix config field. Credentials: aws-sdk-go-v2 credential chain reads from environment variables or ~/.aws/credentials; temporary sts tokens never written to SQLite or log file even at --verbose level. Config file: accounts.yaml must not contain AWS secret keys; validated at startup with config validate. Log output: at default verbosity, resource_id and cost values logged; account_id and role_arn only at --verbose. Output security: cloud-audit-{date}.db files contain financial cost data; README documents internal-confidential handling guidance.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Distribution: goreleaser builds darwin/amd64, darwin/arm64, linux/amd64 binaries on Git tag push. GitHub Release includes binaries, SHA256 checksum file, and release notes generated from conventional commits. accounts.yaml config template included in release assets. GitHub Actions CI workflow: on PR - go test ./... -race -coverprofile=coverage.out, go vet ./..., staticcheck ./...; coverage gate >80% on analyzer and reporter packages. On v* tag push - goreleaser release workflow builds and publishes binaries. macOS Gatekeeper: interim workaround in README (xattr -d com.apple.quarantine ./cloud-audit); future release will add Apple Developer ID notarization. No server infrastructure; zero ongoing hosting cost.
target_output.observability: Local structured JSON log file written per audit run to --output-dir/cloud-audit-{date}.log with fields: timestamp, level, account_alias, action, resource_count, duration_ms, error. CloudWatch API call latency and retry counts logged at debug level. Audit run summary written to stdout and to audit_reports SQLite table: total resources scanned, total monthly cost, estimated savings, lookback_days used, run duration. Users can track savings realized over time by comparing sequential audit_reports.estimated_savings_usd values. No centralized monitoring infrastructure required.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost for CLI tool itself. AWS API costs: Cost Explorer GetCostAndUsage charges $0.01 per request; a 10-account audit with 500 resources generates ~15 Cost Explorer requests = $0.15 per audit run. CloudWatch GetMetricStatistics is free tier for first 10K API requests/month. GitHub Releases: free for public repositories. Total cost per monthly audit run: <$1. Scaling: Go concurrency could parallelize multi-account enumeration with goroutines per account if sequential execution exceeds 5 minutes for large accounts (future optimization).
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): Single-account EC2 and RDS enumeration, Cost Explorer pull, CloudWatch utilization with --lookback-days flag, IdleDetector heuristics, RightsizingAdvisor with EC2 instance family downsizing map, SQLite report with all five tables, stdout table formatter, GitHub Actions CI, goreleaser multi-arch binaries. Phase 2 (6 weeks): Multi-account iteration from accounts.yaml, ELB and S3 coverage, cloud-audit report subcommand with filter flags, JSON and CSV output formats, cloud-audit config validate subcommand. Phase 3 (future): Azure Cost Management and GCP Billing API integration, HTML report export (no design detail yet - requires UX research with finance team), cron-based automated audit scheduling.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go modules with go.sum committed. All AWS API calls wrapped in retry helper using aws-sdk-go-v2 RetryMaxAttempts=5 with exponential backoff. Table-driven tests for IdleDetector and RightsizingAdvisor threshold logic covering boundary conditions (exactly at threshold, sparse CloudWatch data, missing cost records). Integration tests use localstack for EC2/RDS enumeration and a mock Cost Explorer server. Coverage gate: >80% for internal/analyzer and internal/reporter packages; enumerator and collector tested via integration tests with localstack. CLI acceptance test: run cloud-audit audit --dry-run against localstack and assert stdout contains expected table columns.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: CloudWatch data sparsity for recently launched or low-traffic resources produces insufficient data points for idle detection; IdleDetector skips recommendation rather than producing false positives when datapoint count is below lookback_days/2. Risk: Cost Explorer resource-level costs require resource tag enforcement on AWS account; if resources are untagged, costs appear in catch-all line items and cannot be attributed to specific instances; tool warns when >20% of costs are unattributed. Tradeoff: 30-day default lookback catches monthly billing patterns but may flag resources that are only idle during the audit period; finance staff instructed to cross-reference with team resource owners before acting on recommendations. Known gap: HTML report export planned for Phase 3 but has no design detail; finance staff requesting this feature should be engaged in UX design before implementation.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: CloudWatch lookback period resolved: --lookback-days flag defaulting to 30 days; 14-day minimum documented for non-seasonal workloads; 90-day maximum to avoid excessive CloudWatch API costs. CLI subcommand surface fully documented with all flags, types, defaults, and stdout table column specifications. Multi-account YAML config format defined in round 2 (ISS-003 resolved). Remaining: HTML report export design deferred to Phase 3 UX research.

metadata.notes: Round 3 resolves CloudWatch lookback and CLI command surface but intentionally leaves HTML report export without design detail as flawed quality marker. Convergence approaching with two rounds of stable scores.

project_goal.value: Scalable data pipeline that ingests, harmonizes, and warehouses multi-source humanitarian needs assessment data from field surveys, partner reports, and government statistics to produce a unified needs dashboard for coordinated crisis response planning
project_goal.rationale: Core infrastructure need for inter-agency humanitarian coordination

target_users.value: Humanitarian coordination staff, field analysts, and partner NGO representatives consuming harmonized needs indicators
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers are humanitarian coordination officers and partner organization data teams

project_class.rationale: Core deliverable is ingestion, transformation, and warehousing of multi-source needs data; no interactive application UI

capabilities.rationale: Apache Beam batch processing, multi-source ingestion integrations, BigQuery analytics, and Looker dashboard

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-source schema harmonization across 50+ partner organizations, geographic identifier resolution, population double-counting prevention

risk_level.rationale: No personal data in aggregated needs indicators; confidential inter-agency coordination data requires access controls

data_sensitivity.rationale: Partner situation reports and needs indicator data classified as confidential per OCHA information sharing protocols

external_exposure.rationale: Partner NGOs submit situation reports via secure file transfer and access aggregated dashboard outputs

access_model.value: GCP IAM for pipeline infrastructure; partner organizations authenticated via GCP Service Account key exchange for SFTP-equivalent ingestion; Looker SAML SSO for dashboard access
access_model.rationale: Partner access must be scoped to their own submission data and published aggregated outputs only

feature_scope.value: Partner situation report ingestion via GCS secure drop zones, field survey data ingestion via ODK/KoboToolbox API, government statistics file ingestion, canonical needs indicator schema mapping, geographic identifier resolution to P-codes, population double-counting detection, bi-weekly aggregation refresh, BigQuery warehouse, Looker dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: End-to-end multi-source harmonization pipeline

mvp_scope.value: Partner situation report ingestion from 10 initial partners, canonical indicator mapping for 5 core JIAF sectors, P-code geographic resolution, bi-weekly BigQuery refresh, Looker severity dashboard
mvp_scope.rationale: Demonstrate inter-agency coordination value before scaling to 50+ partners

security_baseline.value: GCP IAM with Workload Identity, VPC Service Controls for BigQuery and GCS, partner GCS drop zones with scoped bucket IAM, Cloud Audit Logs for all data access, CMEK for BigQuery datasets
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential inter-agency data requires defense-in-depth access controls

frontend_stack.value: Looker for analytics dashboard; no custom frontend development
frontend_stack.rationale: Looker connected to BigQuery provides sufficient dashboard capabilities without custom UI build

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with Apache Beam; Cloud Dataflow managed runner
backend_stack.rationale: Beam provides portable batch processing; Dataflow eliminates cluster management overhead

data_platform.value: BigQuery as canonical warehouse; GCS for data lake staging
data_platform.rationale: BigQuery serverless analytics eliminates warehouse infrastructure management; GCS Nearline for cost-effective raw data retention

hosting_target.value: GCP europe-west1 with Cloud Dataflow, BigQuery, GCS, and Cloud Composer
hosting_target.rationale: europe-west1 for data residency; Cloud Composer managed Airflow for DAG orchestration

privacy_retention_policy.value: Raw partner situation reports retained 7 years per OCHA records management policy; aggregated indicator data retained indefinitely; personal-level survey records (if any) deleted after aggregation within 90 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: OCHA 7-year records retention requirement for humanitarian coordination records

future_scope.value: Real-time situation report processing via streaming pipeline, predictive needs forecasting using ML, partner self-service data submission portal
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 roadmap after coordination council validation of harmonized outputs

constraints.value: P-code geographic identifiers must use OCHA COD-AB reference dataset; all needs indicators must conform to JIAF 2.0 severity classification; pipeline latency from ingestion to dashboard must be under 24 hours
constraints.rationale: Inter-agency coordination standards mandate JIAF alignment and P-code interoperability

observability_baseline.value: Google Cloud Monitoring for Dataflow job metrics and BigQuery query performance; Looker system activity for dashboard usage; email alerts for pipeline failures and data quality violations
observability_baseline.rationale: Standard GCP observability stack for managed pipeline services

execution_preference.value: Cloud Build CI/CD for Beam pipeline deployments; Terraform for infrastructure provisioning
execution_preference.rationale: GitOps approach with infrastructure-as-code for reproducible pipeline environments

compliance_context.value: OCHA information sharing protocols for humanitarian data; GDPR applicable to any personal-level survey data processed before aggregation; inter-agency data sharing agreements with partner organizations
compliance_context.rationale: data_sensitivity=confidential and external_exposure=partner_facing trigger compliance context requirement

project.goal: Multi-source humanitarian needs assessment harmonization pipeline feeding unified coordination dashboard
project.target_users: Humanitarian coordinators, field analysts, and partner NGO representatives
project.timeline: MVP in 14 weeks, full 50-partner scale by week 28

security.auth: GCP IAM and Workload Identity
security.partner_access: Scoped GCS bucket IAM per partner organization
security.encryption: CMEK for BigQuery and GCS buckets

data.lake: GCS staging and raw archive buckets
data.survey_api: KoboToolbox API for field survey ingestion

open_questions.survey_source: ODK Central or KoboToolbox as primary field survey source?

reasoner_reviews.product: The harmonization pipeline addresses the core inter-agency coordination problem of double-counting population needs across overlapping partner response areas. Geographic identifier resolution to P-codes is the most technically complex step and must be designed to handle both exact-match and fuzzy-match cases where partner-submitted geographic names differ from COD-AB reference. The 24-hour latency SLA is achievable with bi-weekly Dataflow batch jobs.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Apache Beam on Dataflow is the right choice for the harmonization transforms given the Python ecosystem for geographic processing (GeoPandas, Shapely). Cloud Composer managed Airflow provides the orchestration layer without cluster management overhead.
