The canonical indicator schema mapping is the domain-critical design component and must define explicit mapping rules from each partner schema to JIAF 2.0 indicator codes. BigQuery partitioning strategy on district_code and reference_date will be critical for Looker query performance at 50-partner scale.
reasoner_reviews.security: Partner-specific GCS drop zones with scoped bucket IAM prevents cross-partner data visibility during ingestion. VPC Service Controls perimeter around BigQuery and GCS prevents data exfiltration. CMEK ensures GCP cannot access customer data without explicit key grant. Partner Looker access must be scoped to published aggregated indicators only - raw situation report content must not be accessible to other partners.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 24-hour ingestion-to-dashboard SLA with bi-weekly Dataflow jobs means partner submissions received before a scheduled run will appear in the next refresh. For truly time-sensitive coordination needs, a future streaming pipeline would be needed. The JIAF 2.0 indicator mapping must be version-controlled as a configuration artifact to enable backward compatibility when JIAF 3.0 is released.

backend.service_design: Four Cloud Composer DAGs: ingest_dag (file detection and validation per partner drop zone), harmonize_dag (Dataflow job submission for schema mapping and P-code resolution), aggregate_dag (Dataflow job for population deduplication and severity score computation), publish_dag (BigQuery mart refresh and Looker PDT rebuild trigger)
backend.api_patterns: No external REST API. Internal interfaces: Dataflow job submission via Cloud Dataflow REST API from Composer DAG; KoboToolbox REST API polling (GET /api/v2/assets/{form_id}/data) for survey ingestion; GCS event notifications via Pub/Sub for drop zone file arrival detection
backend.business_modules: PartnerDropZoneMonitor (GCS Pub/Sub subscriber detecting new situation report files), SituationReportParser (JSON/Excel/CSV multi-format parser mapping fields to canonical_sitrep_schema), KoboToolboxIngester (API poller with cursor-based pagination), GeographicResolver (P-code fuzzy matching using Levenshtein distance against COD-AB reference table, with manual override support), IndicatorMapper (JIAF 2.0 sector code assignment from source field values), PopulationDeduplicator (geographic overlap detection using PostGIS-style BigQuery GIS functions to identify double-counted admin unit populations), SeverityScorer (JIAF 2.0 IPC-like severity tier classification per district-sector), BigQueryPublisher (partitioned table writes with deduplication on source_id + reference_date)
backend.background_jobs: Cloud Composer DAGs on bi-weekly schedule (Monday and Thursday 02:00 UTC); Dataflow jobs auto-scale workers based on input file volume; KoboToolbox poll DAG runs daily at 04:00 UTC for near-daily survey data freshness
backend.scaling_notes: Dataflow autoscales workers from 2 to 50 based on input byte volume; BigQuery storage auto-scales; Cloud Composer environment scales with Airflow worker concurrency settings

security.auth_design: Each partner organization has a dedicated GCS drop zone bucket with a scoped service account (roles/storage.objectCreator on their bucket only); partner submits files using GCS signed upload URLs or service account key exchange. Looker access via SAML SSO; partner users have Looker model permission scoped to published_indicators LookML model only, not raw ingestion models.
security.secrets_management: KoboToolbox API token stored in GCP Secret Manager; Dataflow workers access via Workload Identity at runtime. Partner service account keys managed via GCP IAM key rotation with 90-day expiry.
security.privacy_controls: Personal-level KoboToolbox survey responses (names, GPS coordinates) extracted and aggregated within Dataflow job; personal fields excluded from BigQuery write; raw survey CSV with personal fields retained in restricted GCS bucket with 90-day lifecycle deletion policy.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Cloud Audit Logs: all BigQuery dataset access, GCS bucket reads/writes, Dataflow job submissions logged. Looker system activity table captures all dashboard queries with user_id and timestamp. Weekly audit log export to GCS archive bucket.

data.entities: survey_responses (response_id, form_id, partner_id, admin_unit_name, admin_unit_pcode, sector, affected_population, assessment_date, ingested_at), partner_situation_reports (report_id, partner_id, submission_date, file_gcs_path, parse_status ENUM pending/parsed/failed, ingested_at), geographic_units (pcode TEXT PK, admin_level INT, unit_name TEXT, parent_pcode TEXT, country_code CHAR3, geom_wkt TEXT, source_version TEXT), needs_indicators (indicator_id, pcode FK, sector TEXT, jiaf_indicator_code TEXT, value REAL, unit TEXT, reference_date DATE, source_partner_id FK, double_count_flag BOOL, ingested_at), population_estimates (pcode FK, reference_date DATE, total_population INT, in_need INT, severely_in_need INT, source TEXT), data_sources (source_id, partner_id, source_type ENUM sitrep/survey/government_stats, file_format TEXT, schema_version TEXT, last_received_at TIMESTAMP), harmonization_rules (rule_id, source_partner_id, source_field TEXT, target_indicator_code TEXT, transform_type ENUM direct/calculated/derived, transform_expression TEXT, valid_from DATE, valid_until DATE)
data.storage_design: GCS: gs://hna-raw-{partner_id}/ per-partner drop zones (CMEK, private ACL); gs://hna-staging/ for Dataflow input staging; gs://hna-archive/ for processed files 7-year retention. BigQuery datasets: hna_raw (Dataflow write target, partitioned by ingestion_date), hna_harmonized (P-code resolved, indicator-mapped, partitioned by reference_date and pcode), hna_mart (aggregated severity scores, published to Looker, materialized tables refreshed bi-weekly).
data.analytics_events: pipeline_run_started, pipeline_run_completed (with record counts), geographic_resolution_failed (with unmatched admin_unit_name for manual review), double_count_detected (with partner_ids and overlapping pcode), data_quality_violation (with rule_id and violation_detail)
data.data_risks: Risk: partner schema changes break harmonization_rules mappings silently. Mitigation: schema version tracking in data_sources table; Dataflow job fails fast on unmapped required fields and alerts pipeline team.

devops.deployment_topology: GCP europe-west1: Cloud Composer 2 environment (Airflow 2.8, small environment), Cloud Dataflow managed service (auto-scaling workers), BigQuery datasets (hna_raw, hna_harmonized, hna_mart), GCS buckets (per-partner drop zones + staging + archive), Looker instance (GCP-managed). Terraform manages all infrastructure.
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build trigger on push to main: python -m pytest tests/ + flake8 + mypy for Beam pipeline code; Cloud Build submits updated Dataflow flex template to GCS on tag push; Cloud Composer DAG files synced to Composer GCS bucket via Cloud Build step.
devops.observability_stack: Google Cloud Monitoring: Dataflow job success/failure metrics, worker autoscaling events, BigQuery byte scanned per job. Cloud Composer: DAG run success rate, task duration. Custom metrics written to Cloud Monitoring via Beam metrics API: records_processed_count, geographic_resolution_failure_rate, double_count_detection_count.
devops.backup_and_recovery: BigQuery table snapshots before each bi-weekly mart refresh; GCS Object Versioning on hna-archive bucket; Dataflow job templates versioned in GCS enabling reruns of historical periods.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a multi-source humanitarian needs assessment harmonization pipeline on GCP. Key decisions: Apache Beam on Dataflow for portable batch transforms with Python geographic processing libraries; Cloud Composer for DAG orchestration without cluster management; BigQuery with CMEK and VPC Service Controls for confidential inter-agency data; per-partner GCS drop zones with scoped IAM to prevent cross-partner data visibility during ingestion. The JIAF 2.0 indicator mapping and P-code geographic resolution are the domain-critical transforms requiring explicit harmonization_rules configuration management.
target_output.title: Humanitarian Needs Assessment Harmonization Pipeline - Multi-Source JIAF 2.0 on GCP
target_output.executive_summary: Apache Beam batch pipeline on Cloud Dataflow processing field survey data from KoboToolbox, partner situation reports from per-organization GCS drop zones, and government statistics files, harmonizing all inputs to JIAF 2.0 sector indicator codes and OCHA COD-AB P-code geographic identifiers, detecting and flagging population double-counting across overlapping partner response areas, and loading aggregated severity scores to BigQuery for bi-weekly Looker coordination dashboard refresh. OCHA information sharing protocols enforced via per-partner GCS bucket IAM, CMEK encryption, VPC Service Controls, and partner-scoped Looker model access. Pipeline ingestion-to-dashboard latency under 24 hours.
target_output.architecture_overview: Four-stage Cloud Composer DAG pipeline in GCP europe-west1. Stage 1 (ingest_dag): GCS Pub/Sub notifications detect new partner situation report files arriving in per-partner drop zone buckets; KoboToolbox API polling ingests field survey responses; all raw files written to hna-raw BigQuery dataset and GCS staging. Stage 2 (harmonize_dag): Dataflow Beam job reads from GCS staging, executes SituationReportParser for multi-format parsing, GeographicResolver for P-code fuzzy matching against COD-AB reference table, and IndicatorMapper for JIAF 2.0 sector code assignment based on harmonization_rules config; writes to hna_harmonized BigQuery dataset partitioned by reference_date and pcode. Stage 3 (aggregate_dag): Dataflow Beam job reads harmonized indicators, runs PopulationDeduplicator using BigQuery GIS overlap detection to flag double-counted admin unit populations across partners, computes SeverityScorer JIAF 2.0 IPC-like tier classifications per district-sector; writes to hna_mart. Stage 4 (publish_dag): triggers Looker PDT rebuild via Looker API and sends completion notification to coordination team. All BigQuery datasets and GCS buckets encrypted with CMEK. VPC Service Controls perimeter prevents data exfiltration.
target_output.technology_stack: Pipeline: Python 3.12, Apache Beam 2.55, Cloud Dataflow managed runner. Orchestration: Cloud Composer 2 (managed Airflow 2.8). Data Ingestion: google-cloud-storage, google-cloud-pubsub, requests (KoboToolbox API), openpyxl (Excel sitrep parsing). Geographic processing: GeoPandas, Shapely, Levenshtein (fuzzy P-code matching). Data warehouse: BigQuery with CMEK and VPC Service Controls. Dashboard: Looker with SAML SSO. IaC: Terraform 1.8. CI/CD: Cloud Build. Monitoring: Google Cloud Monitoring with custom Beam metrics. Secrets: GCP Secret Manager.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Partner Drop Zone Ingestion: each of 50 partner organizations has a dedicated GCS bucket (gs://hna-raw-{partner_slug}/) with scoped service account; Pub/Sub notification triggers ingest_dag task on file arrival. KoboToolbox Survey Ingestion: daily API poll via Composer DAG task fetching new survey submissions with cursor pagination; personal fields (respondent_name, gps_coordinates) extracted before BigQuery write and retained in restricted bucket for 90-day deletion. Government Statistics Ingestion: CSV/Excel files manually uploaded to gs://hna-gov-stats/ by authorized staff; parsed on schedule. Schema Harmonization: harmonization_rules table defines source_field -> JIAF indicator mappings per partner; IndicatorMapper applies transform_expression (direct, calculated, or derived transforms). P-code Resolution: GeographicResolver matches partner-submitted admin_unit_name against COD-AB reference using exact match first, then Levenshtein distance <0.2 threshold; unmatched names written to geographic_resolution_failures table for manual review and rule addition. Double-Count Detection: PopulationDeduplicator queries BigQuery GIS for overlapping admin unit geometries across partners reporting the same population cohort; flags overlapping entries with double_count_flag=true and halves population weight. JIAF Severity Scoring: SeverityScorer classifies district-sector combinations into JIAF 2.0 tiers 1-5 using phase-classification logic from indicator thresholds. Looker Dashboard: published_indicators BigQuery mart exposed via LookML model with partner-scoped access control.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Composer 2 environment (Airflow 2.8, small env, europe-west1): four DAGs managing the full pipeline. Cloud Dataflow managed service: harmonize_dag and aggregate_dag submit Beam flex templates stored in GCS; workers autoscale 2-50 based on input volume. GCS buckets: hna-raw-{partner_slug}/ (50 per-partner drop zones, CMEK, private ACL), hna-staging/ (Dataflow input staging), hna-archive/ (processed files, 7-year Object Versioning), hna-gov-stats/ (government statistics uploads, restricted IAM). BigQuery datasets: hna_raw (partitioned by ingestion_date, CMEK), hna_harmonized (partitioned by reference_date and pcode, CMEK), hna_mart (aggregated severity, materialized tables, Looker-connected, CMEK). GCP Secret Manager: KoboToolbox API token, Looker API credentials. Cloud Pub/Sub: hna-drop-zone-notifications topic subscribed by ingest_dag. VPC Service Controls: perimeter enclosing BigQuery, GCS, and Dataflow service accounts. Looker instance: GCP-managed, SAML SSO, partner-scoped LookML model access. Terraform: manages all GCS buckets, IAM bindings, BigQuery datasets, Composer environment, and VPC Service Controls configuration.
target_output.workflows: Partner Situation Report Ingestion Workflow: partner uploads JSON/Excel/CSV situation report to their GCS drop zone bucket using scoped service account credentials. Cloud Pub/Sub notification fires, triggering ingest_dag task. PartnerDropZoneMonitor validates file format and partner_id from bucket path. SituationReportParser parses multi-format file into canonical_sitrep_schema: partner_id, submission_date, admin_unit_name, sector, affected_population, indicator_values. Raw parsed rows written to hna_raw.situation_reports BigQuery table partitioned by ingestion_date. File moved to gs://hna-archive/{partner_id}/{YYYY}/{MM}/ for 7-year retention. On next scheduled harmonize_dag run (bi-weekly Monday/Thursday 02:00 UTC), new hna_raw rows read by Dataflow job, GeographicResolver matches admin_unit_name to pcode, IndicatorMapper applies partner-specific harmonization rules, output written to hna_harmonized.needs_indicators. Population Deduplication and Severity Scoring Workflow: aggregate_dag Dataflow job reads hna_harmonized.needs_indicators for the current reference period. PopulationDeduplicator runs BigQuery GIS query to detect geographic overlaps between partner-reported admin units covering the same population; overlapping entries flagged with double_count_flag=true and population_weight=0.5 applied before aggregation. SeverityScorer groups by pcode and jiaf_sector, applies JIAF 2.0 phase-classification thresholds to compute severity_tier 1-5 for each district-sector combination. Results written to hna_mart.district_severity_scores. publish_dag triggers Looker PDT rebuild and dispatches email summary to coordination team listing new partner submissions, resolution failure count, and double-count detections requiring review.
target_output.data_model: survey_responses (response_id TEXT PK, form_id TEXT, partner_id TEXT, admin_unit_name TEXT, admin_unit_pcode TEXT, sector TEXT, affected_population INT, assessment_date DATE, personal_data_purged BOOL, ingested_at TIMESTAMP). partner_situation_reports (report_id TEXT PK, partner_id TEXT, submission_date DATE, file_gcs_path TEXT, parse_status ENUM pending/parsed/failed, row_count INT, ingested_at TIMESTAMP). geographic_units (pcode TEXT PK, admin_level INT, unit_name TEXT, parent_pcode TEXT, country_code CHAR3, geom_wkt TEXT, cod_ab_version TEXT). needs_indicators (indicator_id TEXT PK, pcode TEXT FK, sector TEXT, jiaf_indicator_code TEXT, value REAL, unit TEXT, reference_date DATE, source_partner_id TEXT, double_count_flag BOOL, population_weight REAL, ingested_at TIMESTAMP). BigQuery partition: PARTITION BY DATE(reference_date) CLUSTER BY pcode, sector. population_estimates (pcode TEXT, reference_date DATE, total_population INT, in_need INT, severely_in_need INT, source TEXT, PRIMARY KEY pcode, reference_date). data_sources (source_id TEXT PK, partner_id TEXT, source_type ENUM sitrep/survey/government_stats, file_format TEXT, schema_version TEXT, last_received_at TIMESTAMP). harmonization_rules (rule_id TEXT PK, source_partner_id TEXT, source_field TEXT, target_indicator_code TEXT, transform_type ENUM direct/calculated/derived, transform_expression TEXT, valid_from DATE, valid_until DATE). geographic_resolution_failures (failure_id TEXT PK, pipeline_run_id TEXT, partner_id TEXT, submitted_admin_unit_name TEXT, country_code TEXT, best_candidate_pcode TEXT, best_candidate_score REAL, created_at TIMESTAMP).
target_output.api_design: No public REST API. Internal pipeline interfaces: KoboToolbox API client (GET /api/v2/assets/{form_id}/data?limit=100&start={cursor} with Bearer token auth from Secret Manager; cursor-based pagination to retrieve incremental submissions). Cloud Dataflow Beam pipeline launch via Google Cloud Dataflow REST API (POST /v1b3/projects/{project}/locations/{location}/flexTemplates:launch with templateGcsPath, parameters including input_dataset, output_dataset, reference_date, lookback_days). Looker API client (POST /api/4.0/scheduled_plans/{id}/scheduled_plan_run_once to trigger PDT rebuild). BigQuery client for harmonization_rules and geographic_units reads from Beam workers via apache_beam.io.gcp.bigquery.ReadFromBigQuery.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Partner Access: each partner organization authenticates with a dedicated GCP service account key scoped to roles/storage.objectCreator on their specific hna-raw-{partner_slug}/ bucket only; cross-partner GCS access impossible by IAM design. Looker SAML SSO: partner users provisioned with Looker model permission restricted to published_indicators LookML model; raw hna_raw and hna_harmonized datasets not accessible via Looker to partner users. VPC Service Controls: perimeter enclosing BigQuery, GCS, and Dataflow service accounts prevents data exfiltration even by compromised service account. CMEK: all BigQuery datasets and GCS buckets encrypted with Cloud KMS customer-managed keys; key rotation every 90 days. Personal Data Handling: KoboToolbox survey respondent names and GPS coordinates extracted in Dataflow ParDo before BigQuery write; personal fields written to restricted gs://hna-survey-personal/ with 90-day Object Lifecycle deletion; Dataflow workers never log personal field values. Audit Logging: Cloud Audit Logs captures all BigQuery dataset access, GCS reads/writes, Dataflow job submissions; weekly export to gs://hna-audit-archive/ for OCHA records management. OCHA Compliance: harmonized indicator data published only after partner consent confirmation in data_sources table; inter-agency data sharing agreements tracked in data_sharing_agreements metadata table.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Terraform modules: composer_environment (Cloud Composer 2, Airflow 2.8, small env, service account, secret bindings), dataflow_templates (GCS bucket for flex templates, service account, IAM), bigquery_datasets (hna_raw, hna_harmonized, hna_mart with CMEK and access controls), gcs_buckets (per-partner drop zones, staging, archive, gov-stats with CMEK and lifecycle policies), vpc_service_controls (perimeter definition, access levels), iam (service accounts, partner SA key lifecycle). Cloud Build CI pipeline: pytest + flake8 + mypy on PR; Beam pipeline unit tests with mock GCS and BigQuery; integration tests against GCP dev project via Workload Identity. On tag push: Cloud Build packages Beam job as Dataflow flex template and uploads to gs://hna-templates/; updates Composer DAG files via gsutil sync. Environments: dev (reduced Composer env, BigQuery dev datasets, no partner drop zones), staging (full topology with synthetic partner data), production (europe-west1 full topology). Pipeline reruns: Composer manual DAG trigger with reference_date parameter enables historical reruns from versioned Dataflow templates.
target_output.observability: Google Cloud Monitoring dashboards: Dataflow job metrics (elements processed per second, worker count, system lag, failed elements), Composer DAG success/failure rate and task duration, BigQuery bytes scanned per harmonize/aggregate job. Custom Beam metrics emitted to Cloud Monitoring: records_ingested_count (per partner), geographic_resolution_failure_rate (target <5%), double_count_detection_count, severity_score_distribution (count per JIAF tier). Data quality alerts: geographic_resolution_failure_rate >5% for any partner over a pipeline run triggers email to pipeline team for manual rule addition; BigQuery table row count drops >20% vs previous run triggers Slack alert to data team. Looker system activity dashboard tracks partner dashboard query volume and identifies inactive partner logins for license management.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Composer 2 small environment: ~$250/month. Dataflow on-demand workers for bi-weekly batch jobs (10 n1-standard-4 workers, ~2h per run): ~$40/month. BigQuery storage (1TB harmonized + 500GB mart): ~$20/month. BigQuery query on-demand (Looker queries from 50 users): ~$30/month. GCS storage (7-year archive, 10TB): ~$200/month. Cloud KMS CMEK key operations: ~$5/month. Total ~$545/month. Scaling to 200 partners: Dataflow worker count increases linearly with partner volume; BigQuery storage scales automatically; Composer environment upgrades to medium at $400/month. At 200-partner scale estimated ~$900/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (14 weeks): Terraform infrastructure provisioning (Composer, Dataflow, BigQuery, 10 partner GCS drop zones), ingest_dag for situation report ingestion from 10 initial partners, SituationReportParser for JSON and Excel formats, GeographicResolver with exact P-code match (fuzzy matching in Phase 2), IndicatorMapper for 5 core JIAF sectors (food security, health, nutrition, shelter, WASH), bi-weekly harmonize_dag and aggregate_dag, Looker severity dashboard with district-level JIAF tier visualization. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Fuzzy P-code matching via Levenshtein distance, PopulationDeduplicator geographic overlap detection, KoboToolbox survey ingestion DAG, government statistics ingestion, personal data handling workflow, geographic_resolution_failures review interface, scale to 30 partners. Phase 3 (future): Streaming pipeline for real-time situation report processing via Dataflow streaming, predictive needs forecasting ML model, partner self-service data submission portal.
target_output.development_guidelines: Beam pipeline code structured as composable PTransforms with typed PCollections using Beam Python type annotations. Unit tests for GeographicResolver, IndicatorMapper, and PopulationDeduplicator with synthetic admin unit fixtures; integration tests submit real Dataflow jobs to GCP dev project in CI. harmonization_rules configuration stored as YAML version-controlled in Git alongside pipeline code; schema version tracked in data_sources table to detect when partner changes their submission format. JIAF 2.0 indicator classification thresholds defined as Python constants module referenced by SeverityScorer; updates require code review from humanitarian analyst. Cloud Build enforces flake8, mypy strict mode, and pytest coverage >80% for domain transform modules. geographic_resolution_failures reviewed weekly by data team before each production Dataflow run.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: partner schema changes break harmonization_rules silently when transform_expression references renamed source fields. Mitigation: Dataflow job fails fast on unmapped required fields with structured error to geographic_resolution_failures-equivalent rules_validation_failures table; alerting to pipeline team within 1h. Risk: Levenshtein fuzzy P-code matching below threshold produces false-positive geographic resolutions that misattribute needs indicators to wrong districts. Mitigation: conservative threshold of 0.2 (80% string similarity required); all fuzzy matches flagged in geographic_resolution_failures for human review before final attribution. Risk: JIAF 2.0 version upgrade (planned JIAF 3.0 in 2027) requires harmonization_rules remapping for all 50 partners. Mitigation: valid_from/valid_until date range on harmonization_rules enables versioned rules sets; pipeline selects rules valid for the reference_date being processed. Tradeoff: bi-weekly batch vs real-time streaming - batch accepted for MVP per stakeholder agreement; streaming pipeline deferred to Phase 3 adds complexity justified only after coordination use-case validation.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: KoboToolbox confirmed as primary field survey source (ODK Central as fallback for offline deployments). Cloud Composer selected for orchestration over manual Cloud Scheduler for DAG dependency management and retry visibility. CMEK key rotation period set at 90 days per OCHA security baseline. Geographic P-code version: COD-AB January 2026 release selected; version tracked in geographic_units.cod_ab_version for reproducibility when COD-AB is updated.

metadata.notes: Strong first-pass plan with deeply domain-specific JIAF 2.0 indicator harmonization, P-code geographic resolution, and OCHA compliance detail.

project_goal.value: Mobile application for humanitarian cash transfer program managers to enroll beneficiaries, verify identity via biometrics, schedule payment disbursements, and confirm receipt with a modernized CI/CD pipeline supporting rapid field updates
project_goal.rationale: Core operational tool for field-based cash transfer program execution

target_users.value: Program officers and field staff managing beneficiary enrollment and payment disbursements in humanitarian cash transfer operations
target_users.rationale: Operational field staff requiring secure mobile workflows

project_class.rationale: Field staff operate on Android tablets and phones in remote disbursement locations

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, payments, devops, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Mobile frontend with backend API, payment processing integration, biometric auth, and strong DevOps for rapid updates

complexity_level.rationale: Biometric verification, mobile money integration, offline-capable sync, and strict financial controls add significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: Handles beneficiary financial data and payment disbursements; fraud and misappropriation risks in field environments

data_sensitivity.rationale: Stores beneficiary mobile money accounts, disbursement amounts, and payment confirmation records

external_exposure.rationale: App accessible only to authenticated program staff with role-based access

access_model.value: Cognito with biometric verification and role-based access for field staff, program officers, and auditors
access_model.rationale: Multi-factor identity with biometric confirmation aligns with financial transfer accountability requirements

feature_scope.value: Beneficiary enrollment with biometric capture, deduplication registry check, payment cycle scheduling, disbursement confirmation with biometric receipt verification, mobile money account management, transfer reconciliation reports
feature_scope.rationale: Full operational lifecycle for cash transfer program

mvp_scope.value: Beneficiary enrollment with biometric deduplication, payment cycle creation, and disbursement confirmation recording
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum for field pilot with one program in one country

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 encryption for biometric data at rest, MFA required for all staff accounts, device certificate pinning on mobile app
security_baseline.rationale: Financial data and biometric PII require strong encryption and authentication controls

frontend_stack.value: React Native with TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Cross-platform Android and iOS with shared codebase for field device diversity

backend_stack.rationale: DRF provides robust serializers and permissions framework for financial API controls

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ, Redis 7 for session cache
data_platform.rationale: Relational data for financial audit trail with managed HA failover

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in field-proximate region
hosting_target.rationale: Managed containers with proximity to field operations for acceptable API latency

privacy_retention_policy.value: Beneficiary records retained per program country data retention law, minimum 5 years; biometric templates deleted 90 days post-program closure
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Financial accountability and donor reporting obligations

future_scope.value: Multi-country program expansion, automated bank transfer integration alongside mobile money, beneficiary self-service status app
future_scope.rationale: Scaling roadmap beyond initial pilot

constraints.value: Must function offline for up to 4 hours, sync on reconnect; app must run on Android 10+ devices used in field; Buildkite CI/CD for OTA update delivery
constraints.rationale: Field connectivity constraints and existing organizational CI tooling

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM on ECS tasks, structured JSON logging, PagerDuty alerts for disbursement failures and sync errors
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard for financial systems

execution_preference.value: Two-week sprint cycles with Buildkite automated OTA deployment to field devices after each sprint
execution_preference.rationale: Rapid iteration to incorporate field feedback during pilot

compliance_context.value: FATF anti-money laundering guidelines for cash transfer programs, donor financial accountability requirements, GDPR for beneficiary personal data
compliance_context.rationale: International financial transfer and data protection obligations

project.goal: Biometric beneficiary enrollment and cash transfer disbursement confirmation for field staff
project.target_users: Program officers and field disbursement staff

frontend.state: Redux Toolkit with Redux Persist for offline state
frontend.offline: SQLite local store with background sync on reconnect

backend.framework: Django REST Framework with Celery for async tasks
backend.api: RESTful with DRF serializers and OpenAPI 3.0

security.auth: AWS Cognito with biometric second factor via device native biometric API
security.transport: TLS 1.3, certificate pinning in React Native
security.encryption: AES-256 for biometric templates at rest

data.cache: ElastiCache Redis 7 for sessions and task queue broker
data.mobile_local: SQLite via expo-sqlite for offline queue

devops.mobile: Expo OTA updates via Buildkite on merge to main

confirmed_decisions.deduplication: National ID plus biometric hash composite check
confirmed_decisions.mobile_money: Programmatic integration with M-Pesa and Wave APIs

reasoner_reviews.product: The biometric deduplication check before activation is a critical fraud control — ghost beneficiary prevention is a top program integrity concern for donors. The offline queue must be bounded and conflict-resolved deterministically to prevent duplicate disbursement records on sync.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Django REST Framework with Celery fits the async disbursement status polling and mobile money API calls. Redux Persist with SQLite for offline state is the right pattern, but the sync resolution logic needs explicit last-write-wins or server-authoritative rules to handle concurrent field staff edits. Buildkite with Expo OTA is well-suited for rapid field updates without app store delays.
reasoner_reviews.security: Certificate pinning is essential given field devices may be on untrusted networks. Biometric templates must never leave the device in plaintext — only biometric hashes transmitted. All disbursement write operations require server-side idempotency keys to prevent double-payment on network retry.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 4-hour offline tolerance with SQLite queue is achievable. The M-Pesa and Wave API rate limits must be modeled in the Celery task queue to avoid throttling during bulk disbursement cycles. Buildkite OTA pipeline must enforce signing and checksum verification on update packages.

backend.service_design: Django apps: beneficiaries (enrollment, deduplication), payment_cycles (scheduling, disbursement records), mobile_money (M-Pesa and Wave adapter services), audit (immutable ledger of all financial actions).
backend.api_patterns: RESTful DRF API. POST /api/v1/beneficiaries/enroll (biometric hash + enrollment data), POST /api/v1/disbursements/confirm (biometric receipt verification), GET /api/v1/payment-cycles/{id}/status, POST /api/v1/sync/push (offline queue flush endpoint).
backend.background_jobs: Celery tasks: mobile_money.initiate_transfer (dispatched per beneficiary per cycle), disbursement.reconcile (nightly batch matching confirmed receipts against transfer API statuses), sync.resolve_conflicts (processes incoming offline queue records with idempotency check).
backend.failure_handling: Idempotency keys on all disbursement writes. Celery exponential backoff for mobile money API failures. Failed transfers quarantined in disbursement_exceptions for manual review.

frontend.app_structure: React Native with Expo managed workflow. Feature-based modules: enrollment, disbursement, sync, audit-log. Offline-first SQLite queue populated during field operations.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Login (Cognito biometric), Beneficiary Enrollment form with biometric capture, Payment Cycle Disbursement list, In-person Receipt Confirmation with biometric scan, Offline Queue Status badge, Audit Log viewer for program officers.
frontend.state_management: Redux Toolkit slices per module. Redux Persist with expo-sqlite adapter for offline persistence. React Query for server state when online.
frontend.frontend_security_notes: Certificate pinning via react-native-ssl-pinning. Biometric data captured via expo-local-authentication, only hash transmitted to backend. Auto-lock screen after 5 minutes inactivity.

security.auth_design: Cognito user pools with custom auth challenge triggering device biometric verification. Staff accounts issued per field office with program-scoped role assignment.
security.authorization_model: DRF custom permissions: FieldStaff (enroll, confirm disbursement for assigned program), ProgramOfficer (create payment cycles, view reports), Auditor (read-only access to full audit log).
security.audit_and_logging_controls: Immutable audit_events table recording every beneficiary record access, enrollment, disbursement write, and sync event with staff user ID, device ID, timestamp, and GPS coordinates if available.
security.incident_response_notes: Compromised device triggers immediate Cognito account suspension and remote SQLite cache wipe via MDM policy. All pending offline records invalidated for manual review.

data.entities: beneficiaries (id UUID PK, national_id_hash VARCHAR, biometric_hash VARCHAR, full_name_encrypted, mobile_money_account_encrypted, program_id FK, enrollment_status ENUM pending/active/suspended, enrolled_by UUID FK, created_at), payment_cycles (id UUID PK, program_id FK, cycle_name, disbursement_amount NUMERIC, currency CHAR(3), scheduled_date DATE, status ENUM draft/active/completed, created_at), disbursements (id UUID PK, beneficiary_id FK, payment_cycle_id FK, transfer_reference VARCHAR, status ENUM pending/initiated/confirmed/failed, confirmed_by UUID FK, biometric_confirmed BOOLEAN, confirmed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, idempotency_key UNIQUE), audit_events (id UUID PK, actor_id UUID, action VARCHAR, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, device_id VARCHAR, gps_point POINT, event_at TIMESTAMPTZ)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL with all PII fields encrypted via pgcrypto. Biometric hashes stored as salted SHA-256 — raw templates never persisted server-side. Mobile SQLite stores pending enrollment and disbursement confirmation records for offline queue with sync_status field.
data.retention_and_deletion: Beneficiary records retained minimum 5 years per country legal requirement. Biometric templates on-device purged on app logout. Server-side biometric hashes deleted 90 days post-program-closure per policy.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster with Django API and Celery worker task definitions. Separate Celery worker for mobile money tasks with autoscaling based on SQS queue depth. Buildkite pipeline for both backend deploy and mobile OTA.
devops.ci_cd_design: Buildkite: Django unit tests (pytest), DRF integration tests, Docker build and ECR push, ECS rolling deploy, smoke test against staging API. React Native: Jest + Detox E2E on Android emulator, Expo OTA publish to staging channel, production OTA promote on manual gate.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM on ECS tasks with custom spans on biometric verification flow and mobile money API calls. PagerDuty for disbursement_failure_rate >2% and sync_error_rate >5%.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS rolling deploy with health check gate — automatic rollback on task failure. Expo OTA previous channel retained for 14 days enabling rollback via Buildkite pipeline.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a biometric cash transfer mobile app for field humanitarian operations. Key decisions: Django REST Framework with Celery for financial workflow orchestration, Redux Persist with SQLite offline queue for 4-hour connectivity gaps, Cognito with device biometric challenge for strong field-staff authentication without hardware tokens, and Buildkite with Expo OTA for rapid sprint-cycle updates to field devices without app store submission delays. All PII and financial data encrypted with pgcrypto at rest; biometric templates never leave the device in plaintext.
target_output.title: Humanitarian Cash Transfer Field Operations Mobile App
target_output.executive_summary: A secure React Native mobile application enabling humanitarian program officers to enroll beneficiaries via biometric capture, schedule payment disbursements to mobile money accounts, and confirm in-person receipts with biometric verification. Built on Django REST Framework with PostgreSQL 16 and Celery for async mobile money API orchestration, deployed on AWS ECS Fargate with a Buildkite CI/CD pipeline delivering Expo OTA updates to field Android devices after each sprint. FATF anti-money laundering controls, donor accountability audit trail, and 4-hour offline-capable operation address field deployment realities.
target_output.architecture_overview: React Native client on Android field devices connects over TLS 1.3 with certificate pinning to a Django REST API on ECS Fargate behind an ALB. When offline, field staff operate against a local SQLite queue, flushing records via POST /sync/push on reconnect. Cognito user pools authenticate staff with a device biometric custom auth challenge. Celery workers handle M-Pesa and Wave mobile money API calls asynchronously, with SQS as the message broker. PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ stores the financial audit ledger with pgcrypto-encrypted PII. Biometric templates captured via device native APIs — only SHA-256 hashes transmitted and stored server-side. Buildkite builds and signs OTA update bundles on every main branch merge for field device delivery via Expo update service.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: React Native with TypeScript, Expo managed workflow, Redux Toolkit with Redux Persist, expo-sqlite for offline queue, expo-local-authentication for biometric capture, react-native-ssl-pinning. Backend: Python 3.12, Django 5 with Django REST Framework, Celery 5 with SQS broker, pgcrypto extension. Database: PostgreSQL 16 RDS Multi-AZ with pgcrypto, ElastiCache Redis 7. Infrastructure: ECS Fargate, ALB, SQS, Cognito user pools with custom auth challenge. CI/CD: Buildkite with Expo OTA channel promotions. Monitoring: Datadog APM, PagerDuty.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Beneficiary Enrollment: Field staff capture biometric and national ID; deduplication registry checks composite national_id_hash + biometric_hash before activating beneficiary for payment cycles. Payment Cycle Management: Program officers create disbursement cycles specifying amount, currency, scheduled date, and eligible beneficiary cohort. Disbursement Confirmation: In-person payment confirmed with staff biometric scan of beneficiary; creates confirmed disbursement record and triggers mobile money transfer via Celery. Offline Queue: Up to 4 hours of enrollment and confirmation records queued in device SQLite, flushed with idempotency-safe conflict resolution on reconnect. Audit Log: Immutable per-action log of every enrollment, disbursement, and access event with actor, device, and GPS coordinates.
target_output.system_components: React Native Expo app (offline-capable field client with biometric integration). Django REST API on ECS Fargate (enrollment, payment cycle, disbursement, sync, audit endpoints). Celery worker fleet on ECS Fargate (mobile money initiation, reconciliation, conflict resolution tasks). SQS standard queues (mobile_money_tasks, sync_tasks) with DLQs. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ with pgcrypto (beneficiaries, payment_cycles, disbursements, audit_events tables). ElastiCache Redis 7 (Django session cache). Cognito user pool with custom auth Lambda trigger (biometric challenge verification). Buildkite + Expo update service (CI/CD and OTA delivery).
target_output.workflows: Beneficiary Enrollment: Field staff authenticate via Cognito biometric challenge on device. On the enrollment screen, staff captures biometric using expo-local-authentication, inputs national ID and mobile money account. App hashes biometric locally, transmits hash with encrypted PII to POST /api/v1/beneficiaries/enroll. Backend computes composite deduplication key, checks existing records, creates beneficiary with status pending, returns activation confirmation. If offline, enrollment queued in SQLite sync_queue table with idempotency UUID, flushed via POST /api/v1/sync/push on reconnect. Disbursement Confirmation: Staff navigates to active payment cycle disbursement list. For each beneficiary, staff initiates in-person confirmation, prompts beneficiary biometric scan on device, transmits hash to POST /api/v1/disbursements/confirm with idempotency_key. Backend creates disbursement record status confirmed, enqueues Celery task mobile_money.initiate_transfer for M-Pesa or Wave API call. Celery worker calls mobile money API, updates disbursement.transfer_reference and status on success or quarantines to disbursement_exceptions on repeated failure.
target_output.data_model: beneficiaries (id UUID PK, national_id_hash VARCHAR UNIQUE, biometric_hash VARCHAR, full_name_encrypted BYTEA, mobile_money_account_encrypted BYTEA, mobile_money_provider ENUM mpesa/wave, program_id UUID FK, enrollment_status ENUM pending/active/suspended, enrolled_by UUID FK, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). payment_cycles (id UUID PK, program_id UUID FK, cycle_name VARCHAR, disbursement_amount NUMERIC(12,2), currency CHAR(3), scheduled_date DATE, status ENUM draft/active/completed, created_by UUID FK, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). disbursements (id UUID PK, beneficiary_id UUID FK, payment_cycle_id UUID FK, transfer_reference VARCHAR, mobile_money_status VARCHAR, status ENUM pending/initiated/confirmed/failed, confirmed_by UUID FK, biometric_confirmed BOOLEAN, confirmed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, idempotency_key VARCHAR UNIQUE). audit_events (id UUID PK, actor_id UUID FK, action VARCHAR, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, device_id VARCHAR, gps_point POINT, event_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL). disbursement_exceptions (id UUID PK, disbursement_id UUID FK, error_code VARCHAR, error_detail TEXT, attempt_count INT, last_attempted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, quarantined_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: POST /api/v1/beneficiaries/enroll — body {national_id_hash, biometric_hash, full_name_encrypted, mobile_money_account_encrypted, provider}; returns {beneficiary_id, status}. POST /api/v1/disbursements/confirm — body {beneficiary_id, payment_cycle_id, biometric_hash, idempotency_key}; returns {disbursement_id, status: confirmed}. GET /api/v1/payment-cycles/{id}/disbursements — returns paginated list with beneficiary name_masked and disbursement status. POST /api/v1/sync/push — body {records: [{type: enrollment|confirmation, payload, idempotency_key}]}; returns {accepted, rejected_with_reasons}. GET /api/v1/audit-events?program_id={id}&from={date} — auditor role only. Auth: Cognito JWT with biometric-verified session. All endpoints require field_staff or program_officer role.
target_output.security_and_compliance: FATF controls: idempotency keys on all disbursement writes prevent double-payment; audit_events table append-only via PostgreSQL trigger prohibiting UPDATE/DELETE; biometric deduplication blocks ghost beneficiary enrollment. GDPR: biometric templates processed on-device only, hashes encrypted at rest with pgcrypto, retained per country legal schedule. Certificate pinning via react-native-ssl-pinning prevents MITM on field networks. Cognito custom auth challenge verifies device biometric before issuing JWT. DRF permission classes: FieldStaff write-only on assigned program, Auditor read-only across all programs. MDM policy for remote SQLite wipe on reported device compromise. Disbursement exception queue reviewed by program officer before manual resubmission.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Buildkite pipeline stages: Python linting (Ruff), pytest unit and DRF integration tests (90% coverage gate), Docker build and ECR push, ECS Fargate rolling deploy (Django API task definition, Celery worker task definition with SQS queue-depth autoscaling), ALB health check smoke test. React Native branch: Jest unit tests, Detox E2E on Android 13 emulator covering enrollment and disbursement flows, Expo OTA bundle build with code signing, publish to staging update channel, production channel promote via manual approval. Environments: dev (RDS db.t3.micro, single Celery worker), staging (Multi-AZ RDS, 2 Celery workers, staging OTA channel), production (Multi-AZ RDS db.t3.large, Celery autoscaling 2-10 workers, production OTA channel, ECS auto-scaling 3-8 API tasks on CPU 60%). Database migrations via Django migrate in ECS one-off task before rolling deploy. Secrets Manager for RDS credentials, Cognito app client secrets, M-Pesa and Wave API keys with 30-day rotation.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM with custom spans on biometric_verification_latency_ms, mobile_money_api_latency_ms, sync_push_record_count, conflict_resolution_outcome. CloudWatch ECS metrics. PagerDuty alerts: disbursement_failure_rate >2% (critical), sync_error_rate >5% (high), celery_queue_depth >500 (high), RDS connections >80% (medium). Weekly reconciliation report: confirmed disbursements vs mobile money API transfer statuses, flagging any discrepancy for program officer review. Structured JSON logs include program_id, payment_cycle_id, actor_id per request.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Estimated $800-1200/month for a single-country pilot: ECS Fargate (API + Celery) ~$400, RDS Multi-AZ db.t3.large ~$350, ElastiCache ~$50, SQS ~$5, ALB ~$25. Celery worker autoscaling on SQS queue depth handles disbursement bulk cycles without permanent over-provisioning. OTA updates via Expo channel avoid per-update app store fees, critical for weekly sprint deployments to field devices.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 Pilot (10 weeks): Beneficiary enrollment with biometric deduplication, payment cycle creation, disbursement confirmation recording, 4-hour offline SQLite queue with idempotency-safe sync, Cognito biometric auth, ECS deployment, Buildkite + Expo OTA CI/CD pipeline, Datadog monitoring, PostgreSQL audit trail. Covers single-country pilot with one program. Phase 2 (6 weeks): M-Pesa and Wave API Celery integration for automated transfer initiation, disbursement reconciliation job, disbursement exception workflow for program officer review, auditor read-only role and audit log export. Phase 3 (4 weeks): Multi-program support, program officer analytics dashboard (disbursement rates, pending confirmations by field office), MDM-integrated remote wipe trigger, multi-country data residency configuration.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode in React Native; Python type annotations with mypy in Django. All DRF serializers enforce field-level encryption for PII attributes. Idempotency key validation on every disbursement endpoint via custom DRF mixin. Detox E2E scenarios must cover offline-to-online sync for enrollment and disbursement flows. Feature flags via Django Waffle for country-specific mobile money provider routing. No raw SQL in Django views — ORM or repository layer only.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: M-Pesa and Wave API rate limits causing Celery queue backup during bulk disbursement cycles. Mitigation: Celery rate-limit decorator per mobile money provider task, separate queue with autoscaling. Risk: Biometric hash collision enabling ghost beneficiary bypass. Mitigation: composite national_id_hash + biometric_hash deduplication key reduces collision probability to negligible levels. Risk: OTA update delivery failure to offline devices. Mitigation: Expo update retry logic, version compatibility check on sync endpoint. Tradeoff: SHA-256 biometric hash over FHIR BiometricTemplate for simplicity, accepting reduced biometric accuracy for field-deployable Android device compatibility.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected Redux Persist with expo-sqlite over WatermelonDB for offline queue due to simpler integration with Expo managed workflow. Confirmed server-authoritative last-write-wins conflict resolution for sync to prevent duplicate disbursements. Chose Buildkite over GitHub Actions for mobile OTA pipeline due to existing organizational Buildkite infrastructure. Provisioned concurrency not applied to Lambda equivalents as ECS Fargate with auto-scaling chosen for container lifecycle predictability.

metadata.notes: Biometric cash transfer mobile app with Buildkite OTA CI/CD, offline SQLite queue, FATF compliance controls, and M-Pesa/Wave mobile money integration.

profile.domain: Humanitarian Crisis Early Warning ML Pipeline

project_goal.value: Research prototype ML pipeline that analyzes unstructured field reports and social media signals to automatically classify and geo-locate emerging humanitarian crises, supporting early warning and rapid response mobilization for disaster preparedness teams
project_goal.rationale: Early warning capability reduces response lag by surfacing signals before formal situation reports are filed

target_users.value: Disaster preparedness analysts and rapid response coordinators in humanitarian organizations
target_users.rationale: End users identified in discovery sessions with preparedness teams

project_class.rationale: Exploratory ML pipeline for internal research use; not a production-grade operational system

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, ai_llm, analytics, batch_jobs
capabilities.rationale: Streamlit analyst dashboard, FastAPI backend, NLP classification pipeline, MLflow tracking, batch ingestion jobs

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-modal NLP pipeline with geo-location extraction, model versioning, and analyst feedback loop

risk_level.rationale: Internal prototype with no PHI; risk from incorrect model predictions informing operational decisions

data_sensitivity.rationale: Field reports are organizational internal data; social media signals filtered for public posts only

external_exposure.rationale: Streamlit dashboard and FastAPI backend accessible to internal staff only via VPN

access_model.value: Custom JWT with role-based access; analyst role for dashboard review and alert export, admin role for model version management
access_model.rationale: Prototype needs basic auth to prevent unauthorized model promotion

feature_scope.value: NLP classification of field reports and social media signals, geo-location extraction, crisis severity scoring, analyst review dashboard, model version tracking, early warning alert export, analyst feedback collection for model retraining
feature_scope.rationale: Full prototype scope for end-to-end pipeline validation

mvp_scope.value: Field report classification pipeline, MongoDB persistence, Streamlit analyst dashboard with map visualization of classified events, MLflow experiment tracking
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum scope to demonstrate classification value and collect analyst feedback for model iteration

security_baseline.value: JWT auth for API and dashboard, TLS 1.3 in transit, VPN-restricted access, no plaintext storage of raw social media credentials
security_baseline.rationale: Internal prototype security proportionate to medium risk level

frontend_stack.value: Streamlit with Python 3.12
frontend_stack.rationale: Rapid prototype dashboard iteration without dedicated frontend engineering

backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI for async pipeline endpoints; Python ecosystem for NLP and ML libraries

data_platform.value: MongoDB 7 for document storage with Redis 7 for task queue
data_platform.rationale: Document store fits unstructured field report and classification result schema; Redis backs Celery task queue for batch ingestion

hosting_target.value: AWS SageMaker for model inference, EC2 for FastAPI and Streamlit
hosting_target.rationale: SageMaker provides managed inference endpoints with model versioning; EC2 for prototype application hosting

privacy_retention_policy.value: Field reports retained for 24 months; raw social media signals retained for 6 months; classification results retained for 36 months for model evaluation
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Research prototype data lifecycle aligned with organizational data governance policy

future_scope.value: Real-time stream processing replacing batch ingestion, integration with organizational alert distribution systems, expansion to additional crisis type taxonomies
future_scope.rationale: Post-prototype productionization roadmap

constraints.value: Social media data must comply with platform terms of service; prototype budget under $600/month on AWS
constraints.rationale: TOS compliance required; budget proportionate to research prototype investment

observability_baseline.value: MLflow experiment tracking for model versions, Grafana dashboards for pipeline run metrics, Prometheus for FastAPI service health
observability_baseline.rationale: ML-specific observability stack for prototype iteration velocity

execution_preference.value: Research sprints with bi-weekly analyst review sessions to guide model iteration
execution_preference.rationale: Tight feedback loop with analyst users maximizes prototype learning

llm_integration.value: AWS Bedrock Claude Haiku for geo-location entity extraction from unstructured field report text; custom fine-tuned DistilBERT for crisis type classification running on SageMaker endpoint
llm_integration.rationale: LLM handles complex NER for location extraction; fine-tuned classifier provides consistent crisis taxonomy mapping

compliance_context.value: Social media platform terms of service restrict data storage to 30 days for non-research licensed access; organizational AI ethics policy requires human-in-the-loop confirmation before alert export
compliance_context.rationale: TOS and AI ethics compliance required for prototype operation

project.goal: NLP-based crisis classification and geo-location pipeline for early warning
project.target_users: Disaster preparedness analysts and response coordinators
project.timeline: 3-month prototype with ongoing analyst feedback iteration

frontend.approach: Single-page analyst dashboard with map, event table, and feedback interface

backend.api: Internal REST API for pipeline triggers and analyst feedback submission

security.auth: Custom JWT

reasoner_reviews.product: After two rounds of iteration the classification pipeline and analyst dashboard core are solid. The outstanding gap is the analyst feedback loop not being wired into the retraining pipeline — analysts can mark corrections in the dashboard but those corrections are not yet persisted in a format usable by the MLflow retraining job. This is the key remaining gap for the prototype to deliver on its learning objectives.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The SageMaker DistilBERT endpoint and Bedrock Haiku NER are well-scoped for the prototype. The retraining pipeline needs a dedicated feedback_labels collection in MongoDB that the Celery retraining job reads from. MLflow should log a feedback_sample_count metric per training run to track label accumulation. The Streamlit dashboard correction flow should write directly to the feedback_labels collection via the FastAPI endpoint rather than an ad-hoc file.
reasoner_reviews.security: The round 2 fix for social media credential storage via AWS Secrets Manager was correctly implemented. The remaining concern is that the Streamlit dashboard has no session timeout, allowing an unattended browser session to remain authenticated indefinitely. A 30-minute idle session timeout should be enforced.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Social media raw signal retention was corrected to 6 months in the contract. The 30-day TOS restriction applies to raw API response storage; derived classification results are not subject to that limit. This distinction must be documented in the data model section.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The geo-location extraction via Bedrock Haiku is a good approach but the plan still does not specify what happens when Haiku returns no location entity for a field report — the classification result would have a null geo_location field, which breaks the map visualization in Streamlit. A fallback to a keyword-based location lookup table should be specified.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with routers for pipeline (POST /pipeline/ingest, POST /pipeline/run-classification), analyst feedback (POST /feedback/labels, GET /feedback/stats), and model management (GET /models/versions, POST /models/promote). Celery workers handle async batch ingestion and classification jobs. SageMaker endpoint client abstracted behind a ModelInferenceService class.
backend.llm_integration_design: AWS Bedrock Claude Haiku invoked via boto3 for NER extraction from field report text. Prompt template extracts country, region, district, and coordinates from free-text descriptions. Results parsed to a GeoLocation struct. Fallback: if Haiku returns empty entities, a keyword-to-coordinates lookup table covers 200 most-referenced humanitarian locations. DistilBERT SageMaker endpoint called for crisis_type classification returning a label and confidence_score.
backend.background_jobs: Celery task ingest_field_reports fetches new reports from the field_reports MongoDB collection and runs them through classification pipeline. Celery task retrain_model reads feedback_labels collection, exports labeled dataset to S3, triggers SageMaker training job, and logs new model version to MLflow on completion.
backend.failure_handling: SageMaker endpoint call failures caught with exponential backoff retries (3 attempts, 2s base). Bedrock Haiku failures fall through to keyword lookup fallback. Classification failures write error_detail to the classification_results document for analyst review.

security.auth_design: FastAPI JWT middleware validates RS256 tokens on all protected routes. Streamlit session cookie maps to a server-side session with 30-minute idle timeout enforced by a background thread checking last_activity timestamps.
security.secrets_management: Social media API credentials stored in AWS Secrets Manager. SageMaker execution role credentials managed via EC2 instance profile. No credentials in environment variables or code.
security.privacy_controls: Social media raw signals stored in a separate MongoDB collection with a TTL index set to 30 days. Derived classification_results documents do not retain raw signal text, only the classification label, confidence, and extracted geo_location.

data.entities: field_reports (MongoDB: _id, source_type ENUM field_report/social_media, raw_text, ingested_at, processed BOOL). classification_results (MongoDB: _id, report_id ref, crisis_type, confidence_score, severity_level ENUM low/medium/high/critical, geo_location {country, region, coordinates}, model_version_id, classified_at). feedback_labels (MongoDB: _id, report_id ref, analyst_id, corrected_crisis_type, corrected_severity, corrected_geo_location, created_at). early_warning_alerts (MongoDB: _id, classification_result_id ref, analyst_confirmed BOOL, exported_at, export_destination). model_versions (MLflow experiment: run_id, model_name, accuracy, f1_score, feedback_sample_count, promoted BOOL, created_at).
data.storage_design: MongoDB 7 on EC2 with replica set (1 primary, 1 secondary for read offload). TTL index on social_media_signals collection expires documents at 30 days. MLflow artifact store on S3. Redis 7 for Celery broker and result backend.
data.analytics_events: Pipeline run metrics: reports_ingested, classified_count, classification_errors, geo_extraction_success_rate, avg_confidence_score logged to MLflow per pipeline run. Analyst engagement: feedback_submissions_per_day, corrections_by_crisis_type tracked in MongoDB analytics collection.

devops.deployment_topology: EC2 t3.medium for FastAPI and Celery worker. EC2 t3.small for Streamlit dashboard. SageMaker real-time endpoint (ml.t3.medium) for DistilBERT. MongoDB replica set on EC2. Redis on ElastiCache t3.micro. MLflow tracking server on EC2 t3.micro with S3 artifact store.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: pytest with 75% coverage gate, docker build and push to ECR, EC2 deployment via SSM Run Command. SageMaker model deployment triggered separately via MLflow model promotion script.
devops.observability_stack: MLflow tracks all training runs with accuracy, f1_macro, and feedback_sample_count metrics. Prometheus scrapes FastAPI /metrics endpoint for request rate, pipeline duration, and Bedrock call latency. Grafana dashboards: pipeline run history, classification confidence distribution, geo_extraction_success_rate trend, Celery queue depth.

frontend.app_structure: Streamlit multi-page app with Python 3.12; pages for data ingestion, model results, geo-visualization, and alert configuration
frontend.pages_and_flows: Field Report Ingestion, Social Media Signal Monitor, NLP Classification Results, Geo-Location Extraction Map, Early Warning Alert Dashboard
frontend.performance_notes: Large NLP batch results paginated via Streamlit pagination helper; geo-visualization uses pydeck with WebGL for up to 10 000 data points
frontend.frontend_risks: Humanitarian data may include PII from conflict zones; ensure display components do not cache or log raw field report text in browser storage

ISS-001.title: Social media credentials stored in plaintext environment variables
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan specified social media API credentials passed as environment variables; this was remediated in round 2 by migrating to AWS Secrets Manager.

history.note: Identified in security review
Migrated to Secrets Manager

ISS-002.title: Analyst feedback corrections not wired into retraining pipeline
ISS-002.detail: Rounds 1 and 2 plans included a feedback UI in the Streamlit dashboard allowing analysts to correct classifications, but the feedback data is not persisted in a format that the MLflow retraining job can consume, breaking the learning loop.

history.note: Identified in product review
Dashboard correction UI added but no retraining integration

ISS-003.title: Null geo_location on failed Haiku NER breaks map visualization
ISS-003.detail: When Bedrock Haiku returns no location entity for a field report, the classification_result has a null geo_location field. The Streamlit map visualization crashes on null coordinates rather than gracefully skipping or showing an unknown location marker.

history.note: Identified in critic review after round 2

focus_issues.title: Analyst feedback corrections not wired into retraining pipeline
focus_issues.detail: Feedback corrections exist in the dashboard but are not persisted to feedback_labels collection or consumed by the retraining Celery job.

focus_issues.title: Null geo_location on failed Haiku NER breaks map visualization
focus_issues.detail: Null geo_location field causes Streamlit map crash rather than graceful handling.

Add keyword-to-coordinates fallback for Haiku NER failures and handle null geo_location gracefully in Streamlit map

previous_plan.title: Humanitarian Crisis Early Warning ML Pipeline - Round 2
previous_plan.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 ML pipeline on AWS using FastAPI, SageMaker DistilBERT, and Bedrock Claude Haiku to classify field reports and social media signals as humanitarian crises, with a Streamlit analyst dashboard for review and alert export. Round 2 fixed credential storage via Secrets Manager and added the feedback_labels collection schema.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: FastAPI on EC2 with Celery workers for batch classification jobs. SageMaker real-time endpoint serves DistilBERT crisis classifier. Bedrock Haiku handles geo NER. MongoDB stores field_reports, classification_results, and feedback_labels. MLflow on EC2 tracks experiments. Streamlit dashboard on EC2 for analyst review.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: Social media credentials migrated to AWS Secrets Manager in round 2. JWT auth on FastAPI. TLS 1.3. Social media raw signals with MongoDB TTL index at 30 days. AI ethics policy requires analyst confirmation before alert export.
previous_plan.data_model: field_reports, classification_results, feedback_labels (defined in round 2), early_warning_alerts, model_versions collections in MongoDB 7.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 addresses ISS-002 (feedback-retraining loop broken) and ISS-003 (null geo_location map crash). The Celery retrain_model task needs to explicitly query the feedback_labels collection, export a labeled CSV to S3, and log feedback_sample_count to MLflow. For ISS-003, the Bedrock NER path needs a keyword-to-coordinates fallback table and the Streamlit map rendering must guard against null geo_location with an 'unknown location' marker. Architecture structure from rounds 1-2 is sound; these are targeted integration fixes.

fix_report.action_taken: Updated retrain_model Celery task to query feedback_labels collection filtering by created_at > last_training_run_at, export labeled samples to S3 as a CSV dataset, append to the existing training data, trigger SageMaker training job with the augmented dataset, and log feedback_sample_count as an MLflow metric on the new run. POST /feedback/labels FastAPI endpoint now validates corrected_crisis_type against the supported taxonomy enum before persisting to MongoDB.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Analyst corrections accumulate in feedback_labels and are consumed by the next scheduled retraining run; feedback_sample_count metric in MLflow tracks label accumulation over time; ISS-002 resolved

fix_report.action_taken: Added a LOCATION_KEYWORDS_TABLE constant in the NER module mapping 200 frequently referenced humanitarian location names to country/region/coordinates tuples. Bedrock Haiku NER result parsed; if entity list is empty, keyword scan of raw_text against table entries provides best-match geo_location. If both fail, geo_location is set to {country: 'unknown', region: 'unknown', coordinates: null} rather than null. Streamlit map rendering skips events with coordinates null and displays them in a separate 'unlocated events' table below the map.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Streamlit map never crashes on classification results; unlocated events remain visible to analysts; geo_extraction_success_rate metric in Grafana now includes keyword-match as a partial success; ISS-003 resolved

target_output.title: Humanitarian Crisis Early Warning ML Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 research prototype on AWS that classifies field reports and filtered social media signals as humanitarian crises using a fine-tuned DistilBERT model on SageMaker and AWS Bedrock Claude Haiku for geo-location entity extraction. Disaster preparedness analysts review classified events in a Streamlit dashboard, correct misclassifications, and export confirmed early warning alerts. This round closes the feedback-to-retraining loop and adds resilient geo-location handling with keyword fallback and graceful null handling in the map visualization.
target_output.architecture_overview: FastAPI 3.12 application on EC2 t3.medium serving pipeline trigger and analyst feedback endpoints. Celery workers on the same instance handle async batch ingestion jobs and a scheduled retraining pipeline. SageMaker real-time endpoint (ml.t3.medium) serves the fine-tuned DistilBERT crisis classifier. Bedrock Claude Haiku handles geo-location NER with a keyword-to-coordinates fallback for unrecognized locations. MongoDB 7 replica set stores field_reports, classification_results, feedback_labels, and early_warning_alerts. MLflow tracking server on EC2 t3.micro with S3 artifact store. Streamlit analyst dashboard on EC2 t3.small displays classified events on an interactive map, a tabular event list, and a feedback correction panel. All services VPN-restricted with JWT auth on FastAPI endpoints.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, Celery with Redis broker, boto3 for SageMaker and Bedrock calls. ML: fine-tuned DistilBERT on SageMaker, AWS Bedrock Claude Haiku for NER, MLflow for experiment tracking. Frontend: Streamlit with PyDeck for map visualization. Data: MongoDB 7 replica set, Redis 7 on ElastiCache for Celery. Infrastructure: EC2 t3.medium (API+workers), EC2 t3.small (Streamlit), EC2 t3.micro (MLflow), SageMaker ml.t3.medium endpoint, S3 for model artifacts and training datasets. CI/CD: GitHub Actions, ECR, SSM Run Command for deployment.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Field Report Ingestion: Celery task fetches unprocessed field_reports documents, runs DistilBERT classification, extracts geo-location via Bedrock Haiku with keyword fallback, writes classification_results to MongoDB. Social Media Ingestion: Celery task fetches filtered public posts from configured sources (stored with 30-day TTL), runs same classification pipeline. Analyst Dashboard: Streamlit page showing PyDeck map of classified events (skipping null-coordinate events), tabular event list with crisis_type and severity, feedback panel for corrections. Feedback Loop: POST /feedback/labels persists analyst corrections to feedback_labels collection; scheduled Celery retrain_model task reads labels, exports augmented dataset to S3, triggers SageMaker training job, logs metrics to MLflow. Alert Export: Analyst confirms event in dashboard triggering early_warning_alerts document creation; admin can export to organizational response system.
target_output.system_components: FastAPI application (routers: pipeline, feedback, models, alerts). Celery worker (tasks: ingest_field_reports, ingest_social_media, run_classification_batch, retrain_model). SageMaker DistilBERT endpoint (ml.t3.medium, auto-scaling 1-2 instances). Bedrock Claude Haiku NER module with LOCATION_KEYWORDS_TABLE fallback (200 humanitarian location entries). MongoDB 7 replica set (collections: field_reports, classification_results, feedback_labels, early_warning_alerts). Redis 7 ElastiCache (Celery broker and result backend). MLflow tracking server (EC2 t3.micro, S3 artifact store, experiment: crisis_classification). Streamlit dashboard (PyDeck map with null-coordinate guard, event table, feedback form, unlocated events table).
target_output.workflows: Batch Classification: Celery ingest_field_reports task queries field_reports where processed=false, batches 50 documents, calls SageMaker DistilBERT endpoint for crisis_type and confidence_score, calls Bedrock Haiku NER for geo-location extraction, applies keyword fallback if Haiku returns empty entities, sets geo_location to unknown struct if both fail, writes classification_results documents with all fields, marks source field_reports as processed=true. Analyst Review and Correction: Analyst opens Streamlit dashboard, views PyDeck map of events with non-null coordinates, reviews unlocated_events table for null-coordinate events, selects an event with incorrect classification, submits correction via feedback form calling POST /feedback/labels, FastAPI validates corrected_crisis_type against taxonomy enum and persists to feedback_labels collection. Model Retraining: Celery retrain_model task runs on weekly schedule, queries feedback_labels where created_at > last_training_run_at, exports corrected samples to S3 as labeled_samples_{timestamp}.csv, triggers SageMaker training job pointing to augmented dataset, logs new MLflow run with accuracy, f1_macro, and feedback_sample_count, marks new model version in MLflow; admin reviews metrics and promotes version via POST /models/promote which updates SageMaker endpoint to new model.
target_output.data_model: field_reports (MongoDB: _id ObjectId, source_type ENUM field_report/social_media, raw_text String, ingested_at ISODate, processed Bool, ttl_expires_at ISODate for social_media only). classification_results (MongoDB: _id ObjectId, report_id ObjectId ref, crisis_type ENUM conflict/disease_outbreak/natural_disaster/displacement/food_crisis, confidence_score Float, severity_level ENUM low/medium/high/critical, geo_location {country String, region String, coordinates {lat Float, lon Float} nullable}, geo_source ENUM haiku_ner/keyword_match/unknown, model_version_id String, classified_at ISODate). feedback_labels (MongoDB: _id ObjectId, report_id ObjectId ref, analyst_id String, corrected_crisis_type ENUM validated against taxonomy, corrected_severity ENUM, corrected_geo_location {country, region, coordinates} nullable, created_at ISODate). early_warning_alerts (MongoDB: _id ObjectId, classification_result_id ObjectId ref, analyst_confirmed Bool, exported_at ISODate nullable, export_destination String nullable). social media raw signals stored in separate collection with TTL index expiring at 30 days post-ingestion; derived classification_results retain no raw signal text.
target_output.api_design: POST /pipeline/ingest triggers Celery ingest_field_reports task returning {task_id}. POST /pipeline/run-classification triggers immediate classification batch returning {task_id}. POST /feedback/labels body {report_id, corrected_crisis_type, corrected_severity, corrected_geo_location} validates taxonomy enum and persists to feedback_labels, returns 201 {label_id}. GET /feedback/stats returns {total_labels, labels_since_last_training, corrections_by_crisis_type}. GET /models/versions returns MLflow run list with accuracy, f1_macro, feedback_sample_count, promoted. POST /models/promote {run_id} promotes MLflow model version and updates SageMaker endpoint. GET /alerts returns paginated early_warning_alerts with analyst_confirmed filter.
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT RS256 auth on all FastAPI endpoints; Streamlit session cookie with 30-minute idle timeout enforced by background thread checking last_activity per session_id in Redis. Social media API credentials in AWS Secrets Manager fetched at startup via boto3. EC2 instance profile provides SageMaker and Bedrock invocation permissions; no hardcoded AWS credentials. Social media raw signals TTL index at 30 days per platform TOS. Derived classification_results and feedback_labels retain no raw signal text, storing only structured outputs. AI ethics policy compliance: early_warning_alert export requires analyst_confirmed=true set by a human analyst via the Streamlit dashboard; no automated alert dispatch. VPN-restricted access to all EC2 instances and MongoDB replica set.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions pipeline: pytest with 75% coverage gate, docker build and push to ECR. EC2 deployment via SSM Run Command pulling latest ECR image, restarting FastAPI and Celery via systemd, running MongoDB migration scripts. SageMaker model deployment triggered by admin via POST /models/promote after MLflow metrics review. Environments: dev (single EC2 instance, MongoDB single-node), research-prod (EC2 t3.medium API+workers, t3.small Streamlit, t3.micro MLflow, MongoDB replica set, ElastiCache Redis). Celery beat scheduler configured for: ingest_field_reports every 4 hours, ingest_social_media every 2 hours, retrain_model every Sunday 02:00 UTC. Rollback: SSM Run Command redeploys previous ECR image tag; SageMaker endpoint reverts to previous model version via MLflow promoted flag.
target_output.observability: MLflow experiment crisis_classification logs per training run: accuracy, f1_macro, precision_by_class, recall_by_class, feedback_sample_count, training_duration_seconds. Prometheus scrapes FastAPI /metrics for: pipeline_classification_requests_total, bedrock_ner_success_rate, bedrock_ner_fallback_rate (keyword_match), bedrock_ner_unknown_rate, sagemaker_inference_latency_p95, celery_task_duration_seconds by task name. Grafana dashboards: classification confidence distribution histogram, geo_extraction breakdown by source (haiku_ner/keyword_match/unknown), retraining feedback accumulation over time, Celery queue depth per task. Alert: Grafana fires if sagemaker_inference_latency_p95 exceeds 3 seconds or bedrock_ner_unknown_rate exceeds 30%.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: EC2 t3.medium $30/month, t3.small $15/month, t3.micro x2 $10/month. SageMaker ml.t3.medium endpoint $45/month. ElastiCache t3.micro $15/month. MongoDB EC2 t3.small x2 $30/month. S3 artifacts and Bedrock Haiku API calls ~$20/month. Total approximately $165/month, well under $600 constraint. Scaling: SageMaker endpoint auto-scales to 2 instances under classification batch bursts. Celery concurrency set to 4 workers to avoid overloading EC2 t3.medium memory.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - Classification MVP (4 weeks): FastAPI service, Celery ingestion tasks, SageMaker DistilBERT endpoint deployment, MongoDB schema setup, MLflow tracking, Streamlit dashboard with map and event table. Phase 2 - Feedback and Security (3 weeks): POST /feedback/labels endpoint, feedback_labels collection, Celery retrain_model task with S3 export and MLflow logging, Secrets Manager credential migration, Streamlit 30-minute session timeout. Phase 3 - Resilience and Round 3 Fixes (2 weeks): Bedrock Haiku NER keyword fallback with 200-location table, null geo_location guard in Streamlit map, unlocated events table in dashboard, feedback_sample_count MLflow metric, geo_source field in classification_results for observability.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with mypy strict mode for FastAPI and Celery modules. pytest with mongomock for MongoDB unit tests. Celery tasks tested with task.apply() in unit tests and against real Redis in integration tests. SageMaker and Bedrock calls mocked in unit tests via moto and a custom ModelInferenceService stub. MLflow experiment names and metric keys defined as constants in a shared metrics.py module to prevent naming drift across training runs.
