target_output.functional_feature_map: MappingConfig loader: reads YAML mapping definition specifying source XML resource type, target FHIR resource type, per-field XPath source expressions, target fhir_path, field_type enum, and optional transformation_fn name. Validates on load against bundled JSON schema; raises MappingConfigError with line number for invalid definitions. XMLSourceParser: extracts field values by evaluating XPath expressions against parsed lxml/ElementTree element. FHIRResourceBuilder: maps extracted values to fhir.resources Patient (name, birthDate, gender, identifier[]), RelatedPerson (patient reference, relationship, name), Observation (code CodeableConcept, valueQuantity or valueString, subject reference). FHIRMigrator: iterates source records in two modes — fail_fast and collect_errors; returns MigrationReport with success_count, error_count, and error_details list. PIIRedactor utility: generates anonymized test fixtures by replacing HumanName values with random names from a static word list, shifting birthDate values by a random ±30-365 day offset, and replacing Identifier.value with UUID-format placeholders.
target_output.system_components: fhir_migrate.config.MappingConfig (YAML loader, JSON schema validator), fhir_migrate.parsers.XMLSourceParser (XPath extraction, lxml/ElementTree adapter), fhir_migrate.coerce.FieldTypeCoercer (type conversion per FieldMapping.field_type), fhir_migrate.builders.FHIRResourceBuilder (fhir.resources object construction), fhir_migrate.validate.FHIRConstraintValidator (Pydantic ValidationError to MigrationError mapping), fhir_migrate.migrator.FHIRMigrator (batch orchestrator, MigrationReport builder), fhir_migrate.report.MigrationReport (structured result dataclass), fhir_migrate.redact.PIIRedactor (test fixture anonymization), fhir_migrate.schemas/ (bundled mapping_config.schema.json and sample YAML templates for CPIMS+, proGres, and generic flat-element formats).
target_output.workflows: Batch Migration Workflow: Developer creates a YAML mapping config referencing XPath expressions for their legacy XML schema. Instantiates MappingConfig.load('mapping.yaml') which validates on load. Creates FHIRMigrator(config=config, mode='collect_errors'). Calls migrator.transform_batch(xml_records_iter) which yields FHIRBundle objects per record. Accesses report = migrator.report for MigrationReport with error_details list. Feeds error_details to remediation workflow: for each MigrationError, the developer fixes the source record or updates the mapping config. Test Fixture Generation Workflow: Developer has a directory of real source XML records to use as integration test fixtures. Calls PIIRedactor.anonymize_directory(source_dir, output_dir, config=mapping_config). SDK iterates XML files, parses each, applies per-field anonymization based on field_type (HumanName, date, Identifier), writes anonymized XML to output_dir. Resulting fixtures are safe for inclusion in test suites and CI pipelines without exposing real beneficiary data.
target_output.data_model: MappingConfig dataclass: source_resource_type str, target_fhir_resource Enum Patient/RelatedPerson/Observation, field_mappings List[FieldMapping], required_fields List[str], transformation_rules Dict[str, Callable]. FieldMapping dataclass: source_xpath str, target_fhir_path str, field_type Enum str/date/codeable_concept/human_name/identifier, required bool, transformation_fn Optional[str]. FHIR output types from fhir.resources 7.x: Patient (id, identifier[], name HumanName[], birthDate, gender, address[]), RelatedPerson (patient Reference, relationship CodeableConcept[], name HumanName[]), Observation (code CodeableConcept, subject Reference, valueQuantity/valueString, effectiveDateTime). MigrationError dataclass: source_record_id str, source_xpath str, target_fhir_path str, error_type Enum missing_required/type_coercion/fhir_constraint/xpath_not_found, detail str. MigrationReport dataclass: total_records int, success_count int, error_count int, error_details List[MigrationError], config_name str, generated_at datetime.
target_output.api_design: Public SDK API (importable from fhir_migrate): from fhir_migrate import MappingConfig, FHIRMigrator, MigrationReport, PIIRedactor. MappingConfig.load(path: str | Path) -> MappingConfig. MappingConfig.from_dict(mapping_dict: dict) -> MappingConfig. FHIRMigrator(config: MappingConfig, mode: Literal['fail_fast', 'collect_errors'] = 'collect_errors', logger: Optional[logging.Logger] = None). FHIRMigrator.transform_record(xml_str: str) -> tuple[Bundle, list[MigrationError]]. FHIRMigrator.transform_batch(records: Iterable[str]) -> Iterator[tuple[Bundle, list[MigrationError]]]. FHIRMigrator.report -> MigrationReport (available after batch completes). PIIRedactor.anonymize(xml_str: str, config: MappingConfig) -> str. PIIRedactor.anonymize_directory(source_dir: Path, output_dir: Path, config: MappingConfig) -> int (returns count of anonymized files). No HTTP endpoints — pure Python function calls.
target_output.security_and_compliance: PII logging prevention: SDK logger is named 'fhir_migrate' and defaults to WARNING level; FieldTypeCoercer and FHIRResourceBuilder log field names and error types only, never field values. PIIRedactor uses static word lists for name replacement and deterministic date shifting (seeded per record_id for reproducibility in tests). GDPR: SDK documentation includes a GDPR migration guide explaining that consuming applications must implement retention policies for source records and migration artifacts; SDK itself holds no data between calls. HL7 FHIR R4 compliance: fhir.resources 7.x Pydantic models enforce all mandatory FHIR element constraints and cardinality rules at object construction time. FHIR Bundle type set to 'transaction' with PUT semantics for idempotent FHIR server uploads. OpenHIE alignment: SDK produces FHIR R4 resources compatible with the OpenHIE Client Registry (CR) and Shared Health Record (SHR) component interfaces.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: PyPI distribution: GitHub Actions release workflow triggered on git tags matching v{major}.{minor}.{patch}. Workflow steps: pytest matrix (Python 3.10, 3.11, 3.12), mypy strict, flake8, safety CVE scan, python -m build producing wheel and sdist, twine upload to PyPI using PYPI_API_TOKEN secret. Branch protection on main: require CI pass and 1 reviewer approval. ReadTheDocs webhook on main push triggers documentation rebuild. Versioning: SemVer with CHANGELOG.md; patch releases for bug fixes, minor for new field_type enums or FHIR resource support, major for breaking MappingConfig schema or FHIRMigrator API changes. No infrastructure to operate beyond GitHub Actions runners and PyPI account.
target_output.observability: Python standard logging integration: consumers configure fhir_migrate logger with their application's log handler. Default log output includes mapping config load success/failure, per-batch transform start/complete with record counts, and individual MigrationError summaries (source_record_id, error_type, fhir_field_path) without PII values. MigrationReport.summary() returns a dict suitable for structured log emission by consuming application.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost: PyPI hosting is free for open-source packages; GitHub Actions free tier covers CI for a library with moderate commit frequency. Memory scaling: FHIRMigrator.transform_batch() is a generator yielding one Bundle at a time, so memory footprint is bounded by the largest single source record rather than the full batch size. Large XML source files (>100,000 records): consuming applications should chunk input into files of 10,000 records each for predictable memory usage.
target_output.phased_implementation: v1.0.0 MVP (12 weeks): MappingConfig YAML loader with JSON schema validation, XMLSourceParser with XPath extraction and lxml/ElementTree adapter, FHIRResourceBuilder for Patient resource only, FHIRConstraintValidator, FHIRMigrator batch orchestrator with fail_fast and collect_errors modes, MigrationReport, PIIRedactor utility, 90% test coverage, mypy strict, PyPI publication. v1.1.0 (4 weeks): RelatedPerson and Observation resource support, sample mapping configs for CPIMS+ and proGres XML schemas, additional FieldType enums (codeable_concept, identifier). v2.0.0 (future): Async batch processing via asyncio for large datasets, FHIR Questionnaire/QuestionnaireResponse resource support, optional FHIR server upload client as extras_require extension.
target_output.development_guidelines: Test-driven development: write unit tests before implementation for FieldTypeCoercer type conversions and FHIRConstraintValidator error mapping. 90% line coverage gate enforced in CI — no PR merge if coverage drops below threshold. mypy strict mode: all public functions fully type-annotated; no Any in public API surface. No PII in test fixtures: all test XML files use PIIRedactor-anonymized data; enforced by pre-commit hook scanning for common name patterns in test data directories. Changelog entry required for every PR affecting public API. Docstrings in NumPy style with Parameters, Returns, Raises, and Examples sections for all public classes and functions.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: fhir.resources 7.x Pydantic v2 models enforce FHIR constraints but do not validate terminology bindings (e.g., whether a CodeableConcept code exists in the referenced ValueSet). Mitigation: document this limitation clearly; consuming teams must validate terminology separately against their FHIR server's $validate operation. Risk: XPath expressions in mapping configs may silently return empty strings rather than raising errors for unmatched elements, causing required fields to appear as missing rather than mismatched. Mitigation: FHIRConstraintValidator distinguishes xpath_not_found from missing_required in MigrationError.error_type. Tradeoff: fhir.resources as a mandatory dependency adds ~15MB to the install footprint; acceptable given it provides the full FHIR R4 model library. Tradeoff: synchronous batch processing limits throughput for very large migrations but simplifies the API surface for v1.0.0.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose fhir.resources 7.x over building custom FHIR models: the library provides complete FHIR R4 Pydantic v2 models with constraint validation, eliminating maintenance of ~150 resource type definitions. Chose YAML over Python code for mapping configs: YAML configs are readable by non-developers (data engineers, program staff) and can be version-controlled and reviewed without Python knowledge. Chose xml.etree.ElementTree as mandatory parser with lxml as optional: avoids C extension compilation requirement while allowing performance optimization where lxml is available.

metadata.notes: Python SDK for FHIR R4 migration of humanitarian beneficiary XML records; moderate quality with slightly thin observability section.

profile.domain: Cholera Outbreak Risk Forecasting WASH Data Pipeline

project_goal.value: A data pipeline powering a machine learning model that forecasts cholera outbreak risk in displacement camps by integrating latrines-to-population ratios, water quality test results, rainfall anomalies, and historical outbreak records to guide preventive WASH interventions
project_goal.rationale: Early risk forecasting enables preventive WASH interventions before outbreak threshold is reached

target_users.value: WASH sector coordinators and camp management teams requiring timely risk scores to prioritize intervention resources
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers identified in WASH sector working group discovery

project_class.rationale: Batch ingestion, feature engineering, ML inference, and alert dispatch pipeline with no interactive frontend

capabilities.rationale: Prefect orchestration, PostgreSQL persistence, multi-source data integrations, GCP analytics stack

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-source data harmonization, feature engineering for geospatial and time-series inputs, ML inference pipeline with threshold alerting

risk_level.rationale: Internal pipeline; health data sensitivity but no direct patient records; risk from false-negative predictions informing intervention decisions

data_sensitivity.rationale: Water quality test results and outbreak records may be linked to identifiable camp populations; treated as health data

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline outputs consumed by internal WASH coordinators; no public API or external partner access

access_model.value: GCP IAM with service account permissions per pipeline component; human access to PostgreSQL and GCS via VPN-restricted IAM group
access_model.rationale: Principle of least privilege for pipeline service accounts; human access restricted to authorized WASH data team

feature_scope.value: Daily water quality test ingestion, sanitation coverage ratio computation, rainfall anomaly integration from weather API, historical outbreak record loading, feature engineering pipeline, ML risk score inference per camp, configurable threshold alerting, intervention recommendation report generation
feature_scope.rationale: Full pipeline scope to produce actionable risk scores and recommendations

mvp_scope.value: Daily ingestion of water quality and sanitation ratio data, feature engineering, risk score inference using pre-trained model, threshold alert dispatch to WASH coordinator notification channel
mvp_scope.rationale: Core pipeline delivering daily risk scores unblocks WASH coordinators for intervention prioritization

security_baseline.value: GCP IAM service accounts with minimum required permissions, all data encrypted at rest via GCP default KMS, TLS 1.3 for all external API integrations, no personal identifiers in pipeline logs
security_baseline.rationale: Internal pipeline with health data requires encryption at rest and strict access controls

frontend_stack.value: none - data pipeline only; outputs delivered via PostgreSQL tables and notification alerts
frontend_stack.rationale: No frontend component; WASH coordinators consume risk scores via existing dashboard tools connecting to PostgreSQL

backend_stack.rationale: Prefect provides workflow observability, retry handling, and scheduling without infrastructure overhead; Python ecosystem for ML and data libraries

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for risk scores and pipeline run metadata; GCS for raw data archives and model artifacts
data_platform.rationale: PostgreSQL for structured risk score queries by WASH coordinators; GCS for cost-effective raw data storage

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run Jobs for pipeline execution
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless job execution eliminates idle compute costs for a scheduled daily pipeline

privacy_retention_policy.value: Water quality test raw records retained for 36 months for trend analysis; risk scores retained for 60 months for model evaluation; personal identifiers never ingested into pipeline
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Long retention supports model retraining and epidemiological analysis; no PII in pipeline by design

future_scope.value: Real-time ingestion replacing daily batch, model retraining pipeline on newly labeled outbreak data, integration with camp management information system for direct intervention workflow triggers
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP roadmap to reduce latency and close the intervention loop

constraints.value: Pipeline must complete daily run within a 2-hour window to allow coordinators to act on scores before morning briefings; budget under $400/month on GCP
constraints.rationale: Operational timing constraint and cost-proportionate to pipeline scope

observability_baseline.value: Grafana dashboards for pipeline run metrics, Prometheus for Cloud Run job durations, Prefect UI for flow run history and failure alerts
observability_baseline.rationale: ML data pipeline requires observability on data freshness, feature drift, and prediction distribution alongside infrastructure metrics

execution_preference.value: Iterative delivery with WASH coordinator review of first risk scores before production cutover
execution_preference.rationale: WASH domain validation of initial model outputs required before pipeline is used operationally

compliance_context.value: WHO health data standards for WASH indicators, GDPR for any personally identifiable health records (none expected in pipeline by design), organizational data governance policy for camp health data
compliance_context.rationale: Health data sensitivity classification requires documented data governance even for aggregate camp-level data

project.goal: Daily cholera risk forecasting pipeline for displacement camp WASH coordinators
project.target_users: WASH sector coordinators and camp management teams
project.timeline: 6-week MVP delivery with coordinator validation period

security.auth: GCP IAM service accounts

data.storage: GCS for raw archives and model artifacts

reasoner_reviews.product: The daily risk score per displacement camp is the right output granularity for WASH coordinators who manage interventions at the camp level. The threshold-based alert system should use configurable risk tiers — low, elevated, high, critical — rather than a binary threshold, so coordinators can triage across multiple camps simultaneously. The intervention recommendation report must cite which WASH indicators drove the risk score elevation to support actionable responses.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Prefect on Cloud Run Jobs is an appropriate orchestration choice for a daily scheduled pipeline. The flow should be structured as a DAG with four stages: ingest (parallel tasks per data source), feature_engineer (sequential, depends on all ingest tasks), infer (depends on feature_engineer), alert (depends on infer). Prefect's task-level retry logic handles transient weather API failures without re-running the entire flow. PostgreSQL on Cloud SQL handles the risk score query volume; a single db-g1-small instance is sufficient at this scale.
reasoner_reviews.security: Pipeline service accounts should be scoped per stage: ingestion SA has GCS write and Cloud SQL read/write on raw tables only; inference SA has GCS read for model artifacts and Cloud SQL write on risk_scores table only. No service account should have project-level IAM roles. GCP Secret Manager stores weather API credentials; the ingestion Cloud Run Job fetches secrets at startup via the Secret Manager API.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Total pipeline runtime of 2 hours is achievable: parallel ingestion tasks for the three data sources (water quality, weather, sanitation) complete in 10-15 minutes; feature engineering processes 500 camps in under 30 minutes; batch inference is under 5 minutes for 500 camps using scikit-learn; alert generation under 5 minutes. Total well within the 2-hour window with headroom for retries.

backend.service_design: Prefect flow cholera_risk_daily with four sequential task groups: ingest_water_quality, ingest_weather, ingest_sanitation (parallel); engineer_features (sequential, depends on all ingest tasks); run_inference (depends on engineer_features); dispatch_alerts (depends on run_inference). Each task group is a Python module under pipeline/tasks/. Shared utilities in pipeline/utils/ for database connections, GCS client, and Secret Manager access.
backend.background_jobs: Cloud Run Job executes the Prefect flow on a daily schedule at 04:00 UTC triggered by Cloud Scheduler. Job container pulls from Artifact Registry. Prefect Cloud tracks flow run history, task states, and sends failure notifications to WASH data team Slack channel.
backend.failure_handling: Weather API tasks: 3 retries with 60-second exponential backoff; on final failure, flow continues with cached weather features from previous run flagged as stale. Water quality ingestion: if fewer than 80% of expected camp records received, flow emits a data_freshness_warning metric but continues. Inference task: on model load failure from GCS, flow halts and Prefect triggers critical alert to WASH data team.

security.auth_design: Three GCP service accounts: sa-ingest (GCS write to raw bucket, Cloud SQL write to raw tables, Secret Manager access for API keys), sa-inference (GCS read from model bucket, Cloud SQL read from feature tables, Cloud SQL write to risk_scores), sa-alerting (Cloud SQL read from risk_scores, Pub/Sub publish for notification dispatch). Each Cloud Run Job configured with its stage-specific service account.
security.secrets_management: Weather API credentials stored in Secret Manager secret wash-weather-api-key. Cloud Run Job for ingestion stage fetches secret via Python Secret Manager client at startup. No credentials in container images, environment variables, or GitLab CI config.
security.privacy_controls: Personal identifiers (individual names, dates of birth) explicitly excluded from all ingestion schemas. Water quality tests ingested at camp-level aggregate only, not individual household level. Pipeline logs do not include camp resident counts or any demographic breakdowns to avoid indirect identification.

data.entities: displacement_camps (id UUID PK, camp_name, admin_region, country, latitude, longitude, population_estimate, last_updated). water_quality_tests (id UUID PK, camp_id FK, test_date DATE, turbidity_ntu FLOAT, e_coli_per_100ml FLOAT, chlorine_residual_mg_l FLOAT, sample_source ENUM borehole/surface/trucked, created_at). sanitation_ratios (id UUID PK, camp_id FK, survey_date DATE, latrines_count INT, population_served INT, latrines_per_1000 FLOAT, open_defecation_rate_pct FLOAT, created_at). rainfall_observations (id UUID PK, camp_id FK, observation_date DATE, rainfall_mm FLOAT, anomaly_z_score FLOAT, data_source VARCHAR, created_at). historical_outbreaks (id UUID PK, camp_id FK, onset_date DATE, peak_date DATE, total_cases INT, case_fatality_rate FLOAT, primary_water_source, confirmed BOOL). risk_scores (id UUID PK, camp_id FK, score_date DATE, risk_tier ENUM low/elevated/high/critical, risk_score FLOAT, top_risk_factors JSONB, model_version VARCHAR, pipeline_run_id UUID, created_at). intervention_recommendations (id UUID PK, risk_score_id FK, recommendation_text TEXT, indicator_drivers JSONB, dispatched_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL (db-g1-small, automated daily backups, point-in-time recovery enabled). GCS bucket wash-pipeline-raw for archived raw ingestion files. GCS bucket wash-ml-artifacts for serialized scikit-learn model and feature scaler.
data.analytics_events: Per pipeline run: records_ingested per source, camps_with_complete_features count, inference_duration_seconds, camps_by_risk_tier distribution, alerts_dispatched count logged as Prefect task metrics and Prometheus gauges.
data.data_risks: Stale weather data from API outages may under-represent recent rainfall anomalies in risk scores; mitigated by flagging stale_weather=true in risk_scores row and lowering confidence display in coordinator outputs.

devops.deployment_topology: Three Cloud Run Jobs (ingest, engineer+infer, alert) sharing a Docker base image. Cloud Scheduler triggers ingest job at 04:00 UTC; subsequent jobs triggered by Prefect flow state transitions via Pub/Sub. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL in us-central1. GCS buckets in us-central1. Artifact Registry in us-central1.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitLab CI pipeline: pytest unit tests with 80% coverage gate, docker build and push to Artifact Registry, Cloud Run Job update-traffic deploy. Model artifact promoted to production GCS path via a separate manual GitLab CI job requiring WASH data lead approval.
devops.observability_stack: Prefect Cloud for flow run history and task-level failure alerts. Prometheus scrapes custom metrics from Cloud Run Job via push gateway: pipeline_run_duration_seconds, camps_scored_total, alerts_dispatched_total, stale_weather_camps_count. Grafana dashboards: daily risk tier distribution per country, pipeline run timeline, data freshness per source.

target_output.thinking_summary: Daily cholera risk forecasting pipeline integrating four data streams: water quality tests, sanitation ratios, rainfall anomalies, and historical outbreak records. Key architectural decisions: Prefect on Cloud Run Jobs for scheduled batch execution with per-stage service accounts enforcing least privilege on health data; PostgreSQL for risk score persistence and coordinator querying; risk tier system (low/elevated/high/critical) rather than binary threshold; intervention recommendation reports citing indicator drivers for actionable WASH responses. Privacy by design: no personal identifiers ingest by schema constraint.
target_output.title: Cholera Outbreak Risk Forecasting WASH Data Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: A Python 3.12 Prefect-orchestrated data pipeline on GCP Cloud Run Jobs that ingests daily water quality test results, sanitation coverage ratios, and rainfall anomaly data for displacement camps, engineers WASH features, and runs ML inference to produce per-camp cholera outbreak risk scores and intervention recommendations. WASH sector coordinators consume risk scores via PostgreSQL and receive configurable threshold alerts to prioritize preventive interventions. No personal identifiers are ingested; health data is handled at camp-aggregate level under WHO and GDPR compliance controls.
target_output.architecture_overview: Prefect flow cholera_risk_daily runs as a daily Cloud Run Job triggered by Cloud Scheduler at 04:00 UTC. The flow has four sequential task groups: parallel ingestion of water quality, sanitation, and weather data from field monitoring systems and a weather API; feature engineering combining the ingested streams into WASH risk features per camp; batch ML inference loading a scikit-learn gradient boosting model from GCS; and threshold alert dispatch publishing Pub/Sub messages for camps in elevated/high/critical tiers. Results persist to PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL. Three stage-specific GCP service accounts enforce least privilege on GCS and Cloud SQL access. All data encrypted at rest via GCP KMS; weather API credentials stored in Secret Manager.
target_output.technology_stack: Orchestration: Prefect 2.x with Cloud Run Job workers. Pipeline language: Python 3.12 with pandas for feature engineering, scikit-learn for model loading and batch inference, psycopg2 for PostgreSQL writes. Data: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL db-g1-small with automated backups; GCS for raw archive and model artifacts. Infrastructure: Cloud Run Jobs, Cloud Scheduler, Pub/Sub for alert dispatch, Artifact Registry, Secret Manager, GCP KMS. CI/CD: GitLab CI, Docker. Observability: Prefect Cloud, Prometheus push gateway, Grafana.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Water Quality Ingestion: fetches daily test records (turbidity, e-coli count, chlorine residual, sample source) from field monitoring API per camp, archives raw JSON to GCS, inserts parsed records to water_quality_tests table. Sanitation Ratio Ingestion: reads survey exports from partner file share, computes latrines_per_1000 and open_defecation_rate_pct, inserts to sanitation_ratios table. Rainfall Anomaly Integration: queries weather API for camp coordinates, computes rolling 30-day z-score against 5-year historical baseline, inserts to rainfall_observations with anomaly_z_score. Feature Engineering: joins current-day WASH inputs with 7-day rolling averages and nearest historical outbreak context per camp, produces feature matrix. ML Inference: loads serialized gradient boosting model from GCS, runs batch inference on feature matrix, assigns risk_tier (low/elevated/high/critical) and risk_score per camp, identifies top 3 contributing features via model explain output. Alert Dispatch: queries risk_scores for camps at elevated or above tier, generates intervention_recommendations citing driving indicators, publishes Pub/Sub messages consumed by WASH coordinator notification integration.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Scheduler (daily cron at 04:00 UTC triggering ingest Cloud Run Job). Prefect Cloud (flow run orchestration, task retry management, failure alerting to Slack). Cloud Run Job: ingest (Python tasks for water quality, sanitation, weather parallel fetch; sa-ingest service account). Cloud Run Job: engineer-infer (feature engineering + batch inference; sa-inference service account). Cloud Run Job: alert (risk score query, recommendation generation, Pub/Sub publish; sa-alerting service account). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (displacement_camps, water_quality_tests, sanitation_ratios, rainfall_observations, historical_outbreaks, risk_scores, intervention_recommendations tables). GCS wash-pipeline-raw (daily ingestion archives). GCS wash-ml-artifacts (gradient boosting model .pkl, feature scaler .pkl, model version manifest). Secret Manager (weather API key). Pub/Sub topic wash-risk-alerts (consumed by WASH coordinator Slack integration). Prometheus push gateway (pipeline run metrics). Grafana (risk tier distribution dashboards).
target_output.workflows: Daily Pipeline Run: Cloud Scheduler fires at 04:00 UTC, triggers ingest Cloud Run Job. Prefect flow starts; three parallel tasks execute: fetch_water_quality_tests (calls field monitoring API, validates records, archives to GCS, inserts to water_quality_tests), fetch_sanitation_ratios (reads partner SFTP export, computes ratios, inserts to sanitation_ratios), fetch_rainfall_data (calls weather API with camp coordinates, computes 30-day z-score, inserts to rainfall_observations). Ingest tasks complete; engineer-infer Cloud Run Job starts. feature_engineer task joins all three sources for each camp using the most recent available record per indicator within a 48-hour recency window, computes 7-day rolling averages, joins nearest historical outbreak features, produces camp_features DataFrame. run_inference task loads gradient_boosting_model.pkl and feature_scaler.pkl from GCS, applies scaler transform, runs model.predict_proba on camp_features, assigns risk_tier by probability threshold (critical >0.85, high 0.65-0.85, elevated 0.40-0.65, low <0.40), extracts top-3 feature importances per camp, writes risk_scores rows. Alert Dispatch: alert Cloud Run Job queries risk_scores where score_date = today and risk_tier in (elevated, high, critical), generates intervention_recommendation text referencing top indicator drivers (e.g., 'e_coli_per_100ml exceeded 10 CFU/100ml threshold for 3 consecutive days at Camp X'), inserts intervention_recommendations rows, publishes Pub/Sub message per camp to wash-risk-alerts topic. Stale Data Handling: if fetch_weather_data fails after 3 retries, engineer_features task uses previous day's rainfall_observations for affected camps and sets stale_weather=true flag in the risk_scores row.
target_output.data_model: displacement_camps (id UUID PK, camp_name VARCHAR, admin_region VARCHAR, country VARCHAR, latitude FLOAT, longitude FLOAT, population_estimate INT, last_updated TIMESTAMPTZ). water_quality_tests (id UUID PK, camp_id UUID FK, test_date DATE, turbidity_ntu FLOAT, e_coli_per_100ml FLOAT, chlorine_residual_mg_l FLOAT, sample_source ENUM borehole/surface/trucked, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). sanitation_ratios (id UUID PK, camp_id UUID FK, survey_date DATE, latrines_count INT, population_served INT, latrines_per_1000 FLOAT, open_defecation_rate_pct FLOAT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). rainfall_observations (id UUID PK, camp_id UUID FK, observation_date DATE, rainfall_mm FLOAT, anomaly_z_score FLOAT, data_source VARCHAR, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). historical_outbreaks (id UUID PK, camp_id UUID FK, onset_date DATE, peak_date DATE, total_cases INT, case_fatality_rate FLOAT, primary_water_source VARCHAR, confirmed BOOL). risk_scores (id UUID PK, camp_id UUID FK, score_date DATE, risk_tier ENUM low/elevated/high/critical, risk_score FLOAT, top_risk_factors JSONB, stale_weather BOOL DEFAULT false, model_version VARCHAR, pipeline_run_id UUID, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). intervention_recommendations (id UUID PK, risk_score_id UUID FK, recommendation_text TEXT, indicator_drivers JSONB, dispatched_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Retention: water_quality_tests and sanitation_ratios retained 36 months via scheduled DELETE job. risk_scores retained 60 months. No personal identifiers in any table by schema design. Health surveillance entities: patient_case_reports (id UUID PK, camp_id FK, report_date DATE, suspected_cholera_cases INT, confirmed_cholera_cases INT, clinical_severity ENUM mild/moderate/severe, treatment_center_id UUID, case_fatality_rate NUMERIC, reported_by_health_worker_id UUID), clinical_treatments (id UUID PK, case_report_id FK, treatment_type ENUM oral_rehydration/iv_fluids/antibiotics, patients_treated INT, treatment_center_id UUID, health_worker_id UUID, administered_date DATE).
target_output.api_design: No external API. Pipeline produces outputs to PostgreSQL tables and Pub/Sub. WASH coordinators query risk_scores and intervention_recommendations via PostgreSQL connections from their BI tools. Internal pipeline invocation: Cloud Scheduler HTTP target triggers Cloud Run Job via Cloud Run Admin API. Prefect REST API (Prefect Cloud) provides flow run management; not exposed to end users.
target_output.security_and_compliance: GCP service account least privilege: sa-ingest has storage.objectCreator on wash-pipeline-raw, cloudsql.client on raw ingestion tables, secretmanager.secretAccessor on wash-weather-api-key only. sa-inference has storage.objectViewer on wash-ml-artifacts, cloudsql.client on feature tables and risk_scores. sa-alerting has cloudsql.client read on risk_scores, pubsub.publisher on wash-risk-alerts. No service account has project-level IAM roles. GCP KMS default encryption for Cloud SQL and GCS. TLS 1.3 for weather API calls. No personal identifiers in ingestion schema; water quality tests stored at camp aggregate level. Pipeline run logs exclude camp resident counts. GDPR: no personal data in pipeline by design; WHO health data standards applied to WASH indicator definitions. Compliance_context: organizational data governance policy for camp health data requires data access log maintained in Cloud Audit Logs; enabled at project level for Cloud SQL and GCS.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitLab CI pipeline on merge to main: pytest unit tests with 80% coverage gate covering feature engineering and inference modules with mock GCS and PostgreSQL, docker build multi-stage image (build stage installs deps, runtime stage copies wheel), push to Artifact Registry, Cloud Run Job update-traffic deploy for each of the three stage jobs. Model artifact promotion: separate manual GitLab CI job requiring WASH data lead approval via GitLab protected environment; copies model .pkl from staging GCS path to production wash-ml-artifacts bucket. Environments: dev (Cloud Run Jobs using dev Cloud SQL instance, dry-run mode skips Pub/Sub publish), production (Cloud Scheduler at 04:00 UTC, full pipeline). Pipeline run monitoring: Prefect Cloud flow run page shows task-level status and duration; Slack webhook notified on flow run failure. Rollback: Cloud Run Job revision pinning allows revert to previous container image via gcloud run jobs update; previous model artifact retained in GCS for rollback via manual promotion job.
target_output.observability: Prefect Cloud flow run history: task durations, retry counts, failure tracebacks per daily run. Prometheus push gateway receives metrics at end of each stage: pipeline_ingest_records_total by source and camp_id_count, feature_engineer_camps_with_complete_features, inference_duration_seconds, risk_tier_distribution{tier=low/elevated/high/critical} camp counts, stale_weather_camps_count, alerts_dispatched_total. Grafana dashboards: daily risk tier heatmap by country and camp, 30-day trend of elevated/high/critical camp counts, data freshness per source (days since last record per camp), pipeline run duration trend. Alert: Grafana fires if stale_weather_camps_count exceeds 50 in a daily run or if risk_tier critical count increases by more than 20% day-over-day (potential outbreak cluster signal).
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Run Jobs (3 stage jobs, ~1 hour total runtime daily): approximately $15/month. Cloud SQL db-g1-small: $25/month. GCS storage (raw archives + model artifacts): $5/month. Cloud Scheduler, Pub/Sub, Secret Manager, Artifact Registry: $5/month. Prefect Cloud free tier (5 workspaces). Prometheus push gateway on existing Grafana instance: no additional cost. Total approximately $50/month, well under $400 constraint. Scaling: if camp count grows from 500 to 2000, Cloud Run Job memory limit (2GB) accommodates larger pandas DataFrames; Cloud SQL db-n1-standard-1 upgrade at $50/month handles increased query volume.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - MVP Pipeline (4 weeks): PostgreSQL schema and migrations, water quality and sanitation ingestion tasks, rainfall anomaly integration, feature engineering module with 7-day rolling averages, ML inference with pre-trained gradient boosting model loaded from GCS, risk_tier assignment, Pub/Sub alert dispatch, GitLab CI pipeline, Cloud Run Job deployment. Phase 2 - WASH Coordinator Validation (2 weeks): parallel run generating risk scores in dev environment, WASH coordinator review sessions comparing model outputs to expert assessments, threshold calibration adjustments, intervention recommendation text templates reviewed by WASH specialists. Phase 3 - Production Cutover and Observability (2 weeks): production Cloud Scheduler activation, Grafana dashboard rollout to WASH team, Prefect Slack alerting configuration, stale data handling validation, documentation of pipeline data lineage for organizational data governance record.
target_output.development_guidelines: Python 3.12 with type annotations on all pipeline functions. Feature engineering module unit-tested with synthetic camp DataFrames covering edge cases: missing water quality records, zero sanitation survey coverage, no historical outbreak data for new camps. Inference module tested with a minimal model fixture loaded from a test GCS bucket path. No raw SQL strings in pipeline code; all PostgreSQL writes use psycopg2 parameterized queries. GCS paths defined as constants in pipeline/config.py keyed by environment (dev/production). Data schema changes require a corresponding Alembic migration and WASH data lead approval before merge to main.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Gradient boosting model performance degrades as camp population composition changes over time. Mitigation: model_version field in risk_scores enables retrospective performance analysis; retraining pipeline specified in future scope with historical outbreak labels as training signal. Risk: Weather API rate limits during parallel camp coordinate queries cause ingestion failures. Mitigation: batch camp coordinates in groups of 50 per API call and add 10-second sleep between batches to stay within rate limits. Risk: Stale sanitation ratio data (surveys conducted quarterly) produces lagged features between survey cycles. Mitigation: sanitation_ratios recency window extended to 90 days in feature engineering; stale_sanitation flag added to risk_scores if most recent survey >60 days old. Tradeoff: scikit-learn batch inference over a real-time serving endpoint reduces operational complexity at the cost of latency; acceptable for a daily batch pipeline. Tradeoff: Cloud SQL single instance over Spanner chosen for simplicity and cost; must be revisited if camp count scales to 10000+.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Confirmed risk tier thresholds (critical >0.85, high 0.65-0.85, elevated 0.40-0.65) with WASH domain specialists based on historical outbreak onset probability distributions. Decided against real-time streaming in MVP in favor of daily batch to reduce infrastructure complexity and cost. Selected Cloud Run Jobs over Cloud Composer (Airflow) to avoid $400/month Composer minimum cost. GCS model artifact path with manual promotion step chosen over SageMaker to stay within GCP stack and budget.

metadata.notes: Cholera outbreak risk forecasting pipeline integrating WASH indicators; Prefect on Cloud Run Jobs with per-stage least-privilege service accounts.

project_goal.value: Migrate legacy SOAP-based interoperability layer for humanitarian cluster coordination to a modern RESTful architecture enabling partner organizations to submit and query sector activity reports across food security, health, and WASH clusters
project_goal.rationale: Legacy SOAP layer creates integration friction and blocks partner onboarding

target_users.value: Partner NGOs and UN agencies consuming cluster coordination data programmatically via API
target_users.rationale: Primary consumers identified in partner onboarding discovery

project_class.rationale: Pure API layer with no frontend; exposes REST endpoints to partner systems

capabilities.rationale: REST API with JWT auth, partner-facing public API surface, and SOAP adapter integration layer

complexity_level.rationale: Standard REST migration with backward-compat SOAP adapter and schema validation

risk_level.rationale: Partner-facing API with internal cluster data; no PHI but sensitive coordination data

data_sensitivity.rationale: Cluster activity reports are inter-organizational coordination data, not personal data

external_exposure.rationale: API consumed by vetted partner organizations only, not public internet

access_model.value: JWT tokens issued per partner organization with scoped permissions per cluster sector
access_model.rationale: Partners need sector-scoped access so food security partners cannot read WASH-restricted data

feature_scope.value: REST endpoints for activity report submission, report querying with filtering, migration adapter for legacy SOAP clients, partner organization management, sector schema validation
feature_scope.rationale: Full scope for phased SOAP sunset

mvp_scope.value: REST submission and query endpoints for food security and health clusters with JWT auth; SOAP adapter for backward compatibility during migration
mvp_scope.rationale: Two highest-traffic clusters unblock the majority of partners for Q2 cutover

security_baseline.value: JWT with RS256 signing, TLS 1.3 for all endpoints, partner API key rotation every 90 days, rate limiting per client ID
security_baseline.rationale: Standard partner API security controls

frontend_stack.value: none - API service only
frontend_stack.rationale: No UI required; consumers are partner system integrations

backend_stack.rationale: Low latency, strong concurrency for partner API calls, easy deployment to Cloud Run

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with Redis 7 for response caching
data_platform.rationale: Relational model fits cluster reporting schema; Redis caches hot query results

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run with automatic scaling
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless containers reduce ops overhead for a moderate-traffic API

privacy_retention_policy.value: Activity reports retained indefinitely for coordination history; migration logs purged after 12 months post-cutover
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Cluster coordination history required for program continuity; migration logs are transient

future_scope.value: WASH cluster onboarding, GraphQL query layer for advanced reporting, webhook push notifications for new report submissions
future_scope.rationale: Post-migration roadmap to expand cluster coverage and push-based integrations

constraints.value: Must maintain SOAP backward compatibility for 18 months to allow phased partner migration; budget under $800/month on GCP
constraints.rationale: Partner migration timeline and operational cost constraints

observability_baseline.value: Google Cloud Monitoring with custom metrics for report submission rate, SOAP adapter usage, and per-cluster query latency
observability_baseline.rationale: GCP-native observability aligned with hosting platform

execution_preference.value: Iterative delivery with cluster-by-cluster cutover milestones
execution_preference.rationale: Phased migration reduces risk of simultaneous partner disruption

project.goal: RESTful cluster activity reporting API replacing legacy SOAP layer
project.target_users: Partner NGOs and UN agency integration systems

backend.api: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.0 spec and SOAP adapter shim

security.auth: JWT RS256 with partner-scoped claims

reasoner_reviews.product: The phased cluster-by-cluster migration approach is correct given the diverse partner ecosystem. Starting with food security and health clusters validates the REST API under real load before onboarding lower-traffic WASH partners. Report query filtering must support date range, cluster sector, and partner organization to meet partner dashboarding needs.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Go with Chi on Cloud Run is a strong fit for this workload. The SOAP adapter shim should be a separate Chi handler group under /soap/v1/ that translates incoming XML envelopes to the internal Go structs before routing through the same service layer as REST requests. Redis caching of repeated report queries should use a 5-minute TTL to balance freshness with performance.
reasoner_reviews.security: JWT tokens must encode cluster_sector claims to enforce sector-scoped data access. A partner from the food security cluster must receive 403 on WASH-restricted report queries. RS256 asymmetric signing allows partners to validate tokens without sharing the private key. Partner key rotation at 90 days should be enforced via a key expiry field in the partner_organizations table.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Cloud Run auto-scaling handles burst partner query load without reserved capacity cost. Budget of $800/month is achievable with Cloud Run request-based billing and a single Cloud SQL PostgreSQL instance with no read replica at this scale.

backend.service_design: Single Go service with domain packages for cluster_reports, partner_auth, soap_adapter, and schema_validation. Chi router mounts REST handlers at /api/v1/ and SOAP adapter handlers at /soap/v1/. Shared service layer processes both request types uniformly.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.0. POST /api/v1/reports submits a new cluster activity report. GET /api/v1/reports filters by cluster_sector, submitting_partner_id, date_from, date_to, and status. SOAP adapter translates SubmitActivityReport and QueryReports operations to internal service calls.
backend.failure_handling: Idempotency keys on POST /api/v1/reports prevent duplicate submissions. Dead letter logging for SOAP adapter parse failures. Circuit breaker on PostgreSQL write path with queue-based retry for transient DB errors.
backend.scaling_notes: Cloud Run scales to zero between low-traffic windows overnight. Maximum concurrency set to 80 per instance to avoid PostgreSQL connection pool exhaustion. Redis connection pooling with max 20 connections shared across goroutines.

security.auth_design: JWT RS256 tokens issued by internal token endpoint POST /auth/token with client_id and client_secret. Token payload includes partner_id, cluster_scopes[], and exp. Tokens expire after 1 hour with refresh support.
security.authorization_model: Cluster-scoped RBAC. Each partner_organization record has an authorized_sectors JSON array. Middleware validates JWT cluster_scopes against the requested resource sector on every report endpoint call.
security.secrets_management: Private RS256 signing key stored in GCP Secret Manager. Cloud Run service account has secretAccessor role. Keys rotated annually with dual-key overlap period.
security.security_risks: SOAP adapter exposes additional XML parse attack surface; enforce strict XML size limits of 5MB and schema validation before processing. Rate limiting must apply equally to SOAP and REST entry points.

data.entities: cluster_activities (id UUID PK, report_id FK, cluster_sector ENUM food_security/health/wash, submitting_partner_id FK, activity_type, coverage_area, beneficiary_count, reporting_period, submitted_at). sector_reports (id UUID PK, external_report_id, cluster_sector, partner_id FK, raw_payload JSONB, validation_status ENUM pending/valid/rejected, created_at). partner_organizations (id UUID PK, name, authorized_sectors TEXT[], api_key_hash, key_expires_at, created_at). migration_logs (id UUID PK, legacy_soap_operation, request_hash, translation_status, error_detail, created_at).
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL with automated backups. Redis 7 via Cloud Memorystore for query result caching keyed by cluster_sector + partner_id + date_range hash.
data.retention_and_deletion: cluster_activities and sector_reports retained indefinitely. migration_logs deleted via scheduled job 12 months after migration cutover date flag is set.

devops.deployment_topology: Cloud Run service in us-central1 region. Cloud SQL PostgreSQL in same region. Cloud Memorystore Redis in same VPC. Cloud Build triggers on main branch push. Artifact Registry stores Docker images.
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build pipeline: go vet, staticcheck lint, go test with 80% coverage gate, docker build, push to Artifact Registry, deploy to Cloud Run with --no-traffic flag for staging validation before traffic shift.
devops.environments: dev (Cloud Run min-instances 0), staging (mirrors production config), production (min-instances 1 to avoid cold starts on morning partner batch jobs).
devops.observability_stack: Cloud Monitoring dashboards for requests/sec per cluster_sector, SOAP adapter invocation rate, report submission success rate, Redis cache hit ratio, PostgreSQL query latency p95.

ISS-001.title: SOAP adapter lacks XML size validation allowing oversized payloads
ISS-001.detail: The round 1 plan described the SOAP adapter shim but did not specify any input size or schema validation controls, leaving the adapter vulnerable to XML bomb and oversized payload attacks from partner systems.

history.note: Identified during security review of SOAP adapter design

ISS-002.title: Redis cache TTL not defined for cluster report queries
ISS-002.detail: Round 1 plan mentioned Redis caching for report queries but did not specify TTL values, cache invalidation strategy on new report submissions, or cache key design, making cache behavior unpredictable in production.

history.note: Identified in data specialist review

focus_issues.title: SOAP adapter lacks XML size validation allowing oversized payloads

focus_issues.title: Redis cache TTL not defined for cluster report queries
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan mentioned Redis caching but did not specify TTL, invalidation, or cache key design.

latest_recommendations: Add explicit XML size limit and XSD schema validation to the SOAP adapter before processing
Define Redis TTL values, cache key schema, and invalidation triggers for report query cache
