previous_plan.title: Cluster Coordination REST API Migration - Round 1
previous_plan.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 REST API on GCP Cloud Run replacing the legacy SOAP interoperability layer for humanitarian cluster coordination. Partners submit and query food security, health, and WASH activity reports via JWT-authenticated REST endpoints during an 18-month parallel-run migration period.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Go Chi service on Cloud Run with PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL and Redis caching. SOAP adapter shim handles legacy client traffic at /soap/v1/ translating XML envelopes to internal Go structs. JWT RS256 authentication with cluster-scoped claims enforces sector data isolation between partners.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: JWT RS256 with cluster_scopes claims, TLS 1.3, partner key rotation every 90 days, rate limiting 100 req/min per client_id. SOAP adapter handles legacy requests during migration period.
previous_plan.data_model: cluster_activities, sector_reports, partner_organizations, migration_logs tables in PostgreSQL 16. Redis caches query results by cluster_sector and partner_id.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 2 revision focused on resolving ISS-001 (SOAP XML validation gap) and ISS-002 (Redis cache definition gap). Adding explicit XML size enforcement, XSD schema validation, and a detailed Redis TTL and invalidation strategy. The core Go Chi architecture on Cloud Run is sound and needs no structural changes — targeted security and caching fixes will close the blocking gaps.

fix_report.action_taken: Added explicit 5MB XML size limit enforced by Chi middleware before SOAP handler processing via http.MaxBytesReader, plus XSD schema validation for SubmitActivityReport and QueryReports operations using Go's encoding/xml with a pre-compiled schema definition. Oversized or malformed XML returns HTTP 400 with a structured error response before any internal processing.
fix_report.expected_outcome: SOAP adapter is protected against XML bomb and oversized payload attacks; ISS-001 resolved

fix_report.action_taken: Defined Redis cache key schema as cluster_reports:{cluster_sector}:{partner_id}:{date_from}:{date_to}:{page}, set 5-minute TTL for query result caches, and added explicit cache invalidation on successful POST /api/v1/reports calls that invalidates all keys matching cluster_reports:{cluster_sector}:* for the submitted report's sector.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Cache behavior is predictable, report queries return fresh data within 5 minutes of new submissions; ISS-002 resolved

target_output.title: Cluster Coordination REST API Migration
target_output.executive_summary: A Go 1.22 REST API on GCP Cloud Run replacing the legacy SOAP interoperability layer for humanitarian cluster coordination. Partner NGOs and UN agencies authenticate with cluster-scoped JWTs to submit and query food security, health, and WASH sector activity reports over REST while a backward-compatible SOAP adapter shim supports legacy clients through the 18-month migration period. This revision adds hardened SOAP input validation and a defined Redis caching strategy addressing the two blocking gaps from round 1.
target_output.architecture_overview: Stateless Go 1.22 service with Chi router deployed to GCP Cloud Run in us-central1. REST handlers mount at /api/v1/ and a SOAP adapter handler group mounts at /soap/v1/ sharing the same internal service and repository layers. PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL stores cluster_activities, sector_reports, partner_organizations, and migration_logs. Redis 7 on Cloud Memorystore caches paginated report query results with 5-minute TTL and pattern-based invalidation on new submissions. JWT RS256 middleware enforces cluster-scoped authorization before any handler executes. All SOAP requests pass through a Chi middleware chain that enforces a 5MB body size limit and XSD schema validation before reaching the adapter logic.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Go 1.22, Chi v5 router, golang-migrate for schema migrations, encoding/xml with XSD validation for SOAP parsing. Data: PostgreSQL 16 on Cloud SQL, Redis 7 on Cloud Memorystore. Auth: custom JWT RS256 using go-jose/v3. Infrastructure: GCP Cloud Run, Cloud Build CI, Artifact Registry, Secret Manager for signing key. Observability: Google Cloud Monitoring with custom metrics, Cloud Logging with structured JSON.
target_output.functional_feature_map: REST Report Submission: POST /api/v1/reports accepts JSON activity report for a specified cluster_sector, validates against cluster reporting schema, persists to sector_reports and cluster_activities, and returns a report_id. REST Report Query: GET /api/v1/reports with filters for cluster_sector, submitting_partner_id, date_from, date_to returns paginated cluster_activity records from Redis cache or PostgreSQL. SOAP Adapter: Translates SubmitActivityReport and QueryReports SOAP operations to internal service calls with full XML size and schema validation. Partner Auth: POST /auth/token issues RS256 JWT with cluster_scopes for authenticated partner organizations. Partner Management: Admin endpoints to register partners, update authorized_sectors, and trigger API key rotation.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Run service (Go 1.22 binary, Chi router, 256MB RAM, max-concurrency 80). Cloud SQL PostgreSQL 16 (db-g1-small, automated daily backups). Cloud Memorystore Redis 7 (1GB, in-VPC access). Artifact Registry (Docker image store). Secret Manager (RS256 private key, DB credentials). Cloud Build (CI/CD pipeline). Chi SOAP middleware (5MB body limit via http.MaxBytesReader, XSD schema validation before handler dispatch). JWT RS256 middleware (token parse, cluster_scopes claim validation per requested resource sector).
target_output.workflows: Report Submission (REST): Partner POSTs to /api/v1/reports with JWT. Chi JWT middleware validates token and checks cluster_scopes matches requested sector. Report service validates JSON payload against cluster reporting schema. Repository writes to sector_reports and cluster_activities tables. Cache invalidation deletes Redis keys matching cluster_reports:{sector}:* for the submitted sector. Response returns 201 with report_id and submitted_at. Report Submission (SOAP Legacy): Partner POSTs XML envelope to /soap/v1/. Chi body-limit middleware enforces 5MB cap, returning 400 if exceeded. XSD validator checks envelope structure against SubmitActivityReport schema, returning fault on mismatch. Adapter translates validated XML to internal CreateReportRequest struct and routes through same report service as REST path. Migration log entry written with request_hash and translation_status. Report Query (REST): Partner GETs /api/v1/reports with filter params. Cache key computed as cluster_reports:{sector}:{partner_id}:{date_from}:{date_to}:{page}. Redis cache checked first; hit returns cached JSON. Miss queries PostgreSQL with parameterized filters, result serialized to JSON and SET in Redis with 5-minute TTL, then returned to caller.
target_output.data_model: cluster_activities (id UUID PK, report_id UUID FK, cluster_sector ENUM food_security/health/wash, submitting_partner_id UUID FK, activity_type VARCHAR, coverage_area VARCHAR, beneficiary_count INT, reporting_period DATE, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ). sector_reports (id UUID PK, external_report_id VARCHAR UNIQUE, cluster_sector ENUM, partner_id UUID FK, raw_payload JSONB, validation_status ENUM pending/valid/rejected, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). partner_organizations (id UUID PK, name VARCHAR, authorized_sectors TEXT[], api_key_hash VARCHAR, key_expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). migration_logs (id UUID PK, legacy_soap_operation VARCHAR, request_hash VARCHAR, translation_status ENUM success/failed/skipped, error_detail TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ). Redis key pattern: cluster_reports:{cluster_sector}:{partner_id}:{date_from}:{date_to}:{page} with 5-min TTL.
target_output.api_design: POST /auth/token (client_id, client_secret) returns RS256 JWT with cluster_scopes[]. POST /api/v1/reports (Authorization: Bearer, body: {cluster_sector, activity_type, coverage_area, beneficiary_count, reporting_period}) returns 201 {report_id, submitted_at}. GET /api/v1/reports?cluster_sector=food_security&partner_id=&date_from=&date_to=&page= returns 200 {data: [...], total, page}. GET /api/v1/reports/{id} returns single cluster_activity. POST /soap/v1/ accepts SOAP 1.1 XML envelope with SubmitActivityReport or QueryReports operations. POST /admin/partners registers new partner. PATCH /admin/partners/{id}/rotate-key triggers API key rotation.
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT RS256 (go-jose/v3): tokens encode partner_id and cluster_scopes[]; middleware returns 403 if requested sector not in scopes. SOAP input hardening: Chi middleware uses http.MaxBytesReader(5MB) before SOAP handler; XSD validation via encoding/xml rejects malformed envelopes with SOAP fault response. TLS 1.3 enforced by Cloud Run load balancer; no plain HTTP accepted. Rate limiting: 100 req/min per client_id via token bucket in Redis. API key rotation: partner key_expires_at checked on every token issuance; expired keys return 401 with rotation_required error code. RS256 private key stored in Secret Manager; Cloud Run service account holds secretAccessor role only. Partner API keys stored as bcrypt hashes in partner_organizations table.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Build pipeline on main branch push: go vet, staticcheck, go test -race -coverprofile with 80% gate, docker build --platform linux/amd64, push to Artifact Registry, deploy to Cloud Run with --no-traffic for smoke test, traffic shift to 100% on health check pass. Environments: dev (Cloud Run min-instances 0, allow unauthenticated for integration tests), staging (mirrors production secrets via Secret Manager, min-instances 0), production (min-instances 1 to eliminate cold start latency for morning partner batch jobs, max-instances 10). Database migrations run as Cloud Build step via golang-migrate against Cloud SQL proxy before service deploy. Rollback: Cloud Run traffic revert to previous revision via gcloud run services update-traffic in under 60 seconds.
target_output.observability: Google Cloud Monitoring custom metrics: cluster_report_submissions_total by cluster_sector and validation_status, soap_adapter_requests_total by translation_status, redis_cache_hits_ratio for report query path, postgresql_query_latency_p95 for report submission writes. Structured JSON logs to Cloud Logging with fields: partner_id, cluster_sector, report_id, request_duration_ms, cache_hit bool. Alerting: Cloud Monitoring alert if soap_adapter_requests_total with translation_status=failed exceeds 5% of total SOAP volume in a 15-minute window, or if p95 write latency exceeds 500ms.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Run request-based billing at expected 50K reports/month totals approximately $15/month. Cloud SQL db-g1-small at $25/month. Cloud Memorystore Redis 1GB at $35/month. Artifact Registry and Secret Manager negligible. Total estimated $80-100/month, well under $800/month constraint. Scaling: Cloud Run auto-scales to 10 instances under peak partner batch submission load with no manual intervention. Redis 1GB cache handles all active report query result sets with room to spare at current volume.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 - REST API MVP (6 weeks): REST submission and query endpoints for food security and health clusters, JWT RS256 auth with cluster_scopes, PostgreSQL schema and golang-migrate setup, Redis query caching with 5-minute TTL and invalidation, Cloud Run and Cloud Build CI/CD pipeline. Phase 2 - SOAP Adapter and Migration (4 weeks): SOAP adapter shim at /soap/v1/ with 5MB size limit and XSD validation, migration_logs table, partner onboarding tooling for key issuance, WASH cluster schema addition, parallel-run testing with legacy SOAP clients. Phase 3 - Partner Migration Support (8 weeks ongoing): Per-partner migration assistance and integration testing, SOAP deprecation notices at 12-month mark, monitoring dashboards for SOAP adapter usage decline, OpenAPI spec publication for partner developer portals.
target_output.development_guidelines: Go modules with explicit dependency pinning in go.sum. All handlers unit-tested with httptest, repository layer tested against a dockerized PostgreSQL in Cloud Build. SOAP adapter tested with captured real legacy XML payloads from the existing system. 80% coverage gate enforced in CI. Structured logging via zerolog with partner_id and cluster_sector fields on every log line. OpenAPI 3.0 spec generated via swaggo/swag and published to internal developer portal on every main branch deploy. No dynamic SQL; all queries use parameterized pgx statements.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Legacy SOAP partners may not migrate within 18-month window. Mitigation: Migration logs track per-partner SOAP usage to identify stalled partners early; escalation path to cluster coordinators for non-compliant partners. Risk: Cache staleness under high submission volume in a single sector. Mitigation: Pattern-based Redis key invalidation on every successful submission ensures queries return fresh data within 5 minutes maximum. Tradeoff: Single Cloud SQL instance (no read replica) saves $25/month but means report queries hit the primary DB on cache miss; acceptable at current partner volume but must be revisited if partner count doubles. Risk: XSD validation overhead on SOAP path adds latency. Mitigation: Pre-compile XSD schema at service startup rather than parsing on each request; adds less than 2ms overhead per SOAP request.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected custom JWT RS256 over GCP Identity Platform to avoid per-MAU pricing at low partner counts. Chose Cloud Memorystore Redis over self-managed Redis to eliminate cluster management overhead on Cloud Run. WASH cluster deferred to Phase 2 to keep MVP delivery tight. XSD pre-compilation approach confirmed to resolve SOAP validation latency concern.

metadata.notes: SOAP-to-REST migration for humanitarian cluster coordination API; revision fixes SOAP XML validation and Redis cache definition gaps.

project_goal.value: Greenfield mobile app for displaced persons in transit camps to self-register service needs, access information about available aid services, and receive real-time notifications when their case status changes or appointments are scheduled
project_goal.rationale: Replaces paper-based registration queues with a self-service digital channel, reducing wait times and improving case visibility for displaced persons

target_users.value: Displaced persons in transit camps, including low-literacy users in multilingual contexts, and caseworkers who update case statuses and schedule appointments
target_users.rationale: Dual-sided: displaced persons self-register via mobile app; caseworkers update records via a separate admin interface

project_class.rationale: Primary interface is a mobile application distributed to displaced persons' smartphones or shared device kiosks in the camp

capabilities.value: frontend, backend, data, auth, realtime, integrations
capabilities.rationale: Requires mobile UI, FastAPI backend, PostgreSQL data persistence, Cognito auth, Firebase real-time push notifications, and integration with case management backend

complexity_level.rationale: Multilingual support, offline-capable mobile client, real-time push notifications, and case management integration add significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: App holds personal data of a vulnerable displaced population; data exposure or security failure could have serious protection implications

data_sensitivity.rationale: Holds names, household composition, nationality, displacement reason, service needs, and appointment data for a vulnerable population

external_exposure.rationale: Mobile app downloads from app stores and communicates over public internet in transit camp WiFi and mobile data environments

access_model.value: Displaced persons authenticate via Cognito with phone number + OTP (no password required for accessibility); caseworkers use Cognito with email + MFA; RBAC roles: displaced_person, caseworker, supervisor, admin
access_model.rationale: Phone number OTP authentication is accessible to low-literacy users without requiring them to remember passwords; MFA required for caseworkers with elevated data access

feature_scope.value: Multilingual self-registration form (household composition, primary needs, displacement history), service need submission, aid service catalog browsing, case status tracking, appointment viewing and confirmation, real-time push notifications for case updates and appointments, offline mode with background sync, caseworker admin web interface
feature_scope.rationale: Complete self-service digital aid service access lifecycle

mvp_scope.value: Multilingual self-registration (3 languages), household composition capture, case reference number generation, service need submission, push notifications for case status changes, offline data entry with sync
mvp_scope.rationale: Minimum to replace paper registration and provide case tracking to displaced persons in pilot camp

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3 for all API traffic, encryption at rest for personal data in PostgreSQL via RDS encryption, certificate pinning in Flutter app, OTP rate limiting, no PII in push notification payloads, JWT with 1-hour expiry
security_baseline.rationale: Vulnerable population data requires strong transport security; certificate pinning prevents MITM attacks on camp WiFi networks

frontend_stack.value: Flutter 3.22 with Dart; flutter_localizations for multilingual support
frontend_stack.rationale: Flutter provides cross-platform iOS/Android from single codebase; flutter_localizations with ARB files supports the required multilingual UI

backend_stack.value: Python 3.12 with FastAPI 0.111 and SQLAlchemy 2.x ORM
backend_stack.rationale: FastAPI async performance suitable for mobile API; SQLAlchemy 2.x with async support for non-blocking database access

data_platform.value: Amazon RDS PostgreSQL 16 with RDS encryption; Redis 7 via ElastiCache for session cache and notification deduplication
data_platform.rationale: Managed PostgreSQL for relational personal data with encryption at rest; Redis for fast session lookups and push notification deduplication

hosting_target.value: AWS ECS Fargate in eu-west-1 (EU data residency for GDPR); CloudFront for API edge caching of service catalog
hosting_target.rationale: EU data residency required for GDPR; Fargate removes EC2 management overhead; CloudFront caches the read-heavy service catalog API responses

privacy_retention_policy.value: Case records retained for 5 years per UNHCR data protection standards; right to erasure implemented via anonymization of personal fields (name, phone, household data) while preserving anonymized case statistics; push notification logs retained 90 days
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: UNHCR data protection framework mandates defined retention; GDPR right to erasure requires deletion pathway for EU nationals in the displaced population

future_scope.value: Integration with UNHCR proGres case management system, biometric identity verification for duplicate detection, in-app multilingual chatbot for aid service information queries
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2 integrations pending pilot success and data sharing agreement with UNHCR

constraints.value: Support 5 languages minimum (English, French, Arabic, Swahili, Somali); app must function with 2G mobile data speeds (<=50KB/s sync payloads); offline data entry for up to 48 hours without connectivity; accessible to low-literacy users with icon-based navigation
constraints.rationale: Transit camp connectivity is unreliable 2G; multilingual and accessibility requirements driven by target population demographics

observability_baseline.value: Datadog APM on ECS tasks; structured JSON logging with case_reference_id correlation; custom metrics: registration_completions_total, notification_delivery_rate, sync_success_rate, api_p99_latency_ms; PagerDuty for API error rate >2%
observability_baseline.rationale: Corporate monitoring standard; registration completion rate is the primary operational KPI for the deployment

execution_preference.value: Agile 2-week sprints; Flutter app released via TestFlight/Google Play internal track for pilot; ECS Fargate rolling deployments
execution_preference.rationale: Mobile release cadence requires internal track testing before public release; ECS rolling update for zero-downtime API deployments

compliance_context.value: GDPR for EU nationals in displaced population (lawful basis: vital interests Article 9(2)(c)); UNHCR data protection standards for all displaced persons regardless of nationality; WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility for multilingual low-literacy users; data minimization principle — collect only fields required for service delivery
compliance_context.rationale: Displaced populations include EU nationals triggering GDPR; UNHCR standards apply universally; accessibility is legally and ethically required for a vulnerable population

project.goal: Multilingual self-registration mobile app for displaced persons with real-time case status notifications and offline capability
project.target_users: Displaced persons in transit camps and caseworkers
project.timeline: MVP in 4 months, full feature set in 7 months

frontend.l10n: flutter_localizations with ARB files for EN/FR/AR/SW/SO
frontend.state: Riverpod 2.x for state management, Hive for local offline storage
frontend.push: Firebase Cloud Messaging via firebase_messaging plugin

backend.api: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1, JWT bearer auth via Cognito

security.auth: Cognito user pools with phone/OTP for displaced persons, email/MFA for caseworkers
security.encryption: RDS encryption at rest, TLS 1.3, certificate pinning in Flutter

data.local: Hive boxes on-device for offline data entry

reasoner_reviews.product: The self-registration workflow is the highest-impact MVP feature — replacing paper forms with a digital self-service experience will meaningfully reduce queue times in the camp. The case reference number generation on registration completion is critical: it gives displaced persons a trackable identifier for all subsequent interactions. Icon-based navigation with text labels is the right pattern for low-literacy users across all 5 supported languages.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Flutter with Riverpod and Hive is the right offline-first stack for this context. The key architectural decision is how offline form submissions are queued and synced: Hive should store pending self_registration_forms and service_requests as local-only records, and a background sync service should push them to the FastAPI backend when connectivity is restored. Conflict resolution policy must be defined — for new registrations, the server is authoritative and assigns the case_reference_number; local assignments are placeholders only. Certificate pinning in Flutter is critical given the untrusted camp WiFi network environment.
reasoner_reviews.security: Certificate pinning must be implemented via the http_certificate_pinning Flutter package or via network_security_config equivalents on Android and App Transport Security on iOS. OTP rate limiting on the Cognito auth flow is essential — without it, attackers on the camp network could enumerate phone numbers. Push notification payloads must not contain any PII; only case_reference_number and notification_type should be in the FCM payload, with the app fetching actual content from the API on receipt.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The 2G bandwidth constraint (<=50KB/s) means the sync payload for a full registration must be under 50KB. The self_registration_form JSON with household members should be kept below 20KB. The service catalog must be cached locally on the device and updated incrementally via ETags to avoid full re-downloads on every launch. The 48-hour offline capability means Hive must persist all pending actions across app restarts and background termination.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with domain routers: /registrations (self-registration submission, case reference generation), /service-requests (aid service need submission, status retrieval), /catalog (aid service catalog with ETag caching), /notifications (FCM token registration, notification history), /appointments (appointment listing, confirmation), /caseworker (caseworker admin routes for status updates and appointment scheduling)
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.1 spec; async SQLAlchemy 2.x session per request via dependency injection; Redis for session cache (JWT allowlist) and notification deduplication set (FCM message_id); CloudFront caches GET /catalog responses with 1-hour TTL and ETag-based conditional requests
backend.business_modules: CaseReferenceService: generates human-readable case reference numbers in format CAMP-{year}-{sequence:06d} using PostgreSQL sequence; NotificationService: validates FCM token registration, deduplicates notifications via Redis SET NX with 5-minute TTL, dispatches via Firebase Admin SDK; OfflineSyncService: handles batch sync endpoint accepting array of pending local actions with idempotency_key per action
backend.failure_handling: FastAPI global exception handler returns RFC 7807 Problem JSON; 409 Conflict for duplicate registration by phone_number; 429 Too Many Requests for OTP attempts (rate limited at 5 per 10 minutes); async background task for FCM dispatch failures with exponential backoff retry

frontend.app_structure: Flutter app with feature-based directory structure: features/registration (self-registration form flow), features/services (service catalog and request submission), features/case (case status and appointment tracking), features/notifications (FCM handling and notification inbox), features/auth (Cognito OTP flow), shared/widgets (icon-button navigation, language selector)
frontend.pages_and_flows: Onboarding (language selection, phone number entry, OTP verification), Self-Registration Form (household size, members list, primary needs checklist, displacement origin), Registration Confirmation (case_reference_number display with QR code for sharing), Service Catalog (categorized aid services with icon-based filtering), Service Request Submission, Case Status Timeline, Appointments List with confirmation action, Notification Inbox
frontend.state_management: Riverpod 2.x providers: AuthNotifier (Cognito session state), RegistrationFormNotifier (multi-step form state with Hive persistence between sessions), SyncQueueNotifier (pending local actions count and sync status), CaseStatusProvider (fetched on app foreground)
frontend.performance_notes: Service catalog images use compressed WebP at <=10KB per image; app bundle size optimized with flutter build --split-per-abi for Android; ARB localization files loaded lazily per selected locale

security.auth_design: Cognito user pool with two flows: displaced_person (phone_number attribute, CUSTOM_AUTH flow with OTP via SNS SMS, no password), caseworker (email attribute, USER_SRP_AUTH with TOTP MFA enforced). JWT access tokens 1-hour expiry, refresh tokens 30-day expiry with rotation.
security.authorization_model: FastAPI dependency inject_current_user decodes Cognito JWT and attaches user role from custom:role claim. displaced_person role can only access their own case (case.owner_cognito_sub == current_user.sub enforced in all case queries). caseworker role can read all cases in their assigned camp_id. supervisor role can access all camps.
security.abuse_prevention: OTP rate limiting: Lambda pre-auth trigger increments Redis counter per phone_number, returns NotAuthorizedException after 5 attempts in 10 minutes. Certificate pinning via Flutter http_certificate_pinning package pinning to CloudFront ACM certificate public key hash. No PII in FCM payload: only {case_reference_number, notification_type, notification_id}.
security.privacy_controls: GDPR erasure: DELETE /registrations/{case_ref}/personal-data anonymizes name, phone_number, household_members_json, displacement_reason while preserving anonymized case_reference, service_request_counts, and created_at for aggregate statistics. UNHCR data minimization: self-registration form collects only household_size, primary_needs[], and camp_arrival_date at registration; additional fields collected progressively by caseworkers.

data.entities: displaced_persons (id UUID PK, cognito_sub TEXT UNIQUE, case_reference TEXT UNIQUE, phone_number_hash TEXT, camp_id FK camps, household_size INT, primary_needs TEXT[], camp_arrival_date DATE, registration_status ENUM pending/active/closed, created_at DATETIME, anonymized_at DATETIME NULLABLE), household_members (id UUID PK, case_id FK displaced_persons, member_type ENUM adult/child/elder, age_range ENUM 0_5/6_17/18_59/60_plus, vulnerability_flags TEXT[]), service_requests (id UUID PK, case_id FK, service_category TEXT, description TEXT, priority ENUM urgent/standard, status ENUM submitted/in_progress/resolved/closed, submitted_at DATETIME, resolved_at DATETIME NULLABLE), aid_service_catalog (id UUID PK, category TEXT, name_i18n JSONB, description_i18n JSONB, availability ENUM available/limited/unavailable, camp_id FK, icon_asset_key TEXT, updated_at DATETIME), appointment_schedules (id UUID PK, case_id FK, service_type TEXT, scheduled_at DATETIME, location TEXT, confirmed_by_person BOOL, caseworker_id FK users, created_at DATETIME), push_notification_tokens (id UUID PK, case_id FK, fcm_token TEXT, platform ENUM android/ios, registered_at DATETIME), case_status_updates (id UUID PK, case_id FK, status TEXT, note TEXT, updated_by_caseworker_id FK users, updated_at DATETIME)
data.storage_design: RDS PostgreSQL 16 encrypted with AWS KMS; primary instance in eu-west-1a with Multi-AZ standby in eu-west-1b. Hive boxes on device: pending_registrations, pending_service_requests, cached_catalog, offline_appointments. Redis ElastiCache for JWT allowlist (SET with 1h TTL), OTP rate limit counters (INCR with 10m TTL), FCM deduplication set.
data.retention_and_deletion: case records: 5-year retention; GDPR erasure via anonymization of personal fields, anonymized_at timestamp set. push_notification_tokens: purged after 90 days of no FCM activity. case_status_updates: retained for full case lifetime as immutable audit trail.

devops.deployment_topology: ECS Fargate cluster in eu-west-1: FastAPI service (2 tasks min, 4 max, auto-scale on CPU 60%). ALB with HTTPS listener forwarding to FastAPI target group. CloudFront distribution in front of ALB: caches /catalog endpoint, passes JWT-authenticated routes through. RDS Multi-AZ in eu-west-1. ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster mode.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Flutter analyze + flutter test (unit tests, golden tests for registration flow screens), Python pytest with Testcontainers PostgreSQL, Docker build and ECR push, ECS rolling deployment. Mobile releases: Flutter build apk --split-per-abi, Flutter build ipa for TestFlight. Staging ECS deployment on main branch push, production deployment on semver release tags.
devops.observability_stack: Datadog APM sidecar on ECS tasks. CloudWatch Logs structured JSON with case_reference_id correlation field. Custom Datadog metrics: registration_completions_total (by camp_id), push_notification_delivery_success_rate, sync_batch_accepted_total, otp_failures_per_10min. Firebase Crashlytics in Flutter app for mobile crash reporting.
devops.rollback_strategy: ECS rolling deployment with health check on /health endpoint; automatic rollback on 3 consecutive failed health checks. Alembic migrations use reversible operations; rollback migration run before ECS task replacement on failed deployment.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a Flutter/FastAPI mobile app for a high-risk context: displaced persons on untrusted camp WiFi with 2G connectivity and low-literacy multilingual requirements. The critical design decisions are: (1) certificate pinning in Flutter to prevent MITM on camp networks, (2) Hive for offline-first data entry persisting across app restarts, (3) Cognito phone/OTP flow requiring no password for displaced persons, (4) FCM notifications containing no PII per security baseline, and (5) GDPR erasure via field anonymization rather than record deletion to preserve aggregate statistics. The service catalog must be cached locally with ETag-based incremental updates to function on 2G.
target_output.title: Displaced Persons Self-Registration Mobile Application Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A greenfield Flutter 3.22 mobile application for displaced persons in transit camps, backed by a Python 3.12 FastAPI service on AWS ECS Fargate in eu-west-1. Displaced persons authenticate via Cognito phone number OTP (no password required), complete a multilingual self-registration form offline, and receive a case reference number. The app provides icon-based navigation accessible to low-literacy users across 5 languages (English, French, Arabic, Swahili, Somali), tracks service requests and appointments, and receives real-time push notifications via Firebase Cloud Messaging when case status changes. All pending data entry is persisted locally in Hive boxes and synced to the backend within 48 hours. Personal data is stored in EU region (eu-west-1) with GDPR erasure capability.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two-tier architecture: Flutter 3.22 mobile app (iOS and Android) communicating over TLS 1.3 with certificate pinning to a FastAPI REST API on ECS Fargate behind an ALB, with CloudFront caching the read-heavy service catalog endpoints. The Flutter app stores pending registrations, service requests, and cached service catalog data in Hive boxes on device, enabling full offline functionality for up to 48 hours. A background sync Isolate in Flutter monitors connectivity and drains the pending actions queue to the backend via a batch sync endpoint with idempotency keys. Authentication uses two Cognito user pool flows: displaced persons use phone number with OTP via SNS SMS (CUSTOM_AUTH, no password), caseworkers use email with TOTP MFA (USER_SRP_AUTH). FCM handles push notifications with payloads containing only case_reference_number and notification_type — no PII in notification payloads. PostgreSQL 16 on RDS Multi-AZ in eu-west-1 stores all case data with KMS encryption at rest; Redis 7 handles JWT allowlisting, OTP rate limiting, and FCM deduplication.
target_output.technology_stack: Mobile: Flutter 3.22, Dart, Riverpod 2.x (state management), Hive 2.x (local storage), firebase_messaging (FCM push), http (REST client with certificate pinning), flutter_localizations with ARB files (EN/FR/AR/SW/SO), Firebase Crashlytics. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI 0.111, SQLAlchemy 2.x async, Alembic (migrations), Firebase Admin SDK (FCM dispatch). Auth: AWS Cognito user pools (phone OTP + email MFA flows). Database: RDS PostgreSQL 16 (KMS encrypted, Multi-AZ). Cache: ElastiCache Redis 7. Infra: ECS Fargate eu-west-1, ALB with WAF, CloudFront, ECR. CI: GitHub Actions. Observability: Datadog APM, Firebase Crashlytics, CloudWatch Logs.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Language Selection and Onboarding: first-launch language picker (5 languages with native script labels), phone number entry, Cognito OTP verification via SNS SMS, TOTP MFA enrollment for caseworkers. Self-Registration Form: multi-step form with household_size, household_members list (age_range, vulnerability_flags per member), primary_needs checklist (food/shelter/health/protection/education/legal), camp_arrival_date. Persisted to Hive as pending if offline; case_reference_number generated server-side on sync. Case Reference Display: QR code of case_reference_number for sharing with caseworkers; copyable text format. Service Catalog: categorized aid services with icon per category and availability badge; stored in Hive with ETag-based incremental updates. Service Request Submission: freetext description with category selection, priority toggle urgent/standard; queued in Hive if offline. Case Status Timeline: chronological list of case_status_updates with status badge and caseworker note. Appointments: list with date/time/location and confirm button; confirmation synced to backend. Notification Inbox: list of received notifications with case_reference_number, notification_type, and timestamp; fetched from API on app foreground. Push Notification: FCM delivers {case_reference_number, notification_type, notification_id}; app fetches detailed notification content from GET /notifications/{id} on receipt.
target_output.system_components: Flutter 3.22 mobile app (iOS/Android) with Hive local storage and offline sync Isolate. Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) for push notification delivery. AWS Cognito user pools (displaced_person phone-OTP pool, caseworker email-MFA pool). AWS ALB with WAF (OWASP rules, rate limiting). CloudFront distribution (service catalog caching with ETags). ECS Fargate cluster (FastAPI service, 2-4 tasks). RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ eu-west-1 (KMS encrypted). ElastiCache Redis 7 (JWT allowlist, OTP rate limits, FCM dedup). Firebase Admin SDK (server-side FCM dispatch). Datadog APM sidecar on ECS tasks. Firebase Crashlytics (Flutter crash reporting). GitHub Actions CI (Flutter tests, pytest, ECS deployment). AWS SNS (OTP SMS delivery via Cognito CUSTOM_AUTH Lambda trigger).
target_output.workflows: Displaced Person Self-Registration (Offline Start): User opens app in offline camp environment. Selects language on first launch. Enters phone number — app stores pending_otp_request in Hive since offline. When connectivity resumes, Cognito OTP Lambda trigger fires via SNS SMS, user enters OTP, Cognito issues access and refresh tokens. Registration form filled across multiple steps, persisted to Hive as pending_registration with local UUID placeholder as case_reference. On connectivity, sync Isolate POSTs to POST /registrations/batch with idempotency_key=local_uuid. FastAPI generates server-side case reference CAMP-2026-000342, persists displaced_persons record, returns case_reference. Flutter updates Hive box with server-assigned case_reference, displays QR code screen. Push Notification Case Status Update: Caseworker updates case status via admin interface — FastAPI CaseStatusUpdateService persists case_status_updates record, calls NotificationService.dispatch(case_id, notification_type='status_update'). NotificationService fetches FCM token from push_notification_tokens, checks Redis SET NX {fcm_token}:{message_id} for deduplication. Sends FCM message via Firebase Admin SDK with payload {case_reference_number: 'CAMP-2026-000342', notification_type: 'status_update', notification_id: '{uuid}'}. App receives FCM, displays notification, on tap fetches GET /notifications/{notification_id} to display human-readable status update in the selected language.
target_output.data_model: displaced_persons (id UUID PK, cognito_sub TEXT UNIQUE, case_reference TEXT UNIQUE, phone_number_hash TEXT NOT NULL, camp_id UUID FK camps, household_size SMALLINT, primary_needs TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}', camp_arrival_date DATE, registration_status TEXT CHECK(status IN ('pending','active','closed')), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(), anonymized_at TIMESTAMPTZ). household_members (id UUID PK, case_id UUID FK displaced_persons ON DELETE CASCADE, member_type TEXT CHECK(type IN ('adult','child','elder')), age_range TEXT CHECK(age_range IN ('0_5','6_17','18_59','60_plus')), vulnerability_flags TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}'). service_requests (id UUID PK, case_id UUID FK, service_category TEXT NOT NULL, description TEXT, priority TEXT CHECK(priority IN ('urgent','standard')), status TEXT CHECK(status IN ('submitted','in_progress','resolved','closed')), idempotency_key TEXT UNIQUE, submitted_at TIMESTAMPTZ, resolved_at TIMESTAMPTZ). aid_service_catalog (id UUID PK, category TEXT, name_i18n JSONB NOT NULL, description_i18n JSONB, availability TEXT CHECK(availability IN ('available','limited','unavailable')), camp_id UUID FK, icon_asset_key TEXT, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ). appointment_schedules (id UUID PK, case_id UUID FK, service_type TEXT, scheduled_at TIMESTAMPTZ, location TEXT, confirmed_by_person BOOL DEFAULT FALSE, caseworker_id UUID FK users). case_status_updates (id UUID PK, case_id UUID FK, status TEXT, note TEXT, updated_by_caseworker_id UUID FK users, updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL). push_notification_tokens (id UUID PK, case_id UUID FK, fcm_token TEXT UNIQUE, platform TEXT CHECK(platform IN ('android','ios')), registered_at TIMESTAMPTZ).
target_output.api_design: Base path /api/v1. POST /registrations (body: {local_uuid, household_size, primary_needs[], camp_arrival_date, household_members[], idempotency_key}) -> 201 {case_reference, case_id}. POST /registrations/batch (body: [{local_uuid, ...registration_fields}]) -> 207 Multi-Status. GET /cases/{case_reference}/status -> {status, status_updates[]}. POST /service-requests (body: {case_id, service_category, description, priority, idempotency_key}) -> 202. GET /catalog?camp_id={id} (CloudFront cached, ETag, Cache-Control: max-age=3600) -> [{id, category, name_i18n, availability, icon_asset_key}]. GET /appointments?case_id={id} -> [{scheduled_at, location, service_type, confirmed_by_person}]. POST /appointments/{id}/confirm -> 200. POST /notifications/tokens (body: {fcm_token, platform}) -> 201. GET /notifications/{id} -> {notification_type, case_reference, message_i18n, created_at}. POST /sync/batch (body: [{action_type, payload, idempotency_key}]) -> 207 with per-action success/failure. All routes require Authorization: Bearer {cognito_jwt} except GET /catalog.
target_output.security_and_compliance: Certificate pinning: Flutter http client configured with certificate pinning to CloudFront ACM certificate SHA-256 public key hash; prevents MITM attacks on untrusted camp WiFi. OTP rate limiting: Cognito CUSTOM_AUTH Lambda trigger calls Redis INCR phone:{number}:otp_attempts with 10-minute EXPIRE; rejects after 5 attempts with NotAuthorizedException. FCM payload minimization: NotificationService sends only {case_reference_number, notification_type, notification_id} — no names, no case details in FCM payload; content fetched from API on notification tap. JWT security: 1-hour access token expiry; Cognito refresh token rotation enabled; JWT allowlist in Redis (SET with 1h TTL) for server-side revocation. GDPR erasure: DELETE /cases/{ref}/personal-data endpoint sets anonymized_at, replaces phone_number_hash with 'ANONYMIZED', nulls name and household_members_json, preserves case_reference and aggregate stats for reporting. UNHCR data minimization: registration form collects only fields required for service matching. PostgreSQL RLS: case queries filtered by cognito_sub owner check in FastAPI dependency; caseworkers filtered by camp_id claim from JWT.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: ECS Fargate cluster in eu-west-1: FastAPI service on Python 3.12 slim Docker image, 2 tasks minimum scaling to 4 on CPU 60% target via Application Auto Scaling. ALB HTTPS listener (ACM certificate) with WAF (OWASP managed rule set, rate limit 200 req/min per IP). CloudFront distribution for /api/v1/catalog with 1-hour TTL and ETag-based conditional caching. RDS PostgreSQL 16 Multi-AZ: primary eu-west-1a, standby eu-west-1b, automated backup 35-day retention. ElastiCache Redis 7 cluster mode 2 shards for HA. GitHub Actions CI: flutter analyze + flutter test (unit and golden tests), pytest with Testcontainers PostgreSQL, Docker build + ECR push, ECS rolling deployment. Mobile releases: flutter build apk --split-per-abi to Google Play internal track; flutter build ipa to TestFlight; promote to production after 2-day internal testing period. Alembic migrations run as ECS one-off task before service deployment. Secrets in AWS Secrets Manager: database URL, Redis AUTH, Cognito credentials, Firebase service account JSON.
target_output.observability: Datadog APM: distributed traces on all FastAPI route handlers with case_reference_id in trace metadata. Firebase Crashlytics: Flutter crash reports with Dart stack traces, app version, and device info (no PII). CloudWatch Logs: structured JSON with fields: case_reference, camp_id, route, status_code, duration_ms. Custom Datadog metrics: registration_completions_total (labeled by camp_id), push_notification_delivery_rate (labeled by platform), sync_batch_actions_total, otp_success_rate (labeled by camp_id). Dashboards: registration funnel by camp (language_selected -> otp_verified -> form_submitted -> case_reference_issued), notification delivery rate by platform, sync queue depth per ECS task. PagerDuty: error_rate > 2% on /registrations endpoint, p99_latency > 3000ms, ElastiCache CPU > 80%, RDS connection count > 80% of max.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: ECS Fargate 2 tasks (1 vCPU, 2 GB each): ~$70/month baseline. RDS PostgreSQL 16 db.t3.medium Multi-AZ: ~$140/month. ElastiCache Redis r6g.large cluster: ~$150/month. ALB + WAF: ~$50/month. CloudFront: ~$20/month for catalog traffic. FCM: free tier covers 100M push/month. Total ~$430/month for 10,000 registered displaced persons. Scale to 100,000 persons: RDS upgrade to db.r6g.large (~$350/month), ECS scale to 6 tasks (~$210/month), Redis add shard (~+$150); total ~$850/month.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (16 weeks): Cognito phone OTP auth, multilingual self-registration form (5 languages) with offline Hive persistence, case reference generation, FCM push notification for case status changes, basic service catalog (cached locally with ETag), sync batch endpoint with idempotency. ECS Fargate deployment, RDS Multi-AZ, ElastiCache Redis. Android APK only for pilot camp. Phase 2 (8 weeks): Service request submission with priority, appointment listing and confirmation, caseworker admin web interface (React SPA), notification inbox in app, iOS App Store release. GDPR erasure endpoint. Phase 3 (8 weeks): UNHCR proGres case management system integration via REST API, offline appointment creation, biometric duplicate detection via AWS Rekognition optional add-on, in-app multilingual service information chatbot prototype.
target_output.development_guidelines: Flutter: Dart analysis_options.yaml with strict rules; golden tests for registration form screens in all 5 locales to catch layout breakage on RTL (Arabic); widget tests for offline sync queue state transitions. Python: mypy strict on FastAPI route handlers and service classes; pytest coverage 85% gate; Testcontainers PostgreSQL in CI for database-dependent tests. No PII in logs: structured log fields validated against allow-list in CI using custom lint rule that flags f-string interpolation of household_members, phone, or name variables in logger calls. ARB localization files reviewed by native speakers before each mobile release. Certificate pin hash must be updated in Flutter build pipeline whenever ACM certificate is renewed.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Cognito phone OTP delivery via SNS SMS may fail in camps without mobile signal; displaced persons cannot complete registration. Mitigation: camp kiosk mode with SMS OTP delivered to camp coordinator's phone and entered on behalf of the person. Risk: Hive local storage is not encrypted on-device by default on Android; if device is seized, pending registration data could be extracted. Mitigation: flutter_secure_storage for sensitive Hive box encryption key storage; Hive AES-256 encryption enabled using key from flutter_secure_storage. Risk: certificate pinning breaks app functionality if ACM certificate is rotated without updating the pinned hash in the Flutter build. Mitigation: automated alert when certificate is 30 days from expiry triggers Flutter rebuild and app store release with updated pin. Tradeoff: phone number OTP without password is more accessible but less secure than password auth — accepted given low-literacy population requirement and OTP rate limiting mitigation.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose Flutter over React Native: single codebase with better RTL support for Arabic in flutter_localizations; flutter_localizations ARB files support all 5 required languages. Chose Hive over SQLite for local storage: Hive is a pure Dart key-value store without native compilation requirement; SQLite via sqflite requires platform channel compilation. Chose FCM over AWS SNS Push: Firebase Admin SDK provides better delivery tracking and deduplication controls. Confirmed eu-west-1 as AWS region: EU data residency satisfies GDPR requirements for EU nationals in the displaced population.

metadata.notes: Greenfield Flutter/FastAPI displaced persons self-registration mobile app with multilingual offline-first capability, Cognito phone OTP, and FCM push notifications.

project_goal.value: Expose real-time employer donation matching eligibility and rules to nonprofit fundraising platforms via REST API
project_goal.rationale: Core business need enabling dynamic match display at donor checkout

target_users.value: Nonprofit fundraising platform developers integrating employer match at checkout
target_users.rationale: B2B API consumers, not end donors

project_class.rationale: Headless service with no frontend, consumed by partner platforms

capabilities.rationale: API-only with data persistence and partner authentication

complexity_level.rationale: Moderate rule evaluation logic and webhook delivery; no complex ML

risk_level.rationale: No PII stored, internal eligibility data only, partner-facing with JWT auth

data_sensitivity.rationale: Employer matching program rules are internal business data, not personal

external_exposure.rationale: Only pre-approved fundraising platforms receive API keys

access_model.value: API key-based JWT issued per partner platform, scoped to read eligibility and post match requests
access_model.rationale: Partner platforms authenticate with per-client credentials

feature_scope.value: Employer eligibility lookup by EIN, nonprofit eligibility check, match ratio retrieval, match request creation, webhook delivery to employer HR systems
feature_scope.rationale: Full matching lifecycle from eligibility query to HR system notification

mvp_scope.value: Eligibility lookup and match request creation; webhook delivery in Phase 2
mvp_scope.rationale: Deliver core query API first, then async delivery

security_baseline.value: TLS 1.3, JWT per API client, rate limiting 500 req/min per client, HMAC webhook signatures
security_baseline.rationale: Partner API baseline controls

frontend_stack.value: none
frontend_stack.rationale: API service with no frontend

backend_stack.rationale: High-throughput request handling with minimal overhead

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 with Redis 7 cache layer
data_platform.rationale: Relational for employer program rules; Redis for eligibility response caching

hosting_target.value: AWS Lambda + API Gateway
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless scales to burst checkout traffic without idle cost

privacy_retention_policy.value: Match request logs retained 24 months; employer program rules updated on partner sync; no donor PII stored
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Audit trail for match disputes, no personal data retention obligation

future_scope.value: Real-time webhook push to employer HR platforms, bulk eligibility pre-fetch API, match analytics dashboard for administrators
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2-3 roadmap items

constraints.value: Must return eligibility response under 200ms p99; Lambda cold starts must be mitigated with provisioned concurrency
constraints.rationale: Donor checkout latency requirement

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for Lambda invocation metrics, X-Ray for request tracing, structured JSON logs
observability_baseline.rationale: Standard AWS observability stack

execution_preference.value: Iterative delivery with Phase 1 MVP in 6 weeks
execution_preference.rationale: Fast to market for fundraising platform partner onboarding

project.goal: Real-time employer donation matching eligibility API consumed by fundraising platforms at donor checkout
project.target_users: Fundraising platform developers (B2B API consumers)

security.auth: Custom JWT per API client, HMAC webhook signatures

reasoner_reviews.product: The API-first approach is well-suited for B2B integration at donor checkout. Eligibility lookup must be synchronous and low-latency; match request creation can tolerate slightly higher latency given it happens post-donation. The webhook delivery pathway for employer HR systems is critical for completing the matching lifecycle.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Lambda + API Gateway is appropriate for bursty fundraising traffic patterns. Redis caching of employer program eligibility rules with short TTLs avoids PostgreSQL hot reads during checkout spikes. Fastify is the right choice over Express for per-request overhead at Lambda scale.
reasoner_reviews.security: API client JWT scoping is adequate for partner exposure. HMAC signatures on webhook payloads protect employer HR systems from spoofed notifications. Rate limiting per client prevents a misconfigured partner from exhausting Lambda concurrency.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: 200ms p99 eligibility response is achievable with Redis cache hit path. Provisioned concurrency on the eligibility Lambda function avoids cold start penalties during real-time checkout flows.

backend.service_design: Two Lambda functions: eligibility-query-handler for GET /v1/eligibility and match-request-handler for POST /v1/matches. Shared PostgreSQL client via RDS Proxy for connection pooling across Lambda instances.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful with OpenAPI 3.0. Versioned at /v1. Eligibility query accepts employer_id and nonprofit_ein as query params. Match request accepts JSON body with donation_amount, donor_ref, employer_id, nonprofit_ein.
backend.failure_handling: Redis cache miss falls through to PostgreSQL read. Webhook delivery uses SQS with exponential backoff retries on 4xx/5xx from employer HR endpoint. Dead-letter queue for undeliverable webhooks.
backend.background_jobs: Nightly employer program sync job fetches updated matching rules from employer data providers and invalidates Redis cache entries for changed programs.

security.auth_design: JWT tokens issued per API client with client_id claim. Tokens validated at API Gateway authorizer Lambda before routing to handler.
security.authorization_model: Scope-based: read:eligibility for GET endpoints, write:matches for POST. Admin scope for program management.
security.abuse_prevention: API Gateway throttling at 500 req/min per client. WAF rules blocking abnormal query patterns. CloudWatch alarm on per-client request spike.

data.entities: employer_programs (id, employer_name, ein, match_ratio, annual_cap_usd, eligible_nonprofit_types[], active), nonprofit_eligibility (id, nonprofit_ein, employer_program_id, eligible, exclusion_reason, last_verified_at), matching_requests (id, donor_ref, employer_program_id, nonprofit_ein, donation_amount_cents, match_amount_cents, status ENUM pending/submitted/confirmed/rejected, created_at), api_clients (id, client_name, jwt_secret_hash, scopes[], rate_limit_rpm, active)
data.storage_design: PostgreSQL 16 via RDS Proxy. Redis 7 caching employer_programs and nonprofit_eligibility with 15-minute TTL. DynamoDB for webhook delivery state tracking.

devops.deployment_topology: Lambda functions deployed via SAM. API Gateway REST API with usage plans per client. RDS PostgreSQL in private VPC subnet accessed via RDS Proxy. Redis ElastiCache cluster in same VPC.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: lint, Jest unit tests, SAM build, deploy to staging, integration test suite against staging, promote to production on tag. OpenAPI spec generated and published on deploy.
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch Logs with structured JSON. X-Ray traces for Lambda invocations. Custom CloudWatch metrics: eligibility_cache_hit_rate, match_request_volume, webhook_delivery_success_rate.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a low-latency partner-facing eligibility API for employer donation matching. Lambda + API Gateway suits the bursty checkout traffic pattern. Redis caching of employer program rules is the key path to meeting the 200ms p99 SLA. No frontend; all interaction is B2B REST. Weak quality target means thinner sections are acceptable.
target_output.title: Employer Donation Matching Eligibility API
target_output.executive_summary: A serverless Node.js 20 Fastify API deployed on AWS Lambda + API Gateway that enables nonprofit fundraising platforms to query employer donation matching eligibility and submit match requests in real time during donor checkout flows. PostgreSQL 16 stores employer program rules and match records; Redis 7 caches eligibility lookups to meet the 200ms p99 SLA.
target_output.architecture_overview: Serverless two-function architecture on AWS Lambda behind API Gateway REST API. The eligibility-query-handler serves GET /v1/eligibility with Redis-first lookup of employer program rules, falling back to PostgreSQL via RDS Proxy. The match-request-handler processes POST /v1/matches, persists the match record, and enqueues a webhook delivery job to SQS for async notification of the employer HR system. API Gateway handles JWT authorization via a Lambda authorizer validating per-client tokens. Both Lambdas share a VPC with ElastiCache Redis and RDS PostgreSQL via RDS Proxy for connection pooling.
target_output.technology_stack: Backend: Node.js 20, Fastify, TypeScript. Database: PostgreSQL 16 via AWS RDS, Redis 7 via ElastiCache. Hosting: AWS Lambda (SAM), API Gateway REST API. Queue: SQS for webhook delivery. Auth: Lambda authorizer with per-client JWT. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with SAM deploy. Observability: CloudWatch Logs, X-Ray tracing, CloudWatch custom metrics.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Eligibility Lookup: fundraising platforms query employer-nonprofit pair eligibility and retrieve match ratio, annual cap, and any exclusion reason in under 200ms. Match Request Creation: post-donation match records with donor reference, amounts, and employer routing. Webhook Delivery: async SQS-backed delivery of match events to employer HR system endpoints with HMAC signatures and retry logic. Program Sync: nightly job updating employer program rules from data providers and invalidating stale Redis cache entries.
target_output.system_components: API Gateway REST API with JWT authorizer Lambda, eligibility-query-handler Lambda with Redis cache, match-request-handler Lambda writing to PostgreSQL and enqueuing SQS, RDS PostgreSQL 16 via RDS Proxy, ElastiCache Redis 7, SQS webhook delivery queue with DLQ, employer-sync batch Lambda running nightly.
target_output.workflows: Eligibility Query: platform sends GET /v1/eligibility?employer_id=EMP-123&nonprofit_ein=82-0001234, authorizer validates JWT, handler checks Redis for employer_programs entry, on miss reads PostgreSQL and writes to Redis with 15-min TTL, returns match_ratio and eligible flag. Match Request: platform sends POST /v1/matches with donation_amount and references, handler validates employer program eligibility, inserts matching_requests record with status pending, enqueues SQS webhook message, returns match_id. Webhook Delivery: SQS consumer Lambda sends HMAC-signed POST to employer HR endpoint, on success updates match status to submitted, on failure retries with exponential backoff up to 5 attempts before DLQ.
target_output.data_model: employer_programs (id UUID PK, employer_name, ein VARCHAR(10), match_ratio NUMERIC(4,2), annual_cap_usd INT, eligible_nonprofit_types TEXT[], active BOOL, updated_at). nonprofit_eligibility (id UUID PK, nonprofit_ein, employer_program_id FK, eligible BOOL, exclusion_reason TEXT, last_verified_at TIMESTAMP). matching_requests (id UUID PK, donor_ref VARCHAR, employer_program_id FK, nonprofit_ein, donation_amount_cents BIGINT, match_amount_cents BIGINT, status ENUM pending/submitted/confirmed/rejected, webhook_attempts INT DEFAULT 0, created_at TIMESTAMP). api_clients (id UUID PK, client_name, jwt_secret_hash, scopes TEXT[], rate_limit_rpm INT, active BOOL).
target_output.api_design: GET /v1/eligibility?employer_id={id}&nonprofit_ein={ein} returns {eligible, match_ratio, annual_cap_usd, exclusion_reason}. POST /v1/matches body {donor_ref, employer_id, nonprofit_ein, donation_amount_cents} returns {match_id, match_amount_cents, status}. GET /v1/matches/{match_id} returns current match status. POST /v1/admin/programs for program rule updates (admin scope). All responses include X-Request-ID and X-Cache-Status headers.
target_output.security_and_compliance: JWT per-client authentication validated at API Gateway Lambda authorizer. HMAC-SHA256 signatures on all webhook payloads using per-employer secret. API Gateway throttling at 500 req/min per client with 429 on breach. WAF rules on API Gateway for rate anomaly blocking. TLS 1.3 on all endpoints. Secrets stored in AWS Secrets Manager with Lambda environment variable injection.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: SAM template defines both Lambdas, API Gateway, SQS queues, and VPC configuration. GitHub Actions pipeline: ESLint, Jest unit tests, SAM build, deploy to staging, run integration tests against staging API, promote to production on semver tag. Provisioned concurrency set to 5 for eligibility-query-handler to eliminate cold start on checkout hot path. Blue-green Lambda alias routing for zero-downtime deploys.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch Logs with structured JSON including client_id, employer_id, cache_hit, latency_ms on every request. X-Ray distributed tracing across Lambda, RDS Proxy, and ElastiCache calls. Custom CloudWatch metrics: eligibility_cache_hit_rate (target >90%), webhook_delivery_success_rate (target >99%), match_request_volume. Alarm on p99 eligibility latency exceeding 200ms.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Lambda pricing scales to zero on idle; provisioned concurrency for 5 warm instances adds ~$40/month. RDS PostgreSQL db.t4g.medium for program rules (~$60/month). ElastiCache cache.t3.micro (~$15/month). Total estimated ~$120/month at 500K monthly eligibility queries.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (6 weeks): employer_programs and nonprofit_eligibility data model, eligibility query endpoint, match request creation, Redis caching layer, API key provisioning, OpenAPI spec. Phase 2 (4 weeks): SQS webhook delivery with HMAC signatures, employer HR endpoint configuration, DLQ monitoring. Phase 3 (3 weeks): nightly program sync job, bulk pre-fetch API, admin program management endpoints.
target_output.development_guidelines: TypeScript strict mode. Fastify schema validation on all request/response shapes. Jest unit tests for eligibility rule evaluation logic, cache fallback path, and HMAC signature generation. Integration tests run against local SAM invoke. OpenAPI spec auto-generated from Fastify schemas and published to partner developer portal on each production deploy.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Redis cache staleness when employer programs change mid-day. Mitigation: program update events immediately invalidate affected Redis keys. Risk: Lambda cold start exceeding 200ms SLA on eligibility path. Mitigation: provisioned concurrency on eligibility handler. Tradeoff: serverless removes idle cost but adds complexity managing RDS Proxy connection limits across concurrent Lambda instances.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose Lambda over ECS for serverless burst scaling without idle cost. Selected RDS Proxy over direct PostgreSQL connection to handle Lambda connection pool exhaustion. Confirmed HMAC-SHA256 over mTLS for webhook signatures given employer HR system diversity.

metadata.notes: Serverless employer donation matching eligibility API on Lambda with Redis caching for 200ms checkout latency SLA.

project.goal: Visual desktop tool for Theory of Change design and impact evaluation
project.target_users: NGO program managers and M&E officers

frontend.ui_library: Vueflow for graph visualization, PrimeVue for forms

backend.file_io: Tauri file dialog API for CSV/Excel import

security.auth: OS user session
security.encryption: SQLCipher AES-256 with passphrase in OS keychain via tauri-plugin-keyring

data.import_formats: CSV, Excel via calamine Rust crate

devops.ci: GitHub Actions building Tauri installers for Windows and macOS

reasoner_reviews.product: The Theory of Change designer addresses a genuine gap for NGO program staff who currently use Visio or PowerPoint for ToC diagrams with no monitoring data linkage. The evidence gap report at evaluation time directly meets donor reporting needs. Monitoring data import via CSV aligns with how field data actually arrives from KoboToolbox and DHIS2.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Tauri with Rust commands is the right choice: small binary size satisfies the 50MB constraint, Rust handles CSV parsing and SQLite queries safely without memory issues on low-spec field laptops. Vueflow is a well-maintained Vue graph library suitable for the node-link ToC diagram. The IPC command pattern keeps business logic out of the renderer process.
reasoner_reviews.security: With local_only exposure and internal data sensitivity, the main risk is data exposure on a lost or stolen laptop. SQLCipher with passphrase management via the OS keychain (tauri-plugin-keyring) addresses this. Automatic timestamped backups protect against accidental file corruption. No network exposure means no transport security concerns.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Fully offline operation is achievable with Tauri and embedded SQLite. The 50MB installer target is feasible since Tauri binaries typically run 5-20MB. Windows 10 and macOS 12 support is covered by Tauri v2 requirements.

backend.service_design: Tauri command modules: toc_commands (CRUD for nodes and links), indicator_commands (link indicators to nodes, set targets), import_commands (CSV/Excel ingestion via calamine), report_commands (evidence matrix generation and PDF export)
backend.api_patterns: Tauri invoke() IPC: invoke('create_toc_node', {model_id, node_type, label, position_x, position_y}), invoke('import_monitoring_csv', {file_path, column_mappings}), invoke('generate_evaluation_report', {model_id}), invoke('export_report_pdf', {model_id, output_path})
backend.failure_handling: Rust Result types propagate errors to the frontend as serialized error objects; CSV import failures return per-row error lists distinguishing missing columns from type mismatches

frontend.app_structure: Vue 3 SPA inside Tauri webview with Pinia stores for toc_model, indicators, and import state
frontend.pages_and_flows: ToC Designer (Vueflow canvas with node type palette), Indicator Registry (link indicators to nodes with target values), Data Import (CSV field mapping wizard), Evaluation Dashboard (evidence gap matrix with color-coded achievement), Report Export (PDF preview and save dialog)
frontend.performance_notes: Vueflow canvas virtualization for ToC models with 50+ nodes; Pinia store uses normalized entity maps to avoid deep Vue reactivity on the graph adjacency data

security.auth_design: No application-level authentication; rely on OS file system permissions scoped to the current user account
security.secrets_management: SQLCipher passphrase generated on first launch using Rust rand::thread_rng, stored in the OS keychain via tauri-plugin-keyring (Keychain on macOS, Credential Manager on Windows); retrieved transparently on subsequent launches
security.security_risks: Risk of data loss if SQLite file corrupted; mitigated by automatic backup on each successful write, retaining the last 5 timestamped copies

data.entities: theory_of_change_models (id, title, description, created_at, updated_at), toc_nodes (id, model_id, node_type ENUM input/activity/output/outcome/impact, label, notes, position_x, position_y), toc_links (id, source_node_id, target_node_id, label), outcome_indicators (id, node_id, indicator_name, unit, baseline_value, target_value, data_source), monitoring_data_imports (id, indicator_id, file_name, imported_at, row_count, error_count), monitoring_observations (id, import_id, indicator_id, period_label, observed_value, notes), evaluation_reports (id, model_id, generated_at, summary_json)
data.storage_design: Single SQLCipher-encrypted SQLite file at {app_data}/toc_workspace.db; WAL mode for safe concurrent reads during report generation
data.retention_and_deletion: User-controlled; full workspace delete removes the SQLite file and all backup copies from the backups subdirectory

devops.deployment_topology: GitHub Actions matrix build: tauri-action on ubuntu-22.04 (cross-compile .msi) and macos-14 (.dmg); signed installers uploaded to GitHub Releases
devops.ci_cd_design: On version tag: cargo test, vue-tsc typecheck, vitest unit tests, then tauri build per platform. Release notes auto-generated from conventional commits.
devops.rollback_strategy: Prior installer versions retained on GitHub Releases; users can reinstall any previous version. No auto-update server; users check releases manually.

ISS-001.title: SQLite file not encrypted by default
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 plan stored ToC workspace in plain SQLite without encryption, leaving organizational strategy data exposed on a lost or stolen laptop.

history.note: Identified during security review

ISS-002.title: No backup mechanism for local SQLite file
ISS-002.detail: If the SQLite file is corrupted during a write, the user loses all ToC work with no recovery path. Prior plan did not include any backup or export-on-save strategy.

history.note: Identified during data review

focus_issues.title: SQLite file not encrypted by default
focus_issues.detail: Round 1 plan stored ToC workspace in plain SQLite; strategy data exposed on laptop loss.

latest_recommendations: Add SQLCipher encryption to the SQLite data layer with passphrase managed via OS keychain
Add automatic workspace backup on each save event to a dated backup file
