Consumers configure their own slog handler; SDK integrates naturally with zerolog, zap, or any slog.Handler. Benchmark suite (go test -bench) covers health.ReadinessHandler latency under concurrent probe load and config.ValidateEnv with 50-field schemas.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost for the SDK itself. GitHub Actions free tier sufficient for CI (approximately 5 build-minutes per PR). pkg.go.dev hosting is free. Consumer applications benefit from sub-millisecond health check handler latency and negligible memory overhead. The SDK's zero-allocation hot paths (health probe handling) have been benchmarked to avoid GC pressure during high-frequency Kubernetes probe intervals.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 v0.1.0 (4 weeks): deploykit/config with EnvSchema validation and typed coercion, deploykit/health with LivenessHandler and ReadinessHandler with WithCheck functional option, deploykit/lifecycle with GracefulShutdown and configurable drain period, deploykit/secrets with EnvSource and FileSource SecretRef, comprehensive test suite with -race flag, govulncheck in CI, goreleaser release pipeline. Phase 2 v0.2.0 (3 weeks): deploykit/rollout Kubernetes spec generation, Prometheus metrics optional sub-package (deploykit/metrics) with external dependency behind build tag, expanded README with containerization migration guide. Phase 3 v0.3.0 (4 weeks): AWS Secrets Manager adapter for deploykit/secrets, Kubernetes liveness/readiness annotation helpers, contribution guide and CODEOWNERS for open-source governance.
target_output.development_guidelines: All exported functions and types must have godoc comments. Zero external dependencies enforced in core sub-packages via a Go workspace tool check in CI that fails if go.sum acquires non-stdlib entries. Table-driven tests for all validation logic. Benchmarks for hot paths (health handler, env validation). Conventional commits for CHANGELOG generation via goreleaser. Semantic versioning: breaking API changes bump major version per Go module compatibility rules. Examples in _test.go files with Example prefix for pkg.go.dev documentation rendering.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: Go 1.21 slog API compatibility; mitigated by testing on both Go 1.21 and 1.22 in CI matrix. Risk: consumer misuse passing secret values as plain strings to non-SecretRef functions; mitigated by SecretRef type wrapper and documentation examples. Tradeoff: zero external dependency constraint prevents using well-tested libraries like github.com/spf13/viper for config; accepted to maintain air-gap safety. Tradeoff: govulncheck adds CI time but is essential given that stdlib CVEs do exist and the SDK is used in sensitive humanitarian infrastructure.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected functional options pattern over config struct arguments for composable health handlers, following the net/http handler convention. Confirmed slog over zerolog/zap as the logging interface to maintain zero external dependencies in core. Chose goreleaser over manual release scripting for consistent CHANGELOG generation. intstr.IntOrString type used for MaxSurge and MaxUnavailable to mirror the Kubernetes API for familiarity.

metadata.notes: Open-source Go SDK with zero stdlib-only constraint for containerizing legacy humanitarian applications.

project_goal.value: Collect and normalize dossier data from UNHCR and government data feeds into structured case files ready for resettlement eligibility assessment by caseworkers
project_goal.rationale: Replaces manual dossier collation process that delays case assignment by weeks

target_users.value: Resettlement agency caseworkers consuming prepared case files; data stewards managing pipeline exceptions
target_users.rationale: Downstream consumers of pipeline output; pipeline itself is non-interactive

project_class.rationale: Batch ETL pipeline ingesting from multiple external feeds, no user-facing interface

capabilities.rationale: Data transformation and external source integration; no frontend or auth capability needed

complexity_level.rationale: Multi-source ingestion with normalization and exception handling but no ML or real-time processing

risk_level.rationale: Pipeline processes highly sensitive refugee dossier data including protection flags and family separation records

data_sensitivity.rationale: Refugee dossiers contain protection status, family composition, vulnerability flags, and biographic data protected by UNHCR classification standards

external_exposure.rationale: Pipeline runs in closed agency infrastructure; output consumed by internal case management system only

access_model.value: AWS IAM roles for EC2 execution environment; data steward access to exception queue via internal console only
access_model.rationale: No interactive user-facing access; machine-to-machine IAM and minimal human access for exception review

feature_scope.value: Nightly ingestion of UNHCR dossier exports and government data feeds, field normalization to unified schema, eligibility flag computation, quarantine of invalid records, exception reports for data stewards, prepared case file output to PostgreSQL
feature_scope.rationale: Full ETL lifecycle from encrypted source intake to prepared case file persistence

mvp_scope.value: UNHCR export ingestion, schema normalization, eligibility flag computation, exception quarantine and error reports
mvp_scope.rationale: Core pipeline without government feed integration in MVP

security_baseline.value: Encryption at rest for all dossier data using AWS KMS, TLS 1.3 for all data transfers, VPC-isolated EC2 execution, S3 server-side encryption for staging files, all access logged to CloudTrail
security_baseline.rationale: Confidential refugee data requires encryption at every stage and full access audit trail

frontend_stack.value: none
frontend_stack.rationale: Batch pipeline with no user interface

backend_stack.rationale: Luigi provides dependency-aware task orchestration suitable for multi-stage ETL with retry logic

data_platform.value: PostgreSQL 16 for output case files; S3 for encrypted staging of raw source files
data_platform.rationale: Relational model for case file output; S3 for durable staging before processing

hosting_target.value: EC2 with Auto Scaling in VPC private subnets
hosting_target.rationale: Nightly batch workload suits EC2; no persistent idle infrastructure needed

privacy_retention_policy.value: Raw source files deleted from S3 after successful processing; prepared case files retained per agency data retention policy (5 years); exception records retained 12 months for remediation tracking
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Minimize retention of raw confidential source data; preserve processed output for casework lifecycle

future_scope.value: Government data feed integration, real-time pipeline trigger on new UNHCR file arrival, automated case assignment to caseworkers based on workload
future_scope.rationale: Phase 2-3 enhancements beyond nightly batch MVP

constraints.value: Pipeline must complete within 4-hour nightly processing window; source file formats change without notice requiring resilient parsing; all processing in AWS us-east-1 only for data residency
constraints.rationale: Operational SLA and data residency requirements

observability_baseline.value: AWS CloudWatch for pipeline run metrics and alarms, structured JSON run logs to CloudWatch Logs, S3-stored pipeline run reports
observability_baseline.rationale: Standard AWS monitoring for batch jobs

execution_preference.value: Iterative delivery with MVP in 8 weeks
execution_preference.rationale: Fast delivery to unblock caseworker onboarding

compliance_context.value: UNHCR data protection guidelines for refugee personal data, national immigration data security requirements, GDPR for EU data subjects
compliance_context.rationale: High risk and confidential data sensitivity require compliance context; refugee dossiers are subject to strict data protection frameworks

project.goal: Nightly ETL pipeline normalizing UNHCR and government dossier exports into structured case files for resettlement eligibility assessment
project.target_users: Caseworkers consuming case files; data stewards reviewing exceptions

backend.processing: Normalization rules in configurable YAML schemas

security.auth: AWS IAM roles for EC2 execution
security.encryption: AWS KMS at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit
security.access_audit: CloudTrail for all S3 and RDS access

data.staging: S3 with SSE-KMS for raw source files
data.exception: Quarantine table and S3 exception report bucket

devops.hosting: EC2 Auto Scaling in private VPC subnets

constraints.format_resilience: Schema parsing must handle source format variations

reasoner_reviews.product: The MVP scope correctly focuses on UNHCR feed ingestion and normalization. The exception quarantine mechanism is critical for data quality -- caseworkers cannot act on incomplete dossiers and need clear error reports to prompt source-side remediation. Government feed integration can follow in Phase 2.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Luigi is adequate for the dependency-aware ETL tasks at this scale. The 4-hour nightly window is achievable for the expected dossier volumes. S3 staging with SSE-KMS before processing keeps raw confidential data off the EC2 instances until needed.
reasoner_reviews.security: All dossier data is confidential under UNHCR classification. Encryption at rest via KMS and in transit via TLS 1.3 are mandatory. CloudTrail logging of all S3 GetObject events provides the audit trail required for data access accountability.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Source file format resilience is a real operational risk -- UNHCR export formats have changed without notice in the past. The normalization layer should apply configurable field mapping rules loaded from YAML rather than hard-coded parsers.

backend.service_design: Luigi pipeline with four task classes: IngestSourceFilesTask (downloads encrypted UNHCR exports from S3 staging), DecryptAndParseTask (decrypts with KMS, applies YAML field mapping rules), NormalizationTask (maps fields to unified schema, computes eligibility_flags), PersistCaseFilesTask (writes prepared case files to PostgreSQL, quarantines exceptions).
backend.failure_handling: Luigi retry on transient S3 or RDS errors up to 3 attempts. Dossiers failing mandatory field validation written to exceptions_queue table with structured error codes. Pipeline run failure sends CloudWatch alarm triggering PagerDuty notification to on-call data engineer.
backend.background_jobs: EventBridge rule triggers EC2 Auto Scaling launch of pipeline instance at 01:00 UTC nightly. Instance terminates after pipeline completion to minimize idle cost. Pipeline run summary written to S3 report bucket and PostgreSQL pipeline_runs table.

security.auth_design: EC2 instance profile with IAM role scoped to: S3 GetObject on source bucket, KMS Decrypt for dossier key, RDS Connect to case file database. No programmatic credentials stored on instance.
security.secrets_management: UNHCR SFTP credentials and database connection string stored in AWS Secrets Manager. Retrieved at runtime by EC2 instance profile role.
security.audit_and_logging_controls: CloudTrail logging all S3 GetObject, KMS Decrypt, and RDS Connect events. Structured pipeline run log written to CloudWatch Logs with run_id, records_processed, records_quarantined, and duration_seconds per task.

data.entities: refugee_dossiers (id UUID PK, unhcr_case_number VARCHAR, full_name_encrypted, dob, nationality, protection_status ENUM mandate/prima_facie/rejected, vulnerability_flags TEXT[], family_unit_id, eligibility_flags JSONB, source_feed VARCHAR, ingested_at TIMESTAMP). pipeline_runs (id UUID PK, run_date DATE, records_ingested INT, records_normalized INT, records_quarantined INT, status ENUM running/completed/failed, started_at TIMESTAMP, completed_at TIMESTAMP). exceptions_queue (id UUID PK, run_id FK, source_record_ref VARCHAR, error_codes TEXT[], raw_excerpt_encrypted TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMP).
data.storage_design: S3 bucket with SSE-KMS for raw UNHCR export staging, deleted after successful processing. PostgreSQL 16 with column-level encryption for PII fields using pgcrypto. S3 exception report bucket for human-readable error exports.
data.retention_and_deletion: S3 raw files deleted via lifecycle rule 24 hours after successful run. case files retained 5 years. Exception records retained 12 months.

devops.deployment_topology: EC2 Auto Scaling group minimum 0, maximum 1. EventBridge scheduled rule triggers launch. AMI pre-baked with Python 3.12, Luigi, and pipeline code. RDS PostgreSQL in same VPC private subnet. S3 accessed via VPC endpoint.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: flake8 lint, pytest unit tests for normalization rules and exception logic, build AMI via Packer on tag, update Auto Scaling launch template. No streaming deploy needed for nightly batch.
devops.observability_stack: CloudWatch Logs for structured pipeline run logs. CloudWatch Metrics: records_processed, records_quarantined, pipeline_duration_minutes. Alarm on pipeline_duration exceeding 240 minutes (4-hour SLA breach) and on records_quarantined exceeding 10% of ingested.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a greenfield nightly batch ETL pipeline for refugee dossier normalization. Luigi task orchestration handles the multi-stage ETL dependency chain. The confidential data sensitivity and UNHCR compliance requirements drive encryption-at-every-stage and CloudTrail audit logging. Weak quality target permits thinner section depth.
target_output.title: Refugee Dossier Normalization Pipeline
target_output.executive_summary: A nightly Luigi-orchestrated batch pipeline on AWS EC2 that ingests encrypted UNHCR dossier exports, normalizes case attributes to a unified schema using configurable YAML field mapping rules, computes resettlement eligibility flags, and persists prepared case files to PostgreSQL 16 for caseworker assignment. Invalid records are quarantined with structured error reports for data steward remediation.
target_output.architecture_overview: EventBridge nightly trigger launches an EC2 Auto Scaling instance from a pre-baked AMI containing the Python 3.12 Luigi pipeline. The IngestSourceFilesTask retrieves encrypted UNHCR export files from an S3 staging bucket via VPC endpoint. DecryptAndParseTask decrypts using KMS and applies YAML-configured field mapping rules. NormalizationTask transforms to unified refugee_dossiers schema and computes eligibility_flags. PersistCaseFilesTask writes valid records to PostgreSQL and quarantined exceptions to the exceptions_queue table. Instance terminates after completion. All S3 and KMS access events logged to CloudTrail.
target_output.technology_stack: Pipeline: Python 3.12, Luigi task framework. Database: PostgreSQL 16 with pgcrypto for PII column encryption. Staging: S3 with SSE-KMS. Orchestration: EventBridge scheduled rule, EC2 Auto Scaling with scale-to-zero. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager. CI/CD: GitHub Actions with Packer AMI build. Observability: CloudWatch Logs and Metrics, CloudTrail.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Dossier Ingestion: SFTP pull of UNHCR export files to S3 staging with SSE-KMS encryption. Field Mapping and Normalization: YAML-configurable rules mapping UNHCR proprietary XML fields to unified refugee_dossiers schema with format validation. Eligibility Flag Computation: rule-based evaluation of protection_status, vulnerability_flags, and family composition to set eligibility_flags JSONB. Exception Quarantine: invalid records written to exceptions_queue with structured error codes; exception report exported to S3 and emailed to data steward. Case File Persistence: prepared refugee_dossiers records written to PostgreSQL for caseworker access.
target_output.system_components: EventBridge scheduled rule, EC2 Auto Scaling group (min 0, max 1, terminates post-run), S3 source staging bucket with SSE-KMS, S3 VPC endpoint, AWS KMS key for dossier encryption, PostgreSQL 16 in private VPC subnet, AWS Secrets Manager for SFTP credentials and DB connection, CloudWatch Logs and Metrics, CloudTrail for audit.
target_output.workflows: Nightly Ingestion Run: EventBridge launches EC2 instance at 01:00 UTC, instance retrieves UNHCR SFTP credentials from Secrets Manager, pulls export files to S3 staging bucket, Luigi pipeline executes IngestSourceFilesTask through PersistCaseFilesTask sequentially, pipeline run summary written to pipeline_runs table and S3 report bucket, EC2 terminates. Exception Remediation: data steward receives exception report email listing records with error codes, investigates source data deficiencies, requests corrected export from UNHCR data liaison, re-triggers pipeline with corrected input file to reprocess quarantined records.
target_output.data_model: refugee_dossiers (id UUID PK, unhcr_case_number VARCHAR UNIQUE, full_name_encrypted BYTEA, dob DATE, nationality CHAR(3), protection_status ENUM mandate/prima_facie/rejected, vulnerability_flags TEXT[], family_unit_id VARCHAR, eligibility_flags JSONB, source_feed VARCHAR, ingested_at TIMESTAMP). pipeline_runs (id UUID PK, run_date DATE, records_ingested INT, records_normalized INT, records_quarantined INT, status ENUM running/completed/failed, started_at TIMESTAMP, completed_at TIMESTAMP). exceptions_queue (id UUID PK, run_id UUID FK, source_record_ref VARCHAR, error_codes TEXT[], raw_excerpt_encrypted BYTEA, created_at TIMESTAMP). data_normalization_rules (id UUID PK, source_feed VARCHAR, source_field_path VARCHAR, target_field VARCHAR, transformation VARCHAR, active BOOL, updated_at TIMESTAMP).
target_output.api_design: No external-facing API. Internal interfaces: PostgreSQL queries by case management system using read-only IAM-auth role. Luigi task HTTP status port on EC2 localhost for monitoring. S3 exception report bucket key pattern: exception-reports/{run_date}/{run_id}.json accessible to data steward IAM group.
target_output.security_and_compliance: EC2 instance profile with IAM role scoped to S3 GetObject, KMS Decrypt, and RDS Connect. No long-lived credentials stored on instances. SFTP and database credentials retrieved from Secrets Manager at runtime. Dossier PII fields encrypted using pgcrypto before PostgreSQL storage. S3 staging files use SSE-KMS; lifecycle rule deletes raw files 24 hours post-successful-run. CloudTrail logs all S3 GetObject and KMS Decrypt events. UNHCR data protection guidelines satisfied by encryption-at-rest, strict IAM access, and deletion of raw source files post-processing. GDPR right-to-erasure supported by case file deletion procedure in data_normalization_rules metadata.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: EC2 Auto Scaling launch template references pre-baked AMI built via Packer with Python 3.12, Luigi, pipeline code, and startup script. EventBridge rule triggers scale-out to 1 instance at 01:00 UTC. Instance downloads latest YAML normalization rules from S3 config bucket on startup. Pipeline runs to completion, writes summary, then calls EC2 terminate-instances via instance profile role. GitHub Actions CI: flake8, pytest covering normalization rule evaluation and exception routing, Packer build on version tag, update launch template AMI ID. Rollback: update launch template to previous AMI version.
target_output.observability: CloudWatch Logs group /pipeline/dossier-normalization with structured JSON entries per task: run_id, task_name, records_in, records_out, records_quarantined, duration_ms, status. CloudWatch Metrics: custom namespace DossierPipeline with metrics records_processed, records_quarantined, pipeline_duration_minutes. Alarms: pipeline_duration_minutes >240 (SLA breach), records_quarantined >10% of records_ingested, pipeline status=failed triggers PagerDuty alert to on-call data engineer.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: EC2 t3.large on-demand for nightly 3-4 hour run approximately $0.50/run or $15/month. RDS PostgreSQL db.t4g.medium approximately $60/month. S3 storage for staging and reports approximately $5/month. Secrets Manager approximately $1/month. Total approximately $80/month. Reserved EC2 not warranted for nightly batch; Spot instance feasible if run time flexibility allows.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (8 weeks): UNHCR export SFTP ingestion, KMS decrypt and YAML field mapping, normalization to refugee_dossiers schema, eligibility flag computation, exception quarantine and error reports, CloudWatch observability, EC2 Auto Scaling deployment. Phase 2 (6 weeks): government data feed integration with additional field mapping rules, duplicate detection across UNHCR and government sources using case number cross-reference. Phase 3 (4 weeks): EventBridge S3 trigger for real-time pipeline launch on new file arrival, automated case assignment to caseworker queues based on workload balance.
target_output.development_guidelines: Normalization rules defined in YAML under pipeline/rules/ directory, not in code. Pytest for unit tests covering each normalization rule transformation, eligibility flag logic, and exception error code assignment. Luigi task tests use local SQLite for isolation. Exception report format documented in pipeline/docs/exception-format.md. AMI rebuild required on dependency updates -- Packer build in CI enforces this.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: UNHCR export format change breaks field mapping rules mid-run. Mitigation: YAML-configurable rules allow hot-patching without code deploy; schema validation step fails fast on unexpected structure with clear error. Risk: EC2 instance launch latency pushes pipeline past 4-hour window. Mitigation: warm pool of 0 pre-initialized instances reduces launch time; monitor pipeline_duration and alert at 3-hour mark. Tradeoff: Luigi over Airflow reduces operational overhead but limits scheduling flexibility for future real-time trigger patterns.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose Luigi over Airflow to minimize infrastructure overhead for single-pipeline MVP. Confirmed S3 staging with SSE-KMS over direct SFTP-to-EC2 to avoid raw file exposure on instance disk. Selected pgcrypto column encryption over full-disk encryption for field-level PII isolation in case file queries.

metadata.notes: Greenfield Luigi ETL pipeline for refugee dossier normalization with KMS encryption, YAML field mapping rules, and exception quarantine workflow.

profile.domain: Humanitarian Data Science - Crisis Response ML SDK

project_goal.value: A Python SDK for humanitarian data scientists to build, version, and deploy predictive models for crisis response, providing standardized feature engineering helpers, model evaluation utilities, and ONNX export support for cross-platform deployment in low-resource field environments
project_goal.rationale: Standardizes ML workflow across 8 data science teams to enable reproducible crisis prediction models deployable on field hardware

target_users.value: Humanitarian data scientists and ML engineers building crisis forecasting and resource allocation models
target_users.rationale: Primary SDK consumers who build and evaluate predictive models

project_class.rationale: Distributed as a PyPI package, no hosted service

capabilities.rationale: Feature engineering, model evaluation, MLflow integration, and ONNX export; no frontend or auth

complexity_level.rationale: ONNX export pipeline, MLflow tracking integration, and domain-specific feature transforms add significant complexity

risk_level.rationale: SDK handles internal humanitarian program data used as model training inputs; misuse of forecasts could affect aid allocation decisions

data_sensitivity.rationale: Training data inputs are organizational program metrics, not personal beneficiary data

external_exposure.rationale: SDK used by internal data science teams, not exposed as a service

access_model.value: No authentication; PyPI package imported by data science workstations and CI pipelines
access_model.rationale: Standard Python package distribution model

feature_scope.value: Humanitarian feature engineering helpers (displacement_rate, rainfall_anomaly_index, market_price_deviation, population_density_grid), cross-validation with stratified crisis-type splits, model evaluation metrics (precision-recall at crisis threshold, F1 by crisis severity tier), MLflow experiment tracking integration, ONNX model export with metadata manifest, field deployment package builder
feature_scope.rationale: Covers full model development lifecycle from feature prep through field deployment

mvp_scope.value: Core feature engineering helpers, cross-validation with crisis-stratified splits, standard evaluation metrics, MLflow logging utilities, basic ONNX export
mvp_scope.rationale: Delivers immediate value for model building before full deployment packaging

security_baseline.value: No training data cached or logged by the SDK; MLflow tracking logs metrics and parameters only, not raw data; ONNX export writes to user-specified paths only
security_baseline.rationale: Prevents inadvertent storage of internal humanitarian data in shared locations

frontend_stack.value: N/A - Python library SDK, no frontend
frontend_stack.rationale: SDK has no UI; data scientists use it in Jupyter notebooks and Python scripts

backend_stack.rationale: Standard language for humanitarian data science teams; compatible with scikit-learn and PyTorch workflows

data_platform.value: No persistent storage; operates on in-memory pandas DataFrames and numpy arrays; optional MLflow for experiment tracking
data_platform.rationale: SDK is stateless; data scientists manage their own storage

hosting_target.value: PyPI package (pypi.org)
hosting_target.rationale: Standard Python package distribution

privacy_retention_policy.value: SDK does not store or transmit training data; MLflow integration logs only scalar metrics and model parameters, never raw data rows
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Ensures internal humanitarian data is not inadvertently persisted in experiment tracking systems

future_scope.value: AutoML hyperparameter search wrapper, federated model aggregation helpers for multi-country training, model card generation for accountability reporting
future_scope.rationale: Post-MVP model governance and distributed training features

constraints.value: Must be installable without GPU, compatible with Python 3.10 through 3.12, ONNX export must run on commodity field hardware (4GB RAM, x86_64)
constraints.rationale: Field environments have no GPU acceleration and limited hardware resources

observability_baseline.value: MLflow experiment tracking for metrics, parameters, and model artifacts; logging via Python standard library logging module
observability_baseline.rationale: MLflow provides the standard observability layer for ML experiments in the organization

execution_preference.value: Semantic versioning with GitHub Actions releasing to PyPI on version tag
execution_preference.rationale: Standard Python package release cadence

project.goal: Python SDK for crisis response ML model development, evaluation, and ONNX field deployment
project.target_users: Humanitarian data scientists and ML engineers

backend.dependencies: numpy, pandas, scikit-learn, onnx, onnxmltools, mlflow (optional), skl2onnx

security.data_logging: metrics and parameters only, no raw data
security.paths: user-specified output paths for ONNX artifacts

data.formats: pandas DataFrame inputs, numpy array outputs, JSON metadata manifests

devops.ci: GitHub Actions: pytest, ruff, mypy on Python 3.10/3.11/3.12 matrix
devops.publish: PyPI via trusted publishing on version tag

reasoner_reviews.product: The SDK is the right abstraction for harmonizing ML workflows across 8 humanitarian data science teams. Without it, each team implements bespoke feature engineering for the same crisis indicators (displacement rate, market price deviation) with incompatible schemas, making model comparison impossible. The ONNX export with metadata manifest directly solves the 'trained in Jupyter, broken in field' problem that has affected previous deployments.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The crisis-stratified cross-validation splitter is the most domain-critical component: standard random splits leak information across crisis event boundaries, producing inflated evaluation metrics that do not generalize to unseen crises. The deployment package builder wrapping ONNX model plus metadata manifest plus a minimal inference script is the right artifact for field deployment. MLflow integration should be opt-in to avoid requiring a tracking server for simple local experiments.
reasoner_reviews.security: The primary security concern is inadvertent persistence of training data through MLflow artifact logging. The SDK must intercept MLflow log_artifact calls and validate that the artifact is a model file or metric summary, not a raw DataFrame serialization. ONNX export writes should be restricted to user-specified paths with no automatic cloud upload.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: ONNX export without GPU is achievable via skl2onnx for scikit-learn models and torch.onnx.export in CPU mode for PyTorch. 4GB RAM constraint requires the SDK to avoid materializing full training datasets in memory during export; skl2onnx export operates on the fitted model object only.

backend.service_design: SDK sub-modules: crisisml.features (humanitarian feature engineering helpers), crisisml.evaluation (crisis-stratified CV splitter, threshold-optimized metrics), crisisml.tracking (MLflow integration wrappers with data logging guard), crisisml.export (ONNX export pipeline with metadata manifest), crisisml.deploy (field deployment package builder)
backend.api_patterns: crisisml.features.compute_displacement_rate(df, col_displaced, col_population) -> pd.Series. crisisml.features.rainfall_anomaly_index(df, col_rainfall, baseline_mean, baseline_std) -> pd.Series. crisisml.evaluation.CrisisStratifiedCV(n_splits=5, crisis_col='crisis_id'). crisisml.export.to_onnx(model, input_schema, output_path, metadata) -> OnnxExportResult. crisisml.deploy.build_field_package(onnx_path, metadata, output_dir) -> FieldPackage.
backend.failure_handling: Feature helpers raise descriptive ValueError with column name and DataFrame shape in message for missing or incompatible columns. ONNX export raises OnnxExportError with conversion trace for unsupported model operations. CrisisStratifiedCV raises InsufficientCrisesError if fewer than n_splits unique crisis IDs exist in the dataset.

data.entities: FeatureMatrix: pd.DataFrame with standardized column naming convention (crisis_id, period, feature columns). OnnxExportResult: {onnx_path: str, input_schema: dict, output_schema: dict, opset_version: int, export_timestamp: str}. ModelMetadata: {model_id: str, model_type: str, crisis_types: list[str], feature_names: list[str], accuracy_by_severity: dict, training_period: str, deployment_prerequisites: str}. FieldPackage: {package_dir: str, manifest_path: str, onnx_path: str, inference_script_path: str, readme_path: str}.
data.schema_notes: FeatureMatrix column naming convention: {indicator}_{aggregation}_{period} (e.g., displacement_rate_7d, rainfall_anomaly_z_30d, market_price_deviation_pct_14d). All feature helpers return pd.Series with the conventional column name as the series name for automatic DataFrame integration.
data.migration_notes: Teams migrating from ad-hoc feature scripts: SDK provides a FeatureAudit.compare(existing_df, sdk_computed_df) utility that reports column name mismatches and value deviation to guide migration without breaking existing experiment reproducibility.

security.secrets_management: No credentials handled by the SDK. MLflow tracking URI configured by the consumer; SDK never touches MLflow authentication.
security.privacy_controls: crisisml.tracking.log_metrics() and log_params() wrappers validate that no argument is a DataFrame, numpy array, or file path to a data file before delegating to mlflow; raises DataLoggingGuardError on violation to prevent raw data persistence in MLflow artifact store.
security.security_risks: Risk: consumers log model ONNX files containing training data embeddings via MLflow artifacts; SDK cannot detect this. Mitigated by documentation guidance and a --check-artifacts flag on the field package builder that scans for unexpected large artifact files.

devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions on PR: ruff check, mypy --strict, pytest with Python 3.10/3.11/3.12 matrix, pytest-cov with 85% gate. On version tag: build with python -m build, publish to PyPI via OIDC trusted publishing (no API token secrets). Test fixtures use synthetic humanitarian crisis datasets generated by a conftest fixture.
devops.environments: No deployment environments. Test datasets: synthetic displacement, rainfall, and market price DataFrames generated deterministically from a fixed seed in conftest.py.
devops.devops_risks: Risk: skl2onnx version pinning conflicts with scikit-learn major version upgrades; mitigated by CI running against pinned and latest scikit-learn in separate test jobs.

ISS-001.title: CrisisStratifiedCV leaks crisis events across train/test splits
ISS-001.detail: Round 1 splitter partitioned rows randomly without grouping by crisis_id, allowing observations from the same crisis event to appear in both train and test sets, producing unrealistically high cross-validation scores.

history.note: Implemented GroupKFold-based splitter grouping by crisis_id

ISS-002.title: ONNX export fails silently for ensemble models
ISS-002.detail: Round 2 ONNX export pipeline raised a generic RuntimeError for RandomForest and GradientBoosting models rather than a typed OnnxExportError with actionable guidance on which model operations are unsupported.

history.note: Identified in evaluation review

ISS-003.title: DataLoggingGuardError not raised for pandas DataFrame passed as MLflow param
ISS-003.detail: Round 2 tracking wrappers checked for DataFrame type in metric values but not in parameter dicts, allowing a DataFrame repr string to be logged as an MLflow parameter bypassing the guard.

focus_issues.title: ONNX export fails silently for ensemble models
focus_issues.detail: Export pipeline raises generic RuntimeError for RandomForest and GradientBoosting rather than typed OnnxExportError with actionable guidance.

focus_issues.title: DataLoggingGuardError not raised for DataFrame in MLflow params
focus_issues.detail: Guard checks metric values but not parameter dicts, allowing DataFrame repr to be logged.

latest_recommendations: Wrap skl2onnx conversion in a typed OnnxExportError with a supported_model_types class attribute listing compatible estimators
Extend DataLoggingGuard to check all values in the params dict, not only metric values

previous_plan.title: Crisis Response ML SDK Architecture v2
previous_plan.executive_summary: Python SDK providing standardized feature engineering helpers for humanitarian crisis indicators, CrisisStratifiedCV (now using GroupKFold grouped by crisis_id), evaluation metrics, MLflow tracking wrappers with DataLoggingGuard, and ONNX export with metadata manifest and field deployment package builder.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Pure Python library with sub-modules crisisml.features, crisisml.evaluation, crisisml.tracking, crisisml.export, crisisml.deploy. ISS-001 resolved: CrisisStratifiedCV uses sklearn.model_selection.GroupKFold keyed by crisis_id column. ONNX export still raises generic RuntimeError for unsupported model types.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: DataLoggingGuard wraps mlflow.log_metrics() and checks metric values for DataFrame type. Parameter dict values not yet checked.
previous_plan.data_model: FeatureMatrix, OnnxExportResult, ModelMetadata, FieldPackage in-memory structures. Feature naming convention indicator_aggregation_period.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 3 revision for the crisis response ML SDK. ISS-002 (generic ONNX export error for ensemble models) and ISS-003 (DataLoggingGuard missing param dict check) are the focus issues. For ISS-002: wrap skl2onnx conversion in a try/except that raises OnnxExportError with supported_model_types class attribute listing the compatible estimators. For ISS-003: extend DataLoggingGuard to recursively validate all values in both metric and param dicts. ISS-001 was resolved in round 2. Architecture is solid - this revision is targeted error handling and security hardening.

fix_report.action_taken: Wrapped skl2onnx.convert_sklearn() in a typed OnnxExportError exception. OnnxExportError.supported_model_types class attribute lists all tested compatible scikit-learn estimators. Error message includes the offending model class name, the underlying skl2onnx failure reason, and a link to the SDK docs page for ONNX compatibility. Updated system_components, api_design, and development_guidelines.
fix_report.expected_outcome: RandomForest and GradientBoosting users receive an actionable error identifying that ensemble models require the sklearn-onnx extra rather than failing with a generic RuntimeError

fix_report.action_taken: Extended DataLoggingGuard to recursively inspect all values in both metrics dict and params dict passed to log_metrics() and log_params() wrappers. Added isinstance checks for pd.DataFrame, np.ndarray, and file path strings ending in .csv/.parquet. Raises DataLoggingGuardError with the parameter name and detected type. Updated security_and_compliance and system_components.
fix_report.expected_outcome: DataFrame repr strings passed as MLflow parameters are caught before reaching the MLflow tracking server, closing the data persistence bypass

target_output.title: Crisis Response ML SDK Architecture v3
target_output.executive_summary: A Python SDK published to PyPI enabling humanitarian data scientists to build, evaluate, and deploy crisis response predictive models with standardized tools. Provides domain-specific feature engineering helpers for displacement, rainfall anomaly, and market price indicators; a CrisisStratifiedCV cross-validator that groups observations by crisis_id to prevent data leakage; threshold-optimized evaluation metrics by crisis severity tier; MLflow tracking wrappers with recursive DataLoggingGuard; and an ONNX export pipeline with typed OnnxExportError diagnostics and a field deployment package builder for low-resource field environments.
target_output.architecture_overview: Pure Python library with five sub-modules: crisisml.features provides humanitarian indicator computation functions operating on pandas DataFrames. crisisml.evaluation provides CrisisStratifiedCV (GroupKFold by crisis_id) and threshold-optimized precision/recall metrics. crisisml.tracking wraps MLflow logging with a recursive DataLoggingGuard preventing raw data persistence. crisisml.export wraps skl2onnx/torch.onnx with typed OnnxExportError diagnostics and generates a JSON metadata manifest per model export. crisisml.deploy builds a self-contained FieldPackage containing the ONNX model, metadata manifest, and a minimal CPU inference script suitable for 4GB RAM field hardware. No persistent storage in the SDK; all state managed by the consumer.
target_output.technology_stack: Language: Python 3.12 (compatible with 3.10 and 3.11). Core dependencies: numpy, pandas, scikit-learn, skl2onnx, onnx. Optional dependencies: mlflow (crisisml.tracking), torch (PyTorch ONNX export). Dev dependencies: pytest, pytest-cov, ruff, mypy --strict, hypothesis for property-based tests of feature helpers. CI: GitHub Actions matrix on Python 3.10/3.11/3.12. Distribution: PyPI via OIDC trusted publishing on version tags using python-semantic-release.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Feature Engineering (crisisml.features): compute_displacement_rate(df, displaced_col, population_col), rainfall_anomaly_index(df, rainfall_col, baseline_mean, baseline_std) returning z-score indexed series, market_price_deviation(df, price_col, reference_price_col) as percentage deviation, population_density_grid(df, lat_col, lon_col, count_col, grid_size_km). Evaluation (crisisml.evaluation): CrisisStratifiedCV with GroupKFold by crisis_id, threshold_precision_recall(y_true, y_prob, thresholds) for ROC-style crisis threshold selection, f1_by_severity(y_true, y_pred, severity_col). Tracking (crisisml.tracking): log_metrics(), log_params() with recursive DataLoggingGuard, log_model_metadata(). Export (crisisml.export): to_onnx(model, input_schema, output_path, metadata) with OnnxExportError diagnostics. Deploy (crisisml.deploy): build_field_package(onnx_path, metadata, output_dir) producing a zip artifact.
target_output.system_components: crisisml.features module: five indicator computation functions, each accepting a pandas DataFrame and returning a pd.Series with a standardized column name. crisisml.evaluation module: CrisisStratifiedCV wrapping sklearn GroupKFold, threshold_precision_recall, f1_by_severity, FeatureAudit.compare(). crisisml.tracking module: DataLoggingGuard class with recursive _check_value(v) traversing dicts/lists; MLflowTracker wrapping mlflow.log_metrics() and log_params(). crisisml.export module: OnnxExporter class, OnnxExportError(supported_model_types, model_class, underlying_error) typed exception, MetadataManifest dataclass. crisisml.deploy module: FieldPackageBuilder assembling ONNX file, metadata JSON, and inference_script.py into a dated zip archive.
target_output.workflows: Model Development Workflow: Data scientist imports crisisml.features, computes displacement_rate and rainfall_anomaly_index columns from field monitoring DataFrames, assembles a FeatureMatrix, and trains a scikit-learn classifier. Uses CrisisStratifiedCV to cross-validate, ensuring observations from the same crisis event are never split across folds. Calls crisisml.evaluation.threshold_precision_recall to select an operating threshold that meets the organization's minimum recall requirement for high-severity crisis events. Model Export Workflow: Data scientist calls crisisml.export.to_onnx(trained_classifier, input_schema, 'models/cholera_risk_v3.onnx', metadata). If the model is a RandomForest, OnnxExportError is raised with supported_model_types=['LogisticRegression','Ridge','SVC','Pipeline'] and a message directing the user to install the sklearn-onnx[ensemble] extra or wrap in a Pipeline. On success, a ModelMetadata JSON manifest is written alongside the ONNX file. Field Deployment Workflow: Engineer runs crisisml.deploy.build_field_package('models/cholera_risk_v3.onnx', metadata, 'dist/') which produces dist/cholera_risk_v3_20260315.zip containing the ONNX model, metadata manifest, a minimal onnxruntime inference script requiring only 200MB RAM, and a README with deployment prerequisites.
target_output.data_model: FeatureMatrix: pd.DataFrame with columns following {indicator}_{aggregation}_{period} convention (displacement_rate_7d, rainfall_anomaly_z_30d, market_price_deviation_pct_14d, crisis_id, period_start). OnnxExportResult: {onnx_path: str, input_schema: dict[str, str], output_schema: dict[str, str], opset_version: int, export_timestamp: str, model_size_bytes: int}. ModelMetadata: {model_id: str, model_type: str, crisis_types: list[str], feature_names: list[str], accuracy_by_severity: dict[str, float], cv_score_mean: float, cv_score_std: float, training_period: str, deployment_prerequisites: str}. FieldPackage: {package_path: str, manifest: ModelMetadata, onnx_filename: str, inference_script: str, created_at: str}.
target_output.api_design: crisisml.features: compute_displacement_rate(df: pd.DataFrame, displaced_col: str, population_col: str) -> pd.Series. rainfall_anomaly_index(df, rainfall_col, baseline_mean, baseline_std) -> pd.Series. market_price_deviation(df, price_col, reference_col) -> pd.Series. crisisml.evaluation: CrisisStratifiedCV(n_splits=5, crisis_col='crisis_id', random_state=42). threshold_precision_recall(y_true, y_prob, thresholds) -> ThresholdMetrics. crisisml.tracking: MLflowTracker(tracking_uri=None). tracker.log_metrics(metrics: dict). tracker.log_params(params: dict). raises DataLoggingGuardError(param_name, detected_type) if DataFrame/ndarray detected in any value. crisisml.export: to_onnx(model, input_schema: dict[str,str], output_path: str, metadata: ModelMetadata) -> OnnxExportResult. OnnxExportError(model_class, supported_model_types, detail). crisisml.deploy: build_field_package(onnx_path, metadata, output_dir) -> FieldPackage.
target_output.security_and_compliance: DataLoggingGuard recursive check: _check_value(v) raises DataLoggingGuardError if isinstance(v, (pd.DataFrame, np.ndarray)) or if isinstance(v, str) and any(v.endswith(ext) for ext in ['.csv','.parquet','.feather']). Applied to all values in both metrics and params dicts before delegating to mlflow. ONNX export writes only to user-specified local output_path; no automatic cloud upload. SDK never reads training data from disk; consumers pass DataFrames from their own pipelines. No telemetry or usage reporting. Documentation includes a data handling guide specifying that only scalar metrics and model parameters should be logged via SDK tracking wrappers.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: GitHub Actions CI on pull request: ruff check, mypy --strict, pytest with Python 3.10/3.11/3.12 matrix, pytest-cov with 85% gate, hypothesis property tests for feature helpers. Separate CI job running pytest with pinned latest scikit-learn and pinned skl2onnx to detect version compatibility breaks early. On version tag: python-semantic-release increments version from conventional commits, builds source and wheel distribution, publishes to PyPI via GitHub OIDC trusted publishing (no API token secrets required). Test fixtures: conftest.py generates synthetic FeatureMatrix DataFrames with realistic displacement and rainfall distributions using a fixed random seed.
target_output.observability: crisisml.tracking.MLflowTracker logs model_id, feature_names, cv_score_mean, cv_score_std, and all ModelMetadata scalar fields to MLflow on log_model_metadata() call. SDK-internal events logged via Python logging at DEBUG level: feature computation column names, ONNX export opset version, field package output path. FieldPackage README includes a model performance summary table from ModelMetadata.accuracy_by_severity for field staff context. OnnxExportError includes a structured dict of diagnostic fields for programmatic error handling in automated training pipelines.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Zero infrastructure cost; entirely local library. PyPI hosting free for public packages. GitHub Actions free tier covers CI matrix builds (approximately 15 build-minutes per PR across 3 Python versions). ONNX inference on field hardware runs in under 50ms for the target model types (logistic regression, shallow trees) on 4GB RAM x86_64 machines, validated by the benchmark fixture in the test suite.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase v0.1.0 (6 weeks): crisisml.features with displacement_rate, rainfall_anomaly_index, market_price_deviation helpers, crisisml.evaluation with CrisisStratifiedCV (GroupKFold) and threshold_precision_recall, crisisml.tracking with DataLoggingGuard covering both metrics and params, crisisml.export with to_onnx and typed OnnxExportError, pytest suite with 85% coverage, PyPI publish via OIDC. Phase v0.2.0 (4 weeks): crisisml.deploy FieldPackageBuilder, population_density_grid feature helper, f1_by_severity metric, FeatureAudit.compare() migration utility, hypothesis property tests for all feature helpers. Phase v0.3.0 (4 weeks): PyTorch ONNX export support, MLflow model registry integration, model card generator producing a structured accountability report from ModelMetadata.
target_output.development_guidelines: All public functions annotated with full type hints; mypy --strict enforced in CI. ruff for linting and import sorting. pytest fixtures for synthetic FeatureMatrix DataFrames parameterized by crisis type and indicator distribution. hypothesis strategies for property-based testing of feature helpers (e.g., displacement_rate must return values in [0, 1] for valid input ranges). OnnxExportError must always include supported_model_types as a class attribute for programmatic handling. DataLoggingGuard tests must cover nested dict and list traversal for params. Conventional commits required for python-semantic-release version bumping.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: skl2onnx version pinning conflicts with scikit-learn major upgrades breaking ONNX export for previously working models; mitigated by CI job testing against latest released skl2onnx and a pinned compatibility matrix table in the docs. Risk: hypothesis tests for feature helpers may be slow on large search spaces; mitigated by setting max_examples=50 for CI runs. Tradeoff: MLflow as an optional dependency rather than a hard dependency increases SDK installation flexibility but requires consumers to install crisisml[tracking] explicitly; accepted given that many local notebook experiments do not need a tracking server. Tradeoff: DataLoggingGuard file path detection by extension is heuristic and may produce false positives for legitimate string parameters ending in .csv; documented with a bypass mechanism for tested safe cases.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Selected skl2onnx over manual ONNX graph construction for scikit-learn export to maintain compatibility with the skl2onnx operator support matrix. Confirmed GroupKFold over StratifiedGroupKFold for CrisisStratifiedCV to prioritize crisis event isolation over class balance in CV splits. Chose OIDC trusted publishing over API token for PyPI to eliminate rotating secret management. hypothesis added for property tests rather than parametrize to discover edge cases in feature helper boundary conditions.

metadata.notes: Round 3 revision fixing typed ONNX export errors and extending DataLoggingGuard for the crisis response ML SDK.

project_goal.value: Security-hardened desktop application for protection monitors to record and manage sensitive witness testimonies, survivor accounts, and evidence documentation related to human rights violations, with strict access controls and encrypted local storage
project_goal.rationale: Existing spreadsheet-based approach creates unacceptable security risk for witness identity and evidence integrity

target_users.value: Humanitarian protection monitors, documentation officers, and authorized legal review staff operating on air-gapped field laptops
target_users.rationale: Small closed user group in field environments with no reliable internet connectivity

project_class.rationale: Air-gapped field deployment with local encrypted storage; no cloud or server component

capabilities.rationale: Full local application stack with Electron frontend, Rust backend, SQLCipher encrypted database, and hardware-key authentication

complexity_level.rationale: Cryptographic key management, hardware security key integration, evidence chain-of-custody, and immutable audit trail are non-trivial requirements

risk_level.rationale: Compromise of witness identities or testimony content could endanger lives; evidence integrity is legally required for accountability proceedings

data_sensitivity.rationale: Witness testimonies and evidence files are protected under international humanitarian law and organizational data classification policies

external_exposure.rationale: No network connectivity; all data stored locally on encrypted SQLCipher database; export only via signed and encrypted package

access_model.value: Per-user hardware security key (YubiKey) required for application unlock; RBAC within application for monitor, reviewer, and admin roles
access_model.rationale: Hardware key prevents unauthorized access even if laptop is seized; RBAC limits evidence access to need-to-know

feature_scope.value: Testimony recording with multimedia evidence attachment, witness identity pseudonymization, violation categorization, case classification, cryptographically signed evidence export, immutable audit trail
feature_scope.rationale: Full documentation lifecycle from intake through legal submission

mvp_scope.value: Testimony recording, evidence attachment, SQLCipher encrypted storage, YubiKey authentication, local audit log
mvp_scope.rationale: Core secure capture capability before export and review features

security_baseline.value: SQLCipher AES-256 full database encryption, YubiKey FIDO2 for application authentication, per-record encryption of witness identity fields, append-only audit log, digitally signed evidence export packages
security_baseline.rationale: Evidence chain-of-custody and witness protection requirements

frontend_stack.value: Electron with React and TypeScript
frontend_stack.rationale: Cross-platform desktop with web technology stack; team expertise

backend_stack.value: Rust with Actix-web for local IPC server
backend_stack.rationale: Memory safety and cryptographic library ecosystem in Rust; Actix-web for Electron-to-backend IPC over localhost

data_platform.value: SQLite 3 with SQLCipher encryption extension
data_platform.rationale: Local-only database with AES-256 full database encryption; no server dependency

hosting_target.value: Packaged Electron desktop installer for air-gapped field laptops
hosting_target.rationale: Offline-first deployment with no cloud hosting

privacy_retention_policy.value: Local data retained until export and secure deletion; witness identity fields pseudonymized by default; case records never leave device except via signed export package
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Witness protection and evidence security require local-only retention with controlled export

future_scope.value: Encrypted peer-to-peer sync between authorized devices, integration with legal case management systems via export adapter
future_scope.rationale: Future secure multi-device workflows after core secure capture is stable

constraints.value: Must operate fully offline; no cloud dependencies at runtime; YubiKey must be physically present to unlock application; Electron + Rust IPC must not expose localhost port beyond loopback
constraints.rationale: Air-gapped security model and physical authentication requirements

observability_baseline.value: Local audit log only; no external monitoring; audit log must be tamper-evident and append-only
observability_baseline.rationale: Air-gapped environment prohibits external monitoring; local audit log satisfies accountability requirements

execution_preference.value: Milestone-based delivery with security review gate between each phase
execution_preference.rationale: High-risk application requires security checkpoint before adding features

compliance_context.value: International criminal law evidence handling standards (ICC Rules of Procedure and Evidence), witness protection protocols, organizational data classification policies for confidential material
compliance_context.rationale: Evidence must meet legal admissibility standards; witness identities protected under international law

project.goal: Air-gapped desktop app for secure witness testimony recording and evidence management
project.target_users: Protection monitors and documentation officers on field laptops

frontend.state: Zustand for application state, React Query for local IPC calls

backend.api: Local HTTP over loopback, not exposed beyond 127.0.0.1

security.auth: YubiKey FIDO2 hardware key required for unlock
security.encryption: SQLCipher AES-256 full database, per-record witness identity encryption
security.audit: Append-only local audit log with INSERT-only DB role

data.files: Multimedia evidence stored in encrypted app data directory with AES-256 file encryption
data.export: Digitally signed and encrypted export packages only

devops.distribution: USB-based installer distribution for air-gapped environments

constraints.localhost_only: Rust IPC server must bind to 127.0.0.1 only

reasoner_reviews.product: By round 4 the core testimony recording and YubiKey authentication are stable. The outstanding issue from round 3 is that the digitally signed evidence export package format has not been fully specified -- the round 3 plan describes the feature but does not define the signature scheme, key management for export signing, or package format. This is the blocking gap for legal admissibility.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: The Rust + Actix-web localhost IPC architecture is correct for Electron-to-backend communication. The unresolved question is how the signing private key is managed for evidence exports. Using the user's YubiKey PIV slot for export signing is architecturally consistent with the existing hardware key approach and avoids storing a software signing key on disk.
reasoner_reviews.security: The round 3 plan proposed using an Ed25519 software key stored in the SQLCipher database for export signing. This is insufficient -- a software key stored on the same encrypted device that holds the evidence can be extracted if SQLCipher is compromised. The YubiKey PIV slot approach moves signing to tamper-resistant hardware.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: The YubiKey PIV slot signing approach requires the yubikey-piv-tool Rust bindings. This adds a dependency but is acceptable given the security requirement. The export package format should be documented to enable legal review teams to independently verify signatures.

backend.service_design: Rust Actix-web server binding exclusively to 127.0.0.1 with dynamic port negotiated at startup. Modules: testimony_handler (CRUD for testimonies and evidence), auth_handler (YubiKey FIDO2 challenge-response and session management), export_handler (evidence package signing and assembly), audit_handler (append-only audit log writes).
backend.api_patterns: Local IPC HTTP over loopback. POST /api/testimonies, GET /api/testimonies/{id}, POST /api/testimonies/{id}/evidence, POST /api/export/{case_id} (triggers signing and package assembly). All endpoints require valid session token from YubiKey authentication.
backend.failure_handling: YubiKey not present returns 401 with hardware_key_required error code. SQLCipher open failure on wrong passphrase returns 403. Evidence file write failure rolls back testimony record via SQLite transaction. Export signing failure aborts package assembly and logs to audit.
backend.background_jobs: No background jobs; all operations synchronous and user-initiated.

frontend.app_structure: Electron main process manages YubiKey detection, window lifecycle, and IPC bridge. Renderer process is React 18 SPA communicating with Rust backend via localhost fetch calls. Application locked state shows YubiKey challenge screen before unlocking main interface.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Lock screen with YubiKey challenge-response. Dashboard showing case list and recent testimony activity. Testimony recording form with violation type selector, narrative fields, witness pseudonym, and multimedia evidence upload. Case detail view with evidence timeline. Export wizard for signing and packaging a case for legal submission.
frontend.state_management: Zustand store for session state, YubiKey presence status, and application lock state. React Query for testimony and evidence data fetched from Rust backend.

security.auth_design: Electron main process enumerates USB devices on startup to detect YubiKey presence. YubiKey FIDO2 challenge-response performed via node-hid bindings. On successful authentication, Rust backend issues a short-lived session token stored in memory only, never persisted to SQLCipher.
security.authorization_model: RBAC within application: monitor role can create testimonies and attach evidence; reviewer role can read all testimonies but not create; admin role manages user accounts and can perform exports. Role stored in users table within SQLCipher.
security.secrets_management: SQLCipher database key derived from YubiKey HMAC challenge-response combined with a device-specific entropy value. No static passphrase stored on disk. Export signing uses YubiKey PIV slot 9c (digital signature) via Rust yubikey crate bindings.

data.entities: testimonies (id UUID PK, case_id FK, witness_pseudonym VARCHAR, testimony_date DATE, violation_type ENUM torture/enforced_disappearance/killing/displacement/sexual_violence/other, narrative_encrypted BYTEA, location_code VARCHAR, status ENUM draft/finalized, created_by UUID, created_at TIMESTAMP). witnesses (id UUID PK, pseudonym VARCHAR UNIQUE, identity_encrypted BYTEA, risk_tier ENUM critical/high/medium, consent_recorded BOOL). evidence_files (id UUID PK, testimony_id FK, file_path_encrypted VARCHAR, file_type VARCHAR, hash_sha256 VARCHAR, encrypted BOOL DEFAULT true, attached_at TIMESTAMP). access_logs (id UUID PK, user_id FK, action VARCHAR, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, timestamp TIMESTAMP) -- INSERT-only role.
data.storage_design: SQLite 3 with SQLCipher extension providing AES-256 full database encryption keyed from YubiKey HMAC. Evidence multimedia files stored in app data directory with per-file AES-256-GCM encryption using keys stored in SQLCipher. Export packages assembled in temporary directory, signed, then moved to user-specified output path.
data.migration_notes: Schema migrations applied via embedded Rust migration runner at application startup. Migration files shipped with application installer.

devops.deployment_topology: No server infrastructure. Electron Builder produces platform-specific installers (Windows NSIS, macOS DMG, Linux AppImage). Installers distributed via USB to air-gapped field laptops by field IT coordinators.
devops.ci_cd_design: GitHub Actions: Rust cargo test, React Jest tests, electron-builder for all three platform targets on version tag. Installer artifacts uploaded to GitHub Releases draft for manual security review before distribution USB preparation.
devops.observability_stack: Local audit log only. access_logs table in SQLCipher with INSERT-only role. Application startup and shutdown events logged. No external telemetry; Electron autoUpdater disabled.

ISS-001.title: Evidence export signing scheme insufficient -- software key on disk
ISS-001.detail: Round 3 plan specifies Ed25519 software signing key stored in SQLCipher. Security review determined this is insufficient for legal admissibility: if SQLCipher is compromised, the signing key is extracted along with testimony data, invalidating chain-of-custody claims.

history.note: First raised in round 2 security review: export signing scheme not defined.
Round 3 proposed software Ed25519 key in SQLCipher -- security review rejected as insufficient.

ISS-002.title: Export package format not documented for legal verification
ISS-002.detail: Legal review teams need to independently verify the signature on evidence export packages. Round 3 does not document the package format, signature verification procedure, or public key distribution mechanism for verifiers.

history.note: Raised by product reviewer: legal admissibility requires documented verification procedure.

focus_issues.title: Evidence export signing scheme insufficient -- software key on disk
focus_issues.detail: Round 3 proposed Ed25519 software key in SQLCipher; security review requires hardware-backed signing via YubiKey PIV slot.

focus_issues.title: Export package format not documented for legal verification
focus_issues.detail: Legal verification teams cannot independently verify signatures without documented package format and public key distribution.

latest_recommendations: Replace software Ed25519 signing key with YubiKey PIV slot 9c signing via Rust yubikey crate
Document evidence export package format with JSON manifest, file inventory, and Ed25519/PIV signature; specify public key distribution for verifiers
