previous_plan.title: Witness Testimony Documentation Desktop App - Round 3 Architecture
previous_plan.executive_summary: Air-gapped Electron desktop application for witness testimony recording with SQLCipher AES-256 database encryption, YubiKey FIDO2 authentication, per-record witness identity encryption, and evidence export packages signed with an Ed25519 software key stored in SQLCipher.
previous_plan.architecture_overview: Electron React frontend communicates over localhost loopback to Rust Actix-web backend. SQLite 3 with SQLCipher stores testimonies, witnesses, evidence metadata, and audit log. YubiKey FIDO2 challenge-response unlocks application and derives the SQLCipher database key via HMAC. Evidence files stored encrypted in app data directory. Export packages assembled and signed with Ed25519 key stored in SQLCipher.
previous_plan.security_and_compliance: SQLCipher AES-256 full database encryption with key derived from YubiKey HMAC. YubiKey FIDO2 required for application unlock. Per-record AES-256-GCM encryption of narrative and witness identity fields. INSERT-only audit log role. Evidence export packages signed with Ed25519 software key stored in SQLCipher.

target_output.thinking_summary: Round 4 resolves two issues from the witness testimony app. ISS-001 requires replacing the software Ed25519 signing key with YubiKey PIV slot 9c hardware-backed signing via the Rust yubikey crate -- this moves the signing key to tamper-resistant hardware co-located with the authentication key. ISS-002 requires documenting the export package format and public key distribution to enable independent legal verification. Both fixes are targeted and well-scoped.

fix_report.action_taken: Replaced Ed25519 software signing key stored in SQLCipher with YubiKey PIV slot 9c hardware-backed signing. Rust export_handler now invokes yubikey crate to request a PIV signature from the physical key present at export time. Only the YubiKey certificate public key is stored in SQLCipher for inclusion in export packages. No signing private key material ever leaves the YubiKey hardware.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Evidence export signatures are generated by tamper-resistant hardware; compromise of SQLCipher database cannot yield the signing key, satisfying legal chain-of-custody requirements for evidence admissibility

fix_report.action_taken: Documented evidence export package format: tar.gz archive containing manifest.json (case metadata, record inventory, timestamp, signer certificate), encrypted evidence files, and a detached .sig file containing the PIV signature over the SHA-256 hash of all other archive contents. Public key distribution specified via organizational PKI: signer certificate issued by organizational CA distributed to legal review teams out-of-band. Documented in open_questions_resolved and deployment_and_operations.
fix_report.expected_outcome: Legal review teams can independently verify export package integrity and authenticity using the documented format, signer certificate, and standard signature verification tools

target_output.title: Witness Testimony Documentation Desktop App - Round 4 Architecture
target_output.executive_summary: A security-hardened air-gapped Electron desktop application for humanitarian protection monitors to record witness testimonies and manage evidence documentation. SQLCipher AES-256 database encryption with YubiKey HMAC-derived keys, per-record witness identity encryption, and hardware-backed evidence export signing via YubiKey PIV slot 9c provide defense-in-depth for confidential testimony data. Round 4 resolves the signing scheme by moving from a software key to the YubiKey PIV hardware slot and documents the export package format for legal verification.
target_output.architecture_overview: Electron main process handles YubiKey USB detection, challenge-response authentication, and PIV slot access for export signing. Renderer process is a React 18 SPA communicating over localhost loopback to a Rust Actix-web backend bound exclusively to 127.0.0.1. The Rust backend manages SQLCipher database operations, per-record field encryption, evidence file management, and export package assembly. Database encryption key is derived from a YubiKey HMAC challenge-response combined with device entropy -- no static passphrase on disk. Evidence export packages are signed using the YubiKey PIV slot 9c private key, which never leaves the hardware token. Audit log writes use an INSERT-only SQLite role preventing alteration of access records.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Electron, React 18, TypeScript, Zustand, React Query. Backend: Rust, Actix-web (loopback only). Database: SQLite 3 with SQLCipher AES-256 extension. Hardware: YubiKey FIDO2 (auth) and PIV slot 9c (export signing) via Rust yubikey crate. Crypto: SQLCipher for DB, AES-256-GCM for evidence files, YubiKey PIV RSA-2048 for export signatures. Packaging: Electron Builder for Windows NSIS, macOS DMG, Linux AppImage. CI/CD: GitHub Actions.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Application Unlock: YubiKey FIDO2 challenge-response required; SQLCipher key derived from HMAC response. Testimony Recording: violation type classification (ICC categories), pseudonymized witness identifier, encrypted narrative capture, location coding. Evidence Management: multimedia file attachment with per-file AES-256-GCM encryption, SHA-256 integrity hash storage. Case Classification: case_classifications linking testimonies to case files with access tier. Evidence Export: YubiKey PIV slot 9c signs export package; package format documented for legal verification. Audit Trail: append-only access_logs table with INSERT-only DB role, capturing every read and write to testimony and evidence records.
target_output.system_components: Electron main process (YubiKey USB enumeration, FIDO2 challenge, PIV signing interface), React 18 renderer SPA (testimony UI, export wizard), Rust Actix-web IPC server (127.0.0.1 loopback only), SQLite 3 with SQLCipher (testimonies, witnesses, evidence_files, access_logs, export_records), encrypted evidence file store in app data directory, Electron Builder packaging pipeline, GitHub Actions CI.
target_output.workflows: Testimony Recording: monitor unlocks application with YubiKey FIDO2, creates new testimony record with violation type and witness pseudonym, dictates or types narrative (encrypted before write), attaches multimedia evidence files (each encrypted with AES-256-GCM), finalizes record; audit log entry appended. Evidence Export: authorized staff member opens export wizard, selects case, application requests YubiKey PIV slot 9c signing, Rust backend assembles tar.gz package with manifest.json, encrypted evidence files, and detached .sig file containing PIV signature over SHA-256 of archive contents, export_records entry appended to audit log, package written to user-specified output path for USB transfer. Legal Verification: receiver extracts package, verifies .sig against manifest hash using signer certificate from organizational PKI distribution, decrypts evidence files with session key provided via secure channel.
target_output.data_model: testimonies (id UUID PK, case_id FK, witness_pseudonym VARCHAR, testimony_date DATE, violation_type ENUM torture/enforced_disappearance/killing/displacement/sexual_violence/other, narrative_encrypted BYTEA, location_code VARCHAR, status ENUM draft/finalized, created_by UUID FK, created_at TIMESTAMP). witnesses (id UUID PK, pseudonym VARCHAR UNIQUE, identity_encrypted BYTEA, risk_tier ENUM critical/high/medium, consent_recorded BOOL, created_at TIMESTAMP). evidence_files (id UUID PK, testimony_id UUID FK, file_path_encrypted VARCHAR, file_type VARCHAR, hash_sha256 CHAR(64), file_key_encrypted BYTEA, attached_at TIMESTAMP). case_classifications (id UUID PK, case_ref VARCHAR, testimony_id FK, access_tier ENUM restricted/confidential/secret). export_records (id UUID PK, case_id VARCHAR, exported_by UUID FK, package_hash_sha256 CHAR(64), piv_signature_present BOOL, exported_at TIMESTAMP). access_logs (id UUID PK, user_id UUID FK, action VARCHAR, resource_type VARCHAR, resource_id UUID, timestamp TIMESTAMP) -- INSERT-only via audit_writer role.
target_output.api_design: Local loopback IPC only. POST /api/auth/challenge returns FIDO2 challenge nonce. POST /api/auth/verify body {credential_response} validates YubiKey assertion and issues session token. POST /api/testimonies creates testimony record. GET /api/testimonies/{id} returns decrypted testimony for authorized session. POST /api/testimonies/{id}/evidence attaches encrypted file. POST /api/export/{case_id} triggers PIV signing and package assembly; returns package_path. GET /api/audit returns access_logs for admin role. All endpoints require Authorization: Bearer {session_token} header; session token in-memory only, expires on app lock.
target_output.security_and_compliance: SQLCipher AES-256 database encryption with key derived from YubiKey HMAC-SHA1 challenge-response combined with 32-byte device-specific entropy stored in OS keychain -- no static passphrase on disk. YubiKey FIDO2 required for application unlock; application locks on YubiKey removal. Evidence export packages signed by YubiKey PIV slot 9c (RSA-2048 or ECC P-384 per card configuration) via Rust yubikey crate; private key never leaves hardware token; only signer certificate public key stored in SQLCipher for package inclusion. Per-record AES-256-GCM encryption of narrative_encrypted and identity_encrypted fields with per-record keys stored encrypted in SQLCipher. Multimedia evidence files encrypted with per-file AES-256-GCM keys. INSERT-only access_logs role prevents audit trail alteration. ICC Rules of Procedure evidence standards: chain-of-custody maintained by signed export package format and append-only audit log.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Electron Builder produces platform-specific installers on version tag via GitHub Actions. Installers code-signed with organizational code signing certificate before distribution. Distribution via USB preparation by field IT coordinators for air-gapped laptop deployment. No auto-update capability; updates delivered via new USB-distributed installer. YubiKey PIV slot 9c certificate provisioned by organizational PKI team and loaded onto each monitor's YubiKey before field deployment. Signer certificate public key distributed to legal review teams via organizational PKI certificate bundle for independent export package verification. Evidence export package format: tar.gz containing manifest.json (schema version, case_ref, record_inventory[], timestamp, signer_certificate_pem), encrypted evidence files by hash, and evidence-package.sig (detached RSA/ECC signature over SHA-256 of archive without .sig). Verifiers use openssl dgst -verify with distributed certificate.
target_output.observability: Local audit only. access_logs table captures every testimony read and write, evidence access, export event, and authentication. Append-only enforcement via INSERT-only SQLite role audit_writer. Application startup and shutdown logged with user_id and timestamp. No external telemetry or crash reporting; Electron autoUpdater and Crashlytics disabled. Periodic audit log export to encrypted report file for supervisor review.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: No cloud infrastructure cost. Electron Builder CI runs on GitHub Actions free tier for open-source or standard for private repo. YubiKey hardware cost per monitor approximately $60 per device, provisioned once by organizational IT. SQLite scales to thousands of testimony records without performance concerns. No ongoing operational cost beyond field IT support time.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP: testimony recording, YubiKey FIDO2 auth, SQLCipher encryption, per-record field encryption, INSERT-only audit log, Electron packaging. Phase 2: evidence file attachment with per-file AES-256-GCM, case classification model, reviewer role access. Phase 3: evidence export with YubiKey PIV slot 9c signing resolving ISS-001, documented export package format resolving ISS-002, export_records audit entries, organizational PKI certificate distribution. Phase 4 security review gate before any network-adjacent feature consideration.
target_output.development_guidelines: Rust cargo clippy and cargo test in CI with zero-warning policy. React Jest tests for testimony form validation and export wizard state. SQLCipher schema migrations via embedded migration runner with migration files versioned in repo. No telemetry or logging libraries that phone home; all log output to local SQLCipher audit table. Electron context isolation and nodeIntegration disabled; all Node.js access via preload script with explicit channel allowlist. Code signing certificate required for release builds in CI.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: YubiKey PIV slot already occupied or certificate misconfigured on field device. Mitigation: organizational IT provisioning checklist before field deployment; application startup validates PIV slot accessibility and alerts on missing certificate. Risk: SQLCipher passphrase derivation changes between application versions invalidating existing databases. Mitigation: migration runner preserves derivation parameters in SQLCipher header; version-locked derivation scheme. Tradeoff: YubiKey PIV signing over software key adds hardware dependency but satisfies legal admissibility chain-of-custody requirement. Tradeoff: air-gapped architecture eliminates network attack surface at the cost of manual update distribution overhead.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Resolved ISS-001: YubiKey PIV slot 9c hardware signing replaces Ed25519 software key; private key never stored on disk. Resolved ISS-002: export package format documented as tar.gz with manifest.json, encrypted evidence files, and detached .sig; signer certificate distributed via organizational PKI to legal verifiers. Confirmed no auto-update to preserve air-gapped security model. Selected Electron context isolation with preload channel allowlist over nodeIntegration for renderer security.

metadata.notes: Round 4 fixes: YubiKey PIV slot 9c hardware export signing replacing software key (ISS-001) and documented export package format for legal verification (ISS-002).

project_goal.value: Research prototype evaluating automated compliance checking of humanitarian program reports against SPHERE minimum standards using rule-based and LLM-assisted validation to flag gaps in water, food, shelter, and health service delivery
project_goal.rationale: Manual SPHERE compliance review is time-consuming and inconsistent across sectors; prototype tests feasibility of automated gap detection

target_users.value: Humanitarian program quality assurance officers and sector coordinators evaluating SPHERE compliance of partner program reports
target_users.rationale: Internal research users for prototype evaluation; not deployed to end beneficiaries

project_class.rationale: Feasibility study for automated SPHERE compliance checking; production deployment is a future milestone pending prototype validation

capabilities.rationale: Streamlit frontend for analyst interaction, FastAPI backend for rule engine and LLM integration, MongoDB for flexible document storage, analytics for compliance gap visualization

complexity_level.rationale: Rule-based compliance engine with LLM-assisted narrative analysis; moderate integration complexity but limited user scale for prototype

risk_level.rationale: Internal prototype handling internal program reports; no personal data of beneficiaries; medium risk due to LLM integration and internal data exposure

data_sensitivity.rationale: Program reports contain internal programmatic data; not personal health data of beneficiaries; classified as internal

external_exposure.rationale: Prototype accessible only to internal QA staff via GCP IAM; not exposed to partners or public

access_model.value: GCP IAM for Cloud Run service access; Streamlit authentication via GCP Identity-Aware Proxy for internal staff
access_model.rationale: GCP-native access control without bespoke auth implementation for prototype

feature_scope.value: Program report upload and parsing, rule-based SPHERE indicator validation, LLM-assisted narrative gap extraction, compliance gap report generation by sector and indicator, analyst review and correction interface, compliance dashboard
feature_scope.rationale: Full compliance checking lifecycle for prototype evaluation

mvp_scope.value: WASH sector rule-based compliance checking, LLM narrative gap extraction for WASH, compliance gap report download
mvp_scope.rationale: WASH is the highest-priority sector for the sponsoring team; single-sector MVP validates approach before expanding

security_baseline.value: GCP IAP for Streamlit access, TLS 1.3, no program report data sent to external LLM APIs, internal Vertex AI or local model only
security_baseline.rationale: Internal data must stay within GCP project; no external LLM API permitted for program report content

frontend_stack.value: Streamlit
frontend_stack.rationale: Rapid prototyping framework enabling data scientists to build interactive analyst tools without frontend engineering overhead

backend_stack.rationale: Python for ML/LLM integration and rule engine; FastAPI for clean API layer between Streamlit and processing logic

data_platform.value: MongoDB 7 for flexible document storage of program reports and compliance results
data_platform.rationale: Program reports have variable structure across sectors; document model accommodates schema variability better than relational for prototype

hosting_target.value: GCP Cloud Run for both Streamlit and FastAPI services
hosting_target.rationale: Serverless container deployment suits prototype usage patterns with low idle cost

privacy_retention_policy.value: Program reports retained for prototype evaluation period (6 months); compliance results retained for analysis; no personal beneficiary data stored
privacy_retention_policy.rationale: Time-limited retention for prototype; review and delete after evaluation

future_scope.value: Multi-sector SPHERE coverage (food, shelter, health), production deployment with multi-organization access, integration with OCHA ReliefWeb report feed
future_scope.rationale: Production roadmap pending prototype validation

constraints.value: LLM must run within GCP project (Vertex AI or local model on Cloud Run); no program report content sent to external APIs; prototype budget under $500/month
constraints.rationale: Data residency and cost constraints for prototype phase

observability_baseline.value: Google Cloud Monitoring for Cloud Run service metrics, Cloud Logging for structured API logs, Streamlit usage tracked via Cloud Run request logs
observability_baseline.rationale: Standard GCP monitoring for prototype

execution_preference.value: Rapid iterative prototype delivery with 4-week MVP
execution_preference.rationale: Research prototype needs fast validation cycle

compliance_context.value: SPHERE Handbook 2018 standards as normative reference, OCHA sector reporting guidelines for indicator definitions
compliance_context.rationale: Medium risk and internal data sensitivity; SPHERE standards compliance is the core subject of the prototype

llm_integration.value: Vertex AI Gemini Pro via Google Cloud Vertex AI API for narrative section analysis; model invoked only within GCP project boundary; program report content never sent to external LLM endpoints
llm_integration.rationale: Data residency constraint requires GCP-internal model; Vertex AI Gemini Pro provides sufficient multilingual capability for narrative extraction

project.goal: Research prototype for automated SPHERE compliance checking of humanitarian program reports
project.target_users: Internal QA officers and sector coordinators

frontend.interaction: File upload, compliance gap report view, analyst correction interface

backend.processing: Rule engine for SPHERE indicators, Vertex AI for narrative extraction

security.auth: GCP IAP for Streamlit access
security.data_residency: No external LLM; Vertex AI only

data.storage: GCP Cloud Storage for uploaded report files

reasoner_reviews.product: The WASH-first MVP scope is well-chosen for prototype validation. SPHERE WASH standards have well-defined quantitative thresholds (liters per person per day, latrine coverage ratios) that are highly testable with rule-based checks. The LLM narrative extraction adds value for qualitative sections where quantitative thresholds are not reported explicitly.
reasoner_reviews.architect_reasoner: Streamlit plus FastAPI on Cloud Run is appropriate for prototype scale. MongoDB document model accommodates the varying structure of SPHERE compliance reports across sectors without schema migration overhead during the research phase. Vertex AI Gemini Pro satisfies the GCP data residency constraint while providing strong multilingual narrative understanding.
reasoner_reviews.security: GCP IAP for Streamlit access is the right lightweight auth for an internal prototype. The critical constraint is that program report content must not leave the GCP project boundary -- Vertex AI invocation stays within GCP. Ensure MongoDB Atlas cluster is deployed on GCP and not AWS or Azure to maintain data residency.
reasoner_reviews.constraints: Budget of $500/month is achievable for prototype scale. Cloud Run scales to zero when idle. MongoDB Atlas M10 free tier or M10 shared cluster is sufficient. Vertex AI Gemini Pro cost depends on token volume; for prototype scale (tens of reports per day) estimated under $100/month.
reasoner_reviews.critic: The rule engine loading SPHERE standards as structured rules is the highest implementation risk. SPHERE thresholds are defined in natural language in the Handbook; formalizing them as machine-readable rules requires careful curation. The prototype should treat the rule corpus as versioned configuration rather than hard-coded logic to enable iteration.

backend.service_design: FastAPI application with four route groups: /upload (report ingestion and parsing), /compliance (rule-based and LLM-assisted validation), /results (gap report retrieval and export), /admin (sphere_standards rule management). Compliance checks orchestrated as async tasks via background task queue.
backend.api_patterns: RESTful. POST /v1/reports/upload multipart form with PDF/DOCX/JSON report file. POST /v1/reports/{id}/check triggers compliance evaluation. GET /v1/reports/{id}/results returns structured gap_findings with indicator codes. GET /v1/reports/{id}/results/export returns downloadable gap report PDF.
backend.failure_handling: Vertex AI API timeout returns partial results with llm_status=timeout and flags narrative sections as requires_manual_review. MongoDB write failure on result persistence retries 3 times before returning 503 with job_id for status polling.
backend.background_jobs: Compliance evaluation runs as FastAPI BackgroundTask: parse report, apply rule checks per SPHERE indicator, invoke Vertex AI for narrative sections, persist validation_results to MongoDB. Status polled via GET /v1/reports/{id}/status.

frontend.app_structure: Streamlit multi-page app with pages: Upload Report, View Compliance Results, Analytics Dashboard. Streamlit session state tracks uploaded report ID and active result view.
frontend.pages_and_flows: Upload: drag-and-drop file upload widget, sector selector (WASH MVP), submit triggers FastAPI upload and check endpoints. Results: tabular display of gap_findings by SPHERE indicator code, confidence tier (rule-based vs LLM-inferred), and severity. Analyst can mark findings as confirmed or false-positive. Dashboard: aggregate compliance rates by indicator across uploaded reports with Plotly bar charts.
frontend.performance_notes: Streamlit polling for compliance check completion using st.experimental_rerun with 3-second interval. Vertex AI latency expected 5-15 seconds per report; spinner with progress indication.

security.auth_design: GCP Identity-Aware Proxy on Cloud Run service restricts access to GCP workspace users in the authorized IAP audience. No application-level auth required; IAP handles identity.
security.secrets_management: MongoDB connection string and Vertex AI project credentials stored in GCP Secret Manager, accessed by Cloud Run service account at runtime.
security.privacy_controls: Program reports stored in MongoDB with retention TTL index of 180 days. Cloud Storage uploaded files have lifecycle policy deleting after 180 days. No personal beneficiary data extracted or stored.

data.entities: program_reports (MongoDB document: _id, filename, sector, upload_timestamp, parsing_status, raw_text, extracted_indicators JSONB). sphere_standards (MongoDB document: _id, standard_code, sector, indicator_name, threshold_value, threshold_unit, verification_method ENUM quantitative/qualitative, sphere_handbook_section). validation_results (MongoDB document: _id, report_id ref, standard_code, result_type ENUM rule_based/llm_inferred, compliant BOOL, gap_finding TEXT, confidence_score FLOAT, analyst_review ENUM pending/confirmed/false_positive, created_at). gap_findings (view/aggregation: report_id, sector, total_indicators, compliant_count, non_compliant_count, unverifiable_count).
data.storage_design: MongoDB 7 Atlas cluster on GCP (M10 tier or free shared). TTL index on program_reports.upload_timestamp for 180-day auto-deletion. GCP Cloud Storage bucket for raw uploaded report files with 180-day lifecycle rule.
data.analytics_events: Compliance check completion event logged to MongoDB with timestamp, report_id, rule_check_count, llm_check_count, llm_latency_ms, total_gap_count for prototype evaluation metrics.

devops.deployment_topology: Two Cloud Run services: streamlit-app (Streamlit frontend, authenticated via IAP) and compliance-api (FastAPI backend, internal service-to-service calls only). Both in same GCP project and VPC. MongoDB Atlas GCP cluster in same region.
devops.ci_cd_design: Cloud Build trigger on main branch push: pytest for rule engine unit tests and validation result schema tests, Docker build, push to Artifact Registry, deploy to Cloud Run. No staging environment for prototype; direct to prototype environment.
devops.observability_stack: Cloud Logging with structured JSON from FastAPI. Cloud Monitoring for Cloud Run request count, latency, and instance count. Custom log-based metric: compliance_check_duration_seconds for Vertex AI call latency tracking.

target_output.thinking_summary: Designing a research prototype for SPHERE compliance checking. Streamlit plus FastAPI on GCP Cloud Run is the right lightweight stack for prototype scale. The key architectural decisions are: MongoDB document model for variable-structure program reports, Vertex AI Gemini Pro for narrative extraction within GCP data residency boundary, and versioned YAML-based SPHERE rule corpus to enable rapid iteration during research. Strong quality target requires richly detailed sections throughout.
target_output.title: SPHERE Compliance Checker Research Prototype
target_output.executive_summary: A research prototype built with Streamlit and FastAPI on GCP Cloud Run that automates compliance checking of humanitarian program reports against SPHERE minimum standards. A rule-based engine validates quantitative WASH indicators (liters per capita per day, latrine coverage ratios) while Vertex AI Gemini Pro extracts implied service coverage levels from narrative sections within the GCP project boundary. MongoDB 7 stores program reports, SPHERE rules as versioned documents, and structured gap findings for analyst review and aggregate compliance analytics.
target_output.architecture_overview: Two GCP Cloud Run services: a Streamlit frontend protected by GCP Identity-Aware Proxy and a FastAPI compliance API accessible via service-to-service calls only. Analysts upload program reports via the Streamlit interface, which posts to the compliance API. The API stores the report in MongoDB Atlas and Cloud Storage, then triggers an async compliance evaluation task. The rule engine loads SPHERE standards from a versioned MongoDB collection and evaluates extracted indicators against quantitative thresholds. For qualitative narrative sections without explicit metrics, the API invokes Vertex AI Gemini Pro with a structured extraction prompt to infer implied coverage levels and flag probable SPHERE gaps. Results are persisted to MongoDB validation_results and polled by the Streamlit frontend. GCP IAP enforces access restriction to authorized workspace users; no program report content leaves the GCP project.
target_output.technology_stack: Frontend: Streamlit with Plotly for gap analytics charts. Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, Motor async MongoDB driver. LLM: Vertex AI Gemini Pro via google-cloud-aiplatform SDK, invoked within GCP project boundary. Database: MongoDB 7 Atlas cluster on GCP with TTL indexes for 180-day auto-deletion. Storage: GCP Cloud Storage for raw report files. Auth: GCP Identity-Aware Proxy. CI/CD: Cloud Build with pytest and Docker. Observability: Cloud Logging, Cloud Monitoring.
target_output.functional_feature_map: Report Upload and Parsing: PDF, DOCX, and JSON report format ingestion via Streamlit multipart upload; text extraction with sector and indicator field identification. Rule-Based SPHERE Validation: structured evaluation of extracted quantitative indicators against SPHERE threshold values from MongoDB sphere_standards collection; results tagged as rule_based with compliant/non_compliant/unverifiable status. LLM Narrative Gap Extraction: Vertex AI Gemini Pro prompt for narrative sections without quantitative data; model returns inferred coverage level and probable gap with confidence score; results tagged as llm_inferred. Analyst Review Interface: Streamlit table of gap_findings with analyst confirm/false-positive action per finding. Compliance Dashboard: Plotly bar charts of compliance rates by SPHERE indicator across uploaded reports for research evaluation.
target_output.system_components: Cloud Run streamlit-app service (IAP-protected), Cloud Run compliance-api service (internal VPC only), MongoDB Atlas M10 cluster on GCP with TTL indexes and sphere_standards versioned collection, GCP Cloud Storage bucket for raw report files with 180-day lifecycle, Vertex AI Gemini Pro via Vertex AI API (GCP-internal invocation), GCP Secret Manager for MongoDB connection string and service credentials, GCP Artifact Registry for Docker images, Cloud Build CI pipeline.
target_output.workflows: WASH Compliance Check: analyst uploads WASH sector report PDF via Streamlit, compliance-api extracts text and parses section structure, rule engine loads SPHERE WASH standards version 2018 from MongoDB, evaluates latrine coverage ratio (target: 1 per 20 persons), water quantity (target: 15L/person/day), and 8 additional WASH indicators against extracted values, persists rule_based results to validation_results. For narrative sections with no explicit metrics, FastAPI invokes Vertex AI Gemini Pro with prompt: 'Extract implied water access coverage level from the following text and assess compliance with SPHERE standard {indicator_code}: {section_text}', model response parsed for compliance assessment and gap description, persisted as llm_inferred validation_result with confidence_score. Analyst Review: analyst opens results page in Streamlit, reviews flagged indicators with rule type and confidence, marks findings as confirmed or false_positive, confirmed findings exported to CSV gap report for supervisor. Research Evaluation: prototype admin reviews aggregate analytics across all uploaded reports to assess rule engine precision/recall and LLM extraction accuracy for research publication.
target_output.data_model: program_reports MongoDB collection: {_id: ObjectId, filename: string, sector: enum wash/food/shelter/health, upload_timestamp: Date (TTL 180d), parsing_status: enum pending/completed/failed, raw_text: string, extracted_indicators: [{indicator_code: string, extracted_value: float, extracted_unit: string, source_section: string}], uploaded_by: string}. sphere_standards MongoDB collection: {_id: ObjectId, standard_code: string (e.g. WASH-QS-01), sector: string, indicator_name: string, threshold_value: float, threshold_unit: string, threshold_direction: enum minimum/maximum, verification_method: enum quantitative/qualitative, sphere_handbook_section: string, version: string, active: bool}. validation_results MongoDB collection: {_id: ObjectId, report_id: ObjectId ref, standard_code: string, result_type: enum rule_based/llm_inferred, compliant: bool, extracted_value: float, threshold_value: float, gap_finding: string, confidence_score: float (0-1), analyst_review: enum pending/confirmed/false_positive, llm_prompt_hash: string, created_at: Date}.
target_output.api_design: POST /v1/reports/upload accepts multipart form with file and sector field, returns {report_id, parsing_status}. POST /v1/reports/{id}/check triggers async compliance evaluation, returns {job_id, estimated_seconds}. GET /v1/reports/{id}/status returns {status, rule_checks_completed, llm_checks_completed, total_checks}. GET /v1/reports/{id}/results returns {report_id, gap_findings: [{standard_code, indicator_name, compliant, result_type, gap_finding, confidence_score, analyst_review}]}. PATCH /v1/results/{id}/review body {analyst_review: confirmed|false_positive}. GET /v1/reports/{id}/results/export returns gzipped JSON compliance gap report. GET /v1/admin/standards lists versioned sphere_standards with active flag. POST /v1/admin/standards upserts rule with version.
target_output.security_and_compliance: GCP Identity-Aware Proxy restricts streamlit-app Cloud Run service to authorized GCP workspace users in the IAP audience; no anonymous access. compliance-api Cloud Run service configured with ingress set to internal-only, accepting only service-to-service calls from streamlit-app service account. Program report content sent to Vertex AI Gemini Pro only via google-cloud-aiplatform SDK within the GCP project -- no external LLM API calls. MongoDB Atlas cluster deployed on GCP us-central1 matching Cloud Run region to maintain data residency. Secrets (MongoDB URI, service account key) stored in GCP Secret Manager with Secret Manager Secret Accessor role bound to Cloud Run service account. Report files and MongoDB data subject to 180-day TTL deletion per privacy_retention_policy. SPHERE Handbook standards loaded as versioned documents -- rule updates are tracked with version field rather than in-place mutation.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: Cloud Build trigger on main branch push to feature/prototype branch: runs pytest for rule engine indicator evaluation tests, LLM prompt format tests, and validation result schema tests, builds Docker images for streamlit-app and compliance-api, pushes to Artifact Registry, deploys both services to Cloud Run prototype environment. No staging environment for research prototype. Cloud Run min-instances set to 0 for both services to minimize idle cost. MongoDB Atlas M10 free shared cluster for prototype; upgrade to M10 dedicated if throughput becomes limiting. SPHERE standards collection seeded via Python migration script run from Cloud Build on first deploy; subsequent updates via /v1/admin/standards API. Rollback: Cloud Run revision traffic splitting allows instant revert to previous revision.
target_output.observability: Cloud Logging structured JSON from FastAPI with request_id, report_id, operation, duration_ms, and llm_status on each compliance check. Cloud Monitoring dashboards: Cloud Run request latency p50/p95, instance count, and error rate. Log-based custom metric compliance_check_duration_seconds tracking end-to-end check latency for research evaluation. Log-based metric vertex_ai_call_latency_ms for LLM invocation performance tracking. Alert on Cloud Run error rate exceeding 5% triggers email notification to prototype maintainer.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: Cloud Run streamlit-app scales to zero between analyst sessions; estimated $30/month at prototype usage. Cloud Run compliance-api scales to zero when not checking; $20/month. Vertex AI Gemini Pro at 1000 tokens average per narrative section, 10 sections per report, 50 reports/month: approximately $30/month at current pricing. MongoDB Atlas M10 free shared tier: $0. Cloud Storage $5/month. Total approximately $85/month, well under $500 constraint.
target_output.phased_implementation: Phase 1 MVP (4 weeks): WASH sector rule-based compliance engine with 10 SPHERE WASH indicators in MongoDB, report PDF upload and text extraction, Streamlit results view, basic analyst review interface, Vertex AI narrative extraction for qualitative WASH sections, Cloud Run deployment with IAP. Phase 2 (4 weeks): food and shelter sector SPHERE rules, multi-sector report handling, Plotly compliance analytics dashboard, CSV gap report export, rule corpus versioning. Phase 3 (research evaluation, 4 weeks): health sector rules, precision/recall evaluation of LLM extraction against manually labeled test set, prototype findings documented for production feasibility decision.
target_output.development_guidelines: SPHERE rules stored as YAML files under rules/sphere/ directory in version control; loaded into MongoDB on deploy via seed script. Pytest for unit tests of each SPHERE indicator evaluation function using synthetic extracted_indicator values above, below, and at threshold. LLM prompt templates versioned in prompts/sphere_narrative.py; prompt hash stored in validation_results for reproducibility. Streamlit app code in streamlit_app/ directory separate from compliance-api in api/ directory. No hardcoded SPHERE threshold values in application code -- all thresholds sourced from MongoDB sphere_standards at runtime for configuration flexibility.
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: Risk: SPHERE indicator extraction from poorly structured PDF reports produces incorrect extracted_value, leading to false compliance determinations. Mitigation: unverifiable status when extraction confidence is low; analyst review required for all llm_inferred findings. Risk: Vertex AI Gemini Pro narrative extraction inconsistency for non-English report sections. Mitigation: multilingual capability of Gemini Pro; prototype evaluation will measure extraction accuracy by language. Tradeoff: MongoDB document model over relational provides schema flexibility during research but makes complex cross-sector aggregate queries less efficient -- acceptable for prototype volume. Tradeoff: Streamlit over React SPA enables rapid iteration by data scientists at cost of limited UI customization.
target_output.open_questions_resolved: Chose MongoDB document model over PostgreSQL JSONB for schema variability across SPHERE sectors during prototype phase. Confirmed Vertex AI Gemini Pro over self-hosted model to meet data residency constraint within GCP without GPU infrastructure cost. Selected GCP IAP over bespoke auth for prototype simplicity. Chose YAML rule corpus in version control seeded to MongoDB rather than hard-coded thresholds to enable rapid iteration during research without code changes.

metadata.notes: Greenfield research prototype for SPHERE compliance checking with Vertex AI narrative extraction, versioned YAML rule corpus in MongoDB, and GCP IAP access control.
